Not too many valet services help the way the Aloft staff does.
Food inside had a nice variety. Place is spotless and location is primo. Highly recommend
I always enjoy coming here! The ambiance is great, the food is decent, and it just has a different vibe.
Amazing clean healthy local breakfast! Was a little bit of a wait but definitely worth it!

Delicious food, great people and gorgeous scenery. 

Everyone needs to visit this place at least once! Or twice or 10 times.
A good experience with great food. Love the steak and shrimp. GO HIBACHI!! Also the soup was delish!

Went there today to look at minivans and not a soul talked to me. My wife and I have three kids and are busy-we plan to buy a minivan within the next 2 weeks and my wife thinks she really wants an odyessey. I checked it out in the showroom twice--opened the doors, sat in the seats, looked around. Was there about a half an hour. The whole time I was there, no one even asked if they could help me. I thought,  "screw it, I'm out."  Will go to Carmax or Toyota. My wife agrees.

I was introduced to this place by my mom. She was refinancing our house with a law firm a few doors down. The lady she was working with mentioned her sister owned the salon a few doors down. 

My mom was looking to donate her hair to Locks of Love, while I was looking for a good hair salon. My old stylist quit thus I was on the hunt for a new one. I'm so picky when it comes to my hair from past experiences. 

My stylist was Carly (not sure if I spelled her name right) and I've been going back ever since Spring of this year. It's very clean inside and quite a pleasant feeling. It's usually more livelier than it was today. Carly was on maternity leave and two secretary kind of girls were no longer there I believe. The only other people besides the stylist was her baby girl and significant other. 

I've never gotten my hair dyed or nails done here so I can't much insight on that. I always leave smiling about my new haircut. It's a bit of a drive from where I live but it's worth it to me. The past few times I've gone the pricing was pretty fair. Over the summer they had a promotion so I paid about 35 for my cut. Today since I didn't shampoo it was around 25 with tip. 

I haven't had a bad experience yet so you can expect me to keep coming back to see them!

I just love this place. One of the nicer (not cold and out dated) places in the Drexel Hill/Havertown/Upper Darby Area.

The staff is always friendly.  Service for me has always been quick.  Its a BYOB so that's a PLUS!!

 The food is always fresh. I usually do hibachi with family or friends.  But recently did Sushi and Kitchen service solo! And had an enjoyable meal. My server was nice and I enjoyed my meal in peace away from the excitement of the Hibachi room.
I honestly forgot it was there! 

You definitely can have two different experiences in the same restaurant between the two sides. I loved them both! 

I work in center city and have access to dozens of sushi places.  I must say Masami is in the same caliber of quality!!

I'm a parent so it's important to have kid friendly locations in my catalog. And this is one for sure. My daughter loves it when she goes. 

Also great for date night....its a BYOB as I already mentioned so DON'T forget the "Adult Bag".

I've driven by many of times and i'm so glad I stopped.  I ordered the beet salad and added chicken it was outstanding. Beets are difficult to make without them tasting like dirt. Highly recommend this salad. Great clean place and big enough for a group.

First time in after the remodel and was quite impressed.  Decor and patio area are wonderfully and comfortably laid out. Service was friendly and attentive but not intrusive.  Tried several menu options and everything was good.  Salmon was delicious.  Will definitely return.

My wife and I love this place.  Neil Brown his wife and the whole crew there make it dining there a true experience.  My favorite drink there is the Dark and Stormy which I will have on a really hot day.  Bacon Flight and the deviled eggs are a must.  We love to have little escapes there when we get a chance to have date night without the kids.

I changed my review........ overall it's good I just wish they spend more hands on, interpersonal relation atmosphere than a quick in and out. But the staff is super friendly, they are really pretty as well, The physiotherapist are great and it's a fantastic location. So yeah I guess it's OK
Waited 1:30 minutes for an Uber Eats delivery and finally had to cancel. Don't quote 35 min if you can't deliver! Very disappointing.

Excellent, out of this world pulled pork. Cole slaw is also delicious. Put it on top of your pork sandwich with some of their sauce, you will not be disappointed. Wonderful out door seating by the pinellas trail shaded by a big oak tree.
Room service is only open until 11pm and there is nothing nearby. The hotel is clean and they do have a free 5 - 7 happy hour. This place is just o.k.

I get annoyed when I go to a place that's open, yet the doors are locked and the staff makes me wait to come in. If you're open, you're open. If the doors were unlocked you wouldn't have to roll your eyes and come unlock it. 

Other than that food was quick. Faster that the iced coffee. Which she made and immediatly walked away from making me have to wait then eventually get pissed and have to reach over the counter to get. 

Ughhhhhh. Why do I do this to myself
Love this place! Food is delicious and the staff is so friendly. They always go our of their way to accommodate anything.

The employees provide okay service but the food isn't good. The chicken has a weird texture to it that makes it hard for me to eat. I gave this place a few chances but they have fallen short every time with the same problems. I will not be returning.

I'm extremely disappointed. I went in here with my expired coupon and they wouldn't even let me use the value of the Groupon per the Groupon regulations. They said I had to bring a print out which is totally against Groupon. They could've easily marked my coupon as redeemed instead they chose to turn me away when there wasn't even one person in the restaurant.  I wish I could give them zero stars.
The staff was very friendly and the food was quick but disgusting. I had fish tacos and will not be going back

This has some of the best Mexican comfort food in town. Tortillas have to be the best in Tucson. Beans are soooooo good. Never can go wrong with anything here. I've been eating here for a long time. 
The only things I've noticed over time is that the crew that works in the evening isn't as on point as the day crew.

I'm giving it 5 stars for the memories.  My wife took me to Santa Barbara for my birthday in 1996.  Saw "Mission Impossible" at the State Street Theater and then had dinner here.  I just remember loving the burger I had and the company.

I need to go back...

This is a great hotel. I stayed here for a conference and was in one of the updated atrium rooms. The hotel is everything that you would expect of a Gaylord property. There were plenty of restaurants, the grounds were perfectly maintained, and everything was beautiful. The resort fee was a bit annoying, but I've seen it at other hotels before. I will absolutely stay here again.

I came in a few months ago and was very impressed with the customer service. I wasn't ready to buy, but wanted to check out some mattresses. Bruce showed me a bunch of different mattresses and gave me a price range. I never felt pressured and felt like I got the same service I would have if I was ready to buy. I went in a couple weeks later and Bruce wasn't there, but Ryan helped me and gave me the same service as Bruce did! I was a little nervous because I wasn't sure if the experience would be the same as the first time, but it was! I ended up buying my mattress that day and I'm loving it! I was very impresses with the entire situation!

Two years later and I still recommend GVDR for all your inert materials needs. Timely, professional and easy to work with. 
Just got a delivery today and the driver was friendly and professional and he didn't even run over my plants on the way out like some of those dudes do. 
Rock on w Green Valley.

There isn't a bad seat anywhere in the beauty. I have amazing memories in this stadium and I hope it stands for decades to come! 

I would really like to see some more events here, though. With such a beautiful open-air stadium, it would be awesome to see more events and concerts!

Being Jamaican and growing up in a Jamaican Restaurant I have been searching for good Jamaican food in Philadelphia. I have tried several Jamaican / Caribbean places in Philadelphia and this is by far the best place yet. I even took my parents to the Jerk Hut when they came to visit and they were impressed as well. 

The Jerk Hut experience is all about the food (I haven't had the pleasure of hanging outside on the Jerk Hut Lawn/Patio and listening to Reggae Vibes but I am looking forward to it in the Spring and Summer. 

Compared to other Jamaican food places the Jerk Hut is pricey but is more than reasonable when compared to other food sorts.  In my opinion it is worth it. 

Service: Remember you are getting a Jamaican experience, they are on Island Time! 

The Jerk Hut is good if you want to relax and enjoy some quality Jamaican food. It appears that once the outside patio/lawn opens the Jerk Hut would be a great place to accommodate a group of friends to casually get together BYOB, listen to reggae vibes and play dominoes.

I went 4 times in one week while visiting family across the canal. Take out twice, solo dine in and 8 person family lunch. All rocked it. 

There is a great humor and care from the staff. Employee would be to harsh a word, they are working, but it comes from a place deeper than just having a job. 

They
 cared about what we ate, they cared that we came back, and they cared about each other.

All that being said, they had to have great food to spend money here 4x. Crab lump meat Spinach Salad, start there and you'll know. They try more.
Thanks Zydeco's it was worth checking you out.

I was in search of a great nail salon in St. Louis for months and finally found it! Kathy always does a fantastic job with my nails. I had never tried the gel powder before coming to Bello and now I will never go back to regular gel. The gel powder is a bit more expensive but well worth it because it lasts for 3-4 weeks without chips or wearing down my nails. The atmosphere is great too, very relaxing, I am always offered wine. I love this place!
So much hype with ehh delivery. The biscuits and gravy were SO hard and lacking flavor.

Uh oh! They serve (bad) prepackaged spinach artichoke dip here. There's almost nothing worse than knowing your meal was assembled from packaged food by someone no more talented than yourself for any money. Don't waste time on breakfast or dinner here. I don't know that it will last serving food like that.

I came here with a few friends for my birthday celebration and boy was I impressed. We arrived just before closing which we didn't intend to, and the staff didn't seem a tad bit bothered. They attended to us, were generous, and the food was absolutely delightful. I have lived in North County all my life and am excited to have found you all. I will be returning. Thank you for the amazing service!

Walked in unsure of what to expect and walked out amazed. It was one of the best boba drinks I have ever had and to find that in Edmonton of all places was quite surprising! I had the grapefruit green tea and it was so refreshing. The boba was great and the drinks are 5 star because they are made with fresh fruit. They didn't skimp on the boba and it wasn't overly sweet. Everyone I went with loved the drinks they ordered.

Small, but quaint. Only two seating booths and a bar stool seating. I ordered the snow crab and shrimp steamed special for about $21. It's a good portion for two people and it comes with potatoes, broccoli, and one corn. The seasoning isn't too overpowering, and there's a nice bed of butter at the bottom of the tray to soak the seafood while you eat. They also sell small snacks and chips. Will definitely come again.

We did not get a good experience from the start. The moment we walked in, the manager greeted us in a lackluster style. 

The buffet was okay, most of the food was lukewarm...which is never good for a restaurant. The only dishes that shined through were the Chicken tikka masala (delicious!) and the kheer (unusually good with a pinch of cinnamon!). The rest of the dishes were just okay. The rice tasted undercooked. And while we were eating, the manager started closing down the buffet...that is never good while customers are eating! It all felt really rushed :(

All in all, lukewarm food, lousy customer service and the manager was in a hurry.

Have since moved away from Nashville, and when I visit other shelters they feel so sad and depressing. The Nashville Humans Society makes it a place I just liked to visit. Go walk a dog out back who hasn't been loved on! Perfect visit for someone who  can't commit to a dog. 

For anyone who's nervous about getting a dog, NHA was great the few times I did need behavioral support for my dog. And I ran into one of their employees at PetSmart and my dog was so excited to see this sweet man again (who of course knew her by name "JUNO!"). Don't buy at a pet store. Come here.

I went here as part of the bridal party before my friend's wedding. I'm not from the area and am generally nervous when going to new nail places because I have high standards. I was blown away by the amazing experience at Nouvelle.
The entire place looked extremely clean, all the people working there were friendly, and the woman at the front desk went above and beyond to make sure we enjoyed our services - making us mimosas, bringing us water, and offering to get us pizza from the restaurant down the street if we wanted it! The French tips were freehand painted and came out beautifully. It's been almost two weeks now and the polish still looks shiny and new! I got the Orange pedicure and it was super fun and smelled amazing! I thought it was really cool how they rubbed orange slices on my legs, along with warm stones, orange scented lotion, etc.
The prices are also very reasonable for the services provided. I would recommend this place to anyone living in the area or in need of a nail service while visiting the Tampa area.
Food is rediculously good. Had the Brussel sprouts and the artichoke dip for appetizer. Tri tip dinner with cheesy potatoes was amazing. Will definitely be back next time we visit Reno

Has to be the best mom and pop shop in town. Matter of fact forget that best in Tampa. EVERYTHING in this place is GREAT and I do mean EVERYTHING. 

Turkey club is a must have. ( my fav )
Poor customer service.  Oversold rooms and didn't have any available.  Very disappointed with their lack of customer service.

All in all I would say this place is pretty nice the only reason I can't give 5 stars it seems like  Service was decent not great, and the hostess was very unfriendly kind of had   RBF. Tried the beer it was good and the food was pretty decent also I will probly go back

Do not go here!! They claimed my 4 year old child had 2 cavities. The bill, with insurnace,  was going to be almost $800!! I asked for a copy of his xrays. When I got home,  they gave me someone else's xrays (it had the name and address of the other patient on it). I got a second opinion and the dentist said he didn't need the procedure that tlc said he needed. My child did have a cavity for a different tooth (not the one tlc said he had). Also, the dentist I went to for the second opinion would only charge me $60 for the procedure.

Never had a problem with renting tuxes here for weddings in the past but do NOT buy a suit here.

The prices are ehh. I guess that's what they are for designer suits but the sales associates are the most aggressive, degrading and selfish people I've ever encountered in retail.

Dealt with "Sheldon" tonight. Dude would not leave me alone and let me browse the store. Often yelling at me from the register as he's serving customers b/c I was in the wrong size section while I was actually checking out styles. He lectured and lectured me on his expertise in suits and treated me like a baby while I'm 29 and have about 6 suits already. Then told me he was "Giving me 15 seconds to pick out a suit" from a poor selection by the way "or he won't have his tailor do it for me." Seriously, what kind of customer service is that? I am a mild mannered guy, never lost my cool and never felt so disrespected by someone in retail before.

I do not recommend this store as I called to complain and the manager was more concerned with having me come in so they can make a sale than telling me how he was going to deal with his unprofessional employee.

I'm wondering if this has happened to others MW customers in Conshohocken area before. My first and probably last yelp review Ill ever do because I was that fired up.
Great food. I had the baked salmon Benedict. Wife had the Monte Cristo. We will be back.

Ordered a chocolate chip cake with buttercream icing and it was terrible. The worst cake ever at our first visit to this neighborhood bakery. The low quality ingredients and poor substitute for buttercream icing made this cake to visit the trash. Terrible product and one this bakery had very limited experience in producing.

This is my fave spot for Thai in Edmonton. Don't let the old school decor deter you. It's completely outdated, but the green Thai curry over coconut rice completely makes up for it. Totally reasonable prices too. Last year, if you visited them at Taste of Edmonton, this was not their finest work. Try the pad Thai again at the restaurant. So much better!  You'll forgive them for the sad ToE attempt. 

As for busy or dead, it's a crapshoot. I've seen both. Maybe worth a reso...?

Parking on 124th street in front. If that's full, take a right (east) at the Popeyes and then another right into the alley. There is parking out back.

Honey's you are a Philadelphia gem! I was pleasantly surprised when I came to Honey's last Sunday for brunch. I went around 10:00 am and we were able to be seated right away. The place was packed to begin with and the crowds just kept coming throughout the entire time we were there. My suggestion: try and get there early! 

However, the staff was extremely friendly, prompt, and attentive. My water glass was never empty and my coffee mug was always topped off... It was WONDERFUL. I felt like I was receiving the royal treatment. It felt good my friends.

As for the food: I had the Honey platter. I decided on blueberry pancakes (blueberries IN the batter), two scrambled eggs, turkey sausage, and the potatoes. My goodness. I think the thing that got me the most were the pancakes. They were perfectly fluffy and dense at the same time. I'm not sure how they did it. I wish I could have eaten 12 more of them. Next time I may seriously consider adding another order just because they are were so good. The eggs were not too wet or too dry, they were fantastic. They also have a selection of hot sauce on the table!! I was blown away... Cholula , Frank's Red Hot, and Sriracha were all at the tips of my fingers without having to nag our server (though I'm sure it wouldn't have bothered her at all). The breakfast potatoes were spectacular as well. They were Cajun potatoes and man the seasoning was spot on! 

I'll be back to Honey's soon! Maybe to try dinner? Nahhhh those pancakes were too good to not order next time!

My ten years of experience with HP&A has been totally positive from day one.

When I first moved to Philly 20 years ago, I shopped around for a therapist, didn't find anyone I liked and gave up. 

A few years later I was referred to a therapist at HP&A who has been everything I never found in the people I saw before - a great listener, accessible, warm, a true partner in my journey who knows when to challenge me and when to let me find my own way. 

During one period I was drawing a lot and my therapist brought in an art therapist from the practice to work with both of us to make sense of what I was doing. She knew her limits and knew when to get an expert to help. 

I am very glad that I schedule appointments with my therapist and that I don't have to go through another person. It's way more flexible to be able to call or text to change an appointment if necessary rather than go through an intermediary. 

I've witnessed their practice grow and have never seen or heard (until these Yelp reviews) anything negative. I chalk them up to growing pains and am very sorry they had less than stellar experiences. 

Lastly, I'll say that as positive an experience as I've had with HP&A, it might take a few tries to find the person that you click with as a therapist. If they match you with someone you don't like, ask for someone else.

A perfect blend of modern ballpark amenities and old-school, in-city ballparks like Fenway.  Architecturally appealing inside and out, and now that I've been seated at pretty much every level and section of the park I can honestly say that there is not a bad seat in the house.

Vendors are a-plenty, with a great variety in quality and style of food and drink.  Yeah, the prices are nuts but that's par for the course.

And am I crazy, but is the more upscale atmosphere of CB Park actually engendering a more upscale level of behavior among Phillies fans?  Seems that way, I think.  And that's a GOOD thing.... right???

Was fun.   Not sure why "roger"  got flamed in previous posts.   He did a great job.   Fast, friendly and got it all right.   6 people in our party,  and double check.   Food was decent.   You gotta know when you come here you're not getting 400 Beach quality food but it's not half bad.

Off the beaten path, cool wine spot. Music, food, and great ambiance. It's at the corner of the Bywater so I would def take a car at night. Once inside tho it's another world. GREAT wine selection!  You grab a bottle and some cheese and salami and you're set.

Great wine great cheese plate HORRIBLE customer service. From when they checked my ID outside, to the upstairs bar. One bartender (wish I got his name) was nice to me. Other than that, my friend went to check out downstairs with her wine and he said "what are you trying to do with this" UHH BUY YOUR WINE??? Another friend went to the upstairs bar and asked what pino grigios they had, he said "what's on the board" --they were all listed by brand name rather than type. RUDE. JUST SO RUDE.
You must check out the Gyros they are Great! There is a lot of Meat and if you are a Meat head like me! You will really appreciate that! Check this spot out for great Chicago style food!
Thank you so much for opening a Lutz location. Your food is awesome!  Your staff is quick and friendly.
Omg this place! I try and go every time I'm in town. Everything packs a super impressive flavor I didn't know could occur from dishes I've tried so many times before! Try everything!

The food was GREAT if you ever get seated. While I can understand it's a small establishment the person running the seating chart was just awful. They were not seating by any sort of logical reasoning and lots of people who waited one hour were getting cut by those who waited ten minutes. If you go be prepared

Come here for a lovely cuppa (that is fresh ground and never tastes bitter) after those blurry nights of too many whiskeys and honky tonk dancing. The venue itself has a relaxed atmosphere and the coffee is rich, caramel-like and delicious. Clean, tidy and friendly staff. Did I mention it is also very well priced coffee??

This was our first time visiting the tap room. We have been at the main brewery once before where there are twice as many taps, however the selection here is just perfect. Very cool place to sip and chill...bar area, second tier and lounge with leather couches are a wonderful setting to enjoy a few beer samplings. Enjoy!

Who can walk past this jewelry haven and not peek inside? It's a long corridor of shiny accessories--everything from headbands to rings to long and short necklaces. It was my incredibly luck that everything in the store was 50% off. The sale is what pushed me over to the 5 star rating. One staff member explained that they do this once per season to clear out their old merchandise and make room for new. 

I spent a good, solid 30 minutes here just browsing and eyeing different accessories. I walked away with 1 ring, 2 earrings and 2 bracelets all for $13. It has "trend" written all over it. You won't be able to find many classic styles of jewelry here. For instance, they had a whole section of bib-style necklaces, both shiny and neutral. It's organized by color, which makes shopping for a specific outfit incredibly easy.
I was amazed by every dish and so was my entire party. This is real home town momma in the kitchen cookin up some dem some vittles Great!!

I'll start off by saying that the moment I walked in the door, someone rushed to greet me! Thank you!!! That's so important to me, especially if I decide to make a purchase. 

 Walking through the store they have on display, beautiful bedroom, living room, and various accent pieces. If you're in the market... check it out! Unfortunately though, that's not what I was looking for. I came into the store looking for an office desk. My take, their desk display should  probably be removed from the floor, or they should have the pieces replaced. There were a couple of desks that I really liked, but the moment I saw the nicks, fingerprints, and dents on the furniture, I knew this would not to the store to buy from. After all, if I know it looks like that once I wear it down, I might as well purchase a higher quality piece that will be more difficult to damage. Welp! My search will continue...

Wow... a diamond in the rough! The meal we ate here was delicious and I can't wait to go back for more.

This cafeteria style southern cookery boasts a slightly different menu every day (although they offer their deliciously moist, tender, crispy and flavorful fried chicken almost every day). Everything we ordered seemed fresh and NOT from a box!

As sides for my fried chicken, I opted for the loaded mashed potatoes (blended with cheese and bacon) and corn. both were delicious and well seasoned. My husband got the mac and cheese which was oh so cheesy and velvety smooth!! The rolls were light and fluffy, and the cornbread was a little dense but had great flavor.

We also grabbed some dessert. The snickers chocolate cake was moist light and incredibly rich... great frosting too! My husband got the chocolate pie which was thick, with a strong chocolatey flavor, nice crust,  and a layer of airy meringue.

I can't wait to try the rest they have to offer: all you can eat ribs on Thursday's and Saturday's, prime rib on Saturdays, chicken and dressing on Tuesday's... let's keep this place in business!

Mo recently left Aspara, so I am following her to her new salon, Mockingbird Salon on S. 13th Street.  Mo is a phenomenal stylist, and even though it has been more than 3 months since my last cut, it still looks good. 

I found this salon on Yelp a year ago when I really needed a new stylist.  The first time I went Mo sat down with me to find out what kind of cut I wanted, and she gave me a cut that was perfect.  Since then she has done a really good job, but I really wish I had taken a photo of that cut, because she hasn't been able to do it again.

This is a great salon - everyone greets you when you go in, like they saw you just yesterday and were waiting for you to come back.  

I had an experience when I was late for my appointment and asked if they could squeeze me in. They told me no because of the timing - doing the  cuts between coloring, between perms, between drying and styling.  They didn't take me late because it would have screwed up the timing.  That explanation was fine with me, because I would want them to do the same when I'm there.

I lived in New Orleans for several years and have eaten at many of the city's finest restaurants, and Bacchanal stands out as one of, if not my absolute favorite of them all.  

On my visit the "restaurant" was not yet open.  A food truck happened to be serving the food that evening.  The food truck had no name, just 2 dudes making some wicked fare.  By the time I had made my way to the food truck they had 86'd almost their entire menu, except of a Sweet Potato Gnocchi dish that is by far the best pasta concoction I've had in my entire life.  3+ years later I can still recall exactly what I had; it's not very often that happens for me.

I highly recommend this place if you're after something out of the ordinary.  I've traveled all over the U.S. and nothing compares to Bacchanal.

Service was horrible. Lukewarm food and flat beer. It's too bad because the food was good but sat forever while our waiter disappeared so was served lukewarm. The beer was flat so impossible to judge flavor.  It was apparent the wait staff was much more interested in hanging out in the back then doing their job. When we asked about milk in the dessert we were told the owner wasn't present. Maybe that was problem.

Had a fabulous breakfast. Amazing staff loved the bottles of water on the
Tables. Woke up early and arrived at 7.30am. Perfect time. No wait and place only half full. My only disappointment.,,,the use of thick airy doughy white bread in dishes.  Really gagging. French toast dense flavorless after two bites. Amazing roasted tomato and goat cheese omelette  coffee strong and with fresh cream. If there was no thick doughy white bread on the menu would be five stars

I live less than a mile from here and just never got around to trying the brunch. It's just a really good, solid choice for the buffet. Some of the offerings were excellent. The homemade casseroles were as were the omelettes. The fried chicken and the pot roast were very good. I tend to judge places on their biscuits and gravy and those were a bit lacking or they would have gotten 5 stars. The Cherry cobbler, cakes and banana pudding looked good too but I'm avoiding sugar. This is certainly worth a try for a breakfast or brunch.

Anna, the owner, was friendly and listened to my concerns. She provided suggestions that fit my budget and ended up delivering beautiful hand bouquets and centerpieces for our wedding. The flowers were fresh and vibrant.

My husband and I live nearby and this is our "go to" restaurant.  We dine here 1 or 2 days per week.  I am mostly vegetarian and I love the selection!  Every week there are several specials, and they always include a few vegetarian and vegan selections.  The specials are always creative and different every week.  I have had vegan dishes as diverse as tamales or stuffed peppers.
1.  The atmosphere:  Friendly! Relaxed. It is heaven on summer days to sit (or stand!) outside, enjoy a drink and everybody knows your name. Inside the bar is great on winter days, especially in the pub snug with the fireplace. The delightful owner is often at the bar. Though I have never been to a real English pub, this feels authentic.
2.  The food: It is always delicious.  I love the vegetarian staples such as the garden tacos and the vegan sloppy joe.  Everyone loves the fish and chips.  There is delicious pizza and creative dishes with touches of thai, mexican and middle eastern flavors.  Meat eaters have many choices ranging from scotch eggs to sirloin steak.  The weekly specials are creative and always inspired! The price is reasonable. 
3. The service: The servers are attentive and relaxed.  We love Dani, Kristy, Jessie, Dan, Nina and all the rest!
4. Beer:  I am not the best judge of beer and will usually order a red wine like cab. sav. or malbec, but most of my friends are ferocious beer drinkers and go crazy for the home-brewed beer here.
This is as good as it gets for a neighborhood pub with all of the right ingredients. If I could change one thing about the restaurant it would be to put in a new floor in the bar, as the current one has had at least one too many beers spilled on it...  We love ya BR Brewpub!

I'd never heard of Zoup before, but apparently it's an east coast chain restaurant.  Don't let that scare you away, though.  The soups are...well...souper! :)  There were about a dozen different soup selections and they freely provided as many samples as you wished.  Oh, and they do have salads and sandwiches as well.

I ordered the half sandwich/half soup deal - Lobster Bisque with Roast Beef and Pepper Melt.  The sandwich was good, but I'll probably try a different sandwich next time.  The soup, however, was a winner.  Love me some Lobster Bisque!  I also tried samples of the Chicken Pot Pie soup and the Macaroni and Cheese soup (yes, mac and cheese soup!).  All excellent options for my future visits.

And...they have vegetable chips.  I tried some of the samples out on the counter.  Highly recommend!

I'm sure their business will boom throughout the winter ahead.  Hooray for new options in the West County Mall food court!

that the place was cold (no heat) and empty should have been a warning sign: don't eat here.
very expensive, almost no food served, very unsatisfying
the mint tea is watered down
the portions are almost non-existant
no plates, no opportunity to wash hands but you gave to eat with your fingers
terrible place, avoid!
Shrimp and grits was the best dish. The French toast with bananas foster was also amazing but if you can only get one dish shrimp and grits is it!

This is some of the best Thai food you can get anywhere. Very much a local place-- intimidating if you've never been before, but don't let that sway you! There are two sides--dining side and cafeteria side. The cafeteria side is my favorite. You can get two entrees and rice or noodles (get the noodles) for $5.50. It's amazing. My favorite entree is a toss us between the beef curry and the BBQ chicken wings. If you get the chicken wings, make sure you wash your hands.  It's the stickiest, but most delicious, sauce! There is absolutely no way you can be disappointed with this place.

Had Monday "Brunch" as I was in NOLA for the day. Food was exquisite. I had the bourbon banana pancakes with caramelized bacon. Awesome. The shot of Bourbon was good also. Family had variety of things, boudin omelette, breakfast sandwich, the burrito... everybody was satisfied and couldn't clean their plates.  A must for breakfast in the quarter.

This is a great Indian place. The service is excellent. The staff are all very friendly. 

We ordered butter chicken (mild) based on other reviews and it really was fantastic. The sauce was so smooth and delicious. We also ordered the lamb madras with mild to medium spice. It was also delicious. Next time I would make it more spicy though. 

We ordered the banarsi samosa for appetizer which was good. The sauces were what made it great though. Both the green and brown sauces were amazing. 

Garlic naan was good. Not the best I've had but still delicious. 

I absolutely recommend this place! We will be back.

Bacchanal is the icon of what I think a lot of us on Yelp look for - a hidden gem that is so much more than its exterior aesthetic would suggest.  It's seriously the definition of what one might find if open to straying off the beaten (and touristy) path, in any city, much less New Orleans.

The pieces just fit:  crappy, if not total shit hole exterior... odd little wine shop / bar as an entryway, surprisingly large patio, etc.

This is the one place I have to hit up every time I'm in town...

Liz cuts my hair and she's always SO AWESOME. She knows what's good for your hair and she knows exactly what you want, or better. She's so friendly and funny! This place has just been totally renovated about a few weeks ago and it looks awesome!!!!! 

Now when it comes to the other services, I've never tried yet, so this review is for Liz and her hair services here!

This place has lots of great vegan options! I stopped by and got a vegan chicken salad wrap and it was really great. They have many, many vegan selections and it's a great place just to pop in for lunch or takeout dinner!
Love the food here.we have never ever got oily bad food from here.it has always been great.

This year I have had the best enjoyment there.  I love the tent of vendors with amazing freebies!!!  I did not have a partner, so I played the water balloon sling shot (refreshing splash although I've been avoiding the rain today) against a kid involved in the event.  Interesting firefighters challenge!  Recommend not parking at Roy Holland park because the fee is $5.  There are about 4 free bus shuttle locations (train station, The Launch, a school, etc.); just look for the signs.  GOOOOO!!!.

They are taking canned food donation.

I'm not even sure who to be mad at! The florist or flowersfromyou.com. At this point I'm just fuming. I ordered an Easter bouquet and received what looked like a small sympathy bouquet.  Flowersfromyou.com are completely incompetent, the order # on the flowers is apparently different from the online service and the lady on the phone didn't want to give me my order number. I brought the flowers back, but the shop was closed. I left them with the neighbor shop that offered to return them to the owner in the morning.  Still no response from the shop or horrible online service. I wish I would have used 1800flowers.

Besides busy hustle & bustle (which you would expect from any cafe in Nawlins anyway) this place is great! 

These guys serve up some pretty tasty hasbrowns and pancakes. I recommend the big easy breakfast.

2.5 stars.

I wandered in here tonight after getting lost and very, very wet. I stood at the hostess/Please wait to be seated table and no one bothered to make eye contact with me or acknowledged me. Went to the bathroom and came back. Again no acknowledgement until the bartender asked if I needed to be seated. I had the choice of a small table sanwiched right in the middle of a bunch of tables or the bar. I chose the bar.

I ordered a Paulaner and the Copa burger with Spanish fries. The Paulaner came pretty fast and was pretty tasty. It's always nice when you find a place that cleans their taplines. Too many places around here don't. The Copa burger and fries came out after a bit. The fries were smokin' hot so I ate a good portion of the burger first. The Copa burger comes with mild green chiles, Monterey Jack,  and bacon on it. The chiles were decent, as was the cheese and bacon. Unfortunately my burger, which I ordered medium well to hopefully get medium, was more like medium rare. Lots of pink and juices. I like a little juice but not that much. The fries were decent but I'm not sure they were "award winning". And how serving fries with little green chiles and fried onions counts as being Spanish, I don't know.

The service was ok, but not that great. It took a long time to get a second beer. It seemed like they were understaffed for how many people were there. That and/or I was getting ignored. 

I might come back again and see if this was just an off night. Right now I"m thinking it might be a place to get a beer and go somewhere else for dinner.

The owner is super nice and friendly. Great customer service. 

I came here for a few yoga classes, but what I really want to recommend are the massages! Specifically for Shea! She was so amazing, and kind. I was a very stressed out graduate student, with a lot of tension in my back from studying. After 1 massage with her, I had never felt so relaxed in my life. Life changing really. I actually fell asleep a few times during the massage. She has the most healing touch. I also kept dozing off throughout the day afterwards - I was that relaxed! Head to toe relaxation.

Her set up is very clean. I like things to be quiet during a massage, and she respected that :)

I bought a pkg of massages, and was very sad when I used my last one up. I have had many massages since them, high end joints in Center City, as far away as high end resorts in Phuket, Thailand, and I must say I still always think of Shea and coming back to Experience Yoga. 

Only small con, the massage room is in a back room that is separated from a rear exit by a screen. One time someone left through there, and it was a bit distracting.

Good cofffee...
Great service, a small cramped restaurant. Crab omlette was delightful, perfect poach eggs with corned beef hash, needed to caramelize with potatoes and onions a bit more before serving. 
French toast was old school with real loaf french bread soaked and pan fried in butter. Great. 
Biscuits were hard and lacked that soft fluffy interior I enjoy. 
Fresh squeezed grapefuit juice, fresh and cold.
Shrimp and grits to die for. 

T Bert

This is the best hardware store ever!  It is locally owned and whenever I need how to advice on what I need or how to fix something! I know I can walk in and get the help I need.  The staff is very friendly and helpful.  Couldn't recommend it more!
This is a great breakfast spot! All of the employees are very kind and professional. Kathryn was our server and she did a great job! Overall a great experience and I will definitely be coming back.

The name of the game regarding LensCrafters is paying a pretty and painful premium for the convenience of getting your glasses within the hour, and that's IF they have the lenses you need in stock.   


Even when I used a 40% off coupon,  I was still in severe sticker shock regarding the final cost - in excess of $500.00.  In my opinion, it's crazy,  as well as obnoxious and pretentious borderline thievery.   

If you purchase their lens protection/replacement guarantee/warrantee option that you have to pay extra for, be warned that there is a big catch as well. If you want your lenses replaced, (if you scratch or damage them), you have to pay $25 each time.  Many other eye glass places I have purchased this plan from didn't require any additional cost(s).

It's basically nickel and dime cut throat city; and then some.  I just wanted a pair of glasses at a fair price.  In the past, I have literally purchased 2 pairs of comparable glasses elsewhere for the price I paid for one pair at LensCrafters.  

Unless I sell one of my kidneys any time soon, I don't think I will go back to patronize them again.


UPDATE:  3-2018

I called them recently to see how much it would cost to just get replacement lenses in the frames I purchased the last time.  
The guy told me it would cost well over $600.00.  
Excuse me and say what?!
That's at least $100.00 more than the last time when I purchased the over priced frames along with the lenses.  Keep in mind that don't have a crazy and complicated prescription, and I didn't get all the bells and whistles the first time as well.  

It's crystal clear that these grotesquely overpriced thieves seriously do not want my business. 
The guy on the phone basically told me that the company doesn't care and wants to bend me over.  Unbelievable,  and how absolutely rude, disgusting and shocking. 


I would relish the news, and would not be surprised whatsoever, if this company disappears altogether.

Came back for brunch. It's now official, every meal is just as amazing as the last. Ordered the aborada: which is fried dough stuffed with plantains, cheese, guava paste and some other ingredients. So delicious, the sweet plantains and guava and melty cheese - how have I not heard of these before? The papas rellenas are more of a small plate than an appetizer - cheese, rice, beans, seasoned with spices, a totally different dish than American stuffed potatoes. The fuzzy navel is tropical and strong, like most of their drinks I've tried, has a taste of tangerine juice. The dominicana chicken comes with the tender marinated chicken in a side dish and the rice and beans mix in the main plate. The owner is so amazing - I had a party of 7 for brunch, and we called a day or 2 before, and he opened 30 minutes early for us so my out of town friend could experience the awesomeness before she had to hit the road. Love this place!

Our second time here was as great as the first! Drinks are cheap AND good even on the weekends. Our food always comes out super quick and is the best Mexican food I've had in St. Louis, and I have lived here all my life. The service is fantastic and it's great quality food for the price.

I love the Brewpub. It's a great place for hanging out with friends in the evenings you don't want to deal with the craziness of the Broad Ripple strip. It just has a really nice, cozy, laid-back feel. The beers are excellent - I highly recommend getting a sampler. The food is also really just really good pub food and lots of options on the menu. I remember the shepherd's pie I got reminded me of food I ate in the United Kingdom - very authentic!

Ligne Roset is a very old, 1860,  furniture making company with headquarters and factories in France.  The store in Manayunk is one of 200 worldwide.  Their furniture is ultra clean modern in design and of the highest quality.  You will hear fans of Ligne Roset telling you that their prices are great.  Gauged against design and craftsmanship this is true, but it is anything but cheap.  If you are a fan of Italian, modern French and Scandinavian design, scanning their web site, and store, is a must.  And they have a child's line, Les Minis, if you want to spoil the kid from the jump.

After the hailstorm in 2011, they did fantastic work on paintless body repair on my 1 series bmw. I was so impressed that i had them install aftermarket downpipes. 
Huge mistake. They installed the pipes and charged $300 which is upper level of normal pricing and they left a non-bmw BOLT in the drivers side resonator. 
The rattle it caused never bothered me, but trying to sell the car now I wanted whatever the foreign object was to be removed. 
I took the car back to WC body repair and the man there told me it's impossible that there's anything they did causing the rattle, it has to be a 'pipe problem' Uhm hello physics...how can a solid pipe cause a rattle like that..for example..put the car on ramps and took a small hammer to resonator and could hear a clinking sound.
Long story short, took the car to Bradley Motorwork to remove the pipe ..what do you know? a non-bmw bolt falls out-- probably accidentally swept into my pipes on WC auto body's floor by their mechanic. 

Very upset that the ?owner? tried to blame a solid metal pipe on a clinking sound. Not very professional. Should have some labor warranty.

Best part..."it'll cost you $300 for us to look and just as much for us to put the pipes back on', and 'you better have gaskets ready"...cool.

My second stay here. All I did was inform Kenny at the desk during check in that my fiancée and I were about to get married and we were in town for the weekend to celebrate her birthday. What followed was a commotion of niceties from the choc flavored strawberries and champagne brought up to our 32nd floor suite with wondrous view to the complimentary breakfast for our entire stay to unfettered access to the lounge. It was a very upscale and totally enjoyable weekend. I rarely give 5 stars but I will give this time because customer service was great and every employee seemed happy with their work and excited to help us. I would have fussed about parking but I got a very affordable $6 parking at the 'public' parking garage opposite the hotel. 
Thanks Kenny!!
Two visits on my trip. Excellent food and service! Had the oyster Benedict and the seafood Benedict.  Awesome grits as well!

lovely location, whip off the Interstate 74 & the keep turning right (you won't see the sign at first, believe it or not)t & head back, this WAS the Perfect store, until they changed the layout
walmarts are great for late in the middle of the night shopping

I love companion! this really is similar to the bread Co Panera outfits of the world. However, it is just a little bit better. The experience at companion is one that makes you feel like you are in a little French bistro, the ingredients are all top notch and the fresh bread is to die for. 

I highly recommend the fa la la la la sandwich, and any of the delicious soups or salads. Also if you love cornbread like I do, make sure you try a piece of theirs!

I've always hated the Phillies, and went down last Thursday to root for the Giants. Had such a good time. Very nice stadium and the staff there is tremendous. Very friendly, with good humor, and the Philly fans did not live up to their reputation of being horrible - they were actually a lot of fun! I had a really good time, and like the Phillies just a little more now! And the Giants won!! Let's see if I feel the same brotherly love when I go down to root for the Yankees! =D

This place is always at the top of my list for taking out-of-town friends and its good for bigger groups, or just for two. It mixes all the charm of a neighborhood cave-esque hole-in-the-wall with an amazing and ever-changing beer list and delicious BBQ (with vegan BBQ options too!!). What more could you ask for?!?

I would recommend the pulled pork, and it's awesome on their nachos as well (but I am a total nacho lover already). Be careful with the little goblet beers because some of them have upwards of 10% ABV and the tipsy comes quickly! My only regret is that I have now dined here six times and haven't gotten the bacon grease popcorn yet... 

The service is also always fantastic. Last night, they messed up our order a bit, but then we just ended up getting to try another kind of BBQ in addition, so I don't have a single complaint. Other restaurants should take note (*cough* Prohibition *cough*) and just give patrons the extra food when they accidentally confuse orders... it makes for a much happier experience overall :)

You are pleasantly surprised by this restaurant.  It looks like your average pub but it is so much more.  The menu is diverse with normal pub food like wings and burgers.  But they also have osso bucco and potato encrusted grouper.  I had the grouper and it was delicious.  Seared on the outside and moist and succulent on the inside.  My husband had the fish tacos.  They served 3 tacos with huge chunks of grilled mahi mahi.  He loved it. They have outdoor seating if you don't want to be part of the bar scene which is loud and noisy with lots of TVs going.  The outside overlooks a pond with a fountain.  Almost every night they have a $3.95 special.  That night it was meatloaf with mashed potatoes and gravy and a veggie.  My mom ordered it and she said it was killer delicious.  The only caveat is you have to order a drink with the special.  She had a decaf coffee so she was a cheap date that night.  But the prices on the menu are very reasonable and the amount of food is plentiful.  Last time we were there I had the house pasta with chicken.  It is done in a light cream sauce and is enough for at least two meals.  So good.

Okay so pizza is def in my top 3 fav foods so as you can imagine I've tried my share of pizza places. This is place is amazing pizza. As a newly former vegetarian I opted for Alfredo's special and Wow was it good. So good. I highly recommend this place. Great prices also. I'm new to the area so I'm so happy to have found such a gem.

Severs and bartender running around like crazy and can't get caught up. One bartender making all the drinks for the restaurant and has the entire bar section to wait on. Everyone at the bar told the manager she needs to have more help because it's not fair to the staff or the clients. For an area of town that is trying to recover and rebrand I suggest the restaurant get in step or get out.

A friend of my wife recommended Ice Cream 504 to her and so we checked it out.  Oh WOW!  Were we impressed with this place.  The "High Priest of Ice Cream", Michael, met us as we entered the shop and asked us if we would be interested in sampling his newest flavor.  Of course we said yes, because who turns down ice cream--especially free ice cream.  We tried the new flavor, a mixture of peanut butter, bananas, and honey, and found it to be incredible along with 10 other flavors--all great. 

The ice cream at this place is as fresh as the ice cream you used to churn on your back porch on Sunday afternoons.  It's that fresh because Michael does it the old fashioned way, one small batch at a time.  Because of this attention to the old fashioned method, all of the flavors we sampled were amazing, even the basic chocolate was delicious.

Ultimately, I chose a vanilla toffee/new flavor mixture in an in-house made waffle cone.  I considered two-scoops but stop before you attempt it.  What the High Priest calls "One scoop" on the menu is really two decent sized scoops.  

If you go into this lovely shop, don't read the menu and go, "$4.25 FOR ONE SCOOP!".  You may say for that price you could get Ben & Jerry's.  B&J are going to sell you a bunch of preservatives and maybe some ice cream.  Ice Cream 504 is going to sell you an experience---and oh yeah, some GREAT ice cream.

Stopped in here for breakfast and it was delicious! We ordered the Fleur de Lis omelette which was great! My sister and I split the food and it came out separated already which was definitely appreciated! Our server could have been more attentive but overall good experience, will definitely be back.
after the 4th visit, they remembered my name and drink order. great customer service and amazing barista skills. can't wait to come back to Nashville and stop in for another Cortado.

I love going to the Gaylord Opryland Resort.  Even though I live here - it is fun to go there and enjoy this venue.  Especially during the Christmas holidays.  It's a holiday tradition for my family.  I am so proud to see President Trump visited this location this week.  That says a lot!

The happy hour can't be beat and now I'm spoiled when I go anywhere else and only one craft beer is on happy hour or when the beer list is less than 5 or 6.  All of the bartenders are extremely nice. This isn't exactly a "dive" as people are calling it but it's not like the other bars in Old City.  They don't serve wine or specialize in elaborate mixed drinks (totally fine by me, just an fyi).  Go here for delicious beer, the food is standard "bar food" so I don't usually go here for a meal.

Positives: Mac and cheese, pulled pork, PO boy sandwich, biscuit, mashed potato, beer selection, nice server, portion size

Negatives: loud, fried tomatoes 

Overall: go for bbq and value

I love these guys and wish I could see them over in the Central STL region!  They are so popular, the line is always long, but here's the kicker - it is still one of the fastest moving lines of any food truck I've ever seen!

You must try the Short Rib Tacos!  They are so tender, they just melt in your mouth, and the flavors on this taco are unique!

This tour is so amazing. I came here as a child 10 years ago and then came back yesterday to experience it again. I was blown away. The tour guides were very knowledgeable, kind, and willing to answer all our questions. The tour was a good price and free beer at the end is always a plus. This company really knows what it's doing. 10/10 would recommend to anyone looking for something to do in St. Louis. Thanks again for the great experience.
Wow!!!  Whatta place.  Great eats and shops in this monstrous hotel.  Don't get lost!

I came here for the first time a couple days ago and I loved it. My friends suggested getting there early around 4:30 and they were right. We snagged an outside table and held it all night. This place fills up quick! The happy hour specials include $5 mixed drinks and appetizers. Throughout the whole night we split apps, flights, and ordered more drinks. We planned on sitting outside to play games, but ended up relaxing all night just talking. Food comes out pretty quick or maybe I was drinking too much to notice. Service gets spotty when it picks up, but our server did a great job of checking in. I haven't had a full meal to critique the food, but for an overall fun time I highly recommend this place. I'll definitely be back. 

We ordered from the appetizer menu: tater tots (not for me but maybe for you? big enough to split for sure), mac and cheese bites (not enough to split so I ate them all and it was yummy!), pretzel (split it bc wow it's a treat), and the margarita flatbread (we split this easy but I would suggest 2 to split between 4 it was my fav app). 

Families are welcome! So many kids and dogs!

Edit: I came here for brunch recently! I would recommend that as well. Pictures are below.
I have been here a couple times now with some great friends and the service is always amazing and the food is even better. Its is totally worth the wait to get seated!
Consistently good and big enough menu that you won't get bored.
Went in to eat and the shells were cold and there was little meat to the tacos.  My advice:  Go the one in Avon.

I'll never go here again! Sat at our table for 30 minutes without any waiter or waitress coming to our table. We walked out, and as we were leaving NOT ONE PERSON ASKED US WHY WE WERE LEAVING WITHOUT PAYING. I even went up to the manager or owner (blonde lady) and said we've been waiting quite a long time, could you send someone to our table? No one ever showed. Can't comment on the food because didn't get to eat!!!! Never again! And if I hear of anyone I know thinking of going there, I'll tell them don't bother

This place has the best chicken tikka masala curry in town.  I used to frequent this place often, but now I will only go there on Fridays or Sundays when they have the chicken tikka masala curry in the buffet.  The waiting staff is very attentive and friendly, too.

A quaint little Thai restaurant that probably, I'm guessing, doesn't get much attention due to location. And boy, is that unfortunate, because this place is great!

Our food came out in a matter of no time, one of the quickest wait-times I've seen actually. I don't think we had to wait longer than 5-7 minutes for our order. Why can't it be like this everywhere you go?

The green curry tofu is delicious, with lots of bamboo shoots, carrots, green peppers, and tofu balls. Piled on top of your coconut rice, it's simply divine. It's not too spicy, but a nice spice that builds up in your mouth the more you eat, with a full and deep flavor. Great vegetarian option if you're not into meat!

Also highly recommended is the garlic chicken served with a bowl of hot sauce on the side. It's got lots of toasted garlic on it, so full of flavor and kick!

We finished off with the black sticky rice and fresh mango, sooo good ... The mango was just as a mango should be -- sweet and juicy! The sticky rice was okay, I liked the sweetness but some of it was oddly hard and crunchy, and I prefer my sticky rice to be chewy and gooey. But still good! The condensed milk they serve on the side makes everything really sweet, so if you don't like things too sweet, skip the milk. It tastes fine just the way it is sans milk.

Besides the food, VERY fast and friendly service, and a beautifully decorated, cozy dining space. And FYI, parking is in the back.

Will definitely be back for more!

The food is pretty good. They have a wide variety to pick from which I like. I love the Thai red curry and green curry dishes. The scallion pancakes are better than most places too. My boyfriend is hooked on the coconut chicken. I wasn't a huge fan of their sushi, but then again, I only get the vegetable rolls (I got the oshinko roll), so don't go off my review if you get the "real" sushi. I've never eaten here but the delivery drivers are always nice. They are fairly priced too. Tres van & wu is usually my number one spot, but it's much more pricey. I've been going to Asian chef more because the prices are more reasonable and the quality is comparable.

Grab a taco on Tues. If it's too smoky in the bar, I'll bring the tacos to the bayou and watch ducks swimming along while washing down the grease with $2 Tecate. Then head back for more.

The only bar in town that I enjoy going to the pisser.

People love Finn's. I love Pal's more.

I organized a dinner here for me and 10 of my friends after reading all the great things about this place on Yelp. It definitely lived up to its expectations considering the huge group we had and the large quantities of alcohol we consumed. 

Two things to remember about this place. First its a BYOB - which is great, with no corkage fee! They happily gave us ice when we asked for it too which was really wonderful. The second, this place is CASH ONLY! so be prepared. They do have an ATM on the premises, but better to bring cash with you (to avoid the ATM fee).

I ordered their turkey burger and fries and it came out perfectly tender and juicy. Many of my friends ordered burgers as well and were extremely satisfied. We ordered fried green tomatoes and vegetable latkas for appetizers and they all disappeared. I would definitely recommend the latkas (!!) they were really delicious, and come with a great apple sauce!

I really appreciated how we got seated quickly for a party of 11 since they don't take recommendations, and the fact that they let us linger on that big table in the back. The wait staff was wonderful, and many of my friends said they would return! 

This is also great value for the dollar, since most entree dishes are under $15.
Amazing food. Pork belly and stuffed avocado are to die for. Be sure to ask for Dusty, she's hot and feisty.

What used to be Jagers is now the Irish Pol.  They did a great job of making it an enjoyable bar.  The beer selection is really good and the prices are great.  They have some good happy hour deals.  The bartenders were nice guys and remembered my name from my tab.  I thought that was pretty awesome.  Another hidden gem in Olde City.  Olde City needs more bars like this for sure.

If I could give Hungry Howie's a zero, I would. Their employees are always too high to function, they have a hard time remembering orders and constantly leave things either at the store or in their car. I ordered from Hungry Howie's on Pantano last night, it literally took an hour for the food to get here and then he forgot the drinks and the garlic sauce. He had to go back to the store to get it which literally took about 15 to 20 minutes so by the time our drinks got here we were already done eating. And another thing, do not use their split technique online, I split my payment between my debit card and cash and it put it all on my debit card. The delivery driver didn't even have me sign the receipt so I didn't realize that it was paid in full until I looked at my receipt. I literally complained about the service and this delivery driver thought that I gave him a $13 tip, didn't even say anything. I go in today to get my $10 back, because I only meant for him to have a $3 tip because his service sucked, and they're only excuse was, I'm sorry he's new. So you don't train your new guys to have the customers sign the receipt when they use a card? Because if he had, this whole ordeal would have been avoided. Never order delivery from Hungry Howie's on Pantano!

Husband and I finally tried this place for a date night a few months ago and am so glad we did! Super cool place and their outdoor area is very pretty. The workers are nice and helpful. We both ordered drinks, got an appetizer, and two entrees. The crab topped mac and cheese was SO GOOD! We definitely look forward to coming back.

Great food - check!  Awesome chips and salsa - check!  Cheap - check!  Quick service - check!  I love that they provide individual bowls for salsa dipping and my usual go to meal is Arroz Con Pollo... so good!

Went here after seeing a matinee show at the Broadway Theater at Pitman across the street. Upon walking in, we saw just one guy in the kitchen and one waitress, with several tables full of people who had no food yet. We had a bad feeling that this was going to be a longer than desired dining experience and debated leaving. We should have done so.

It took a while to get drinks, all of which were served in plastic cups. The sangria pitcher arrived, and we had to pour the bottle of wine ourselves - fine, as I assumed there were legal reasons for this. But we got nothing to stir it up with, so the wine just sat there on top. 

Eventually the waitress came back to get our food order, which took forever to arrive. My chorizo tacos were barely warm. The rice was hot at least? Again, everything was served on paper or plastic. It was sort of like eating at a glorified food truck. The food itself was ok, but generally pretty bland. Chorizo should not be bland, so I am not sure what went wrong there. My husband had a quesadilla, and my friend had mushroom enchiladas. Both said they were average. The rice was really the only thing I can say we enjoyed.

Finally, we go to pay the bill, and only then are we told that the place is basically cash only. This isn't posted anywhere. Not on the door. Not on the menu. Not mentioned by the waitress. She then says she can do a credit card if it's an emergency. Well, in our case we didn't have another choice. So she took our credit card and left the restaurant to go run it at another business or something - I don't know.

Considering the slow service and luke warm flavorless food...we won't be back.
My new favorite spot for frozen yogurt. Delicious options and great toppings. Farther then I would like to travel at times but whenever I'm on South St. it's a must have!

By far the best craft beer in Indianapolis. Their menu is extensive and very affordable.  The service is usually pretty good, but you should definitely show up early to get a good table.

I have been here every year for my birthday dinner and it never disappoints.  They have lots of vegetarian options and tons of great craft beer.  It's the oldest brewpub in Indiana--which says something!

Great service by Elena! She was attentive and helpful! The seafood benedict was exactly what I expected - delicious- the coffee was delicious, bloody marys were SUPER strong! bacon bloody mary has so much bacon! And the grits are perfect - no need for any extra cheese or butter!

I have been going to Honey's since 2006.  This place was one of the first restaurants to come to No Libs before it became the hip area it is today.  And, throughout the whole time their quality has remained consistently high.  I have never had lunch or dinner there.  When they first opened, they were only a breakfast place and by the time they began lunch and dinner service, I had become addicted to their breakfast offerings.  Thank goodness for the ability to get just one pancake.  This allows me to achieve full food coma and order a pancake with two eggs, biscuit and latke.  Everything is fresh and portions are generous.  The only downside is that on the weekends and holidays the wait can be atrocious.

Highly recommended.

I do a ton of shipping from various auction houses, and Craig and his team have been the most pleasant, dependable and easiest to deal with, by far.  I find their prices have been fairly reasonable and competitive...and I haven't had many of the issues that I have had elsewhere.  I'm very happy to have found them!
Migas with chorizo and avocado and fresh squeezed orange juice!! Totally hit the spot. Delicious biscuits and potatoes!!
Showed up on time and did a fantastic job for a low price.  Highly recommended!

Very modern, colorful decor which I enjoyed.  Definitely not your average hotel. Many seating areas in the lounge, along with a bar and a pool table. They've a decent infinity pool that also features many seating areas for groups and modern pop music playing outside and in the lounge area as well. Rooms were clean and comfy but there was a fair bit of traffic noise at night. I'd stay here again though.

One of the highlights of our trip to NOLA. We gobbled up our charcuterie in delight, sipping on delish wine as we took in the hearty music and warmth of the heat lamp. Great place to wind down from travels with friends. My partner accidentally dropped and left her ID here, but we were able to get it from the counter no problem the next day. Good place.

Apparently, QV Laundry is one of the few places in Philly where you can still smoke inside. I'm not sure why this is the case, but it does help me save money - I no longer have to go to McGlinchey's to get my clothes to stink like smoke... 

On the positive side, QV Laundry is relatively cheap and typically clean. It also happens to be the only laundrymat in the neighborhood. So until I suck it up and buy a washer/dryer, smokey-clothes it is.

Overall Bacchannal is just what any cool city needs....
After a long Essence weekend, a friend and I wanted to go out to somewhere chill so we tried Bacchannal.  It's a cool space.  It almost felt like a tree house upstairs.  It was a Sunday evening, and it rained quite a bit earlier in the day, so the bar wasn't crowded at all.

My friend and I bought a bottle of wine and headed upstairs...  The people weren't great at directing new guest very well.  So I asked the bartender upstairs where do we get glasses.  She gave me some wine glasses and said some somewhat snarky words (basically I was supposed to get wine glasses downstairs).  It's a hipster vybe so no big deal on that one. The band was cool and lively.  

My friend also ordered food.  The food was light, fresh, and very not New Orleanian (like in the seasoned category)... but still refreshing because food can be so heavy in NO.  

This is the funny part:  I ordered food later in the evening (with a few more snippy comments from the bartender), all the tables around me had gotten their food and the band was breaking down, so I headed to the bar and asked the somewhat snarky bartender to check on my order.  She seemed friendlier at that point. I ordered something from the vegetable menu, that I can't remember.  The then friendlier bartender goes to check on my food and about 8-10 mins later she offers us drinks on the house for the inconvenience while arriving with a dessert dish that I definitely did not order hahaha.   I tried a few bites, before realizing I ordered savory food and not sweet food.  But it was a REALLY good dessert and it was late, so I didn't bother complaining.  So you may not get what you asked for but it will probably be good lol

We love this place. Everything from huevos rancheros, grilled cheese, great salads, bananas foster French toast (fabulous), boidan, fresh squeezed juice. All lovingly prepared. Been there two days in a row - will again at some point this week. It is small and there tends to be a bit of a line so factor in 10 to 15 minutes. Cash only too but ATM in restaurant.

Awesome service, and even better work!! My nails turned out more than what I expected! Highly recommended, staff is super nice and will take care of you! Definitely my most favorite nail salon in town!

Updating my last review from a year ago. Still go to this place :) my friends and I love the boba, Pad thai, and the Pho is pretty good not the best I've had but its good. The shaved ice is the best too!!!!! YUMMMMMM!

I would give this place a 3.5 but i'll round up to a 4 because i like the concept. They have hot donuts, specialty donuts, and fried chicken. I got a pumpkin spice donut which was really tasty, but i do wish they served it hot. That would make this place AMAZING.  The friend chicken was very moist and flavorful. I would actually recommend trying that. They have a bench outside where we ate which was nice, bc its very limited seating inside.

Must review Lush, I went today and picked up a few bath bombs because I had been impatiently waiting while they were out of stock for a week. I came in and was very kindly talked to by the sales girl, she showed me all the wonderful products they carry and also gave me a hand demo of the Turkish Delight and also Ocean Salt. Both feel phenomenal. very soft hands and very pleased at the knowledge and friendly girls who work here. I was a Lush newbie and I'm glad they helped with all my questions. I was very happy at my visit and will very likely come back after I test out the very popular bath bombs. Also got some freebies to take home and try out!

This place was once my favorite but this recent experience wasn't the greatest. The waiter left us waiting for 15 minutes without ordering. We waited another 30 minutes for our drinks. It's not excuse for "being busy". There needs to be a better plan on time-management, customer service training, and for proper communication etiquette. Always takes forever for service yet so impatient for the check. Also, there's not enough vegan options and chocolate/caffeine-free desserts.

I have been here twice and have never received service. Both times sat at the bar and both times was ignored by the too cool for school hipster bartenders. I will never go back. First time I thought it was a fluke, but when it happened a second time, I was done.

Another bad service center.  You think you're getting better service from the pros when you go to an actual dealer and maybe don't mind paying for that supposed premium service.  What you don't know is that they cut corners and work on fixed hours.  The manufacturer defines how long it takes to do an oil change so they can't go over that, even if it requires them to remove certain parts in order to get to your oil filter instead of bending the part down for quick access, or not at all.  This happens at many dealers.  If Conicelli didn't do this to me, they just neglected to tell me that the pans underneath were missing or being held on by one screw.  I have a newer SUV too.

I had them do my brakes too.  Mistake.  Been back there 2-3 times because they squeak as if they were old.  They can't figure it out and they won't even put more anti-squeak goo on there to prevent me from coming back for more "no charge" looks at the situation.  I guess brakes are too hard for them to do.

I've even gone there normal hours when they said a mechanic would be available and when I showed up he was gone.  Apparently there are no other mechanics who understand Toyotas there?

I won't be going here again.  Clowns.

Me & my husband use to go here on the weekends for breakfast. But the last time we went we were seated & weren't given menus or silverware. Just seated & left there at the table we waited 20 min. Until I called over a waiter so we can place our order because we knew what we wanted. When I told the waiter that we had been waiting for 20 min for someone to come take our order she yelled at me "Can't you see that we're busy!" I've never been treated so badly here for so many years. Me & my husband got up & walked right out of there & went to Waffle House & were treated very good there.

Sad, poor little chicken. Overrated. SALTY to the point of being inedible. Does the" you got it dude" sample what he prepares? Obviously not.  The saving fare was the donut. Better stick with that. Unique flavors and very fresh.

The food here is fantastic, but the service. The service is OUTSTANDING. I went out to dinner with my mom and my 5 YO daughter and we sat at the bar bc it was available. Several different staff members stopped by and offered various things for my daughter: pizza dough, fruit to snack on, etc., for no reason other than to be friendly (food wasn't running late, she wasn't misbehaving). They offered us a free artichoke small plate because of a kitchen mistake. Lastly, I am on crutches and my mom didn't feel well so she went out to car early. One of the gentleman that waited on us carried our boxes out to the car and held my daughter's hand crossing the parking lot because he saw I couldn't carry anything while maneuvering my crutches. This restaurant has nailed it in terms of hiring and training. It was clear they have a staff of genuinely decent human beings and it was such a wonderful experience for us. Oh yeah, and the food is amazing! Highly recommend the artichoke small plate as an app!
Very very good restaurant! The food was sooo good and the service was great too!!!! Definitely coming back again:))

OMG! This place is AWESOME. Here on a trip and I hit the Jack pot. Not only n is the beer terrific but the food is amazing. The duck sliders are to kill for and the hanger steak was possibly one of the best I  have had.
They are a so, so when it comes to customer service. I feel as though they often low ball but what pawn shop doesn't?

I came here for a date thinking that this place would be classy and decent enough for that occasion. I was very disappointed first of all with our server who was rushing us and it was only 7:30 pm and the way he served our food he had no server etiquette at all! He just rushingly placed all our orders on the table like some dog food and the meat wasn't cooked the way we asked for which was medium rare. We couldn't find him and he was having attitude problems when we tried to ask for a medium rare steak politely  because we know it wasn't his fault . I really had high expectations for this place!  We still gave him an ok tip despite his service. So much for a goid dinner date but we ended up talking about the terrible service and food on the way home instead.

The wait time on St. Patrick's day was acceptable. The server was attentive. Our salad wasn't quite stirred enough, and lots of the salad didn't have dressing on it. I was able to quickly remedy that. The bread and cinnamon butter are always good. I ordered the ribeye medium-rare for dinner. The first one came out bland and over cooked. It's a very thin ribeye, and 60% was well done. The second one came out rare, but got to medium rare as I ate it. It too was bland. Anyone can throw a steak on a grill and cook it. Cody's, do you even season your steaks? I've had many good items on the menu, but this one needs some flavor. You may want to talk to your cooks, unless this is how it should come out. Baked potato with butter and sour cream was cooked well. You may want to get a new vendor for your ribeye, and a new cook.

I just wrote another review titled "getting it wrong."

This review is called "getting it so so so SO right."

Today was my third visit to Prep and Pastry.....in one week.
I read the reviews, prepared to be disappointed by the hype, and was not. There is typically a wait on the weekends (I have heard anywhere form 40 to 90 minute waits) so we chose to go weekdays.

Our first visit was to a very busy restaurant; however, we were seated and sever quite promptly. We started out with a maple dossant. My husband lovedddd it and I really liked it. It was just a precursor of the good things to come. The coffee was "expensive" on the menu. When we tasted  and drank the coffee we knew why. 
1. it is exo and majorly delicious 
2. They keep the refills coming
3. They offer to go cups

I ordered the banana foster filled french toast and the hubby got the poutine.
Delicious does not even describe the meal. I am not normally one to order sweets but the description reeled me and I am very thankful for that. 

On our second visit we got a little unlucky. They ran out of the cinnamon dossants from the time we sat to ordered. Okay, take two. They ran out of the blueberry earl grey scones in time we made the second order and he got behind the counter. Take three we ordered the snicker doodle cookie because we could see a dozen. It was delicious. I switched my order this time and ordered the pork belly beni and my husband got the am relleno.
