One of the things my husband remarked about was the size of the pepper (quite small). Turns out this was not a normal size as (without mention or prompt from us) a side of potatoes arrived at the table with someone (Manger? Owner?) telling my husband that the peppers were not up to their standards  and to please have some potatoes. My beni was actually kind of cool and while still good, I can only imagine how it would have been hot. I was overall happy with our meal and service when our server gave us as bonus cookie since we had a bit of bad luck on our visit. My husband raved about the initiative taken by the restaurant.

Today we returned and chose the french toast stuffed with berry compote and the tri tip sandwich. We also finally got to try the cinnamon sugar dossant. Amazing amazing amazing. I have loved most of the dishes and the two today did not disappoint. i was pretty jealous of the tri tip and will definitely be getting it the next time I am there (which will undoubtedly be soon). 

Prep & Pastry, you are getting it so so right. Keep up the good work. You have gained some big fans!
Nice atmosphere, professional staff. Emphasis is on pastry, and they do a good job. I would suggest more options that appeal to vegetarians though.

The positive - the building is beautiful.  Came in on Sunday around 7:45 after seeing a movie.  There was an hour wait for a table of two, but the bar was pretty empty so we decided to eat there.  

They had four of their own beer on tap. We tried the IPA and the Saison.  Decent but not great. I preferred the IPA.  The menu prices were a little higher than we expected ranging from $14 for a burger to near $30 for other menu items.  

We ordered the special for the evening, Lamb Gyro and the Roasted Chicken.  The lamb was tender but it had very little tzatziki sauce.  It needed some fresh tomatoes and onion.  I just used homemade ketchup that was served for my fries.  

They really must like fries here because that is what was served with the chicken as well. No veggies - just fries and chicken with some ketchup and honey mustard sauce.  For $18 a plate there had better be some more than meat and a starch. The chicken was on the dry side. 

The bartenders need to engage all of the customers better.  At least that is how I felt until some young 20 something ladies came in.  They seemed to get all the attention they wanted and more from the staff that night.   I am an out going person and tried to make small chit chat with them but they weren't having any of it. I guess us 30 something couples are left to the side as we don't have date potential for the staff. 

We did get our chicken comped due to the dryness. The bartender said that according to the sous-chef they are going to be changing the menu up a little in a couple of weeks.  I hope that adding veggies are included in the changes..  Not sure if we will be back to see if they do.

I wanted to like this place - I really enjoy spice shops - but this leaves much to be desired. It's very dusty and run down - I felt like I had stepped onto the set of an episode of "Dr Quinn Medicine Woman," and not in a good way. 

It's one thing to provide customers with an old timey experience, but you shouldn't be so dusty that they feel wary about purchasing food products from your establishment. 

There was nothing that they sold that was so well priced or so rare that I had to have it, so I left empty handed. 

Sadly, the spice shop is becoming extinct because if you really need something rare, you can always order it online for a good price from a company like Monteray Spice Co. 

But, if you're in Philadelphia and need something quickly or in just the right amount, head to "The Head Nut" at Reading Terminal Market.
Great atmosphere friendly staff and reasonably priced cuisine.Try the egg bake with a side of their terrific bread pudding.
Waxing is never fun but this place was very professional. The staff is very friendly. They accommodate me as a walking and I didn't even have to wait. Whitney is an amazing girl, very friendly.

I had a party here last Saturday night and I have to say it exceded my expectations! Our teacher Neil was amazing he kept us laughing and truly entertained us the whole time which was completely unexpected. We had a bit of a roudy crew but he went with it and made the night such a good time! I will defiantly be coming back for a private party in the future!

Holy Amazing.

Serious truffle cheese for only 29.00? Shut your mouth, add some grilled olives, and I can die peacefully. 

Add a splash of humour from the guys behind the counter, and you got a formula for success.

The JW has to be one of the prettiest buildings in downtown Indianapolis. Sleek and shiny, this hotel was built for the 2012 Super Bowl and boasts just over 1,000 rooms. Did ya happen to catch the neat wrap in May to promote the 100th running of the Indy 500? 

I stepped inside the JW for the first time this week to attend the PRSA International Conference. I was astounded at the size of the conference spaces, which span three levels and includes a Starbucks. However, the layout is not super intuitive and even with a map, I had to ask for directions. For example, the first floor of the conference space is not accessible via the first floor hotel lobby; you have to go up to second floor, over and down on an escalator.

It is well-equipped to host large meetings and conferences. However, the Starbucks could use some speedier staff to keep the line moving...

There aren't very many dining options within quick walking distance of the JW (particularly for breakfast) but High Velocity on the first floor is okay; I've heard better things about Osteria Pronto. 

*Note that parking costs an arm and a leg - it's about $40 for a full day in the JW garage, but that's the same as the White River garage across the street. Pick your poison, I suppose.

The place looked interesting enough, the live music was pleasant enough, the food and drink were over priced and marginal at best, but the coupe de grace was delivered when I received the check. I had asked for an additional portion of herbed mayonaise that accompanied my Cuban sandwich, upon examination of the check, I found a $1 charge for a table spoon of mayo. Unbeliebvable. I said something to the manager who did nothing but say "that's how it is."
Don't be lured by the interesting exterior......this place is not worth your time or money and the management is completely tone deaf on customer perception of value; $8 for a 10oz glass of common IPA, $18 for lack luster fish tacos plus mayo for $1. There are much better options in that town or accross the river.

I know there's that other Greek Festival every May out in Des Peres, but this remains my favorite Greek Festival in the city. 

Every Labor Day weekend, St. Nicholas opens up its doors, and Barnes opens up one of their garages, and the entire area around Kingshighway and Forest Park is inundated with all things Greek.  

The food is AMAZING and you can fill up for under $10 easily.  Buy a bottle of wine or a few Greek beers, sit back and enjoy the music.  The pastries are the best.  I could eat my weight in this baklava.  Quite frankly, as much as I love Olympia, I can't help but admit, this Greek food is better and the atmosphere certainly cannot be beat. 

This festival runs for the entire Labor Day weekend, and is filled with dancing, music, (as I said and couldn't shut up about before) amazing food, and plenty of OOOOPAAAAAAAs.  You can also buy plenty of items at the Flea Market of Athens such as T-shirts, olive oil, and other Greek goodies.  Certainly something to put on your calendar next Labor Day weekend.

Got take out yesterday and it was hands down the best I've had since moving to Apollo Beach. 
The Greek salad was on point and so was the mixed grill combo, would definitely recommend Pita Kabob

Went for a brunch on a sunday morning and wow didnt realize the wait would be so long for basic food. Heard from many people how good it was and was disapointed. Had the chicken and waffles and was a chicken strip with super dry syrup on a waffle. Expected more for the price
Great froyo spot! Lots of topping selections and the girl at the counter was incredibly nice. Would definitely go back!

For the price of the room we should of had better service and more perks. Free Wi-Fi would of been nice and they also blocked my hotspot from working not a good place.
I am glad the FCC found this out.

Best breakfast I had in New Orleans. I got their pancakes which were amazing and tried grits here for the first time. Of all the grits I tasted over the course of my trip these were the best. It's not a particularly fancy place and it is cluttered but that said for what it is- its great. Prices are very fair and the food is solid.
Good brew and good food!  Have been there a couple of time and never disappointed.

I love marathon! I have been in here few times and every time, I never had trouble waiting for a table or not getting anything below perfect! The prices are super reasonable and the menu selection is very diverse!

YumO 
This place is a little hidden Gem. Nothing fancy. Good food, nice people, good service. My husband got a individual Pizza. just the sauce alone was Fabulous and he's Italian and we know sauce.  I order the Chicken Jalapeno Sandwich... I was very satisfied. Our waitress was accommodating offered recommendations, checked on us several times.  Usual beach restaurants are pretentious and uppity. But Carpentaria. People there have a hip vibe, relaxed and welcoming. We were there for the Avocado Fest... and the theme was, Peace Love and Guacamole. We found our little gem to return to again. check it out if you are looking for a nice, relaxing, nothing fancy lunch or dinner. You wont regret it!
Just did take out last week. Both dishes were delicious. Gave us a good amount of fresh bread to dip. Really happy although the wait was longer than they said.
Had a surprisingly delicious tofu breakfast scramble. Good service, chill atmosphere. In a sea of Rittenhouse cheese steaks, this was a nice relief.

Amazing! Great food! Great ambiance! Great service! I got the bacon cheeseburger on a gluten free bun. The best burger I have ever had. I love finding great places that offer gluten free options. I'm definitely going to come back here! Prices are not to high. My burger was $10.39. The price is great for how big the burger was!

A one-stop, swanky shop for all window treatments, which I happened to stroll by last week. Just a peek inside and you will forget the name Home Depot. They make buying window shades easy (or should i say easier) and relaxing, something I didn't experience at the large stores. They also have the most up-to-date selection and handle everything for you including installation. They're on the "new" retail strip that has been emerging on Chestnut Street, near Rittenhouse.

We had not been to NO since Yelp was even developed, but have learned since to trust it's reviews. If you are in or near the French Quarter and want excellent breakfast food combined with world class service at a fair price, don't miss this place. Some of the best breakfast fare I have ever eaten!

Vegetarians beware! I went to Fez last night with my husband and two of our friends. Our friends ordered the 8-course meat feast, and my husband and I got the vegetarian feast (we're strict vegetarians, and for me, it's a serious ethical/moral issue). They brought us the bastilla (a pastry filled with egg and cinnamon - one with chicken, one without), and halfway through our course, I realized that the bastilla the waiter labeled as vegetarian actually had little bits of chicken in it. Not okay. Then, they took away my husband's drink before he was finished with it (he wasn't looking and didn't notice - but it still was about 1/3 full). The service was terrible - we had to ask for bread three times before anyone brought us any, and the waiter was very inattentive. My friend decided we'd had enough and asked to just pay for the portion of the feast that we'd already eaten. They gave him a hard time but finally complied. SO not worth what we'd paid. It's a shame- I used to love this place, but I won't be going back. The last time we were there with friends, we each had maybe 1 drink each (6 people), and they put SIXTEEN drinks on our bill - yes, 10 drinks more than what we'd consumed - and tried to tell us we were too drunk to remember what we'd had. It seems like a lot of other Yelp reviewers have had similar experiences with fishy charges being added to the bill, so if you do go (I don't recommend it!), make sure you check your bill very carefully.
Really great place hidden in nature. The service is very friendly and food wasn't bad. This place is a hidden gem that looks like someone's house.

This was my favourite breakfast on my first trip to NOLA.  I had the avocado and Brie omelet with a crawfish creole sauce on top, it was entirely delicious and I'm hard to please with breakfast. Also had the biscuit and it was very good, light, crisp and fluffy with good seasoning. My partner had the shrimp and grits which was also very good.  I recommend.

It's the most ginormous resort I have ever seen. The reception was a mad house with hundreds and hundreds of people checking in for New Year's eve. You would have to see it to believe it. We walked from one themed area to another, and another and another, you could lose a few pounds walking around, improving your memory as you get lost from the turns and corners while navigating around and spend lots of money. There was a ice sculpture event going on also which we didnt go to. However in one of the atrium there was a water fountain show which was spectacular. There were many convenient store where one could find bath salts or lotion or souvenir, many F&B outlets to hang out. It's quite a place to check out....

This place has great produce.  For a small shop, certain items can get pricey, but there is a good range of healthy options.  I found organic pears for $1.69 per pound - and they are tasty!  Milk cartons are an average of $4.50 for organic milk.  They even have calestravano olives (the bright green juicy/sweet/salty ones).  Great options of snacks.  Organic cereal can even be found on sale 2/$7.00.  Not bad knowing that cereal these days is an average of $4.50!  Great go to shop, but I would probably still need to go to Whole Foods to do the bulk shopping.  This market is great for a quick stop.

p.s.  they also have a hot food counter/deli.  Food is fantastic, but pricey.  Mushrooms are amazing.

this place is amazing. don't let the exterior fool you. 

i had lost all hope for any decent ethnic food in tucson after trying almost a couple dozen, but praise the lord, thanks to  sher-e-punjab, all faith is restored. 

my bf and i had a great dinner last night. so many flavors wrapped up in each dish, even the rice had clove in it!!! the chicken tikka masala was fantastic, creaming flavorful goodness, unlike the tomato paste over at Saffron. the chicken in the korma pretty much fell apart - so amazing. and my favorite dish that i always order at an Indian restaurant, the navraton koorma; it was bursting with flavor and tons of amazing veggies and paneer!!!

we ordered everything 'spicy' and it was absolutely perfect. they aren't scared to put heat in the food, which most places in tucson seem to go the opposite route to appease all of the old retirees i assume. 

thank you for one of the very few great dinners i've had in tucson!!!

Great and friendly service. Put together a beautiful arrangement for me on the spot, and within my price range. I had a vague idea of what I wanted, and the result was better than I could have imagined. Will be visiting this shop for all my florist needs in Philadelphia. A bonus is that it's open on Sunday!

I became very frustrated with the nail salon I have been going to, because of the inconsistent quality of manicures and pedicures. Since I was getting married Sunday, I wanted to enjoy a nice manicure with my daughters, and so, I decided to look for another salon. After reading reviews on Yelp, I decided to try Bello Nails, and I am glad I did. 

I got a french manicure and it looks pretty good. I appreciated that the technician asked if I wanted thin or medium tips (my nails are pretty short). I do not think I've ever had a manicurist ask me that. The white polish is a little thick in spots, but overall, it looks good. 

The salon is really beautiful and clean, and overall I was pretty happy with our manicures. One of my daughters felt hers was rushed because of another customer coming in. Since we had appointments, that was a little disappointing. I looked at my daughter's manicure when we got home, her nails looked ok, but not the best quality. The polish was thin and rough on the sides of her nails. 

I would also say this salon is a little on the expensive side, and with my daughter's experience I am a little unsure about the place... but I liked enough I will try it again. (Update coming when I do!)

Wow.. nice!

This place is an old school, white table cloth Italian restaurant.  Not only is the bar well stocked, the wine list is vast and the menu has everything you would expect.

Very nice atmosphere and great service.  I recommend to anyone in Nashville looking for a nice dinner at a traditional restaurant.

this place is neat.  very cute decor and i like the candy station right up front.  there's a private parking lot and outside seating.  a group of peeps at work ordered to go from this place and we all enjoyed our lunch.  i ordered the mushroom sandwich and liked it.  the fries were yummy too.  will be back.

Great beverages. I'm a new fan of the mamacita caliente cocktail, medium spice. So delicious. The space is clean and modern. Haven't had a chance to visit during the mead & knead event or yoga yet, but I love the idea. The staff is friendly and able to answer questions and make recommendations. I also tried the breakfast magpie - cider & coffee? Interesting!

We got there, the place was nice, nice environment, but had a little bit of a diner feel to it (because of the layout).. but then the sign that signals good food... Local Police. Once we saw local police were eating there, it was a sign that this was a good establishment.

Foot was cooked perfectly. Everything was delicious and it was reasonably priced, especially considering we were in the city center. 

Great selection for everyone.

Worst place ever. Too expensive. Save your money and go down the road. When terminating our lease, I told the leasing agent why We hated it here and her exact words were."you didnt have to live here" 
What the hell? I do like being by the water. But that's pretty much it. Walls are super thin and you literally hear EVERYTHING!
You pay 35$ for trash removal and they have missed my trash several times. They mow the lawn around 8am on certain days instead of later in the day. So if you have a late schedule, good luck getting any sleep. People have sex in the pool and dump trash and dog shit wherever they please. I hate it here and I'm glad I'm leaving. If you do decide that my review isn't enough and you still want to give shitty Camden Bay a try, then stay in building 10 apartment 102. You will be wishing you took my advice. All I have to say is paper thin walls!!!! 
The pool and work out area is nice.

I really enjoyed my 3-day stay at this hotel. Parking is in a garage (not the open air lot) across the street from the front door, and it is a special of $12 per 24 hours, just have your ticket validated at the hotel desk.  Our room was clean, and we received complimentary bottled water every day. The bathroom was nicely updated, though we did not receive towels one day, which was not a big deal to ask the front desk for on the way up. The room was "cozy", but you had everything you needed, including a refrigerator/freezer, microwave and safe (*NOTE* they will charge you $2 a day for the safe, but won't tell you the code unless you ask for it...you agree to pay this "tax" when you sign the paperwork at check in). The other reviewers are correct, and there is no individual thermostat in the room. We did get too warm the first two nights, but the windows open, so that did help. Despite these things, I am giving four stars because of the LOCATION, which is fantastic. We walked everywhere, and it was a real treat to not have to drive back to the hotel at the end of the day or worry about where to park each morning. For the price we paid, this was a great deal, and I will stay here again if I am in the area.

11 different protein options. Each are 3oz. Unlike other places that squash their patties flat and removes most of the juices, these are thicker and juicer with more flavor, they are just smaller in diameter. These are truly mini burgers with many fresh toppings. Sliders are typically 1oz or less. The sweet potato fries are awsome.

What really sold me to get a membership here was their unlimited personal training. In most gyms, personal training is a separate price & can be very costly at times, but at Planet Fitness, it's already included in your membership! Though, the gym may be slightly small, it has everything you need to start your fitness journey. The staff are very friendly and welcoming whenever I walk in the door. All the machines and restrooms are super clean, it doesn't smell like sweat or feet when you walk in. Lol. 
Jake, their personal trainer, is awesome! He definitely pushes you to your true potential & believes in you every step of the way. Though, some workouts are challenging & will probably leave you sore the next day, he gets you through it.
Greatest bagel I've had since last year when I went to NY! Lots of locals, very busy plan on standing in line especially on the weekends but totally worth it!

I stayed here for a trade show at the convention center across the street. 

Great hotel for a business trip. In room internet was plenty fast, the hallways quiet at night. Fast and tasty room service. FedEx store on the second floor. Terrific views in either direction. They have low floor and high floor express elevators so you don't have to wait long for an elevator. There's a bar right by the elevator so you can grab a drink to take to your room after a long day on the show floor. 

You can use the skywalk to get to the convention center so no jacket required in the winter. If you know your way you can get all the way over to the mall from the JW  without going outside. 

All in all a very nice place to stay, one of the nicest hotels I've stayed in while in Indianapolis over the last 17 years.

For a new place, you can tell the staff are learning, bit very courteous and friendly. The food, of which I had the Cuban, and shared the Mofungo [very interesting taste]. The atmosphere genuinely feels Caribbean/Spanish influence. Definitely will be back to try not menu items.

Everyone should stay at a Gaylord resort at least once in their life.  You truly won't be disappointed.

Our agenda this last trip to Nashville was strictly going to see a show at the Opry.  Luck would have it, I was able to snag a room on a Saturday night when the town was hopping with concerts galore all over the city.  I have seen parts of this resort in my other trips there but realized pretty quick that there's more to see!

Once on the grounds, parking is a bit tricky.  Finally found a human who directed us to self parking - they offer valet but it was closed this particular day.  There are not a lot of open spots in self parking so you may want to valet.  I'm not sure of the cost of valet but self was $29 with in and out privileges.  Once in the lobby we were directed to the correct check in line as this place was packed!

The hotel is broken up into 3 main categories - Cascades, Delta & Magnolia.  Each one is its one little world and all are connected as well.  We were in Magnolia, found our way to our room via map and got settled in.  Room was typical, there is outside access from each room with a patio which is nice.

The Opry and the Opry Mills mall are all easily accessible from the resort by resort shuttle, which is included in your daily resort fee.  The run shuttles for both literally all day and night, which is super nice.  The drivers are awesome and very engaging with the guests.  Your resort fee also includes other fun perks but we were not there long enough to enjoy them.

I'm giving 4 stars because of check-out, which is really my only issue.  One of the staff members told us, and another couple, that if we gave them our room key, they would be checked out and our bill would be emailed to us.  So we did that...and I never got the final bill.  I tried contacting and never heard back.  No biggie in reality but if you're going to tell your guests that, please do it.

If not in Nashville for downtown, check out Gaylord.  It was pretty amazing!

We have made khyper one of our must go when we are in town. We often go several times during our time in Philly just because it has such a cool vibe. I would give this place a 5 but we had a really weird experience with 2 of their bar backs on New Years. They were pretty rude to a couple of Mummers in costume. Probably upset bc they where throwing confetti- but come on, its new years and its Philly for goodness sake....

Any way- nice, really nice selection of beers. They make a lovely Pimm's cup my most fave drink right now. I don't care how cold it is outside, this drink makes me think of happy times... Tried their wings recently and they were a repeat. 

If you are in the area, make sure to stop by. I need to make sure to try their brunch next time we are in town- seems like it is very popular. 

Go enjoy. You won't regret it.

EXCELENT FOOD, ok service. Would give it 5 stars if service was better we came around 2pm and there were only about 4 tables with customers. Had to tell the waitress to fill our drinks and bring ketchup, twice. 
Food was outstanding! Had the almond catfish. Best catfish I have ever had. Light breading, fluffy, fresh and very tasty. Wife got the shrimp and oysters. Shrimp were huge and fresh. 
My 6 yr old son never compliments the food, but couldn't stop talking about how tasty the chicken fingers were. 
Would definitely recommend and come back again.

love. this. place. grab a quick bowl of tasty pho and grab a bahn-mi to go. not the most authentic flavors, but close enough for the price. my only complaint is that bok choy was the only veggie in the veggie pho. c'mon now, can i get a carrot or piece of broccoli please?

If I could give this zero stars I would. I contacted the Electric Run to see when the rescheduled 2014 run would take place since they kept posting on their website "July 2014 - coming soon!". We were promised last year of registration would be deferred to 2014. I got an email back basically telling me I'm sh*t out of luck since they won't be doing an Electric Run in Philly in 2014 or ever again and if I wanted to do the run, I had to go to Baltimore which is the next closest city. They totally scammed me along with thousands of other people. The organizers never sent an email out telling last years registrants that they were not coming back to Philly in 2014. I demanded my money back (I couldn't dispute the charges on my credit card since it was past the 90 day window to dispute charges) and they flat out told me they would not be refunding my money. Again, a bunch of scam artists. They led us to believe they would have another race. They need to do the right thing and refund. They did not fulfill their end of the deal. Shame on Electric Run and all of their organizers. They are completely unprofessional and have shown they are amateurs at hosting events of this nature.

I tore up some Bottom of the Sea on a Sunday night.  

My expectations for this place were pretty low after walking in--unimpressive and basic decor, somewhat dirty bathroom, and understocked bar. 

But then....it was like our waiter was sent from heaven as he slid a HUGE plate of crab legs smothered in garlic, spices, and butter in front of me. As I cracked and plucked the succulent crab from its shell I felt like a kid on Christmas day opening presents to find all the goodies inside.  The corn and brocoli were good too, but the crab was definitely the star!  ATTENTION ALL OTHER SEAFOOD RESTAURANTS:  season your seafood  even if it has a shell---it makes a difference! And I'm not just talking about sprinkling some Old Bay on top.  

My friend ordered the fried shrimp and crab cakes which were equally as good as the crab legs. In fact, it was the best fried shrimp I've ever had.  We did not talk much during our meal....we both had a mutual understanding that this food was too good to waste it on some chatter.  

The $4 martini special was nice, however the martini glasses were small and the drinks were on the weaker side.  Opted for beer for the next round....can never mess up beer or wine.  

Our waiter was very friendly and attentive.  However, I think he could tell that he did not need to check in on us often because he probably heard all the "Mmmmmms" and "Ooooooos"  and "OMG this is so good" coming from our table.

I have not been so satisfied with a seafood meal in a long time.  At the end of my meal I was saying "Red Lobster Who?"  since this restaurant put the Chain King of seafood to shame.  You're not going to find foofy-foofy seafood here, but you will find FLAVORFUL seafood and HUGE portions.  

If this place remodeled and paid a more attention to cleanliness it would be phenomenal, but then again maybe I should overlook this so I can always get a table quickly.

Made appointment online for a week out. They send me an email to pre register my info but the link has nothing to do with updating my info.  They call me in the middle of the week and ask me for my insurance info. "Ok we will sees you Saturday." Show up and they tell me they don't accept my insurance. WTF?

I am from the south and evidently the whole boiled hot dog thing is a northern thing? I don't know what i was expecting but the boiled hot dogs threw me a bit. The atmosphere is incredible and the people are so nice! I got a corn dog and fries and I was not disappointed. The boiled hot dog just takes some getting used to. My kids wouldn't eat it because they're super picky. But I , well, let's just say I love a good corndog!!

AAA does a terrific and professional job of installing glass in cars.  They're modern, quick to get back with a quote and get the job done in exactly how long they say they will. 

I had my car broken into a week back and my driver's side window was broken.  I called them on a Saturday afternoon and got a quote and an appointment for Monday morning.  I was busy in the morning so took the 3 p.m. appointment.  When I reached there the guy told me it'll take 2 hours (the same time he had said on the phone) and I was prepared for that because AAA is really close to the Transcend on 99th street.  So I walked down and got my coffee fix and some work done, while they fixed my car. 

Good service, would go to them when I finally decide to fix my windscreen (still waiting for a couple of cracks to complete their propagation).
Spring rolls werent our favorite. Chicken panang was awesome tho! ! Some of the best I've had!  I'll be back forsure. Great lil spot.

The menu looks 50 years old. The inspirations for the dishes are the same vintage. The pizza tasted like cardboard with a jar of generic pizza sauce spread on it. The bland ribs were cooked at one point but mine were reheated from a previous night. The salad bar was stale and limp. The meal was far too overpriced for the quality of the food.

Here's my dilemma. We want this place to succeed, it's local, good food, great ambiance, friendly wait staff. But I am sitting here right now in a half empty restaurant with my husband (happy Father's Day!) and my 5 year old and 9 month old and we have been waiting an hour for our food. An hour!!! With kids!! The waitress is being so lovely about it, very apologetic etc, brought us a free appetizer, but still. About 6 weeks ago we had the same experience here, long waits for food, mixed up orders, apologetic wait staff. What on earth is going on with management? It's like they are 3 people short back there. We waited 20 minutes for bread! Please don't fail, CVGE. Please just replace your management staff.
Not a retail location.  Came up on a Sat morning. Closed?? This looks more like a dentist office then a juice shop.

We parked at the Freret Street Parking Lot to grab some lunch, and noticed this colorful building on the way back to the car.  Excellent find!  The gentleman working behind the counter was friendly and informed us that the ice cream (several flavored to choose from) was home made.  Home made!  It was delicious, especially on a warm summer day. We will be back to Ice Cream 504.

Can't imagine why this place is still around.  From the skanky exterior, who would park and enter?  The food, nothing special, the service is a roll of the dice and the place is not exactly Lysol friendly. Why?

I have wasted too many hours of my life in this "Irish Pub". It's basically a place where dumb business people, stranded travelers and suckers go to eat standard pub food that is overpriced and underwhelming. 

I was unfortunately a stranded traveler on Monday and instead of standing out in the blistering heat where the streets reeked of steaming garbage, I decided to suck it up and head into Slainte. 

Oh? You want a drink? I hope you have 25 minutes to wait for it. Oh, you want some Whiz Fries? I hope you like fries that are still frozen in the middle. 

This place has two redeeming qualities. Clean bathrooms and proximity to 30th Street Station.
Sunday brunch. Waited for the server to acknowledge me and take my order. Waited quite a while for my adequate food. Waited for my check. Won't be back.

If you look at my reviews, you'll see that I'm not a hater or trasher of businesses. Today a coworker got lunch for our team at work, we ordered individually online via the group ordering option, which is really handy and makes things very easy for the person picking up for the group. We'd ordered from there before, and my lunch back then was so-so, but based on the reviews I gave it another try. The gyro claimed to be sliced broiled beef and lamb. It was not. It was those premade frozen ovals that every cheapo place that wants to have a so-called gyro on the menu but doesn't want to bother with a vertical roaster or anything close to cooking it fresh. They appeared steamed or lightly fried. I will admit there were several slices. Good fresh pita bread is hard to find, and this wasn't it- it began to fall apart after the first couple of bites, which pita will do if it's old. There was maybe a half teaspoon of tzatziki on it, I couldn't taste it at all due to its being hardly there. There was less than half of a red onion slice, a couple of tiny chunks of tomato, and what little lettuce there was on it was WAY too old, brown, and soggy to account for its having been driven the 2-1/2 miles from the restaurant to work. The whole sandwich was pretty awful. 

I also got dolmades. I LOVE dolmades, even the canned ones. These were even mushier than canned ones, and they unaccountably tasted just like burning plastic smells. 

The whole thing was over $15. I honestly think this was the worst lunch I have ever had. There was nothing appealing about it, and I see no circumstance where I'd try it again, as there wasn't a single element of it that I could say was good, especially for the price. Except for the group online ordering.

This Companion is close to work, so I dropped in the other day for a sammy. My first thought was - it's SMALL! It gets super crowded at lunch and is also small. I wouldn't want to eat in unless you want hoards of people around and people watching you eat and begging with their eyes for you to finish already so they can take your table. Parking is actually ok since there is a side street that is easy to park on and walk since it's on the corner. 

That being said, Companion has awesome bread and sandwiches. I love the cocktail party - brie, apples and apricot marmalade on some sort of walnut bread. Toasted. YUM!! It doesn't come with anything but you can make it a bag lunch easily. The soups and entrees come with a roll or bavarian pretzel on the side. I highly recommend the pretzel. They are so good! I buy them in Schnuck's or Straubs frequently. Warm them up and they make a great snack. Companion also has local products like Fitz's. It's pretty quick once you order to get your food. 

I was pleasantly surprised to see that the foil they wrapped my sandwich in kept it really warm when I got to work and was ready to eat it. I also got a frosted sugar cookie, which I didn't like much. The frosting was kind of gummy tasting and the consistency of the cookie wasn't great. That being said, there are a lot of cool designs on them. 

This is definitely a primo sandwich stop!

What a fun place to come and walk around.  We were staying in downtown but took at 15 min Uber ride over to check it out.  The decorations for the holidays and great and fun.  This place goes on and on and on. Lots of tour buses and tour groups but it didn't feel to crowded.  This is a money making machine so you'll see bars every 100 ft, gift shops and they have things designed for you to spend.  We took 15 min boat ride which was kinda funny and it let us rest our legs - it was $10.50.  We walked over to the Grand Ole Opry and went by a christmas gift store - again designed to make you spend money, when we got to the Opry - it was $26 for a 45 min tour - we decided that was too much - we would have paid $5 for a peek!!.  This was a great spot to get in some steps, look at the beautiful gardens inside the hotel and check off the bucket list.  We did not stay on site so have no idea what the rooms are like.

Just like the name states.. everything is SO fresh! My daughter first turned me on to Sofresh! We stopped by today to pu a couple of salads for lunch. I had the Signature and she had the Med salad with Chicken. I ve how they pack the protein in a separate container. The Balsamic dressing is incredible! 

The only downside? the strip mall its located in. SOOOOOO busy, can never get a spot and even just dropping her off to run in to pick up the to go order was a hassle.(actually a complete cluster...) 

I love the salads and will check to see if they are on Uber Eats to avoid the hassle getting in and out of the parking lot!

Day 3 of my Chinese Food run and I chose Asian Chef. I have to say, I think I found a winner here.
I ordered the same thing I have ordered at the previous two restaurants, General Tso's chicken lunch special.  First off, I was pleasantly surprised that I was not charged for asking for white meat only. The only negative thing about placing my order is that the man I spoke to hung up before I was done speaking. Not sure if he realized I was still talking so I didn't mind too much. 

Upon arriving at the location, it appears to be a typical strip mall Chinese food place. The restaurant itself was small, but very cute inside. It was very clean, and in order. The place was empty except for one other person waiting for their order. The gentleman I dealt with was very nice. My order was ready when I got there and I just paid and left. The General Tso's chicken and rice were pretty good and my favorite of the three places I have tried. The only negative thing I can say about the rice is the large pieces of onion. But it was easy to pick them out. 

Overall, I will definitely go back to this location.  Great Job Asian Chef!
I had the Seared Ham and Mushroom Omelette topped with spicy creole tomato sauce with fresh fruit and freshly squeezed orange juice!!! Amazing! Would definitely come back!

l love the wood-fire chicken at Pita Jungle and order it every time I go.  The garlic dip is delicious too but my husband doesn't let me eat it because it makes me unkissable.  I also had the greek salad and it was fresh and tasty.  I really want to try something else on the menu but can't resist the chicken.  My husband usually orders a gyro and he says they are ok.

Loved the breakfast here. I don't think this place needs anymore good reviews because we were there on a cold morning in January and it was packed. In fact that's the only thing I didn't love about it - it's a bit cramped and noisy and the tables are sort of crammed together in an effort to squeeze in as many eager diners as possible. I get it, it's the French Quarter and most places are on the small side. But anyway...the food was delicious. About as good a breakfast as you're going to find anywhere. A bit on the expensive side but I think that's another thing you just have to expect when visiting here. Great food and good service, I'll go back if the line's not too long :)

Well being locked up isn't bad when it's your choice in a beautiful hotel. After attending a conference for 5 days at this remarkable city within a city hotel I decided to extend my stay and to sight see. Well the flu that I originally brought with me decided to get worse at the end of the conference and I ended up spending my last days and nights mostly locked in my bedroom. I was glad to get a visit from their on-site EMT and was more amazed that he gave me a voucher to get to the closest clinic although I got there minutes after the staff at the clinic had left - I was actually glad to return to the hotel for rest. The beds are comfortable and my room had a balcony overlooking their magnificent gardens, so I was not completely isolated. I stepped out briefly to eat and was glad each time I did as the restaurants and staff are very, very pleasant. Would I recommend this hotel for others to have their conferences, reunions, wedding the answer is most definitely! Cheers to Gaylord Hotels for making a huge resort experience comfortable.

Came here for Saturday brunch. I ordered the Breakfast bomb which was a pleasant experience. The wait was long but if you're out for brunch, it would have to be absolutely miserable out for you to be seated at any restaurant in less than 10 minutes.

The wife had a chicken sandwich which she enjoyed. The kid got the chicken fingers and fries and actually finished it off.

If I lived in the neighborhood I'd come frequently.

Overall a great business - quick, friendly and affordable. I was wary of the Yelp reviews because they are all so glowing, but I was not disappointed. It's rare to find a repair place that gives it to you straight and doesn't try to screw you over with pricing and unnecessary repairs. I am even more sensitive to this as I don't have the first clue about cars so I put 100% of my trust in to people like this. 

I came to Keith's with one major problem and 7-8 minor issues with my Nissan Murano. They went above and beyond in terms of getting everything taken care of in an efficient and cost-effective manor. From the moment I walked in the door, they were trying to save me money. For example, they did not charge me for a tire rotation because I needed my brakes replaced. They rushed the parts they needed over to the shop and stayed an hour after closing time to get my major issue fixed. Unfortunately, the problem was bigger than what they had anticipated, so I was back in the shop when they opened the next morning. Now whether this bigger problem was missed the day before or would have been missed regardless, I don't know. But they took the time the next day (4 hours) to comb through the entire car and make sure everything was in place and working properly. 

I would say that by going to Keith's rather than the dealership (my usual go-to for everything car-related), I saved at least $1000, if not more. My side mirror got knocked off - the dealership was going to charge me $400 to replace it, Keith's is doing it for $150. My key fobs stopped working - the dealership was going to charge me $120 PER FOB, Keith's is doing it for $100 for both. I originally took my car in for a horrible noise it was making - the dealership was going to charge me $150 just to put it on the rack and take a look, Keith's charge is $18. These are just examples of some of the cost-savings I got at Keith's.

I will definitely be going back. And I'll be disappointed when I move away from New Orleans as I'll never find another place like Keith's.

While I love supporting local businesses and it's nice to have an alternative to Whole Foods, I find this place lacking on quality and freshness. I got a pre-made vegan sandwich from here before and was not impressed considering the steep price - very dry and lackluster. A lot of the produce is bruised and slightly wilted. They also don't carry staples like baguettes.

It's okay if you just need to stop in to grab a few items, but you'll find better quality and prices a few blocks farther down South Street at Whole Foods.

I've never done laundry here either but walking around the laundry side appears very clean. Staff is on-site from open till close. You'll find large TVs on walls, tables for laptops and coffee available. I will use for large blankets and quilts in the future.

I've been tanning here for years. The beds and rooms are clean with friendly staff. They offer specials each month on tanning lotions with free tanning weekends to all a couple times a year.
 
Want an instant bronzed tan without the standard orange day-glow you've had before or seen on others? NO MORE ORANGE! Check out the Versa Spa spray tanning booth here. It's terrific, fast, you choose the shade of tan you desire. I was terrified first time but NO orange after glow. In just a few hours your tan looks like you spent a few weeks on the beach. Versa Spa is perfect for brides and wedding parties, vacations or that special occasion. Try it once and you'll be a believer!

This was the worst experience! I checked in after midnight and found I had no towels, these were brought up by a rude person. I then found that I also had no soap, again the rude man came. Manager made it up to me with free "breakfast" and a drink. The bar was loud and obnoxious music playing. I went to print my boarding pass to find that the printer "for some reason" was not working. After 30 minutes,  I was offered more free drinks which I did not want but they allegedly gave me points instead. These points have not shown up on my rewards program yet.  When we went to redeem this "free breakfast" it turns out that it was worse than a fast food joint! Plus, they no longer made half of their menu or were out of it (at 9am). The worst was the coffee urn was empty, never heard of such a thing in a place where it is offered 24 hours. Was told it would be about 20 minutes!! I asked for manager to be told that there was no on on sight! This was so bad, I travel to Tampa once/month and have been trying all of the local hotels. This was the priciest and the absolute worst! I gave it one star b/c the employees at the front desk and valet tried very hard and were very nice.

Ho. Ly. Crap. This is place is unlike any place I've ever been in my life. It's huge. It's got everything you need. W didn't even spend the night. We just walked around the place for hours. So much stuff to see and do. And it's a great location in relation to other attractions in Nashville, if you ever get bored here, which is unlikely. We will definitely be back to spend a few nights.

In this rustic, new spot in Mt. Airy, a great menu is well balanced with great craft beer selections.  Free parking in public lot behind, often street parking available, as well.

Mason jar appetizers were interesting and, seemingly, pricey at $8.50 yet actually a  good value because they were well and carefully crafted from delicious ingredients.  Salads were large and had so many great ingredients and just the right amount of excellent dressing.

The short-rib grilled-cheese sandwich was good, the meat tender, with a jam, but lacked a bit of acid or horseradish (but their horseradish cream is too understated -- it came with another diner's jar of beet salad, so I sampled a little).  (I took half the sandwich home to try to perk it up with pickles, or a Siracha ketchup.) 

Others in our party had salads of various kinds, and loved them.  The grilled chicken on one was perfectly cooked, the dressing "delectable".

Their great weakness in accommodating wheat/gluten or other allergies definitely hurts and limits this restaurant.  The server knew the beers well, but not food menu ingredients.  And the restaurant's "accommodation" of serving a "sandwich without the bread" just doesn't cut it any more.  It's just too easy to have a loaf of GF bread and some rice flour on hand to use as needed.  It's customer focused which makes it good for business.  (Didn't even bother to see if they had GF beers, of which there are several good ones out there.)  14% or more of the population has issues with wheat, among other ingredients so that's a big potential clientele to be dismissing.

Took a butterscotch pudding home, to enjoy over several nights, but the taste was off -- possibly from the very large plastic container they put the small dollop into.  Smelled kind of like dishwater.  Perhaps enjoyed on premises, it would taste far better.

Good meal, but not a destination dining spot.  This kind of creativity was needed in this area.
This was a GREAT Buffet!!! I would recommend it to anyone that passes through town. They have all crabs and seafood your looking for

I saw a sign that said free for kids on Monday. This was false advertising cause they only have this promotion in the fall. 

Can't complain about the food or service. Fast and very friendly. Chose seating outside, the view of the fountain was beautiful. 

My only complaint was how god awful the bathroom was. Not only was it old and very outdated. It was filthy. In my stall someone smeared their feces on the wall. 

I think management has to do a better job of maintaining the facility a little bit better.
Love the 8 pc nugget. So happy to have a new location. Drive through is almost always busy, but inside is usually empty.
Great place for a quick breakfast. If you're staying on Canal do yourself a favor and walk down to this place. The staff is friendly and the serving sizes are hangover friendly.
Rude, rude, rude! Hung up on me on the phone twice when I was asking about wait time. Food can't be their redeeming quality if you never get that far.

I came here for lunch with a few friends. The place was packed, but we were able to push together a few tables. The staff was quick and friendly. Did not have any trouble.

Everything I had was good, but nothing special. I ordered the Frenchie sandwich. It was good, but not enough sprouts on the sandwich. The bread was good, but I should have gotten it toasted. There was a total of 10 in my group and we all received our food quickly. If I went back, I would probably do the soup & sandwich combo. 

For dessert, I got the gooey butter cake. It was really good and a good sized portion. It was so large that I ended up sharing it with everyone in my group.

Overall, I would recommend this to others and go back there myself.

Y'know how you have that impossible person on your gift list and you really have no idea what to buy them. BUT lucky for you, they are obsessed with their pet.  This is the place to buy them a gift.  Yes, it is mostly doggie stuff but I have found items for the cat lovers in my life.  

Plus you can bathe your own dog here in their self-service bathtub which is up on a platform which really could save your back.

Ordered from here twice. Poor customer service and unimpressive fish. 

The sushi was very meh the first time, but my roommate and I decided to give them one more try based on reviews. I think a lot of people leaving the good reviews have never had good sushi because Makiman is definitely sub par. 

Anyway, the second time I ordered some kind of fancy/special shrimp tempura roll and a salmon avocado roll. I don't know how hot the tempura roll was when they packaged it, but it made the salmon roll really warm. Not the rice, but the whole thing. Warm raw fish is not good and it grossed me out to where I couldn't eat it. I could have let this slide, but they also gave me a regular shrimp tempura roll instead of the one I ordered and paid for that cost double. I gave them a call about the roll and mentioned the warm salmon. The guy was super rude and told me that he's "been packaging sushi for 10 years and no one ever complained about this." Ok, jerk. I've been eating sushi for 10 years and never complained about this because it's never happened. 

He told me he would credit the extra $7 for the shrimp tempura from my next order. Told him there would be no next order after the conversation we just had and he said OK and hung up.

I want to love this place, but I have tried it twice and just can't give higher stars.  I have tried the Crab omlette, Caribean Sandwich, and the poached eggs......nothing special.  Don't get me wrong, it is good but I feel like the chef is still trying to find their voice.  

PRO:  The only thing on the menu worth writing about is the butternut squash soup.  Nice consistency and I appreciate the spices that were used.  This is not a bland soup at all so if you do not like spicy you will not like this.

CON:  Really......$5 for a small glass of OJ!  I realize that it is fresh squeezed, but come-on!

I love this place, great local spot especially for holiday brunch. The food is consistently good, service is always friendly despite how busy they always are and portions are fare for the price. Favorite is the open faced turkey and brunch selections.

Went to Revivals for Easter Brunch Buffet total disappointment!   Over cook eggs, lunch meat ham,  lasagna with no sauce, bizarre sausage,  limp bacon, and horrendously over price at $15.99 for such low low quality food.   I don't have a problem paying for food if it's good but this was not.    Contacted the restaurant via email they quickly emailed me back to tell me no one else complained but the other Yelp reviews back what I'm saying here.

HORRIBLE!!!! The food is great but the servers (just a few) are the most arrogant people I have ever seen in my life!! They were extremely horrible and rude to a man who has served his country for 25 years! Will never eat there until they fire unpleasant Wendy and the black girl that only wanted to fight me after standing up for the Vet!! The absolute most horrible experience! You took our names and phone numbers and promised action but I have yet to hear from management. Until I get results, I am on a mission posting reviews everywhere about how horrible the service is and how you treated a veteran!!

The food was great, however despite having a reservation we had to wait for quite a while to get a table and be served. We barely had enough time to get to our concert. The place was quite noisy too and i think it may have been louder than the concert! I would go again on a less busy night.

Would definitely recommend calling before trying to shop here to make sure they're open. Drove all the way out here Sunday- after verifying their hours on Sundays online- and the place was closed. Even the business hours on the door indicated they'd be open well in advance of the time I arrived. Was excited to see the place after hearing such great reviews, however, won't ever be shopping here now. 2 other customers in the parking lot were also confused. Plenty of smoke shops in the area- don't waste your time driving to this one for no reason!

I have been a customer here for a year and a half. Unfortunately, I will not be returning. I went in for a repair. After 4 days my gel was peeling. When I went in the man who  was tasked with removing the gel began peeling it back with his nails. No soaking, no tools. I was so grossed out I asked him to stop. When I asked about soaking he picked up the Dremel. You gotta be fooling me if you think I am gonna let the dude who was picking off my polish near me with a Dremel tool. Now I am starting my week with one nail bare and only one half of another in tact. #fwp

This place is awesome, hipsters included.  The music is awesome, even the sweating is awesome. They have PBR on the menu over and over (PBR $2, But Lite, $4, PBR $2, etc.)  This made it even better.  

Why yes, I think I'll have a PBR.

I ordered the meal deal with his six nuggets and fries and BBQ sauce and a large  sweet. Receive my order and I touch my fries and they was cold. Is be young guy in the window to your have any hot fries so I gave him my the cold fries and he hollered back to the person that was cooking. They said yes we have hot fries. So he went back to get them and when he was walking to the window he just hand me the hot fries in this hands. He should have put them in a bag or wrapped a napkin around them so I got another order of fries.

Stopped by for a quick coffee and snack and the Cumberland Island was wicked good, had an awesome refreshing after taste and still gave that great caffeinated coffee taste. Not from around here but definitely could be a chill study spot.

Also snagged a Ham and Cheese croissant that was also delic!

Revisited the Depot for a 5th time last night. The place was packed but we had a reservation so no worries. Once we were seated, it took about 15 minutes for our waiter to come over and take our drink order. This was after my husband went to the hostess stand to request a server. I was afraid the whole night was going to go that way but luckily I was wrong.

The food was excellent as usual. The menu had something for everyone in our group from the vegetarian to the meat eater. Food came out in a timely manner, hot and delicious.

The building is gorgeous. I highly recommend a reservation on weekend nights.

VERY disappointed in the service here  ! My daughter and I walked into the store and were ignored by the girls holding the little carry bags. As we walked around the store we passed many ladies holding the little carry bags and still were not offered one or even asked if we needed help. Alas, we actually didn't need a little carry bag after all because we walked out and headed straight for the MAC store.

Great fries.  Basic bar food at decent prices.  My friend swears that she had a bottle beer refilled and served back to her here.  Still, we usually come back whenever I visit for the fries and the people watching.

This is DJ Alley Cat spinnin' all your favorite 60s hits. Next up is a reimagining of the Lovin' Spoonful's classic hit "Summer in the City..."





Hot store, Silver in the City
Buying bacon gum and magnets of kitties
Near downtown, the space is itty bitty
That makes it cramped and what a pity

All around, people looking at stuff
I see a coffee mug with Crime Dog McGruff

But one side is a different world
You'll see guys buying jewelry for their girl
Come on, come on, the price is right
And if you ask nice they'll wrap it up tight

And now I'm done tryin' to be witty
Just to talk up a store I know
Called Silver in the City
It's Silver in the City

I was here on a Saturday night and probably the oldest person in here. I had a salad which was really good. I also did a flight of beers and a flight of hard liquor. I had 2 bourbons and the 2 ryes. All the alcohol was quite good. I sat at the bar, so my service was great, I can't speak about table service. The one thing about the place is it is REALLY loud. Seemed like a lot of locals were in here, but it's definitely got the trendy hipster thing going. I'd definitely go back when I'm in town again.

Small, comfortable Puerto Rican restaurant in the Gulch area. Space / decor is minimal and bright. Try to snag one of the cozy semi-circular booths if you are able. Food was fresh and tasty. Lots of interesting choices. Plantain, spinach lasagne was amazing. Couldn't fully evaluate the Mofongo Relleno given a mashed plantain shortage, but the substitute (potato???) used  as the base was quite nice. Reasonable drink prices, friendly, attentive service despite a full house.

Yum! I'm so happy we stopped here. 
Made a quick stop from tour de America to have lunch. I got the vegetarian burro (which was a big burrito) and I asked for potatoes in it. Oh man, so good! They're tortillas are homemade and so delicious. 
I'm constantly eating mexican food.  I think burritos should be apart of the main food groups. This place has one of the best burritos I have ever had. Ever. 
I also tried the horchata which I also really enjoyed. Not too sweet, not too watered down. 
The woman behind the counter was very helpful and willing to answer questions. 
If I lived in the area I would be here all the time!
I would rate this a 5 star, but usually I leave that up to consistency.

I recently went to pita jungle with a good friend of mine for dinner. We were sat promptly and our server was very attentive and efficient, I believe her name was Lacy. We ordered the jalapeño cilantro hummus for an appetizer and the flavor was amazing; however the pita bread was sub par, definitely not homemade. My friend ordered the portabella burger and raved about how delicious it was. I had the vegetable ginger stir fry with tofu and the tofu was definitely the best cooked tofu I have had at a restaurant. The manager (tall dark haired guy) came by the table and asked us how everything was and refilled our drinks, which I thought was a nice touch to the experience! All in all I would definitely recommend pita jungle for people looking for fresh food, especially vegetarians!

I just love a bookstore staffed by knowledgeable readers and cats.  The organization is easily figured out (though they have helpful maps at the front and in the back), it's much better than the corporate chain bookstore that was my other choice.  Although there are both new and used books, the used book selection is merely good, not great, I ended up with a new book on sale I had been meaning to read.  Interesting collection of collectible rare books.

Been to Broadripple Brew Pub many times. This place is always packed! There is usually a wait, but its totally worth it. Its a traditional Irish Pub with a twist. They have many typical Irish dishes, but also have pizza and other options as well. You HAVE to try the scottish eggs. They are probably the best Ive ever had! 

Prices are reasonable, atmosphere is great, service is usually pretty good. We had an issue with our server the last time we were there, she obviously did not want to be there. Other than that its typically been a great experience.

Came here not expecting much, a bigger hole in the wall restaurant:

man, probably my new favorite Mexican place in town.

I'm a fan of the tastier and higher quality food that this place brings. Chips and salsa are on the house and were extremely good. 

We ordered a Carne Asada, a burrito supreme, and an appetizer was like a cooked jalepeno. Definitely hit the spot.

Portions are a little smaller than your typical hole in the wall restaurant, (most Mexican places give enough food to take home for a smaller meal at home) but this place was more on the , perfect for the night portions.

Definitely recommending this place for anyone who loves Mexican food

I like this place. 
I like this place a lot.

So all you peoples better stay away. 
Get off my block and eat somewhere else. 

When we are hungover (or just lazy), this is a block's walk.
So go elsewhere.
Im warning you.
Yes you.

Im grumpy when Im hungover.
Pizza...too small too much had better way better especially for the $$. Like wood fired pizza but not into cremated crust.

Glad my company paid for my stay for a conference. I would not waste your money!
From customer service to expectations all around (particularly in the dining category), disappointing and worse- overpriced!

If you like mega hotels where you literally walk miles from one end and back- this is the place for you.

I give praise to the horticulture team because the gardens are amazing!!!

Checked in a prepaid party of 5/7 of our rooms.  It was early am when we landed so of course they weren't ready for us but would call us when they were, no later than 3.  There was some confusion at check-in but we thought it had been taken care of.  Only to find out later, it was still messed up.  We received notice that our room was ready at 4:30 and collected our keys and attempted to get into our room, our keys were not programmed.  Luckily there was a house staff member who also tried our keys as well but eventually used his key to gain entry.  Once inside, the check-in issue that we thought was resolved was not: 2 female colleagues, 1 king bed.  Originally, we had booked & pre-paid for 2 separate rooms, they had asked of we would share- it was unexpected, but we were flexible.  That is until we found out 'sharing' meant the same bed!

We immediately contacted customer service only to wait in the phone system for 20 minutes and another 5 before they thought bringing us a roll away would be acceptable.  NOT EVEN!  So after going back and forth our mysterious reservation appeared and we both ended up with king rooms; however we missed happy hour with our team.

That leads us to dinner....the steakhouse was too pricy and Solario, the Conservatory bar and Ravello's were closed, JD's had a 45m wait so we continued to Delta Island where our disappointment grew into aggravation.  Now completely starving, frustrated and tired from travel and learning- we ended up at a fast food version of pizza, including: a cafeteria line, plastic trays, plastic ware and plastic to-go boxes.  A far cry from a nice sit down and get served kind of meal we were looking for.

My feet are sore from walking all over this place, I'm still hungry for real food and I'm exhausted and, well, very unhappy.

The only thing I don't like about this place is that they allow smoking. They have odor eaters but the smell still hits you when you walk in. The waiter we had was friendly and attentive. They have a pretty limited craft beer selection, but a little something for everyone. They had the football games on the TV and we will be back for another game and to try their food.

This place is terrible.  The hostess was very rude.  The waiter was pretty nice however.  The food was awful and terribly overpriced.  We went when there were very few people there.  The manager fell all over himself trying to make a nearby party welcome while totally ignoring us.  What a disappointment.  AVOID AVOID AVOID

Worst service out of any establishment in Nashville. The buns and burgers are so dry and have zero flavor. They're VERY stingy on the toppings. The cheese fries are the BEST in Nashville though, they're the only thing worth getting here.

Loved every food item we ordered! The cioppino was just right spicy, and the sweetbreads appetizer was surprisingly crisp and flavorful. Pork belly tacos were a big hit.
The food is creatively prepared and the ingredients are all fresh.

The servers were all delightful people, but the food/drinks/seating were uneven, left us thinking they have some kinks to work out.

The building is as great as everyone says.
Went here for dinner on 10/9/2014 with a party of 6. Food was good and service was excellent. A great long standing place on 4th Ave in Tucson.

So I have been a member here for a few months now...I originally started out with a Groupon for 2 weeks and eventually signed up.  This is an insane workout.  You do 15 minutes of "warm up" which I put in quotes only because for most people this "Warm Up" will put them on the floor sucking wind.  After that, you are given 1 minute to put your gloves on and begin a 3 minutes, 8 round session where you follow the instructors command on hits.  This portion, you honestly get what you put into it.  I've seen people just tap the bag and some stand there and not really go after it....I understand the "warm up" was tough, but come on...hit the thing! :)

After the 8 rounds, you grab a medicine ball and begin Core Strength workout for the remaining 10 minutes of the class.

All in all, you are there for 60 minutes and tend to burn 600-1000 calories (depending on output and fitness level)

It is a great way to get in shape and I do love the workout.  1 Star off for the price.  It is roughly $70/mo if you sign up for a year but then they want to try and get you to pay 25$ for 30 minute private training sessions.  I don't like that...but hey, they have a business to run I guess.

I highly recommend you try a free class...maybe I'll see you there!
Service & atmosphere are great, the food is particularly bland. Portions are large but flavor is lacking.

I love a good hole in the wall restaurant. They're often my favorites. But I was disappointed with China Empire. The broccoli in my broccoli beef was piping hot, but not at all cooked. It was hot, raw broccoli. The chow mein was bland and I took two bites before giving up. My husband's meal was somewhat better, but we probably won't be back.

Spoon Me is my favorite froyo spot out of all the local chains - Berry Creamy in Deptford and the Yogo Factory in Somerdale don't compare.

It is located next to Panchero's, so I usually am there for a main meal and follow it with Spoon Me for dessert. It's in a good area near other stores and shopping areas in Voorhees.

It has a small seating area inside to the right when you walk in along with an uncomfortable metal table directly next to the door outside for alfresco seating.

The yogurt flavors vary daily, but only a few. The most popular ones that are always present are Irish mint, vanilla, chocolate, yellow cake batter, but I've also seen red velvet cake, cinnamon roll, cheesecake, and more. They also have sorbet that is very fruity and tasty when I've tried it. I believe it is 49 cents an ounce like all of froyo places seem to be.

They also do smoothies and drinks made from yogurt. They offer Belgian waffles topped with froyo. They have oatmeal in the morning.

The topping bar is always very neat and the pieces are cut to the perfect bite size and shape. There are sprinkles, chocolate chips, candies, cereal like cinnamon toast crunch, sauces like marshmallow, peanut butter, white and dark chocolate, and hot fudge. There are fruit and those Asian pearls for drinks, and there are brownies and cheesecake bites as well. I love the brownie bites!

The decor is also not alarming or ugly like the Yogo Factory in Somerdale. It's contemporary, playful, and tasteful. Mellow colors, futuristic chairs.

They have punch cards where they offer the tenth or so yogurt free.

The staff usually high school kids who are friendly enough. I'm in my early twenties and can't stand annoying kids, which happens to be the majority of their clientele. Don't go on Friday or Saturday night because the place is overrun with preteens on their iPhones and in their ugly uggs. I usually go during the day on week days to avoid this.

Overall, good froyo and annoying crowd.
Hummus trio was tasty and fresh. Entrees were served quickly and hot. Portions were a good side too. Service was on point and attentive. Solid lunch!
Got stuffed shells and italian hoagie, very expensive and the food and the ppl who work there suck...stay away

Guac and chips are fab!  I seriously don't know how to handle one of those big a** burritos so I got a bowl w/rice and the chicken was excellent...real char-grilled chicken not the psuedo chicken crap that T-Hell passes off as chicken.  The only reason it's not five stars is the seating/atompshere sucks. Metal tables and noise level was horrific like school cafeteria days.

I was convinced by hubby to try this place this morning and I'm glad I did. I'm a picky eater and was on the fence. We walked in and were seated right away. Service was fast and attentive. Menu was simple but offered many options for foodies. I ordered the omelette with home fries and side of grits. Every was good. For once, I have no complaints about his choice for breakfast. Tables are small but it feels like you are at someone's home and I assume that was by design. We live jersey and I can honestly say I'll become a regular. Parking is terrible but that's expected in PA.

I was pretty impressed. Sadly, they were out of chocolate banana so we tried the s'mores fancy donut, which at $2 was tasty enough but nothing spectacular. Maybe I should've tried one of the weirder flavors, like razzbery[sic] balsamic or halva pistachio. Or the plainer donuts, like lavender vanilla.

However, the chicken was quite good, particularly the dry-rub za'atar. I think the dry-rubs are the way to go, because they really showcase the perfectly-crisp crunchiness of the fried chicken. The chicken breast was juicy and not dry at all, which was great. The chile garlic was tasty too, but it seemed like they were trying to copy Korean fried chicken places and failed to execute it quite as well. The chile was a bit overwhelming in terms of flavor (spice level was fine though), and the fried outer shell didn't hold up quite so well to the glazed coating. They came with a tiny container of Japanese-tasting pickles, and honey-glazed donuts that were not-too-sweet and perfect with the chicken (think chicken and waffles). 

Overall though, I was bit surprised about the value we got from this place. I expected to spend a lot of money for not a whole lot of food, but the portion sizes were pretty decent for the price, and the chicken came out hot, fresh, and tasty.

Such a wonderful and friendly staff!! 

We've actually ate her me twice. Their curry is absolutely delicious which I will definitely order again... 

However their Tom Ka was in my opinion not very good. 
(Why I rated 4 stars) 

My boyfriend really loves their dish called "tiger tear". Get them to make its native Thai and your sure to be sweating for awhile!! 

I was very sick last night and had the craving for green curry. It was 9:45 and they welcomed us in and were so friendly even though they closed in 15 minutes. They have gained a new regular and I'm looking forward to trying their other dishes!

We love this place!  The food is great, portion sizes are ample, and easily shareable if you don't want to overspend/overeat.  Top if off with their pastry, and mimosas and you've got a great breakfast joint!

You can expect to wait for at least 20 minutes on most weekends, however, and there isn't a lot of room to do that inside the restaurant.  That said, this place is worth the wait!

In so far as McDonald's goes, this is the best. Well run. Clean. Efficient given the inherent disadvantages of staffing. The food is the food. The manager works tirelessly to ensure that the standards are high. Go Jennifer.
It was good and no lack of food.
frienly stuff confortable beds and free HBO What else you need. lol

This place is pretty much always packed, but they seem to have everything you might need for art and craft projects.  It's a bit out of the way for me, so I don't stop in here often unless I'm the area and I need something.
One of my fav Mexican spots in town! Very delicious and great pricing! The quesadilla a la Diablo is a must try with rice. Yummmm

Delicious brunch....mimosas, strawberry lemon aid vodka, burrito and eggs Bahia. 
Yummmm

Shhhhhheeeeeeeeiiiittt.

The place is black and graffiti. I love it. It has all the makings of a great small venue. I went for the Jang Night with Spank Rock and friends. Good B-more club banged out of their surprisingly awesome sound system. I also appreciated the projector. The bar portion was pretty rad too. They had your run of the mill hipster beers and a few good beers on tap. Also the prices were cheap. I'm talking liver damage cheap. 

I'll be back to shake it again Barbary. Thanks.

first off, I think it was crazy to charge for 3D glasses , I go to the movie tavern and king of Prussia all the time and there is no charge, the manager on duty came to the window and had a small debate with me about how king of Prussia charges their guests on 3D showings, once he did that I knew it would be my last time there, I live about 8 mins away and believe me even with the renovations I will tell all my friends not to go there ever, its better for me to travel to king of Prussia and to the movie tavern to enjoy my movie time,

The people here were rude, lazy and they lied. I asked to have a 10×13 image printed and they said it would take 4 hours...interesting. Not willing to argue, I asked if they would print a test sheet just so I could see how the colors looked. I told them I would come pick it up at 2pm the next day. I showed up at 2:30 the next day and nothing was done and they couldn't even find my flash drive. Great! After searching for 20 minutes and blaming the night staff with no apology, it magically appeared. This time they said I could wait and they would print. I guess miracles happen over night and print time can decrease from 4 hrs to minutes. I had to head back to work so I told them I would be back at 5pm. I show up at 5:30pm(again trying to give them extra time). Surprise, nothing had been done. Now frustrated, the lady says she will do it right now. She puts the flash drive in and explains that it is blank. Somewhere through this long process someone has deleted my file. Not sure how that's possible they printed me a copy the night before. After 3 pointless trips, I left with no print out, no file, and higher frustration than ever. 

Terrible customer service trumped all here. A phone call or an apology or any sense of sincerity would have been nice. 

Drive a little further and get the job done right.

Horrible. Apparently not enough employees working, or not enough doing their job. Immediately upon waking in there was a huge puddle of soda on the floor, went to get a table and high chair, not one was clean...in the whole place. Tables still had food on them from other people that had long been finished. The floor was sticky and food was all over.  Went to get food, another puddle. After getting everything together puddles still in the floor, my sister fell in one puddle and I slid through another. An employee actually stepped over a puddle to get himself a soda. Made no effort to clean the soda up until I said that my sister had fallen and they needed to be cleaned up before someone got really hurt. 
We had toddlers so we were there to go in to the tiny play area. No one supervising at all in that area. Children that were well over toddler were all over the place. Unplugging machines and running over smaller children. 
The only reason that this got a star was because the food was actually decent, and the poor girl that worked behind the ticket redemption counter was super nice and actually seemed to care about the customers. 
The place could use some employees that actually walk around and clean and keep things even slightly under control.

Very good beer selection but slow service and lousy food. It took too ing for the food to come to our table.  I had a hamburger and it was nothing more than a frozen piece of meat.  Don't come here for the food.  Just order the beer.

Bowling - crowded lanes & seating for your lane. Often bumping into people in the lane beside us. All the balls were very heavy so not much fun to bowl with.
We ordered onion rings & fries as well as a pitcher of pop. There was 4 of us, 2 adults, 2 kids under age of six. The food was not too good at all.
