This complex is certainly not worth the rent it's charging. I would avoid it at all costs.

Could be very good! Had lunch there today for first time. Breast was a tiny overcooked piece of crust with part of the back intact. Horrible cut of chicken! They should be embarrassed to serve his. Spice and flavor was good just needed more than three bites of meat. 
Had the slaw. Clearly smelled spoiled. Had to spit it out. 
Wings were tiny three-biters but at least weren't burnt to a crisp like breast. 
If you want true quality hot chicken go to Big Shakes in Franklin.

Snotty hostess, unless you like stink eye. Runniest hollondais ever.
Good otherwise. 
Don't bother - otherwise

We were directed here by someone at another brewery. That, for me, is a good sell. I was told that it may be too crowded, and to go to some other place if it was. But there was a spot right there on the patio. I was mainly looking for beer, but we were hungry.
Parking was a bit atrocious, but it's more the neighborhood than anything. It was also very difficult to get in to the area. We ended up only about a block away though, in a free spot.
The patio is nice enough, but features cheap lawn furniture. I'm not looking for white table cloths, but when my chair is from Wal-Mart and cost less than half what I'm paying for my meal, it's annoying. But I'm glad we were outside because of what the inside looked like: dank is the word I'd use. Everything was dark, musty and kind of strange. If they were going for the British pub look, they didn't succeed. The size of the bar really disappointed me: it's supposed to be a brewpub, so why have a bar that's only 5' or so long?
And that should have signaled to me, right there, that this is the sort of brewpub that focuses on food, not beer, because that seems to be the case. The beer wasn't awful, but everything was very safe, and a lot of it just wasn't very good. I was impressed that they did nitro/cask pours, but they were served cold.
I was told later, by a local bartender who also spoke highly of this place, that their beer is supposed to be more English style. That actually redeems the beer in my mind a little, because it makes sense why some of them tasted as they did. But they're still not good.
The food also was not good. I mean, it wasn't bad, where I'd throw it down and say "This is awful" and refuse to eat it, but it was just so mediocre. I got the sausage sandwich, which was served with sauerkraut from the can on a slightly stale roll. And the sausage was undercooked. I got the mashed potatoes for a side, which were good, but were topped with a watery gravy that looked, had the consistency of, and tasted more like sweet coffee than anything.
My companion got the vegetarian sloppy joe, which was more like vegetarian chili than anything. She got a side of the vegetables, which were undercooked.
The service was relatively slow throughout the meal but the girl was nice, until I asked about a charge at the end of the meal. Both of our standard sides had been fries, but we both decided to change. I noticed (the list of sides along with their prices are at the bottom of the menu) that my mashed potatoes with gravy were $0.25 more than the standard fries, while my companion's veggies were the same price. On the ticket, instead of being upcharged $0.25, I was charged the full $1.75. The server said that this was how it was and it was more a fluke that my companion wasn't charged than me being charged. My whole thing: if you're going to charge the full price for a side substitution, then give me the other side. It's just fair. But we protested and she huffed off, not to return for about 10 minutes after that.
I was also irked by the sampler price: $1.25 each, which added up to $10. $10 for eight samples? That seems high, and it would have been nice to know that beforehand.
We could have gotten away with "That place kind of sucked" and left it there, but the issue with the ticket, and the fact that I had been deliberately led here by others left me feeling so slighted that I started saying "I should say something to the manager." I'm not the type to usually talk to the manager. In fact, I've done it all of twice in my recent memory: once here and once at the worst Boston Market I'd ever encountered in suburban Atlanta, where I told the guy that his franchise should be taken away and that he should be ashamed of his operation. Here, I just mentioned that it was a bad experience all around and that he should consider worked on the sampler pricing, because $10 is absurd for the quality of that beer. I had already paid, and wanted nothing but to give them feedback that I hoped would help them.
Will it? Probably not, because this place is so busy that they don't really need me and they don't need my feedback. I'm sure I was written off as just a self-righteous asshole. I would offer this to readers though: there are four brewpubs in the Broad Ripple area so, if you're looking to patronize one for dinner, your options are not limited and are much better elsewhere.
1*, but 2* when I realized that the beer was supposed to be English style. It would have helped to have known that, just as it would have helped to have known beforehand the sampler pricing. A sign outside saying "This is going to suck" also would have been helpful, but not necessary.

Update! TWO MONTHS later and my nails are still in HORRIBLE condition. They still literally HURT when I bump them. I cannot wait for them to be fully grown out!

I filed a complaint with the better business bureau and TT's never even had the decency to respond to BBB! 

Stay away!!!!!!!

Rooms are WAY overpriced for what you get. You get more at a super8 or best western for 1/4 of the price. No microwave in room, only a tiny fridge. Beds were comfy but very standard. There wasn't any breakfast or free parking. We had to park at the mall and walk over, considering it's $24 (plus tax) a day to park. And all of the activities are triple the price they should be. We bought some alcoholic drink wavers (3 tickets for $50) and one voucher scored us a sample of beer in a Dixie cup. Restaurants are overpriced and we had the worst service. Food was cold, burger was cooked rare when it was supposed to be medium, etc.

It's beautiful, and there are some fun activities (we really liked the ice sculptors) but there's no point in staying here. Just come look around and do some of the activities for free (they didn't even card us to get into the indoor swimming area) and spend the night at a cheaper hotel. You'll be happier in the end, trust me.

I bought a car two months ago and my battery would not keep a charge. When I brought it back there they wouldn't warranty my battery unless I waited for a 3 hour deep cycle charge. I told them I didn't have 3 hours to wait and I already knew the battery was shot because I had been boosting it all month. They tried to explain to me how batteries work and how it was probably just the cold. I told them I didn't need to know how a battery worked I just need to have the car start when I push the button. They wouldn't help me so I had to buy a real battery at Canadian Tire.

They care more about waterfalls than people.  Stood standing in my room on the phone waiting for someone to pick up so I could order room service.  No dice.  Called the front desk.  They put me back in the phone tree.  More wasted time and told to wait.  Called back and asked for a manager.  Shelly said I'd have to wait.  All she had to do was take my order so I didn't have to stand in the middle of the room any longer.  Of course not.  40 minutes later I wound my way through their stupid jungle and sat at the bar. No food there.  Had to go over to their less than relaxing sushi bar for $40 central TN fish.  Thanks Gaylord.  I was exhausted and wanted in room delivery.  Seemingly a simple request for a trillion dollars per night.  They failed miserably.

When I walked in the place was filled with smoke. I asked the guy at the counter and he said it was because he just emptied the pans. I asked if the wings were fried or smoked since the smoky atmosphere got me in the mood for smoked meat but they are fried. The cook came up and told me I could get smoked wings but I would have to call ahead a few hours in advance. I ordered ribs with coleslaw and baked beans. The BBQ sauce was awesome but lacking and when I went for the bottle on the table it was wing sauce! The meat was cooked well and tender but NOT SMOKED. Smoked meat has a natural ring around it that shows it was cooked correctly, slow and low and this was lacking. I got a picture of the cut open rib. The coleslaw was bland but it's hard to find good coleslaw that isn't homemade. The beans were awesome. I guess I should have checked the sign before I went in because they started closing up shop around  me. For BBQ there has to be a better place than Alex's around Carrollwood.

We were very displeased by this restaurant. There was one server on the floor. We received dinner salads before our appetizer even came out. Then waited about ten minutes after salads to get the apps. Our pasta dish we were splitting didn't come for another 20 minutes. Our waters were empty about 15 minutes in and never refilled. Along with this our server was not very pleasant. The food was average. Will not go back and do not recommend. Being that I work in a restaurant myself, I try to give the benefit of the doubt but this was ridiculous.

What can I say?  Its someone's backyard, which is nice and its help yourself, which is okay.  For a city with no rules, this place has a lot of rules.  The hangar steak is well done.  The fish balls are a salty mess and should be avoided.  If there is a good band playing, its a good time.  Go there to hang out and have a glass or two.

If I could give a zero 0 0000!!!!!

On the wrong style and type of coach from SF to Reno and the drivers are at the mercy of dispatch and management 

This sucks so bad that we are taking and paying for a coach that is not ours and we paid for reserved seats and the drivers don't even help arguments between passengers by placing reserved seat signs 

I will ask for all my money back and probably FLY home 

I will never ever take or recommend this bus again in my life. 

This is typical public service issues and with nasty attitudes to boot
This is the worst Dunkin'. Coffee is never right food has a weird chemical taste. Rather drive a few more miles to avoid this place.

Good sir (or madam), have you spent your evening doing battle with your liver and now wish to sate a burning hunger for non-specific meat products sold from a cart on the sidewalk? Then today is your lucky day! Buy lotto tickets! But first, have a Lucky Dog.

It was only in adulthood that I came to enjoy hot dogs. Even now I can't stomach the cheap ones but the higher grade hot dogs usually hit the spot. Enter Lucky Dog. Like many others I'm going to label Lucky Dog a "New Orleans tradition", much like getting hammered on Bourbon St. It's something you do once in your life, like a right of passage.

So, as someone past the "let's go to the Quarter and get blind drunk" phase, would I eat a Lucky Dog (sober)? Probably not and here's why. It's not so much the quality of the hot dog itself, that's something I can handle, but for me the true glory of a hot dog is the fixings and Lucky Dog doesn't even come close to matching some better options on the city. 

The second thing that makes me hesitant to eat a Lucky Dog is the vendors. Well, most like the vendors' hygiene. Or lack there of. I've never seen one of the vendors use so much as hand sanitizer after handling money and food in some pretty dirty French Quarter conditions. 

But if you're inebriated and got one of those random drunken cravings, stumble up to the giant plastic hot dog shaped cart and have a foot long. You can have the whole thing, I'll pass. I already ate. Really.

Bland and lacking. When you order cauliflower rice as a base it's expected to get more than a tablespoon or so of it. Tofu was tasteless and not cooked long enough (seemed to be cooked on only one side). Even with the dressing (which was good on its own) it tasted pretty boring. I don't expect much from vegan options in Ta, and it seems it will be that way for a while. The staff was very friendly at least.

The guy who used to do my nails quit. I returned a few times, a sweet lady who I could tell was a new employee gave a great hand massage and I tipped extra for that! The next time I returned, she was on vacation and I decided that would be my last visit. I stopped going because the other employees who worked there were just killing the vibe! They made the experience miserable and I just couldn't justify returning. The language barrier didn't matter, Even if they weren't bad mouthing me, their attitude definitely made me feel as though they were. Their work was sloppy, what else can I say? This place just reeks of discontent.

My husband went here last night to pick up (3) 12" cheese steaks (Large on their menu) and (2) two orders of plain fries. The bill came to $51.89! Here's the kicker, they charge $1.50 for fried onions! Are you friggin' kidding me? We also added green peppers and mushrooms to ours for another $6.00....wow. So, now the 12" cheese steak goes from $10.99 to $15.45. That is rediculous. Like I said, my husband ran out to grab dinner, if I was there, I would have walked out. I understand an additional charge for mushrooms, even peppers...but fried onions? And at $1.50 per add on, the sandwich should have been loaded...but I had to search for the onions and peppers...I did spot the mushrooms...but I would no way say it justified an additional $4.50 on a 12" cheese steak. In all fairness, the cheese steak was good, but if I'm paying $4.50 to add fried onions, peppers and mushrooms, I shouldn't have to search through the sandwich for them. I'd say the peppers and onions were between diced and minced in size. Will definitely NOT be ordering here again. Just a heads up too, although the fries at $3.99 didn't seem a bad deal...they sealed them in a round foil pan with a lid..so they were  damp with no crunch at all.

Don't come in at 9:28 after a movie, even though they don't close until 10; even if you order quickly and skip appetizers and dessert, they will make you feel as awkward as they can and make your food inedible. I LOVE spicy food so I ordered the vegan vegetable curry - Medium, and it was the spiciest thing I've ever tasted. It left my lips burning and my stomach churning. The waiters response: it'll open up your sinuses. My girlfriend got hers Mild and it was still spicy, but at least edible. I attempted to finish my meal, as I hate wasting, but simply could not. We finished hers and paid for both. As we were leaving we noticed we were both more bloated than we have ever been before, though we didn't even eat much food. They clearly did something to the food out of spite; their displeasure was obvious from the moment we walked in. 

I have worked in restaurants at all levels so I know the feeling when customers arrive late at night and you just want to go home (I've had people come in 5 minutes prior to closing, but we still treated them with respect), but this is ridiculous.

This was by far my worst dining experience and I would advice all others to avoid this place at all cost.

My boyfriend and I were very excited to try La Collina for our Valentine's Day dinner, as we'd heard that the food was really good.  The ambiance was nice and we live in Manayunk so the location was great for us...however, we were very disappointed with our food.  We started with fried calamari which was probably the best part of our entire dining experience..and it wasn't even THAT great!  I ordered the linguine de pesce--linguine with crabmeat, shrimp and clams--and i was sorely disappointed.  The pasta was swimming in butter sauce--way too much.  The amount of crab was little to none, and the clams seemed a bit under-cooked.  My boyfriend got the surf and turf ($45!) and was also disappointed.  The middle of the lobster was some sort of stuffed crabmeat, which wasn't on the menu and he doesn't like crab at all.  Oh plus, our waiter was VERY odd...a bit Italian dude who was just weird, and our food came out on the bottom tray of a cart that he used to roll out someone else dinner on the top part of the cart. I have to say, I've never seen that before.  The food is PRICEY  here too, and I wouldn't have minded paying if the food was worthwhile but it really wasn't.  We won't be going back here again.  Oh, and NONE of the desserts are homemade.  I dont think the pasta is either..so, how "authentic" of an Italian restaurant could this be?? I would recommend against going here...

Modern style and beautiful view of UT and the river. Unfortunately, the food was lacking in originality, quality and taste.  The waitress was very nice and accomodating when we asked to send our food back.

Even with a discounted tickets using groupon, this tour was a waste of money.  The guide must have been new because he didn't have the background and stories that we had hoped to hear about the history  and blending of cuisine in Cajun and creole cooking. It was the only tour we took that we weren't warned to stay together and keep a portion of the sidewalk clear for other passers by. in some instances he didn't even stop to address the group zbut rather talked as he moved forward with all following behind. Needless to say, we didn't hear much of what he did try  to say.

If this tour happens everyday, it was not well organized. 2 of the 4 restaurants we visited were not prepared for us and we were left standing on the corner or outside the door. In one instance I had to tell customers that there was not a line to get in or the restaurant would have lost those customers.

The restaurants we visited were generally 2nd class with somewhat bland jumbalaya, an OK gumbo and po'-boy. The nicest surprise was a little pizza shop that made their own focacciaa bread and offered  a nice variation on a muffaletta (-Little Vic's). Not necessarily traditional, but nice. Oceana, Napoleon House, Tujague were  the others.

In general the afternoon was a disappointment and I could not recommend this tour.

I have no complaints about portion size as it was just a TASTE of New Orleans.

I guess it all depends how valuable your time is to you. Came here for an oil change off of Groupon, still had to pay about six dollars in "Servicenter fees ", And the whole thing took... Wait for it... 2 1/2 hours! I mean that's completely ridiculous. So that's the decision you get to make.

The worst experience I've ever had. A beautiful hotel in a great location.  All ruined by an incompetent and unprofessional staff. I have stayed at Marriott properties all over the world and have never had an experience like this... it's a shame because I had high expectations due to my other JW experiences..

I came here a while ago to buy some running gear -- tights, socks, etc. 

Aside from the prices being absolutely ridiculous, the service was nonexistent. I went on a weekday afternoon, and the store was practically empty. 

I mosied around the running section for at least 15 minutes. I saw lots of employees walk past me, yet no one stopped and asked if I needed any help. 

Although I eventually found what I needed, it would have been nice to have been offered some help. 

My boyfriend was upstairs in the camping section and experienced the same thing. No service whatsoever. 

I'll never come back. Next time I want to pay an exorbitant amount of money for fitness gear, I'll hit up REI.

Mediocre all the way.

We were looking for a good bfast, and was sorely disappointed. The food was mediocre at best and the service was terrible. We waited for 40 minutes for our food, all eggs and pancakes. When it came out it was not very warm, and was blasé. The server did not check up on us and only mentioned in passing that they were busy and that was the reason for the delay, which is interesting as a group who came in after us got their food, ate, and left before we got our food.

I've been to this establishment 3 times. The 1st time was within their 1st month in business...I asked the fellow behind the counter for a cup of whatever their best coffee is (I love coffee and have grown accustomed to Starbucks reserve coffees)..anyway the guy told me he'd make me an "Americano" and (I thought at that moment) this is either prepared poorly, or their stuff isn't good...one of the owners were there but she didn't even say hello and was talking on the phone to what sounded like her friend for our entire visit... 
2nd time: met a buddy for coffee there (also had to give them another chance). Spent $4.xx for my coffee...tasted a bit like moms "Tasters Choice" from when I was a kid...brought back a good memory, but wasn't happy with my coffee nor was my friend...but...before we left that same owner said hello and even gave my son a chocolate chip cookie..the gesture was so nice I had to come back.
3rd time with another friend: 
3rd(and last time I stopped in). My friend wanted coffee while our kids were at Hebrew school up the street...he's a "Dunkin' coffee drinker...I'm a Starbucks coffee drinker...so I said "let's stop at the coffee house" ..it was about 6pm. As we walked in the other owner (husband) was there and my friend asked him what he suggested as he had never been in...the owner said "oh, it's been quiet today so I am closeting....if you really want something I guess I can make you something.." mind you it says 7 or 730was closing...we left...told him not to do us any favors...Not very happy...my friend refuses to ever go due to the experience...such a shame too! I love supporting local businesses, but not when I have to be sorry to friends for the recommendation...

Used to love St. Louis Breadco, but the quality and quantity has decreased significantly.  The flatbreads are small and not very good.  The only thing that has not changed is the price- very expensive for what you get.

Honestly, I wanted to like this restaurant as it is convenient to the County Theatre.  The waitress was very friendly and provided timely service.  However, the food was the tex-mex equivalent of thai cooking.  Tom Kha soup tasted more like warn milk with no spices, veggie dumplings were tasteless and at $7.95, a disappointment.  The basic Pad Thai was soggy and tasteless and the seafood curry @ $20 was also bland, with almost no vegetables and seafood that was tasteless as well.

The pizza was fairly decent but nothing special.  The dessert bar looked like it had a lot of good items but none of them tasted good.  How do you mess up dessert?  Even the ice cream was strange.  The vanilla looked it had some chocolate mixed in (and I didn't get the swirl) and it didn't even taste like ice cream.  The salad bar was horrible.  Everything looked dried out and old.  We were there on a Sunday morning right when they opened so I expected a little more from the salad bar.  They had iced tea but it came from a machine and tasted terrible.  No wonder you can get a coupon for $0.99 meal when you buy a $17 game card.  The food was worth about $0.99.  The game room was just what you would expect - loud and obnoxious.  The kids had fun and that's why we went but honestly I would never go back and if I ever do I won't be eating the food.

My friend and I both received gel manicures with Living Social vouchers on Friday evening. We were the only ones in the place, so we didn't have to wait. The free massage was nice and the techs were pleasant. 

The problem is that I requested a glitter on just my ring fingers and the tech insisted on using regular nail polish over the gel color but under the gel topcoat. After two days, the glitter is pealing up from under the top coat (I will post pics). Also, my other nails are chipping after only 4 days. You can also see that my cuticles were not well groomed. 

Other salons where I've received gel manicures, my polish lasts 10 days easily, 2+weeks at great salons, like The Nail Club in Longhorne.

This locations leaves something to be desired. 
Long wait times & no strawberry cones leaving my two year old daughter pretty sad. It's just 'okay' & I'd rather go to Twistee Treat.
Was told this was an "Italian" eatery. The metal plate had more taste the the Egg Plant Parm. It was more like "Sauce from a can" heated up together in the oven. Taco Bell has better Italian food!

I have been to Franklin Nails a number of times and it seems the service and quality of the work has gone downhill considerably since my first visit last winter.  As someone else stated, they are not Un friendly, but they are definitely not  friendly!  I don't know when people are going to learn that their business would do so much better if they just went out of the way to have a conversation.  It's like pulling teeth to get them to talk.  I've been in there with at least three others getting work done and not a single legible word is spoke the entire time.  It's not a very comfortable environment.  

The number one BIGGEST issues I found was cleanliness!  I was in having acrylics done and it took quit some time so I was able to watch the technician next to me complete two manicures and start a third.  After each one, she got up went to the sink with the tools rinsed them in water, dried them off, pulled a sterile seal bag from her desk and re-sealed them in the sterile bags.  The items were NOT sterilized.  When the next guest came in she reached in the drawer and pulled out the bag, making the client think they were getting sterilized tools!  This is NOT COOL!!!!  This was a deliberate act as everyone knows what those bags represent!!

Not sure how they are for just a mani/pedi, but the work being done on acrylics has been not very good.  I have had the acrylic chipping off at the sides within there or four days of them being done.  This has never happened at any salon I've ever been to.  They also do a terrible polish job.  One male technician, I had never seen before, put the polish on so quickly, it pooled and crinkled as it was trying to dry.   I have had the acrylics done by Tina and Kenny as well as others.  The only one who has done it without chipping was Kenny.  

 It's a real shame because this place is so convenient from my work.  I think like many business' they got a little to comfortable with their success!

My roommates & I decided to try this place out. The pho is okay but it's pretty pricey. We also got pork fried rice, beef chow fun and the tofu pad thai at about $9.45 per plate. All of the dishes were mediocre at best. I would definitely try somewhere else. However, their smoothies/drinks are very yummy but again pretty pricey starting at 5$.

This place SUCKS!!!!!!! It has definitely fallen off since back in the day. The wait staff set u up to pay more money and do NOT inform you of the specials. Plus the specials end earlier than usual and they close way too early. There are so many better places to go in the area that you should NEVER ever have to go here. Save your money and enjoy yourself somewhere else! If you really want Copa Banana, go to the one on South Street.

Wish I could give this place more stars because the atmosphere is great, staff is nice but my meal was just terrible. I forced half of it down. I got a chicken dish, the sauce was tasty but my chicken was barely able to be cut and very rubbery. I have no idea where it came from but I honestly was disgusted by it. My dates penne pasta was tasty and prepared nicely. Maybe it was just a bad run... :-/

Seems to be a family operation where the people have far more ambition than ability.  We stopped in based on several favorable on-line reviews, but found a hokey decor that was a bit grubby, and really disorganized.  Ridiculously high prices given the ambiance and food. Also, the full bar that apparently used to be here, has been replaced by a seafood-ish buffet that cost anywhere between $28.99 and $32.99 (based on staff word-of-mouth; it wasn't listed yet on the dirty, worn-out menus). 

All they had drink-wise was a boring collection of bottled domestic beers, and dependable old Abita Amber in a bottle.

I just returned from lunch with two friends at Pita Jungle. The food was OK, not spectacular, and the service was just OK as well. They got one friend's order wrong and we waited quite a while before our server checked in and she could send it back. They corrected it quickly, though.

I dropped the restaurant a star, though, because they brought our water with plastic straws. The server said they add them automatically unless you request NOT to have them. When we asked for to-go boxes for our leftovers, those were plastic, too. I can't remember the last time I saw a non-biodegradable take-out box. Get with the program, Pita Jungle.

terrible services here.  tried it twice and both times took 30 minutes to get my food and turtle paced service for drink refills.  actually walked out on my second visit due to not having my food yet and the manager telling me that it would be ready in "about 10 minutes".  i recommend going across the street to sauce where the service and food are much more worthy of your business.

I've tried twice to get in on separate occasions! Once when I was 22 with a NJ state ID but I wasn't allowed in and was told I needed a drivers license or Had to be 24 to get in with regular state ID. Now I'm 23 with a probationary auto license and yet I can't get in still! I am of age and have been served alcohol with my probationary license so I don't understand why this bar is making such a big deal! Funny thing is I wasn't even going to drink on those two occasions I went to hang out with some of My fiance's friends! I honestly am over the fact of getting into this bar to be honest! I cant  say much for the service as I can't get in but this whole I'd thing is just plain stupid!  Its a bar not a damn airport!

Horrible experience. I waited more than an hour before I was told I needed an appoint. They only have 2 stylist and no time in between. Why no have the courtesy and tell people to no waste the time waiting?

Would give a zero for this ponzi scam wannabe. We had them for so many years and thought we were being discounted. Cant believe American Airlines associates themselves with this company. They also scam when you realize you being ripped off and make it 2 more months of being ripped off. We only found out until we moved out the house and our electric bill was the same even with SOLAR!. Insane.

This restaurant is beautiful inside and out but the food isn't that good and it's expensive. ZERO options for vegetarians and vegans. The only choice I had was a Caesar salad.
The Depot is also in the worst part of Reno by the bus station downtown. Many, many transients, prostitutes, meth, etc. I'm glad they restored the building but the location is horrible.

Went last night for a private event. I was looking forward to it as I really love their rest. at Union Station. I was very disappointed. I can only believe the food we were served was not the food they serve regularly. The Pork Chop was dry, the salad lacked flavor and the "twice baked potato" looked like the frozen ones you microwave, clearly not fresh. Not at all what I expected from this long standing restaurant family.

My wife: Can I get an almond pretzel?

The cashier: No.........

......... We left...........

I am fortunate to have the WORST car buying experience ever from Crest Honda. We went to crest Honda to buy a car for my friend who has been in Nashville for approx 2 years and I am the co- buyer since his credit history is less than. 2 years old. We decided to buy the vehicle from crest honda and told the salesman "Ray" Raymond Thompson that we would like to proceed. We locked down the prices and the estimated Apr based on our credit scores. Ray said that he will have to get the car from another dealership and it would take a couple of days. We were in no hurry and told him that we will take the delivery after a couple of weeks on 15 th November. 

Ray asked us for some additional documentation for the paperwork  which we provided to him on Monday 11th nov. My friend (Buyer) spoke to him a couple of times during the week informing him that we will b there on Friday 15 th November to take the delivery and the salesman confirmed of the same. 

We go t the dealership in a taxi as we were sure to get the delivery of the vehicle and reach there at 2:00 pm. As soon as we enter the dealership I inform ray that I have to pick up my kid from school at 4:30 pm and we need to complete all formalities as soon as possible. After making  us sit around for a hour or so we are asked to sign some paperwork, we are taken to another accessories tech who explains us the standard stuff, while we are going through the explanation on the accessories, Ray walks in and says that there is a problem, he had committed a price earlier which equated to approx monthly payment of 363 per month and now says that the monthly payment would be 393 per month. The reason being we didn't get approved for premier rates from the finance company and we given standard rates. We ask him to work out a solution for the same and get back to us. But he or the finance manager are absolutely not ready to negotiate.

It is extremely frustrating when you discuss rates with the dealerahip , provide necessary documentation a week before but see a major jump in rates on the day of the delivery. This is absolutely not impressive or not called for. Most importantly this information is placed in front of us at 4:00 pm and we had to leave then. I was extremely late to pick up my kid from school as we had call for a cab again and it reached crest at 4:30 pm. 

While we were waiting we informed the finance manager to work out deal to provide us some additional accessories to compensate for their inefficient people or processes. He said he will try to and will let us know in a couple of days. 


Irrespective of what decision Crest Honda takes this has been the worst experience I have had In a  dealership and I dont think there can be lower levels of service than the one we received.

We will be reaching out to Honda Global Service to register this complaint as well just to make sure there is some level of governance on such dealerships and their customer service skills. Its not fair that Honda gets a bad name due to such irresponsible, insensitive dealers.

Let me start this by stating: I LOVE DENNY'S. NOT Philadelphia Denny's, though. I am the traveler who books accommodations based on the proximity of Denny's locations. I know...I know. Please don't judge me. 

Now, I've been to this location a number of times and it can be a hit or miss. If you are lucky enough to get Ron as your server, your experience will be that much better. I always order a breakfast skillet, but at my most recent visit, the portion was literally cut in half. Not that I need a full skillet of potatoes and cheese, but..hey. I mentioned it to the server but didn't make a big deal about it. I'm not sure what that was about but it will definitely decrease my chances of returning and stick to my "out of town" Denny's. My niece ordered a salad and was told they were out of eggs, so they wouldn't be able to put any on her salad. However, they compensated by adding more steak to the salad. No argument there. 

Hopefully, if I return they don't force me into portion control and they have eggs. It'd make for a more enjoyable experience.
Not for me. The patties are flappy and some meat had an after taste. Red Robin would be a better choice. The wings are fried slathered in their chili sauce. Won't be returning.

$94 for the worst Italian I've ever had.  The place is empty for a reason.  Calamari was chewy, seafood dish was chewy, veal was sub optimal.  Best part was the wine.  Find another place on Main Street.

I've been here before; food's okay especially when you have an MSG craving. But this last visit surprised me. Greasy food and dirty surroundings put us off this place forever. The restaurant was empty on a Saturday night, and no one bothered to replenish the empty pans.  This place has gone to the dogs.

Went here twice this past weekend, once for a late dinner and once for brunch. The dinner was pretty good...food was enjoyed by all (despite taking an unreasonable amount of time to arrive), decent drink selections, fair to middling service. This was when the six of us were just about the only party there. I'd chalked the slowness up to it being late so maybe they were short staffed or in the middle of cleaning. It wasn't a big deal then because we were content to lounge around for a while. Brunch today was another story. I called twenty minutes before we were to eat to see if they could accommodate a party of eight. They said no problem, and would be awaiting our arrival. We get there and the host said the table still wasn't ready but he'd make sure we were seated soon. He did nothing of the sort. Rather, he went ahead and sat no fewer than three small parties (which had arrived after us, naturally) at large tables while we stood there taking up space. After we'd been there for twenty five minutes, with not so much as a glance from the host, someone leaving the restaurant came up to one of us and said "Just leave now, the service is obviously terrible and the food is even worse." So we turned around and went to the Continental instead. Which was lovely.
Went here with my best friend (the groom) for an appointment. We were ignored for over an hour past the appointment time. Very disorganized

Well, at least their drink prices were awesome during happy hour!

Stopped in with the family during a day of drinking up and down the coast.  Naturally I was feeling a little hungry so I decided to get some oysters and some crab cakes to snack on.  The oysters were great, but all you need is some fresh oysters and you win.  The crab cakes, and the collard greens that accompanied them were terrible.  Who would've thought the worst crab cakes I ever had would be in Florida?  The collard greens were over cooked and also the worst I've ever had.  Still don't hate this place though, I'd go back in a heartbeat for 2 dollar whiskeys!
3 pools, acres of indoor gardens, 20+ restaurants, 1 PLACE to GET COFFEE!!!   And a line of 30 people waiting.  Come on...

I purchased a fundraiser voucher for 12 months of small arrangements to support a teacher friend and his school.  For $20, it was a steal, I felt otherwise after my first visit to this florist.  Their hours are only until 5pm on the weekdays, so I made a b-line to their place this past Saturday, realizing I could make it there (barely!) by 3pm to get my very first bouquet.  I called @ 2:40pm to confirm it was ok to pick up and that I would be arriving just at 3pm.  They confirmed to me that yes to come and that we close at 3 as I realized.  
I literally pulled in a 2:59pm and the woman was out in the lot, putting out their closed sign when I parked.  I confirmed it was still ok for me to come in and I barely got a response.  She led me to the counter, had me complete the form, told me a couple things that I could clearly understand on the voucher and handed me my dainty bouquet of flowers.  Mostly small roses (4 of which will never open), but a nice little surprise for my 8 year old daughter who loves getting flowers.
I realize I pushed the limits by showing up when they close, but I called first to get confirmation it was ok, advised I'd be getting there at the very last minute and I felt like they could not spare an extra couple minutes to possibly appreciate a potential new customer.  Oh well, I expect too much from businesses.  I will hope for a better experience when I arrive in the middle of their work day.  If not, I will get my $ worth for what I spent and spend my hard earned money somewhere else.  It's as simple as that.

Sorry to say I cannot add anything else to Yelp b/c I was not given any extra time to take a peek around or check out prices, etc....

**Revised to add a note that the owner was not the one who helped me on this day and they generously sent me a gift certificate with an apology which I find very classy, so I am headed back to check them out real soon**

Terrible.  Really takes all the joy out of a normally fun hobby.  One time I asked if they had a certain type of screws I needed to mount a hard drive in a name brand PC.  They said no without looking at what I had.  I then asked if they had any used  screws.  No.  I turned and looked at their giant repair shop full of old computer parts.  You don't have any screws back there?  No.  My poor experiences go back to their earliest days...

Cliiiiche. I do love irish theme pubs, but this reminded me of the Olive garden of irish-themed pubs.  No real standouts, the same kitchy things stuck around the place as most other places like it.

The only benefit I can see is that it is the fastest place to get a drink prior to getting on a train.

Last night we had a craving for Chinese food & tried China Taste.  It was not the best experience.  First impressions are also important, & upon entering the restaurant I immediately noticed the facility was very dirty.  The inside needs a good cleaning.  I ordered the Szechuan Chicken.  The serving size was good but the flavor was not so good.  I basically received thin strips of chicken breast cooked with an enormous amount of onions.  The onions were not cooked very much, so the flavor was like that of eating a raw onion.  I quickly had to push most of the onions aside.  The only flavoring on the chicken that came through was sesame oil & salt, salt, salt.  This dish was saturated in salt.  The rest of the evening I unfortunately had to keep guzzling water.  I have to say I won't be giving China Taste another taste again.

We placed our first order from Wang Cai this evening.  With the exception of the very nice delivery guy, it was the worst experience I have had in a very long time.  All of the food was hard as a rock and tasted terrible!  I called the store (I cannot call this place a restaurant) and calmly explained my concerns with the food.  I was told, "It's the same as always".  I explained that I don't know about "always" because this was my first order.  I was then told that when I order next time, I should come in instead of getting delivery so that I could check my order first.  I explained that if this is the way the first always is, I would not be ordering again.  "Okay" was the answer.  My husband then took the food up there to show them what the problem was and see if they would offer a resolution.  The guy opens the container, sticks his hands in it and breaks open a piece of chicken and looks at my husband as if to say, "what's wrong with it?"!!  We will not be doing any further business with Wang Cai and we will never recommend this place to anyone.  Pathetic! Disgusting!  Horrible!

This place redefines mystery meat.  It's called Kelly's Cajun Grill, yet they have nothing that's spicy.  They have an appealing combo deal, but the food is disgusting.  I couldn't even eat half of it.

I have been somewhat of a regular at Sushi Haru for the past year. Well, tonight they lost my business forever. I ordered the tempura japenese bagel, which I always ordered. I also ordered a Mexican roll and a gator roll. The tempura roll tasted bad,  as if the oil it had been cooked in was old. I had 1 piece  and didn't eat anymore if it. The waitress/owner never came back to the table after the food was served. She didn't even refill my water glass, which sucked because that was all I was drinking. Once my friend and I had finished eating she came over to take our plates which still had a few pieces of the other rolls on them. I told her to please don't get mad, but the tempura roll tasted bad. At that point she became hostile towards us. She said it was popular and I agreed but that tonight it did not taste good. I said I'd like a box for the few other pieces and if we could get our check.  she said she would not give us a box because it was happy hour. Then she said if she was going to give me a box then she was going to go ahead and charge me for the uneaten  sushi roll that I told her did not taste good. I wasn't planning on taking it anyway.  I have never been treated so rudely at a restaurant that I frequent often by someone who is the owner. She lost a good customer over $3! By the way, the food isn't that good anyway. Was just a convenient spot to get in and out of because they never have any customers.

Totally gross. Will never eat here again. Ugh.  I can scrape better food off the bottom of my shoe. It's really hard to mess up Chinese food, but this place has it covered. There are other places in Carmel if you want something good.

Convenience is a factor when the convention takes place here but not when you have to call 4x for one dry cleaning pickup.   

One must navigate a maze of telephone prompts to speak with someone in reservations before arrival and trust me- it doesn't get any easier in-house.   I had to spend 45 minutes last night on the phone to get thru to someone to explain the laundry wasn't picked up.  They kindly offered a $75 rebate on the room for a new shirt at J&M but the least expensive shirt cost $107.07.   Compensation and a new shirt can be appreciated but I would have preferred to have my clothes laundered as expected.  

Furthermore, it wasn't delivered on day 3...  this resulted in even more time on the phone and another wait for it to be delivered.  Odd, given the fact that I saw a man delivering dry cleaning at 5:50pm.

"Consider it done" service might net you 500 points but it could cost you 5+ minutes to reach someone on the phone.   Furthermore, you might be left wondering:  did I get my points?   I requested points in lieu of service but the room was cleaned, nonetheless.  

On another note:   the shower curtain had boogers on it which was utterly disgusting and a bottle of cleaner was left on the counter.  Dripping bathtub was mitigated by shutting doors and opening drain.   Fantastic presentation but lackluster execution.   This hotel needs to review operations and guest service.   

If I were to consider convention space, I would look elsewhere.  Sure, it's a full-service hotel but it seems to be riding the coat-tails of location and amenities rather than service and customer-centric attitudes.

This place used to be excellent however the past four or five time I've been to this location.  Ive seen a decline every time.  The last time I was there the server was nice however he was slow in getting to us or even letting us know he'd be with us.  Once he finally got around to our table he was sure of the specially they were offering, he forgot about the drinks we had ordered for atlas the 8 minutes.  Once our appetizer arrived they had forgot half of it and the dipping sauce that went with it, along with burning it.  Then we got our burgers and they weren't even done proper mine was burnt on the bottom and the bun was a little hard.  However the finch fries were perfect and the dessert was done well.  I really like Red Robin as an great burger place however this one just hasn't held up to the standard that its had in the past.  I will return and try again, hopeful they will have improved at this point...

This has got to be the worst pharmacy in Philadelphia. The amount of pharmacy tech's almost always outnumbers the amount of people in line for prescriptions, yet somehow, they are all too busy to acknowledge the people in line, or worse, actually HELP us. Every single time I go here, and I go to the pharmacy at least twice a month,I have to stare down someone for a few minutes before one of them tells another one to help me. Don't even bother to ask them to hold a prescription for you, because they will ignore you. The pharmacy techs here do not give a shit about you, or doing their job efficiently. I have had so many issues with this incompetent staff that I can't recommend this pharmacy to anyone.
I guess vintage all depends on how old you are. I really wish you wouldn't spray the air freshener makes me nauseous and sick. The women from Carmel won't even going there anyway;)
The pizza was cold and stale. The choices were very limited, and the workers were not friendly.

Worst hotel I've ever stayed at. Do not, under any circumstances, stay at this place. No AC in the rooms, dirty and dingy as hell, rude staff who won't do anything to help you (and won't treat you well). All in all this place is absolutely horrible.

I will preface this with a comment about how we did have a larger party and I understand that serving a party is no easy task, but....

This was by far the WORST service I have ever encountered! We sat down and waited a FULL 30 mins before our server even took a drink order. Then we waited another 40 mins to order. Over an hour to just order??? When we spoke to the manager about the wait he just responded with ' i will see what i can do'. See what you can do??? How about grabbing a note pad and taking our order?! It was not that busy and our 'lunch' took about 3 hours from start to finish. My husband and I love to take brew tours across areas and we were excited to visit here but based on the attitude of the service in general we will NEVER be back here. 

ps. The food was meh and the beers were nothing spectacular. Brugge is way better and much nicer people!

Sam's....I don't like Sam's.  And I have no rational reason why.  There's douches here, but who cares?  It's a sports bar, that's who goes to sports bars.  I myself am probably a complete asshole and don't even realize it.  It's crowded, but so what?  Crowds are good sometimes.

Their food is alright from what I've had.  Their drinks are overpriced but so it goes for Hillsboro.

I think the real reason I don't like it is because cheating is so rampant at trivia here and it's so packed that there's nothing they can do about it.  But again, that's kind of trivia everywhere.

Or maybe it's something more sinister. Perhaps I was abused here and  repressed it.  But I doubt it.  Sometimes we just don't like places.

Online customer service sucks!
They were rude, accusatory and do not value your bussiness.


What can you expect from an American company.

So we are lucky the we have had a few but one gets free blizzard! Actually very cool! But the last time we came to this store we ordered a royal blizzard but got a reg. So me working in customer service thought the manager should know! When we told him his response wasn't sorry we will do better next time nor just sorry that happen to you! No what we got was well you got a coupon for buy one get one free!! Shame on him to assume we were trying to get one free ( we already were) just wanted him to KNOW since he ran the place! Thank for the ice cream is good!

I really do like the flavor of the burgers it's pretty good but what I don't like is how small the burgers are ... and it was almost 10 dollars I could of went somewhere for the same place for something bigger that would fill me up also the wait was way too long for something that small

tried calling half a dozen times around noon today with no answer.
call went directly to answering machine. needless to say I tried.
then went to buudy's deal to purchase 50% coupon and they were locked up.
bad day for piezanos and a good day for oreganos. sorry!

Contrary to what some reviewers have written I found our tour guide Preston to be an awful representative for this business. He was difficult to understand because of his speech patterns and chronic mumbling, and he didn't seem to care that most of us could neither understand or even hear him (we were a group of 15). 
Preston rambles on a lot - including his personal stories - but came up short on the historic information we came to learn. 

Our tour ended 30 minutes early - at 5, not 5:30 - and apparently we were supposed to go somewhere for bread pudding according to other reviewers but Preston eliminated that. When asked why were were done early he simply said go enjoy your Friday night, no mention of cutting out one of our taste samples. 

I wish we had the other tour guide (Bob), he seemed much more concerned with giving a great tour.

I went for breakfast last Saturday. Hubs ordered the meat omelette with American cheese. He said it was "meh". I ordered the eggs Benedict florentine. The eggs were raw. I mean clear not white. Not cooked. Disgusting. Never again.

Before I hear from all you lovers of the tavern let me say that maybe I didn't love it here because it was an extremely busy holiday weekend, or maybe it was because I had a tick bury itself into my foot! 

Let me say, I'm not a camping or woodsy guy. I would much prefer a nice hotel with a five star dinner than being at a tavern in the middle of no where. With that, I do enjoy new things. 

New things here they come...we arrived around 11:30AM at the tavern on a holiday weekend and most of the shaded spots had been taken up. It was hot and I was ready for a beer. With beer in hand we decided we needed to try one of their famous tri-tip. 
Tip: You need to purchase the ticket at the bar to pick up the sandwich outside. 
So on to get the sandwich. While chatting with some friends in line I was interrupted by a stranger with a "hey, you got a tick on your foot." Not really knowing what to do, I played it cool and squeezed it out of my foot. After a bit of blood, I followed with a polite "thank you." I then went on with my chatting and drinking, all the while thinking....I had a tick in my foot...can't you get some sort of disease from that?...I'm glad he knew it was a tick because I had no idea....I really should know stuff like this...why didn't it hurt?...I've only had a sip of my beer so I still should feel it, right?...and why the hell am I here anyway? 

The debate and questions in my head raged on. I finally approached the tri-tip bbq area where I was greeted with a wall of smoke that had now blinded me, albeit a nice distraction from my tick situation. I got my sandwich which was basically meat and bread. They had some bbq sauce and a couple other condiments on the side, but without a plate and beer in hand it was hard to manage. 

We finally found a spot to sit and eat. The sandwich was good, not great. The band started playing some music and the day got a bit better. 

I probably won't return, but I have now educated myself on ticks and if I encounter one in a plush hotel in the future I will know just what to do.

Sticky floors and flat drinks.... My wife and I were excited to try this place and chose to do so last night. Immediately upon walking into the place, my shoes were getting stuck on a VERY sticky floor. Sticky might not properly convey what the floor felt like. It was as if someone from the night before had mopped the floors with sugar water. This is definitely not a good first impression for any restaurant to put forth let alone a restaurant serving raw fish. 

We sat at the bar due to some of the other reviews that we had read regarding the wait staff. The bartender seemed nice but he could have explained their happy hour and alcohol selection better since there were no beer/drink/happy hour lists for customers to review. My wife asked for a flavored vodka with tonic water. I decided on a tequila and 7up. The 7up was flat. I asked my wife about hers and she said the same. Normally I would have alerted the bartender to this but, given that their floors felt like fly tape, I decided that they might just not care. 

As far as the food: I tend to judge restaurants that serve sushi based on how good their miso soup is. My experiences have shown that if a restaurant has crappy miso soup then they tend to not have the best sushi. Normally, miso soup is either complimentary or very inexpensive. This place must have the best miso soup on the planet because it was $5. I told the bartender that I wasn't looking for a bowl of miso and asked if they had a smaller size. He motioned to show me how large the soup was and apparently that -was- the smaller size... a cup, not a bowl as I would have expected for $5. Needless to say, I did not test my miso soup theory here.

My wife wanted the chorizo and goat cheese pot stickers. They no longer have any of the three kinds shown on their menu. Why are they still listed? I don't know. We settled for pork and also had an order of sweet potato fries. The pot stickers were nothing special but weren't bad. The fries were flavorful but limp and soggy. If you cannot cook sweet potato fries correctly then why are they even on the menu (especially an Asian fusion menu....)?

We ordered three rolls to split. I also ordered the tuna/salmon sashimi combo platter and some tobiko. The rolls were okay given the half price happy hour. I would have been really disappointed had we paid full price. The sashimi didn't have a lot of taste... definitely a let down. The sushi chef gave me 9 pieces instead of the normal 7 which I was excited about before I tried it. I appreciated the gesture but, given the quality of the fish, the extra pieces were kind of like throwing extra polish on a turd.

There was an awesome jazz band performing and I would have to say that they were the only real highlight of the night.

Before we left, both of us had to use the restroom(s). There is one very large restroom, stalls only, coed. No wonder, as another reviewer mentioned, that this bathroom becomes a sex free for all at night. Another thing on the bathrooms... they put ice in the sinks. This is the only restaurant that I have seen do this. Normally, this is something you do with urinals. We were told that this was to keep smells from coming out of the old pipes. Isn't that what a drainage trap is for? 

Since I have never, ever, ever seen this done in a sink at any other establishment, I have to assume something else can be done. By the way, the sinks are set kind of low. So, I have a question for the owner... When everyone is drunk at night, the stalls are full of people getting it on, and the sinks look like urinal troughs due to the ice in them, how long do you think it will take before people start emptying their bladders into the same basins where people are washing their hands?

Bottom line: Where is the owner of this place? If he isn't there to notice the sticky floors then shouldn't someone else? Anyone who walked through the restaurant was very loud as their shoes snapped away from the floor. And, what about the workers? Even if you have an owner that doesn't give a shit, where is your work ethic? Those floors were so terrible that I would've gladly mopped them myself (as a customer) for $10 just so that it didn't feel so gross. I hope they mopped them before the dancing started. 

To the owner:

Don't serve sushi. Your menu is extremely limited and the quality is not good enough to back it up. We have Kampai, Mizu, Cafe Mochi, Sekuisi (sp?), and even Sushi Ai... plus many more in the metro area. Your sushi cannot compete with any of these places based on the quality of it and the lack of selection. You HAVE to know this. 

Your place has many little things that will make people go elsewhere. It seems like you must be very disengaged from your business. So many simple things could be done to bring more customers into SIAM. Start asking your customers of ways you can improve... by taking their constructive criticism while they are there, maybe that will be one less bad review that makes it to yelp.

First: I Brought in for brake inspection due to noise on back brakes. Told me I needed new front brakes and the back ones were fine. Even gave me measurements of the brakes pads on back. Hour after I leave the back brakes are squeaking like crazy. Next day I bring to another Firestone and I am told I need new back brake pads and rotors. Decided to do it myself and upon removing back brake pads I noticed they were almost to the metal plate. The noise I was hearing was the metal indicator on the pad that alerts you to change the pads. They missed it completely because they did not properly inspect them. 

Second: I was told the job would take an hour. It took 2 1/2 hours. 

I am personally shocked that a place would skimp on a brake inspection considering the liability involved. Not to be trusted based upon my experience.
Not as good as I expected. Waste of time and money. The lamp was very tough. And when the waiter gave us the dishes, his thumb touched the sauce in a disgusting way!

When I hear the name "Wang Gang" asian cuisine doesn't come to mind.  After dining there, it never will.  

Wang Gang's identity is convoluted.  It is trying to be some upscale "asian cuisine" type restaurant, but the actual name is "wang gang".  For those who don't have an imagination at all: penis, wang stands for penis.  The sizes used to be "big wang" and "little wang".  Lovely.   I remember almost laughing out loud when I walked in the new location and the lights were dimmed.  I should of asked the greeter, "is this the same place where I can order the size of my food by big or little wang"?

The actual restaurant atmosphere is like being in a cafeteria with nice decorations.  We literally could not hear the waitress.   Perhaps because the waitress treated us as if this were her last day on the job.   She rushed everything, and brought us the check while we were half way into the meal. 

The only small positive is that the food was not bad.  However, the temperature was lukewarm at best.  Moreover, for what you are paying, you are better off walking into the door next door at Shangri-la.  If you really want "asian cuisine" go to Oriental Spoon in Edwardsville.  That is an actual quality restaurant in every regard: food, service, atmosphere and normal business name.

If I could give this place 0 stars, I would.  The Gaylord clearly valued bringing in big groups, like the Beta Club National Convention, over providing the Country Christmas experience they heavily promoted.  

I had hoped to begin a new holiday tradition, and begin a low-stress all-inclusive option this holiday, but navigating thousands of some of the most poorly-behaved and disrespectful middle-schoolers was not what I had in mind.  I witnessed more pushing, swearing, and running through crowds than I have ever seen at a family event (and that includes the St. Patrick's Day parade in Chicago).  

Gaylord missed the mark. They could have managed this situation better. They could have had the student group together in one of the 4 hotel areas, rather that dispersed among the families trying to have a relaxing holiday. The food lines are ridiculously long. They don't have enough staff to manage the demand. And the staff are clearly stressed as well -- they, too, have gotten increasingly rude in the last 24 hours.  

Total Fail, Galylord!
Long wait times and TERRIBLE service. Gave them chance after chance to impress us, they failed every time.

Plain interior, ok service, bland food. Not bad, just not worth it in the plethora of French Quarter options. Mid-day on a Sunday and they're out of grits, shrimp, and seafood Benedict?  Really??

Boudin was dry and tasteless. Eggs and hash browns were good, but heck, even I can fix those well. Bavarian chocolate iced coffee was decent.

Too many employees and they don't know what they're doing. I ordered chicken tenders with honey mustard, they gave me a cut up chicken breast with packs of actual honey..... Not worth it for $8, probably won't be back...

My wife and I decided to try it out and probably will not be going again. The server Monique was extremley rude. My wife ordered boneless wings with extra buffalo sauce, manager brings out our food and my wife gets bone-in wings she tells the manager that she ordered the bonless wings not bone-in, we know mistakes happen no big deal. Our server turns around and glares at us. When we got done with our meal we get the check. On the check is bone-in wings my wife lets the server know so she can adjust the check. The server then tells her in a very rude tone "Oh its because you changed the order in front of the manager" My wife tells her that she never ordered bone-in wings and the server is practically arguing with us on what WE ordered. That behavior and attitude doesnt belong in customer service.

Hassled us about our service dog, messed up both of our orders. I'm pregnant and can't have raw egg so we told the waitress she said no problem. I got an over medium egg, raw in the middle, so we sent it back. They just scrapped off the fried egg and put scrambled on but there was still raw yolk all over the plate. Waitress tried to be very nice about it and blamed it on new kitchen staff but we wouldn't go back.

Worst pool service available. They "took care" of my pool for a year and never cleaned the filter, didn't know how to operate the system (Whenever I asked how to turn the pump on or heat my pool, they said they had no clue how to work it...?), my ex husband had to put an algae killer in my pool when it turned green cause they didn't know what to do, and then a couple months later my filter was finally so filthy that my pool turned completely green and they did NOTHING about it!! They would come every week and see the murky green color, walk around the pool and LEAVE without saying a word- LAZY SCAMMERS!! I finally let them go and got a new pool company who immediately looked at the filter which was so full of crap that they had to come twice in one evening to change it.. I threw away hundreds on this pool company, I wish someone would have warned me before I did! BEWARE OF BLUEPOINT!!!!
If you like flys flying around your head, in your salad, and baked in your pizza, you will enjoy this place.

If you are a fan of long winding walks to get around the hotel, slow food service, over priced items, then Gaylord is for you.  Where do I start? (business trip for a conference)
1) Breakfast a the Cascades, at least one coffee cup or glass was dirty at the table each morning.
2) Breakfast buffet, besides being over priced with limited choices, the item were usually not hot.  I am sure some people like cold biscuits and scrambled eggs.
3)  Findely Irish Pub, over an hour to get sandwiches, while we watched other tables get food.
4) Thermostats are limited to how cold the room can get, I know this is being picky but I like to be able to get the room cooler than 70. 
5) Since it is a 'resort' type hotel, then everything is nickle and dime extra charges.  For example, I will FedEx items to myself at the hotel that I will be staying, for hold pick up when I check in.  Since FedEx has a small office near the convention section, they have an agreement with Gaylord that item will not be sent to the hotel for pick up.  Instead you have to pick up at the onsite FedEx, and FedEx gets to add a 'handling fee' for holding the package.  
6) $15 / day 'resort' fee a way to make pay high mark up for limited wi-fi service, limited to your room.  I fyou want wi-fi in the hotel area that is extra.  Even for the Elite Marriott members, which is free at other Marriott's.

I would not return to this hotel 'resort' again.

I've had mixed experiences at Broad Ripple Brew Pub. I like that it has a weekly special menu along with a permanent regular menu. Some of the best items I've had there are on the weekly special menu - but then some of their "regular" foods are a bit disappointing. 

Last night my BF ordered the Fish and Chips off the regular menu - the fish was drenched and drowning in oil, and and all we could taste was the oil. I ordered the Vegan Dolmades plate - it was tasty, but my dolmades could have had a lot more stuffing in them. They were mainly grapes leaves with little filling. I also think $9 for the light portion is a little steep.

Finally, the service was SO slow. They were moderately busy, but the restaurant should have been able to handle that size of a crowd just fine.

In sum, the quality of the food could be much better. Their menu has a lot of items, but maybe they should focus on what they can make consistently tasty.

JD's would get a "0" if they had it. Arrived at 11:27am today, were seated, and handed the Breakfast menu, only to find out 5 minutes later that breakfast was no longer severed. We were given the lunch menu, and then informed it would be a few minutes as the kitchen changed over. Thirty minutes later, we were allowed to order. And, another 40 came our meal.

Jerky is reasonably priced but not high quality by any means. Have had much better Jerky with fewer ingredients and better taste in many other places. None of the Jerky is made in-house, instead this is a chain operated by a sole proprietor. It was a great idea for him to open this place bc it attracts tourists like a magnet, but being a local I think my first time shopping there will be my last. Bought the Turkey jerky in Teriyaki and Pepper flavor and I have to say even the bagged Jerky from the grocery store tastes way better. The Sales guy was kind of meh, but I wouldn't say he was rude or aloof, just not overtly friendly. To each their own, but I think they're trying to push a mass produced jerky that only appears to be homemade. Overall, kind of a tourist trap.

The food here is yummy and it is clean but today I had the worst experience but it wasn't the first time I have.

Right away it took forever for someone to help me and I was the only person standing there. 
Second, right away I ordered my usual bruito bowl with extra rice. The girl puts less rice then you typically would receive if you didn't ask for extra. The second I realized I said, "can you please add more rice. Sorry" 
She looks at me annoyed and says sure only to turn to the girl next to her and complain about me right in front of me about wanting more rice. Then she proceeds to give me so little half and half chicken & steak that I couldn't even tell I was eating any meat!



After finally getting everything made I had to stand there forever while people just kept walking by just to pay. I honestly was about to tell them I didn't even want it anymore! I can honestly say this was definitely not with the 8 something I paid.

If I could give this place zero stars I would. The sushi is not worth the money, too fishy so it's obviously not fresh. And no this is not the first time I've felt this way about this place. I have given Sushi Nara many tries, and they are always mediocre or horrible. The experience, service, and food are lackluster. Don't waste your time or money.

Two of us went on a Saturday night with no expectations since it was prime time on a weekend but it was worse than we could have expected. When we walked in, there were several (5-7) employees standing in the entrance way but we never saw them again. Ten minutes after being seated our serve came over to us and took our drink order. Probably about 15-20 minutes after that we actually received our drinks and they were watered down, glasses were sweating and it was clear out cocktails had been sitting on the bar for quite some time. 

We ordered a second round right away since we knew it would take awhile to get another. We ordered a BBQ bacon burger and a black and blue burger. Bacon tasted worse then something you would order from  McDonalds.  Will never go back.  service was terrible and food was very bland..  0 stars don't waste your time or money. The servers seemed confused and uninterested.

I took a pair of jeans to Sandys during the first week of June 2013 and asked for a hole in the knee to be patched. I even supplied the patch! They told me they were kinda busy and it would be a coupla weeks. I said fine and actually waited 3 weeks. I called to see if they were ready at the end of the month. No they weren't.... could I call back in a week. I waited another 2 weeks and called again. Sorry the lady who does jeans isn't here. Come by and pick them up next week. Went by at the end of July and there was a sign up saying "On vacation until August 7th". Went back August 7th and here's the funny part. When I walked in, the lady who served me said "Good morning, how may I help you " in perfect, flawless English with no trace of any kind of accent. However, once she found my jeans and they STILL had not been patched, she was suddenly struck by some strange affliction and could only repeat in pigeon English/Italian that " we a been onna da vacation .... you comma backa later"  When I asked "since the first week of June?", she just kept repeating that same statement with her fake,immigrant impression. Needless to say I took my (unpatched) jeans and left.
            These people are a joke. They were unable to put a simple patch on a pair of jeans after being given more than eight weeks to do so! Oh I almost forgot.....they lost the denim patch I supplied too!

I've only been once, so I won't go down to a single star on the assumption it can get better (a visit to the Tampa location was delightful).

I won't return, though, unless compelled by a group.  The food was simply bad.  I know fresh fish is hard to come by in these parts, but if your specialty is seafood, you can at least learn how to cook it and throw away the freezer burnt pieces.

Nothing to review.  We were a party of 6 who walked in on Mothers Day for drinks and dinner.  There were quite a few empty tables.  We stood by the hostess stand for a while.  A couple of waitresses walked by us and said nothing.  The bartender stood there looking at us but not speaking.  No one said anything like, "feel free to seat yourself" or "please wait, someone will seat you shortly."  We just stood there for 10-15 minutes and left.

In short, decent but overpriced.  

I stopped in to have lunch on a Saturday, and was shocked to find common take-out entrees priced at around $20 on average.   I asked if there was a lunch menu, and was told that I was looking at it. 

I ordered, hoping that the food would make up for the high price, but sadly it was only somewhat better, and the potion was a lunch-sized portion.  It was simply not worth the money.

On the plus side, it is a sit-down restaurant with a nice decor, and the service was good.

Unfortunately, that's not enough to make me come back for these prices.

Back when I first bought my car I brought it here to get everything checked out and some work done. They did the all the work that they said needed to be done. On the way home from here I noticed a piece on the floorboard that came from underneath the hood. It happened to be the metal strap that holds my battery in. The next morning my car wouldn't start (surprise surprise!). So I called them and they came out to my house, jumped my car, and took it back to their shop. They said I needed a new battery.  So I paid for the new battery, the installation, and their trip out to my house. (grr !!.) A couple more days went by and my engine light started coming on. So I stopped back by their shop, they looked under the hood and said nothing was wrong. On my way home from there my car overheated.  We got it home, looked under the hood, and found a tiny cut in a hose going from my radiator! I filled up my radiator with anti freeze, drove down to napa auto parts, bought the $3 piece to fix it myself, and found a new mechanic! 

I may be a girl and not know too much about cars but I am not stupid!

This place was terrible.  One guy working all alone, so it took forever.  Everything in styrofoam, including the cup of water that we asked or.  The Gyro was just ok, a little dry, but better with more yogurt sauce.  The french fries were literally the worst that I've ever had.

This is the worst take care clinic I have ever been too (and the last one I went to wouldn't even give me my actual diagnosis, they just gave me a prescription and went on their merry way). I arrived today at 1 pm, they had a long line so I had to sign a piece of paper confirming me to come back at 3:20 for an appointment time. I get there again at 3 pm to make sure I do not miss my appointment time. Two people who were there before me go in before me also, no big deal, they were obviously there first. But then two more families show up and check in, who obviously did not have appointment times and I can hear the doctor discussing that they just have to take a pre-screening and they will move over to the other room to get started. Now I'm pissed, it's 3:40 and I was supposed to be seen 20 mins ago. So I get up and go to the kiosk and notice that you can check in for your appt, I quickly throw my information in and it says "appointment not found". Now if I had to check in before my appt time and didn't realize it, that's my fault. But I don't think that's the case. The doctor had no one in her room so I knocked on her door and explained how I had an appt at 3:20 and still hadn't been seen and she starts accusing me of making things up when the girl in the room over exclaims "no I had her sign in she was here earlier". Then the doctor starts accusing me of being late and telling me that they close at 4:30 and there is nothing she can do. At this point I'm almost screaming and I told her I've been sitting here since 3 pm and I'm pretty pissed off, so she finally just takes me in her room. She can't find my name in the computer from the previous times I've been there and starts to tell me there isn't anything she can do so I give her my old last name and sure enough I come up and she seems agitated by this. So now she's testing me for strep throat and being as un-thorough with follow up questions as you can possibly be. I finally ask her if she knows any recommendations for doctors who treat vertigo since I have suffered from it for a while. Her actual response was "vertigo is usually just caused my inner ear stuff.. but it's mainly caused by drugs". Excuse me? She actually low key accused me of doing drugs. That was the last straw and I really just wanted to get the hell out of there. The best part of this whole situation is that I wound up paying $135 to basically be insulted the whole time. Oh and I didn't even have strep throat. But she did remind me that "just because your throat hurts doesn't mean it's strep throat". Wow doctor thanks I had no idea I could just get a sore throat!!!! I will honestly probably never go to Walgreens in general ever again because of this. CVS here I come!

I really like how spacious this location is - the different rooms, and the lounge with a full bar. The staff is friendly. However, the food has not translated well from the mothership in Seminole Heights.  
My first visit was brunch, my ramen was barely room temp. when it arrived, and the broth was thick and gloppy - the place had very few customers at the time. I was too pressed for time, to send it back, so I had to eat it as is.
Had lunch there recently and decided to try the Miso ramen.  The broth was ok, but somewhat overpowered by the taste of that prechopped garlic some restaurants use for convenience - it does not flatter the broth at all.  Prechopped garlic is a travesty.
Also, I would much rather have tofu or seitan, than Impossible burger (what little there was).  The final thing that really dragged it down is that there was literally almost a whole onion's worth of barely cooked, large onion slices.  I had to pull a bunch out, so I wasn't getting a giant mouthful of onion with every bite. If there were bean sprouts in there, I really couldn't detect them. With a veg. ramen, it would be nice to have some kind of mushrooms - just more texture in general.  It feels like the keep it boring so you'll pay more money for extras.  It's $13.50 already.
