No lamps, just ceiling mounted Spotlights.  Tile floors with no rug.  Honestly, I've stayed in better motels in Tijuana than this.  And to top it off, $200 a night!  Folks, I stayed at the Rome Hilton for less than that!  Truthfully though, I wouldn't have stayed here any other time, but Santa Barbara was full because of commencement.

I was really disappointed in this tour. I have done a different swamp tour with another company in the past and it was great. A friend that was with me had never been on one, so we called in for a reservation when we were in NOLA. The man on the phone told us it was $70. So we booked two spots. 

During the tour, there was not much action. It was quiet and there were not many gators out. Our guide desperately searched around to find some. He would slow down in some spots looking around but didn't see anything. He stopped and fed a gator some marshmallows in the beginning and then another one towards the end. And that was the jist of the tour. Honestly, it was a bit boring. The guide mentioned that it was mating season and the gators aren't as active during that time (so maybe you shouldn't charge as much during that time?).

I later found out (after checking bank statement) that the man on the phone charged us $95 a person. Perhaps there was a miscommunication but I would not have agreed to doing the tour for that price. I was disappointed thinking I paid $70, so when I found out I actually paid $95 it was a bit gut-wrenching. I am a very well-traveled individual and I can tell you - this was not worth that cash. I called to inquire on why the total was $95 a person when I was told on the phone it was $70. The man couldn't give me an answer to this. I informed him that I was very disappointed but he did not seem to care either.

The other tour I went on a few years ago let us hold a baby gator at the end. That was not the case here.

If you want to see some gators in a natural setting - I highly recommend going to Jean LaFitte park. We saw 7-8 ft. gators right up along the walking path. It was a beautiful swamp experience and better yet, it was free!

Sorry nato's...  Not good.  Maybe it's because I live in los Angeles that people who have never had real great authentic food know the difference?  Maybe the worst fish taco, and chili verde I've ever had.

Sorry!  I couldn't even eat it.  It sucks when a mom and pop can't succeed- but I suggest you rededicate to the business.  My verde burrito was pre cooked pork reheated on grill to serve, tasted very old and super dry!  Yuck.  Fish taco was as poorly cooked fish stick in a dry tortilla smeared with cream and old lettuce.

Be warned, if you love Mexican food....  Pass natos and head to del taco.  Atleast that may be edible.  Sorry to be harsh.

I really want to love this place, and have tried- I swear it.  The people who work there are great, really nice, and friendly, they seem to know almost everyone by name.
BUT
Is this the emporer's new chicken?  So  much hype led up to the opening of FD, and I was pretty excited about the place, but four tries in, and I just don't get what the fuss is about the chicken.
I feel like the 2 women in the old joke complaining about the food at a Catskills resort- one says 'the food here is is terrible' and her friend replies 'yes, and the portions are so small'... 
Pieces are small, and often dry, never juicy as great fried chicken should be.  Last time I got it, it wasn't even crispy.  I have tried three flavors and I like the flavor of the ranch, but not enough to pay $9.00 for 4 tiny pieces.  I am sorry, but I think I am giving up on FD for chicken and continuing my search for truly great fried chicken.

we were very disappointed with an appetizer we ordered. Felt like we got robbed and wasted our money. We ordered the hummus and we were only provided with about 5 or 6 chips which was shocking.. We asked for more and we were told it would cost $2 and we said no. I paid for a bowl of hummus I couldn't eat. Thanks a lot.

I had lived in scandia apartments back in my college years with another friend and had always felt as if this was the "safest"place to live in the castleton area apartment wise. So recently after visiting friends here i was in for quite a shock to see the current conditions of the apartments. Arriving at my friends apartment building, i was reminising to my husband about the old college days and how i had lived here etc. The first thing he noticed were a string of beer bottles surrounding the outside of the apartment hall which would never had been there back in the day.  People leaving trash outside in the hall, instead of taking it to the dumpster, pieces of wood rotting off, its reallt showing its age! It seems as if yhe current residents dont take pride in their homes and just see it as a "place to stay the night" for a while. Hopefully the owners will take a walk around the complex and get these things changed for the better! My friends lease is up in 3 months and shes not planning on renewing, and i cant blame her.

If I could give a negative rating I would. It took 5 minutes for our drinks and 20 for rolls and then 40 minutes later we finally got our food and the steak is over cooked and the management just really falls behind here. Go to chili's it's better service and food for your money.
How exactly do you send out an order of Thai Basil Rice and forget the Thai Basil?  This is the second time this has happen.

Talk about a fly in the ointment... or in this case Moths!!!  I have been to this restaurant 3 times now.  The first two times we had no problems.  The food was decent, the decor interesting (I am a car guy and there are vintage petroleum signs everywhere), the service was passable.  The third time was a disaster of biblical plague proportions.  

Lets start at the beginning.  We came in and had to wait 15 minutes for a table.  No big deal.  Rather than giving out beepers like everyone else in the chain restaurant world, we were handed a playing card... Say what?  Are we eating or playing poker?  I remember thinking "Crap it was a 6 of hearts.  Not gonna win any hands with that one."    I was right.  We should have folded.  When we were seated, they put us in an alcove near the front entrance.  I had a TV infront of me so that I could watch football so at least that was good.  But the problems started almost immediately.  Or rather nothing started immediately because we waited... and waited... and waited.  Finally as we were about to walk out, a waiter came over and asked if we wanted a drink.  We ordered and then we waited... and waited... and waited...  And then he came back to take our order but we didn't have any drinks yet.  We sent him away to find them and because my mother in law, who was visiting (please send condolences to... ), didn't know what she wanted.  He left and the waiting continued.  We didn't see him again for nearly 15 minutes.  All in all I think it was 15 minutes before anyone noticed that we exist, 30-35 minutes before the server attempted to take an order, 40-45 minutes before we got our drinks, and 45 to 50 minutes before we actually got an order in.  I was ok at this point because I had football but really??? 

Then came the moths.  There were pantry moths everywhere.  The manager said that they were coming in on the peanuts and that they were trying to get rid of them.  Well I get that you want a roadhouse feel but I would rather not have peanuts than have bugs all over.  I killed 8 of them, handed 2 to the server to say what the heck man, and then gave up except to brush them out of our hair and away from our food.  Gross.

All this time the service still sucked.  We were there nearly 2 hours just for dinner.  It cost me nearly $100 for 3 people to eat while chasing away bugs and being forgotten about.  Then the server took our card to pay and left for 15 minutes without returning it... I had to get a manager to get it back.  And to top it all off... he forgot to give me a pen to sign the slip with. 

So why not 1 star?  Well the food is actually pretty decent.  And we did not have that bad of a problem the other 2 times though the service was still slow.  And I almost forgot, when I told the manager about the problems, he offered us 2 free apps the next time we come back.  I wonder if they will charge extra for the bugs?

This KFC sucks. The young guy at the counter was pretty nice and allowed the option to sub any side instead of mashed potatoes in the family fill up, but still, it's dismal in that place. This is not a place in which you want to dine so get it to go.

All the sides were the standard and the chicken was on the bland and dry side because it had been sitting out a long time once we got there around 7pm on a week night. 

Whoever runs this KFC, do you just not care?

Front of house service was good but food was really disappointing, almost everything at our table of 6 was bad.  Chicken sandwich was scrawny and tasted like fish oil, sweet potato fries undercooked, burger unseasoned and undersized, even the kids complained their chicken tenders were dry and "tasteless" (which I confirmed).  Only good thing was Mac and cheese, which was way overpriced at $10 for a small side. I overheard the table next to us complaining how bad their food was and getting items removed from their bill, which I didn't have the heart to do, but I won't be back anytime soon unless just for drinks (which were fine).

I'm done with this place. The food is mediocre on a good day. I've tried giving them chances but I'm done. I ordered General Tso's, extra spicy. It was drowning in sauce and was mushy. It wasn't spicy at all. My fried rice was under cooked, severely. The owner isn't welcoming at all. The service is horrid. Save your money and go somewhere else.

My wife went in to purchase the lease of our car. It took 3 and a half hours. We knew the length we wanted of our purchase and how much it would cost. We also knew we didn't want any up sales. The staff was combative when we didn't want to any adders. In addition, they were defensive instead of apologetic for the ridiculous amount of time it took to complete. Overall a very poor experience.

Received another Groupon offer for Cindy at this place.  After talking with the owner, Mary Ann, about her regarding an earlier incident, I can not believe Cindy is still there.  Either Cindy really cleaned up her act, or Mary Ann just doesn't care who leases space in her salon.  Only time will tell.....
I called to schedule annual HVAC maintenance for my home. Didn't show, never called. The end.

This used to be my favorite restaurant in the city, but a couple of months ago it started to deteriorate starting with the mango lassi. We just visited again and it is now called Mayuri. The (buffet) dishes overall are lacking the flavor that Ruchi once served, and even the naan was unappetizing. What a loss. They still have the best variety of Indian desserts though.

Summary: Unfriendly server/host, frozen vegetables used and sub-par tikki masala sauce. Naan was excellent.

Host was very awkward, unfriendly, seemed  miserable/borderline angry and stared through my soul like the grim reaper. Seems like kids were running the place..Horrible first impression. 

Two diners, we ordered tikki masala and vegetable korma. Tikki sauce was too thick, sweet and tasted like it was store bought. 6 chunks of chicken for $15?? Rice wasn't flavorful at all. Tasted like long grain white rice.

Vegetable korma tasted like frozen vegetables that had been reheated. Naan was excellent--only reason I'm giving 2 stars. Maybe lunch buffet is better?

Ok place for coffee, but over priced for lunch or dinner.  You order at the front counter, similar to a fast food place.  Ordered their IPA, but they were out, so had their Amber Ale.  The wait staff needs some additional training, started out with 1/2 of the class foam, they finally got it to 1/4 foam and handed me the glass and a number so they could find me to send my food out.  My friends meal was served almost 3 min before mine, totally not cool.  Fast food service with fine dinning prices.

Rude staff!  Never made it past the hostess.

Last night, I took my 9-year old son and elderly parents to this Benihana's.  we were ushered thru a 75% empty restaurant to a table in the back where another family was holding their 3 or 4 year-old's birthday party. Cute kids, polite-looking family, but having a good time and slightly noisy. Since my parents are hard of hearing and my son is very soft spoken, I quietly and calmly explained this to the hostess and asked if we could be seated elsewhere. The hostess said it's all group seating, and I answered that I was aware of that, having been at Benihana's before, and could we please be seated with another group. I counted at least three tables (for 8-10 diners) with two or three diners each; two were families with teenagers. There were about 15 or more empty tables. The hostess said we'd have to wait up front. We walked to the front and after talking with another person at the hostess desk, we were told there was no room for us at the restaurant. I was shocked.  There was no manager in sight.  No more Benihana dinners for this family!

Purchased a Groupon and thought we would try this place out. The fact that the place has always looked deserted whenever I've passed by should have been my first clue, but we tried it anyway. 

The dining room is small and cramped with back to back tables, very uncomfortable. We had to wait 45 minutes for appetizers while people around us (who had been seated 20 minutes after us) were finishing theirs already. Someone finally came with the appetizer but it turned out to be wrong or for another table and we had to wait even longer. 

Once our entrees arrived we were starving. My sister's lasagna was completely over-cooked and the pasta was hard and chewy. My brother in law ordered a veal dish which was also over cooked and extremely chewy. I had to wait for my chicken dish since they brought me the wrong entree on the first try, but once they got it right it was not very good. Had no flavor at all. My brother in law had to return his meal and ended up just leaving without eating dinner (not to mention he had to move our car since dinner had taken so long and our meter had run out). The manager (who was also our waitress) offered a weak apology after we expressed our dissatisfaction and offered to get us a comp glass of wine, but we were not interested. She did not return to the table.

My sister and I picked at our meals until we decided just to leave as well and go get frozen yogurt at Whirled Peace (which is awesome!). We still had to wait another 10 minutes until we could flag down someone to bring us our check, only to find that they overcharged us on bottled water. 

All in all, we will not be back! The food is not good, the service is awful and the atmosphere poor.

Compared to other high end department stores (Saks, Ogylvies, Harrod's, gallery Lafayette) this place sucks. While I am often tempted into buying more than a few beautiful clothing items by Ogylvies when I'm in Montreal, I haven't purchased anything at this store in years. The service is horrid. Truly bad. You can walk around browsing for hours and no one will ever acknowledge you. I've visited when I'm decked out in designer duds and when I'm just schlepping around the downtown, it makes no difference, I'm never acknowledged. If you ever did get one of the ice queens to acknowledge you, there's nothing to buy. The plump section doesn't seem to be there any more. The lingerie section hosts about 3 bras and a single package of stockings. The scarves are humdrum and the shoes, while some are lovely, never come in my size. The makeup section is so-so. They have some unique brands but make no mistake, this isn't Sephora, you can't try things out easily.  One big plus, Holts is great at inspiring me to save my cash for for vacation shopping.

My friends and I had tickets to a Paint Nite that was being hosted by Barrelhouse. We walked in unsure of where to go and stood waiting because there was a "please wait to be seated" sign, but no hostess to be found. Servers literally walked by us and ignored us for like 2 minutes, until someone finally asked if we were there for Paint Nite and directed us around the corner. Uhh okay. 

So we take a seat and waited for someone to take our drink order. No one comes, so we head to the bar and again, wait two minutes to be acknowledged. The bartender literally walks by us to a guy eating next to where we were standing, asks if his food is alright, and then walks by us again. Uh?? Finally we flag her down and we hand her our IDs and she takes a look at mine and immediately says that she can't accept my ID. I recently moved to Boise and still have my out of state license, and it is vertical, but it is still valid and has not expired. I ask her why not and she says that the cops have been on them about fake IDs and she can't take mine because most fakes are vertical. So, we ask to see the manager. The manager tells me the same thing. Apparently my valid, unexpired, legal driver's license doesn't look or feel right to her. At this point, we were getting annoyed. I totally understand policy and being cautious of underage drinking, but she was so unprofessional and rude about it. Finally she says she'll look into it and takes it into the back. 

So five minutes later she takes it into the back and asks to see my credit card, and compares my ID with my credit card. She then says it's ok. No apologies or explanations. She also took a three minute long look at my friend's (horizontal, and also valid) Oregon license. Funny thing is, when a new guy FINALLY came around for the drink order he didn't even ask for my ID!!!! 

Anyways, food was good but overpriced. The chicken on my salad looked like those frozen Tyson's strips that you stick in the microwave. Service was slow, and they forgot our silverware, and we never received it after asking. We had to go up and find some. Drinks were good.

They were busy so I can be sympathetic for slow/forgetful service, but the whole ID situation totally put a bad taste in my mouth. I have never had that happen to me in Boise, not even downtown or at the liquor store! It was embarrassing and the bartender & manager made me feel like I was not worth their business or time. I will not be back.

This place tries hard, and the sushi is okay, but something is not working here. The staff is very friendly seemed very concerned that we enjoy our meal, and the space is your normal sushi restaurant vibe, but there are also TVs everywhere and a giant screen for sports because this is St. Louis.   

So I ordered my sushi and my boyfriend ordered teriyaki, then I received my dinner, and his was...? We gave it about ten minutes, but as I became dangerously close to finishing my meal, we had to enquire about his dinner. Our sever looked confused as if it were normal to have a table of two order three rounds of saki to share while only one person dined on sushi. Maybe that is normal! Who knows. She came back and told us it would be another ten minutes. We decided that I would finish my dinner and we would ask for his to go. They were able to accommodate that request, and they comped one of our bottles of saki, but it was not a great experience. 

I'm not sure where the problem lies here, but the restaurant does seem like it wants to be better than it is, and they are trying. Unfortunately, I just don't think this is the sort place that I would return to or recommend.

Typical "Irish" (again, I'm not sure what makes this place Irish - the name? but whatever) sports pub that includes:

- a menu where you're hard pressed to find something that ISN'T deep fried.  Artery clogging, brocoli bites, onion rings, chicken tenders and repeat. Just envision a kitchen that isn't a kitchen, but a room with multiple fryer stations and freezers.
- aloof, forced, unfriendly, repeatedly forgetful service both from waitstaff and bartenders.  
- A generic atmosphere and ambiance with lots of plasma TV's strewn everywhere and beige as far as the eye can see.
- Cheap domestic draft beers, if you're on a tight budget and don't mind drinking Miller Lite, their pitchers are $5!  You can't beat that deal anywhere.
- Crowd content varies, although I'd avoid this place during the weekend at all costs

Came in with a group when the restaurant was rather empty, the staff was not very helpful or accommodating - the bartenders would not spare any high chairs for our table nor did the restaurant have any extra chairs for a big top.  

Staff struck me as rude while no one really seemed to care or to try to solve the issue.   Our group was there for at least four or five hours during which no bartender or waitstaff cracked a smile.  At the end, I had a hard time mustering up energy to even give them a decent tip. (Uh, hello staff, you're in the SERVICE industry)

In all, a Blah-bar that's decent for cheap blah-drinking and watching a sports game or two while clogging your arteries with some fried fare.  The extra star is for affordability - the only aspect that saves this place.

Food was good and restaurant was clean. Two tacos made incorrectly, one extra taco not ordered, nachos were made with crushed chips and no mild sauce anywhere in the place.  

I did experience workers practicing clean as you go.  The kitchen was very clean and tidy. The manager was visible and staff was friendly and helpful.

Very bad experience with this practice and with front desk Ashley:
1. I made the ppt through the zocdoc, they had several time options. But in reality that was a fake time/date so I think they use it to make more patients. 
2. I rescheduled it but it was very difficult for me because I have a a little baby at home.
3. I came in the office 10 min late, because of the driver couldn't fine the building and I was looking the building they have on the photo here but it is different.
4. Ashley didn't try to resolve this issue and right away told me I have to reschedule and asked me talk to the manager.
5. He was super rude (I don't know who it was but this man was awful, he started his conversation with me telling that he is super busy, is it ok yes???), he didn't listen to me and talked to me like I am his student in the school and I did a very big mistake. I was almost crying there after that and Ashley like robot continues repeat we have to reschedule, I can't do anything etc...
6. She called me to cancel the appt through the zocdoc the day before and she never mentioned I have to come earlier but in every practice I was (yes I was in many!) the front dest asks to fill out the paper work online or come earlier or they try to find the best way to solve but don't behave so terrible with the people.
So no, never coming back here!

Consitantly awful. The staff is unprofessional, rude, and let you know they don't care about their job or serving you the second you walk in the door. My call in orders magically disappear when I get to the store. Once I get for my smoothie its watery and gross. Everything about this franchise from management, to employees is such a joke. If your in the mood for tropical smoothie try the 66th street location, I will be driving 30 mins out of my way to avoid this mess of a store.

The Delancey Street Bagel shop in the Bala Cynwyd Shopping Center on City Ave (aka City Line) has been closed for several years.  The shop in the Wynnewood Shopping Center is still open.  The photo you are running is of the Wynnewood branch, not Bala Cynwyd.  BC does not now accept credit cards.

We're new to the area, so we thought we'd give this local restaurant a try for some carry out chinese. Yikes. My wife ordered the chicken fried rice and I ordered a shrimp dish. One issue right off the bat - we received pork friend rice instead of chicken. It had a funky dirty taste to it and my wife hated it. I tried it and agreed - pitched. My shrimp dish was supposed to be spicey. It was not. It also had a very odd flavor to it. Almost sweet, which it shouldn't have been. I picked the shrimp out and tossed the rest. The egg roll I ordered was good, but that's hardly a saving grace. I cannot recommend and will not be returning. Hoping to find something much better in the area soon, as we love Chinese.
One other note: we're not picky at all, so this was bad.

Made reservation for Friday for Saturday night, advising "Joseph" we had a groupon. He said "no problem" and took our names When we arrived we were told they wouldn't be able to seat us.  The place was full. The girl at the desk said if we came back in two Hours they would be able to seat us.  We suspect the management overbooked and saw we were using a groupon and decided he would rather have someone paying full price rather than half fare.  Kinda made us a little mad.   Called groupon and received a credit for the groupon. Will not be going back....

No alcohol...wtf?  I wanted a draft beer and the server said they don't have draft beer.  I then said no problem, I will just have a vodka tonic...."we don't have alcohol either"...lol.  So I just had an iced tea which was fine.  The food, however, was terrible.  The restaurant looks like it hasn't been updated in 50 years and was just a dump inside.  Being from New Jersey and being a chef I have had great Italian food in NY, NJ, and many other states. This was very poor quality Italian...the red sauce had no flavor and tasted like they just opened a can of sauce.  The cheese was a cheap mozzarella also with no flavor.  The menu was very limited and it was hard to order anything because everything sounded so basic.  I had the chicken parm,  the serving size was small, it was overpriced, it didn't come with any pasta under it or on the side...just chicken on plate.  Very poor presentation as well.  I had a side salad with the meal and McDonald's makes a better salad than his place.  If you want good Italian food...don't go here:

WTF, Honey's, when did you start serving artificial maple syrup? I don't normally order pancakes at breakfast places because they're easy to make at home and are generally one of the more over-priced items on the menu. On a brisk fall day, however, the pumpkin pancakes caught my attention. But, seriously, artificial maple syrup? As a former New Englander, I feel that any breakfast joint worth its weight shouldn't be able to get away with serving that stuff. After one bite, I could tell I wasn't eating the real deal, so I asked the server about it because it reads "maple syrup" on the menu, and he told me that they use a "maple blend" (whatever that is) because maple syrup is ridiculously expensive and would make the price of pancakes exorbitantly high. Uh, yeah, 'cause $9.50 for two pancakes isn't already exorbitantly high.

Nice place.  Great view.  Food is excellent.  But the place lacks charm.
Also, valet parking is required, even though i practically parked it myself.  And when i asked the host to break a 20, so i could tip the valet (for driving my car 14 feet to park it), he gave me a 10 and two 5's.  I hate that crap.

I tapped the back of a fellow suburbanite a few weeks back, that did about $1,200 damage to my Honda. Kudos to the Nationwide Insurance people who worked quickly to put things in order. The Spina and Adams collision people that Nationwide referred me to were courteous and did evidently good work. 

They promised my car back in three days, but it was delayed a few more days, it happens. No biggie. What I found after I got the car back, was that Spin and Adams had neglected to put a turn signal bulb back in, I didn't know this until the weekend when they were closed. So I was driving around with a potential to be cited for lack of a working turn signal, because they forgot to put a bulb back in. This demonstrated a lack of proper procedure - checking a vehicle before giving it back to the customer.

My most recent visit was for dinner, which was strictly mediocre.   Crabcakes, mac and cheese, and green bean casserole.  Sounds like yummy comfort food, right?  The crabcakes were not flavorful at all. The mac and cheese was Kraft Deluxe.  I am not kidding.  The green bean casserole was the best part of the dish, and I believe the portion was about 1/4 of a cup.  

Their breakfast is their specialty, in my opinion.   Stick with breakfast there and you can't go wrong!

I used to like this place but just had breakfast there and was disgusted by the number of health code violations I witnessed while forced to watch the kitchen from my table. How do you feel about having the cook eat while he's cooking your food and moves his hand from his mouth to  your food? Thought I'd complained to the manager who was expediting, but she had toast propped on her bosom touching her t-shirt. My decision is not to eat there again.

Had my oil changed, transmission flush, engine flush, and radiator flush done to my car last week. It took about 2 hours to get done and the manager Gerry was great. He hooked me up with a great deal and we had no issues with the car. Now today my husband found out our check engine light came on and went to Jiffy Lube by our house to find out what's wrong. They couldn't so they said go try the one on Broadway and Pantano as they should know more and their more experienced. They then tried to find out and couldn't find out so they said we can't run the codes to your engine but if you go to Meineke they can run the codes and help you diagnose it. 

The Meineke rep then told us there's an issue with our transmission and asked if any work was done on it. We told him we got s flush he checked it out and said the people at Jiffy Lube didn't perform the procedure right and wasn't supposed to totally flush every little bit out especially since the car only has 130,000 miles. He told us to contact corporate for their company to fix his transmission and give us a free rental car until the transmission is fixed for free. That situation should not have happened and he said if it's not fixed and we keep driving it could get clogged and were out a car in which we just fully paid off. Such a disappointment. My husband is currently trying to call corporate and get this situation handled.


Other than that I would say they did have great customer service and were very polite. But ruining a car is a big no, no :(

Hmmm , Have you heard the old expression "Captive Audience " that's the scenario affecting all levels of service at this resort .
Here's a few ..........
1) The head Chef in the Sushi / American Diner .
He was excellent big tall guy look like a Left Tackle think his name was Clint . He made a Fish dish for my wife because the menu was so inept .
2) Mexican restaurant was repulsive , we must be eating different Mexican food in Houston because this was not Mexican food . This made Taco Bell late night drive through look good.
3) The maps and confusing elevator scenario leaves you in a perpetual " Lost Mode " the whole time you are here . The brand new App that they gave us locked up every time we went upto another level so it was useless . The maps where designed by someone with XRay vision !
4) Room service cannot get the simple " Large pot of coffee for 2 people with cream and sugar " 
Every day this sentence had to be repeated , 2 of the 4 days they called back to see if we wanted cream and sugar ! 
5) no visible management appears to be company policy ( That ties into the " captive Audience " mantra 
6) The convention room was so cold ( mid to low 60s ) that we asked for blankets . The blankets never came . One day the presenter came on stage with gloves on ! That was funny !
7) convention meals Day 1 Chicken Day 2 Chicken 
nasty slab of frozen boiled chicken each day .
8) Irish pub was excellent 
9) Sports Bar was excellent
10) Ultra rich only Steak restaurant was Fair 
(We are spoilt for Ultra steak restaurants in Houston ) 
I would not return to this resort voluntarily and do not expect a response as no manager I spoke with the entire week seemed interested in my opinion , despite my extensive travel experience .

They have a great beer selection but they lack on customer service. I have gone here a few times over the years and was not always pleased with the staff, but  my last visit was the straw that broke the camels back .I had a terrible experience at this place. I went in to get a lottery ticket and paid with cash. After the women handed me my ticket she never gave me my money. When I informed her that she still owed me. She proceeded to tell me she did give me my change and I must have hidden it somewhere. When I told her again after emptying my pockets, she came from behind the counter and frisked me and said that I must have hidden it under my cloths. Still not wanting to give me my money, she opened the draw handed me my change in coins and said I was a thief and told me never to come back again. I have never had an experience like this before. Don't go hear unless you want to be demoralized and attacked when buying a product from them.

Sam's could be so much better than it is currently. The food is pretty tasty for bar food. There are tons of TVs with a wide variety of games on. If you like trivia nights they have that too. 

However, ownership needs to invest in the restaurant. It is terribly slow on Friday and Saturday nights, and that shouldn't be the case considering it's prime location. They should fix the floors, upgrade the bathrooms, and actually bring back real glassware instead of plastic crap. The staff is generally pretty nice, but turnover is atrocious, which leads to poorer service than should be expected. 

Sam's used to be much better several years ago. I think they can get back there with a little work.
Worst place in philly for hookah and drinks.. Pathetic service..we waited for 30 mins to get out order placed even placed was not packed.. Beer was not chilled and servers were rude..

I walked into this restaraunt around 9pm one night, the door was open the light on.... But the woman at the front behaved very much like they were closed. When I walked up I said, Are you guys open? She said, "Order now!" A little flustered, i picked up the menu and chose some items that I'd want. She rang up a total of like $17.00. I said, Are you kidding me??? I ordered one meal. She started screaming, literally screaming, my order back! I told her that the pork fried rice should be chicken fried rice, and she started screaming at me even louder, mostly in Chinese.... Needless to say, I left right then... and the whole way to my car, could hear Chinese screaming from this restaraunt, directed towards me - because she misheard my order... No idea what she was saying, to or about me. But I wouldn t recommend this restraunt to my worst enemy, let alone any of you.... Save yourself a trip, and put them out of business. No business should treat customers this way.

DO NOT ORDER FROM HOOTERS VIA GRUBHUB!  I made that mistake, spending about $60 total.  My order was completely screwed up -- breaded wings instead of naked, cheesecake placed on top of hot food so was melted/mushy, a mysterious order of mozzarella cheese sticks that I did not order -- without any marina or anything to dip them in. 

In addition, wings were SWIMMING IN GREASE!  They use "cheese sauce" which is NASTY; there is no REAL CHEESE ON ANYTHING (on tots and in sauce for pretzels), etc. 

Just NOT!  NEVER again!

This place used to be great.  second time in a row that the fried rice was dry, the egg rolls were burnt, my kung pao shrimp had 10 shrimp and 10 stalks of celery, the crab rangoon had zero crab, and they RAISED THEIR PRICES about 15 to 20%.
Last time, never again.
Creamer was unlabeled. Only offered stevia as a sugar substitute, which I cannot stand. I returned the coffee and was offered no refund.
Waaaaay too huge.  It takes forever to walk to your room or a really overpriced restaurant.  The staff everywhere is very helpful and polite but this place drives me nuts.

If I could give a negative I would I "purchased" car for the first time last Friday and I have gone back 3 times to finalize paperwork that should of been done Saturday, first of all they sold me a car with a broken bumper seems like a 3 year old put it on 70 %of the pins were not on, they let me drive it off the lot as a borrowed car til the credit was approved which should of been Monday now they call me once again to send them more information to hope that the credit is approved, this made my first car buying experience an absolute nightmare it's ether yes or no , so I'm going back tomorrow to give the car back and get my down payment back and move on and get a car at another dealership this is by far the worst experience and I recommend to leave crest honda at the bottom of your list .. If there was a negative I would of chose it

The sushi here is amazing. Is it so delicious. HOWEVER, the service is god awful. The happy hour used to be from 5-7, but they cut it back to end at 6:30. Seems more like she just stops it when she feels like. By she, I mean the woman who has waited on us the last 2 times we have been here. I suspect she may be one of the owners, as i think its a family owned place. We came in at 6:20 tonight and she began barking at us about how happy hour is almost over. She then came over and flung the menu over and walked away. She came right back, urging us to hurry, and ripped the menu out of my boyfriend's hands before he was done making his selections. So now I'm sure he will be hungry even after we leave. Its a real shame, because this place is so good, but its not worth dealing with the owner. She should stay behind the scenes.

Normally I would give this place a higher rating, however a group of girlfriends and I had planned to go there all week for our girl's night out only to arrive and have them tell us they decided to close early.  They made this decision based on the lack of business throughout that evening.  This is a big issue with me in regards businesses in general and can hurt you more in the long run that just biting the bullet and keeping your staff there and your lights on.  My friends and I typically spend a couple of hundred dollars every time we go out which we did at 54th street cafe on Lindbergh.  I am a business owner for over 20 years and this is just one of the little big things that can make or break a small family owned  business.  Get a grip!  People count on you and plan their week with your place in mind.  Such a let down.

Horrible! Liars. Called at 2:28 for to go order and said they close at 2:30. Ok so take my order. Funny how she says the chef left at 2:15 when the restaurant closes at 2:30. I will take my business elsewhere!

So, I've been waiting over a month now for that refund and haven't gotten it and they're not responding. So, yeah, that clinches it. Thanks for promising a solution to the problem and not following through.

The leggings I purchased XL were way too big.  They looked like parashoot pants.  I couldn't believe the sizing is that off.  The Shirts/sweaters I bought ran small!  Go figure??  The return process is horrible!  I requested a return label 3 - 4 times and the last time I requested one, the girl said she could put the amount in dispute.  The store cc ruined my credit score after having 100% on time payments for 4 years!  I told them I didn't want to pay for clothes I'm in the process of returning.  They said "we understand" and then hit me with missed payments a about 5 late charges!  They expect me to pay more in late charges than my original purchase!  Including the returns!  This is the 1st CC I've had where you can't set up auto payments other than with your own bank.  Your bank doesn't know what your balance is and when not to send payment!  UGH!!  I recommend just paying for your purchases out right an forget Comenity Bank!  There's no communication between the store and finance company.  Sad!  Not to mention outsourcing your calls and taking jobs away from America!!!!  All outsourcing should come with a hefty penalty tax which could possibly lower sales tax for the state's that are hurting because of high sales tax.

Arriving in Santa Barbara, I called the number for this motel and the national reservation desk answered.  I ask for a room rate for two people, two beds.  At first he gave me a rate of $187.  That seemed high so I ask for the AARP rate.  He said wait just a minute and came back to me with the rate of $147.  That was acceptable to me and he gave me the address so I could navigate to the motel.  

Once at the motel, I relayed the quote to the front desk person.  She said they did not have a rate that low.  Something like $211 dollars, later to say that the best she could do was $187.  I told her the quote I got was the AARP quote from the national person was $147.  She basically called me a LIAR as there was no quote that low for this motel.  Somewhat humiliated, I ask if she would talk to the national person I talked to.  She seemed reluctant.  

I then dialed the same number on my cell phone and luckily got the same person who remembered our conversation 5 minutes prior.  I handed my cell phone to the front desk person and ask her to talk to Scott.  She agreed to talk to him. 

After a minute of talking, she said to Scott  "I did not know we could quote a rate that low".  Scott must have assured her it was OK.

She said fine to me, we can give you that rate.  I said first "you can give me an apology for calling me in so many words, a LIAR.  She seemed embarrassed and but she apologized.  I accepted.  She then offered us a bottle of wine, which we accepted.  

But, even the wine did not totally erase the feeling of publically being called a liar.  
The room was fine, but the front desk and national assistance need some more training on rates and diplomacy!

Tl;dr - I was disappointed with how my extensions turned out, but even more disappointed by the lack of effort made to fix things once they knew I was a dissatisfied customer.

I have been interested in lash extensions for quite a while, and when I was invited to be in my brother's wedding, I decided it was the perfect opportunity to give them a try. I did a lot of research on area salons and chose Wink based on their excellent Yelp reviews, as well as a recommendation from a friend of a friend.

My initial impression was positive - the salon was clean and the décor was cute. The stylist asked me what kind of lashes I wanted, and when I mentioned that I was going to be in a wedding, she said that she would add a few volume lashes in with a full set of classic lashes. The last thing I said before she got started was that I wanted my lashes to look "realistic but awesome."

When I was finally able to open my eyes and see the finished product, I was instantly concerned. The extensions were extremely obvious. They reminded me of those old-fashioned dolls whose eyes blink - a uniform fringe without any of the fluttery texture found in nature. I was hopeful that they just needed a little time to "relax," but unfortunately there was no change when I woke up the next morning. I quickly got a couple of second opinions from family and friends, who all agreed that something needed to be done to fix them.

I called the salon and asked to speak with the owner, hoping that there was something that could be done to achieve a more realistic look - and if not, I was willing to get the extensions removed. The owner told me that her schedule was fully booked and passed me to the stylist who initially applied the extensions. She said she would need to check her schedule. I reminded her that I would be heading out of town later in the week for the wedding, and she promised to text me (she never did). Neither she nor the salon owner made any attempt to see if another stylist might be able to accommodate me.

Food was OK, nothing special.  Overpriced.  Service is incredibly SLOW, whether they are busy or not.

Looks nice from the outside, as you are driving by.  Ends the minute you actually walk in.

We came to the restaurant about 2 weeks after they opened.  We had the worst service, and the food was not what I expected.  I ordered a salad with salmon and my husband ordered the French dip.  Our food came about 45 minutes later, and no salmon on my salad.  When they did bring it, it was several pieces of cold dried out salmon. I was not very satisfied or happy about that, and sent it back.  Our waitress never came during our wait  to communicate with us a about when our food was coming.  However, she did come back about 15 minutes after she gave us our food and gave us  a gift certificate to come back and try them again, without an apology.  She knew  we were disappointed with the food and service.  I'm not even sure I'd go back there.  Hopefully they have figured out the kinks.

Second visit.  First time I thought "average food, average prices" - this could be a go to, quick, when you're hungry place!

Second visit I noticed they have a habit of taking hot food items and setting them at the end of the counter - off any heat or heat lamp - health code anyone?  All of the food was overly greasy, either very hot or very cold, and bland!  My dining partner was the same both times and agreed the second visit was not enticing and a step down from visit #1.

Sigh.  I'll now keep looking for that solid establishment that I had hoped this was.

I am not quite sure where to begin... I have had breakfast at this IHOP on multiple occasions and it has never been great but it has never been as bad as it was this past Monday. It was their 58th anniversary so that most likely contributed to how awful it was but I will never eat at this IHOP again and I will be telling everyone I know not to waste their time or money at this establishment. 
We arrived relatively late in terms of when people would typically choose to eat breakfast, it was more like lunch time (after noon for sure) but very busy. We waited 10 minutes for a table and were seated at a booth right next to the kitchen. Our server was polite enough but there was definitely a language barrier. 
After bringing us coffee and water he took our orders and this is where things started to go south. I am not a fan of runny or wet eggs in the slightest and I would rather be specific with my order to avoid having food sent back so as I ordered my egg whites to be cooked WELL done the server just nodded and kept scribbling. My boyfriend ordered his food, specifying red skin potatoes instead of hash browns, ham and 3 over easy eggs. After he finished ordering I then thought I should perhaps emphasize that I do not like wet eggs just so that it was clear to the server and there was no miscommunication.
He assured me that he would definitely have the eggs cooked well and that they would come out as requested. Soon after, our food arrives and as soon as he places the eggs on the table I can tell that they are very soft and undercooked. Instead of wasting time, I very nicely ask if I could have them cooked longer. He picks up the plate, doesn't say a word and walks away. Not only that but my boyfriends meal wasCOMPLETELY wrong. He gets 2 eggs instead of 3 and hash towns instead of redskin potatoes. However, he is very easy going and chose not to say anything. His ham was also two quarters instead of 4. Now that my eggs are gone I decide to have my pancakes while I wait. To my disappointment, they are stone cold. I put the first bite in my mouth and I was immediately turned off by he temperature and chewiness of the pancakes. I then touch them thinking that I may just be overly sensitive but they are cold to the touch as well. He goes to try his and they also seem like they have been siting out for quite some time.
The waiter returns with my egg whites which seem to be slightly more cooked but nowhere near well done (edible now at least). Let down by the pancakes, I mention that they've come out cold. The server then rolls his eyes, picks up the plates and walks away. He comes back not even 30 seconds later with new pancakes that aren't even cooked and still the colour of pancake batter. 
At this point my boyfriend and I are both unbelievably frustrated so he gets up and asks for our bill so that we can go to a different restaurant and have some actual food. The server does not question him at all even though neither of us have touched our food and gets him the bill.
I should also mention that the owner has been circling around the entire time and saw us get up with all of our food untouched and walk to the register. I will also add that they charged us thirteen dollars for three egg whites which is absolutely ridiculous. As my card is literally in the machine, our server runs over and decides that now is the time to ask if everything is okay. (Right before I get to the tip option) We calmly tell him that it is not and his response is to take only the egg whites off of our bill even though the pancakes were a nightmare and my boyfriends entire meal was wrong.
The worst experience I've ever had at a restaurant and the food is absolute garbage. GARBAGE.
I let my feeling settle before writing this review because I want it to be fair but the fact that I'm still baffled by everything goes to show just how bad it was. If you feel like eating cold and chewy pancakes and wet eggs or an entirely different meal than you ordered, IHOP is your place to go!

WORSE PLACE I HAVE EVER LIVED!!!!

This apartment complex is very deceiving. It looks great on the outside but it was built with little care and extremely cheaply. We were the first people to live in our unit and since moving in we have experience the following problems:

*All smoke detectors replaced within 60 days of moving in.

*Air conditioner failed not once but TWICE needing replacement both times.

*Deadbolt lock broke and locked us into the apartment for a few hours - was told if Fire Dept was called to break the door down we would be liable for damage. This is a HUGE safety problem.

*Master bath toilet wax ring needed replaced TWICE - leaked so bad the ceiling below us needed replaced.

*Kitchen sink leaked for unknown time period and caused damage to the cabinet.

*Kitchen faucet leaked and required maintenance to repair it.

*Front gate has been inoperable for over 3 weeks. Security issue.

*Key fob for fitness center has been inoperable for over 3 weeks.

*Fire alarm was blaring for close to 2 hours before notifying residents of the issue. Management seemed unconcerned and shut down their office to go home before residents were able to get back into their unit. Complete lack of customer service and a slap in the face to residents who were displaced while waiting for the problem to be fixed. Did they offer anything for our trouble? Nope.

*Palmetto bug infestation that took over a week to have pest control take care of it. We killed 5+ palmetto bugs in 3 days.

*Management has no concern about the condition of their units or if it affects the quality of life for residents.

*Almost every person in our building has broken their lease to get out of this community. 

*Community changes "rules" on a weekly basis it seems. One new "rule" put in place was residents aren't allowed to store bicycles or plants on their balcony. Mind you the units do not have any hall/coat closets. They expect residents to keep their bikes and other items in their living room.

*Extremely thin walls and floors. EXTREMELY THIN. I can hear our neighbors turn on and off the shower, open drawers etc. You can imagine what else is heard.

*Property developer used the cheapest materials and components possible.

*Carpet is so thin that you can feel the tack strip on your bare feet when transitioning from carpet to the hard surfaces.

*Ceiling fan in master bedroom can be heard running from other side of the apartment.

*Dryer stopped working and needed serviced.

*Received a fine for a dog turd found in the bushes at the DOG PARK. However there has been trash and metal banding in the bushes by our garage for MONTHS and it has yet to be picked up. Amazing that they go looking for dog waste to charge residents but don't keep up on the trash/debris throughout the community.

SAVE YOU MONEY AND SANITY!!! DO NOT RENT FROM HERE!!!

Ripped off!

My wife and I went to see a movie at this venue.  We drove 50 minutes , paid for parking, and entered the venue.  Within minutes of the start of the film, we realized the the WRONG FILM WAS BEING SHOWN.  It was not the advertised film.  

I immediately advised the staff and requested a refund.  I was told "all sales are final."  I then contacted the executive director of the Screening Room but I got no response and the posted phone number isn't working.

We like supporting local theater, but this one is disorganized and the staff obviously just doesn't care.

6/9/18 I went to this restaurant for the very first time. I was in the mood for a calzone, called ahead and ordered one to go. I picked it up, and starving as I was, I began to pick at it in the car. The taste was funny. I pulled another piece off, and it tasted odd also. I then looked at the calzone itself and that's when I noticed it was NOT COOKED completely. The bread itself was doughy on one side. I tore another piece off and stretched out it with my fingers, horrified. I turned the car around and spoke with the P.I.C. and was a given a refund. I will never go back to this place again.

Honestly, very disappointing. A lot of people really seem to like Caruso's, but I think it's not really that they genuinely like the food or the service so much as they like the convenience (location) and the fact that everyone in town seems to know the name. 

We went with a big party a while back and while the whole thing was bad, here's a few highlights:

- Despite having a reservation for close to 20 people, we had to wait outside in the rain for up to 30 minutes for them to have our table ready. There wasn't enough room in the lobby for us all.

- One of our party ordered a special: seasoned chicken. It was served as just that, literally. No veggies, no sides, no condiments even. Just a chunk of chicken rolling around on an empty plate. 

- The service was unacceptably slow and food was served in waves instead of all at once. 

- The birthday girl was given one of those little complimentary sundaes which turned out NOT to be sundae, but a pile of whipped cream with a candle stuck in it. What? Who does this? Why not just NOT offer the free sundae. Way to say 'thanks for your business, but we have more important things to do than worry about making money'.

- Towards the end of the night, one of the other guests in our 'room' (the dinning areas are divided up into smaller, more intimate rooms) got extraordinarily drunk and started arguing with another guest at a neighboring table. The one-sided argument escalated into the drunk woman screaming obscenities and vulgarities about the shocked guest's vagina (yes, her vagina) while the waitstaff and manager looked on. No one did anything. No one asked the drunk woman to leave. Instead, our entire table and the victim and her date left, shocked. 

We will never, ever return to this restaurant. There are plenty of other sub-par Italian places in Tucson to eat and even some very, very good ones.

If I could give this placed a negative star I would I was impressed with the staff she was helpful with restaurants and their locations. However the Sandy reef in is the most disgusting dirty place I've ever seen there eye homeless people hanging out at the smoking area. The shower curtain roof was rushed and I took pictures the hinges on the bathroom door were also rusted the valley smelled awful the television was something dating back to the 1990's. There is no tin in the room shower only who in the hek don't take baths particularly if you have kids the desk had burn status as if someone left their cigarettes on the wooden desk the dresser look like something that was pulled out of a thrift store not a good will the deadbolt security lock on the door is missing and nothing personal but I don't want to see your cultural influence in the hotel room just make it nice for the guests I would never return and don't recommend anyone to ever stay there even if it's free the place is absolutely disgusting and should be red tagged in the owner forced to modernize update and correct all the issues at that died he runs the hotel like a slumlord

YO, This place is SHIT! I walked in, HUNGRY and as a fellow middle eastern Man, I loved the decor...Until I got served by a chick who didn't know what PAAN RASS AND OR PAAN ANYTHING WAS, I swear looks can be deceiving, anyway back to my point, I'm walking in here HUNGRY and as soon as I sat down I ordered, Now idk if they were killing the damn Lamb or not, but I had to wait 1 HOUR for my food, and 20 min for my damn tea, Really? This is how you treat people? As I was ready to spend a little over 100.00 on the food and Hookah, which btw, NEVER came out, it's all good. And here's a fun fact, I walked right out, and the owner got the balls to tell me about the tea? And Hookah? FOOL! I was hungry and you knew that shit, how the hell do you forget my order? But it's all good, tend to the large groups and forget about the small couples. FUN FACT- I noticed the majority of bad reviews are from my fellow brown people, Respect. Nice to know I'm not the only person who thinks his culture is being white washed to shit. I smell Brown on Brown Crime!

Dropped them too late.  They price gouge.  They charged 13.6 cents a KWhour last month.  Do not take their service for any award programs.  That is just a scam to let them in the door.  Keep in mind that you can be stuck with them for 2 months after you drop them and the rate goes even higher then.

So I was there this past Saturday night with a birthday party. A guy from high school(11 yrs ago) showed up with 2 of his friends and sat down with our table. We had a group of 5 plus the guys who sat with us. The guys from hs left and then one by one the people that I came with closed their labs and left. The birthday day girl and I were the last 2 left we asked for our tabs payed them and tipped our waitress well(over 20%) even though she wasn't very good but we have all been servers before so we are good tippers. The waitress then told us that one of the guys sitting with us did not pay his tab ($31) we apologized and said sorry and explained to her that he had mentioned  something about coming back up here. She was rude,  I told her I was trying to get his # and let him know he still owed money. I called him and texted him= no response. The manager then came over and said that if we didn't get him back up here to pay or if we didn't pay that they were calling the cops on us. I said sorry this has happened but we are not responsible for him. We already paid for our food and drinks. We go to leave and they barricaded the door and would not let us leave. They then called the Berry Hill Police Dept on us. They treated us like criminals. It was awful I felt they bullied us and tried to use scary tactics to try and force us to pay for a tab that wasn't ours. The police did not take our name or charge us for anything bc we obviously had not done nothing wrong. The whole ordeal was infuriating and humiliating. The guy finally came back and paid for his tab.
 The next day I called to talk with the general manager and he informed me that  their "policy" is if you sit at table with a group of people that you the customer are responsible to make sure everyone had paid for their tab.?!?!?
I've been to plenty of bars and I've never in my life heard of such a bullshit policy. I've spoke to lawyers and metro police offices and they all agree it was complete bullshit. That is a horrible way to run a business. I will be contacting the better business bureau and will never step back into that place again! Don't go there u may get arrested for someone else not paying their tab!

Inconsistent. Slow service. Long lines. Long wait times. Under staffed. All of these are mentioned in most of the reviews. The answer to that is management and training. Good management take the lead, lead the team and set the pace. This place is chaotic. No one takes charge in a managerial way. Often times staff on duty is high on pot! Owner take note!
I'll spend my money where I get good service from well trained, polite and sober staff.

This place used to be owned by a super sweet family. Then I go in one day, and I'm halfway through my pedi before I realize all the furniture and paint is different. Then other workers began showing up and I realized the place was being run by new people, mostly men. The man working on me was nice and did a decent job despite seeming very unsure of himself. As I was sitting at dryer--kicking myself for not being a more loyal patron to the nice family--the owner went out of his way to amusedly point out to me that their giant fish in the fish tank were eating one another. I was not amused. It was incredibly eerie.

The atmosphere is excellent- you feel as if you've stumbled into a stereotype of an Italian restaurant. Live piano music, mildly accented waiters, extensive wine list- it's got a great feel to it. However, the food on all three occasions I've been there is good, but nothing to write home (or on Yelp) about. Maggiano's has better and fresher food at less cost.

Basically, everything is impressive about this place except for the food, which is only decent. I'd imagine it's a good place to go on a date or impress clients, where the appearance of extremely fine Italian dining is more important than the actual dining itself.

Inauthentic jamaican flavors. 
Visited today and ordered two of the simplest dishes served in almost every jamaican home (chicken soup and jerk wings). The soup was so overly salty, it was inedible, additionally, it was plated in a takeout container typically used for dry dishes like rice. 
The wings were dry, shriveled and hard like they sat around too long. Grand price for this sad excuse for jamaican food-$18+

My wife and I went here on our first date way back when, so you would think we felt a kinship with this place, but on the few times we have since been back it has never been more than so-so. We went on Friday with our kids because it was St Patrick's Day, but the Irish stew was way over-priced and indifferent, and lukewarm, plus my son's had almost no meat in it, and had been slopped over the bowl into the plate with the soda bread. The latter, as well as now being soggy, was almost nothing like real soda bread (which, for starters, does not contain raisins). The stout I ordered was flat, and the waiter tried to tell me this was "normal" for English beer (I am English originally, so can say with authority that this is not true). I ordered a replacement Scotch beer that was spectacular, and was not charged for the flat stout, but still. And my wife ordered salmon cakes that contained little salmon and did not taste home made. In all, got the definite impression they were phoning it in, and we will not be back.

Had Cha Cha Chow at Food Truck Friday in Tower Grove park this past Friday and I must say I am not impressed.  I ordered the Lobster Taco, which came with cabbage slaw, cilantro, lemon dressing that just tasted like lemon and sour cream, and 3 of the tiniest pieces of lobster I have ever been served. No joke, they were about the size of a small cocktail shrimp tail or tiny crawfish tail. The taco was mostly cabbage and a fried flour tortilla, which was actually the best part of the taco. And to top it off it cost either $8 or $9, I can't remember which. Either way it was not memorable and was without a doubt the worst thing I had at Food Truck Friday.  I might try them again to see if anything else on the menu can redeem this place but on value alone it was extremely disappointing. It took me about 2 bites to eat. And the flavor was just ok. The friends that were with me ordered the lobster taco also and we were all let down.

Freakin horrible!!!!! So pissed I drove all the way up here with my family and wasted my money!! Everything and i mean everything tasted like shit!! Service was horrible!  Over priced small portions no taste long wait for food.  So disappointed

On my third visit, the service was still sub-par.  Unlike previous experiences,  a waiter actually came to take our orders and I didn't have to go to the bar.  We never did get the water we asked for twice.

Unfortunately, three drinks in and only after inquiring about the stated prices for the specials were we informed that the price had gone up by 25 cents - the new menus just weren't printed yet.  I had to take it the manager who, fairly nicely but after some argument, agreed to charge me the stated price.  As both the waiter and manager told me, I'm aware it's "only" 25 cents, but woudln't Melrose get upset if I arbitrarily decided to short them 25 cents in my payment?  I'm sure they would.

Reading other reviews, I have to agree and also wonder if some of the funny business with tax and prices isn't going into someone's pocket.  Three tries was enough for me.  The place isn't worth it.

Don't stay here! My gf brought home bedbugs from this nasty place. She had to pay big money to treat her home for the bedbugs she got at sandy land reef inn. Se also had to see a doctor and get medication to help her skin. She is now permanently scared from this infestation. Luckily I walked away with no issues. It was loud and the people who worked there were....odd, to say the least.

Very very poor communication, posts on social media of my dog clearly not happy and harness on wrong, set off security system and did not notify me--I found out from the alarm company first, brought in new walker without telling me, didn't tell me my dog was peeing on the floor and sofa out of fear when she was greeted by them--had to find out from roommates (thank god for them or I would never even know of these issues). I work in a stressful ICU and want to be sure my dog is safe and happy, but these walkers only added more stress.

I wish I could throw up!  The worst sushi I have ever come upon. I don't know how they are still in business serving that horrible food. I thought ordering teriyaki chicken would be safe for my son. Boy was I wrong!  It tasted like they took the gravy from dog food and put it on his chicken!  Then I was served some kind of box roll called the Montecito. I took one look at it and knew it shouldn't be consumed by anyone. But I gave them the benefit of the doubt. I took one bite and paid dearly for that. It tasted like watery Dijon and poppy seed on raw fish! Ahhhhhh!  The other rolls we had (can't recall their names) were all equally terrible. Weird sauces and mushy seafood combinations come to mind when I am forced to remember those awful rolls. The best part of the meal was the moment I was able to leave. If you choose to eat here, eat at your own risk.

Was hoping for a spark of good mexican food in St.Louis and this was not the place to come to. Originally coming from southern california, I have had my share of mexican. 

Food: horrible. Chips and salsa came out fast but thats where the good part ends. Tasteless dinner leaving me and my boyfriend asking why we ever attempted going. 

Service/staff: friendly

Decor: great, warm feeling inside. Perfect for families

The wife and I wanted to get sushi, so we tried this place which is closer to our house than other sushi restaurants.  My first impression was that the place was small and it was a mom-and-pop place, which can be better than the larger chain restaurants.  Unfortunately, this sushi restaurant was sub-par.  The majority of the filling in the sushi rolls were cucumbers, and there was very little actual fish/seafood.  The portions and size of the rolls were underwhelming as well, and we were not impressed with the taste.  Needless to say, we wouldn't recommend this place for sushi.  However, we didn't try any of the main entrees, so we have no opinion on that.  Oh, and you need to bring your own beer and/or wine.

Horrible beyond words. I was STARVING and it was still so bad I couldn't eat the "pad Thai" with chicken. Waffle House has better food--I knew I was making a mistake when the place was EMPTY at 6 pm. NEVER AGAIN!

Unappealing Service, Food, & Drinks. Room for improvement.

This place has a great looking store front. All of the entrees are $17 & up. Mixed drinks are $8 & up.  Service was ....Ok.... the server did seem confident in describing the drinks & entrees really well in appealing descriptions with great enthusiasm.  I only wish the items we ordered lived up to the server's hype. 

The place is smaller inside than I expected.  It was a Friday night and there were about 6 people sitting at the bar & about 6 tables with guests - no parties over 4 tops. With myself included in a party of 3, we were the 6th or 7th table in the ENTIRE place. The layout of the place is strange - the space is not used to it's full potential and the decor is only ok - seemed like they found a few nice pieces and stuck them up somewhere without putting much thought in decor design. 

Upon entering, one of the servers/hostesses(?)  greeted us. After establishing that we wanted to have a table, she pointed at a table for us to sit at. The table was about 30 feet away....I THINK we sat at the one she designated for us by pointing at it from far away....

Drinks:
I ordered a whiskey drink which the server was really adamant about me liking if I liked whiskey - I tried it and was greatly disappointed. It tasted like one of those drinks supposedly made out of an exotic fruit you find in international markets that shouldn't be drinks at all...splashed with some type of whiskey.  My friend ordered some type of coconut looking drink, with the description of the drink it seemed fitting for my female friend - when we tried it, we both felt her drink was milky which is fine but it was too sweet & simply not good.  Their attempt at these experimental recipes didn't quite work out for us. The server (much later) asked us about our drinks after we only consumed 20% of the drink with most of the ice already melted  - we told her it was "alright" with a tone of disappointment , she sensed that we didn't really enjoy the drinks & didn't probe any further. At this point, our food had not arrived. 20 Minutes and counting.....

Food:
Took waaaaayyyy too long to arrive. Only 6 other tables in the restaurant and about 6 people at the bar, it was about 8:30 on a Friday night & our food took probably about 35 minutes to make it to our table.  Im not quire sure what was the reason for the hold up.  We didn't see our server anywhere for a very long time and flagged down another server to see if she could inquire about our food. Our server then comes out to tell us she just checked on our food and reported it would be right out - she agreed that the food was taking a while......  At this point, I was sort of ready to just leave and grab food elsewhere. I was puzzled why the kitchen would be so busy given the volume at this time of night where Friday should be primetime for any restaurants to shine. I felt the restaurant was now wasting my Friday night away when I chose to come here to unwind & enjoy myself after a long week of work - instead I came here and was irritated by the entire experience.  

I had pulled pork. And in fact, it was pulled pork with a special seasoning that wasn't really great tasting -- it was watery. I also had seasoned veggies which were not particularly outstanding. My friend had this beef lasagna type dish but instead of pasta, it was layered with plantains, this item was actually pretty good and very flavorful.  The plantains were not overly sweet and the beef tasted great -- however, for $20??? 

Considering my overall experience, I hate to say that will never come back to dine at this establishment.  I really do hope the business owners exponentially improve on a lot of aspects of their restaurant and become successful - though at the same time, I would not be surprised if this placed was closed 6 to 12 months from now.

Thought we would stop in eat after the gym. It was a little busy. Well,  we were never approached by anyone after we got sat, not even a hi be right with you or anything. Totally ignored. So we just left, after stopping at hostess stand to tell them that we were never waited upon or even acknowledged. She was like okay would you like to see the manager ?  Not even a apology :(  we didn't speak to the manager but I think we should have. May go back and give it another chance.

Absolute Worst Hotel I've ever come across! Booked a room 3-4 months in advance for a convention and trade show I was attending. And arrived to the hotel to have the staff tell me and everyone else in line that their hotel is "first come first serve" and that our reservations apparently don't mean anything. They had given our room away, and gave us vouchers to stay at a hotel 20 minutes away! Front desk staff had a shitty attitude,  and the manager didn't seem to care much that they overbooked the hotel and had to send everyone away! Decided to give their vouchers back and stay at a Hilton, rather than give them anymore business! DO NOT STAY HERE!!!!!!!
Just got take out and they didn't include pickles or cole slaw with my or my husband's food. Come ON!! Ughhhhhhh.

HERE'S THE FINE PRINT: If you have your screen replaced and it pops out afterwards, they will not replace the screen if there's any damage to the screen. 

Okay fine. So my negative review is to reflect the fact that I even had to come back. Weeks after I had my screen replaced, the screen started popping out. As a business owner, ESPECIALLY around Christmas time, it was impossible for me to get back to this place. I made numerous attempts to come back at "my earliest convenience" and well, today was my earliest convenience. 

A few days ago, I was having brunch with a girlfriend when I dropped my phone and a tiny little crack appeared below the "home" button. Today I come because I finally had the time out of MY busy schedule. They said the warranty no longer covered it because of the crack. The guy said, "Had you come in earlier..." well, I would have loved to come in earlier. I would have loved to come back as soon as it happened. Well, I run an entire business almost by myself and I did not have the time. 

So, if you want your screen replaced, they'll do it. It looked great afterward. But within a matter of weeks, the screen popped out and it's YOUR responsibility to make sure you get it back to them WITHOUT A SCRATCH. Pretty high expectations, but that's not what this review is about. 

This review reflects the fact that I should not have had to go BACK in the first place had they done a good job the first time around. I have proof that I notified them of this occurrence two months after the replacement was made - just to show the timeline of how quickly the screen popped out. 

UPDATE UPON RESPONSE OF BUSINESS:
I've been an iPhone user since 2007. This is the first time in my life the screen has ever popper out - after coming here. End of story.

I would be embarrassed to bring anyone here.
Sat for ten minutes and only had a menu tossed in front of me. Later!

I have been staying at the JW Marriott since the hotel was built. Over the last couple of years I have probably stayed over 50 nights at this location. I have never had such an non welcoming experience as I have the last 2 nights in concierge lounge. They literally start closing down at 9 while guests are still trying to enjoy the lounge. Food is picked over and not replenished, no coffee or hot water - it had been put away. When a colleague joked that it wasn't like this when Laura ran things. Savanna said "then maybe you should leave." Then Kyle starts passively aggressively giving us a count down on when they were closing and at exactly 10 he shut off all the lights and said "we are closed". We were headed to the exits prior to Kyle kicking us out. So rude! I called him out on it and he was simply indignant. Either these 2 aren't cut out for the service industry or this hotel has jumped the shark.

Pros
Very clean and modern facility
Reasonably priced
Pets are current on vaccines
Free toys and bag of food

Cons
Staffs are not so friendly and seems like they don't want to work there
All puppies are kept separately - probably to prevent diseases but it is not good for socialization of dogs.

I adopted 8 weeks old puppy about a year and half ago. He is my first dog so i didn't know much about dogs. Now i think back, my dog was not in a good condition.  He was kept separately from other siblings. He and all of his siblings were afraid of people. They all stayed back of the cage and didn't want to come out. 8 weeks old puppy alone with no parents or siblings, probably not good for his mental health.
I'm having hard time making him socialize with other dogs and people.
Visit Williamson county animal shelter, it is little dirtier and crowded but they keep puppies and many dogs together in the same cage.  It might change your opinion about Nashville humane association.

Sorry but its just too damn HOT in this place anytime ive gone there, the side of the building is a garage door and its always up, only 2 small A/C wall units, one aims at the band and the other one is at the cash register, both at either end of the place, so be prepared to sweat, beer is $6 a pint,not cold enough and yes they have a bad aftertaste

Deli food on the Main Line = EPIC FAIL

When the one food in your restaurants name in fact stinks , you have a serious situation on your hands. To the best of my knowledge the area in which this restaurant/deli is located is mostly jewish. Why these jews don't pick up axes and wooden stakes and storm this place demanding better bagels is beyond me. Terrible bagels. Im seething as I write this. 

Not only do the bagels stink but the kids that work here are so absolutely disconnected from everything that I don't think my order there has ever been made correctly. So, the bagels stink and then to top it all off Britney or Madison or whatever the high school chick who is watching the counter is named NEVER GETS MY ORDER RIGHT. 

"I'll have a poppy seed bagel with nova and onion , no cream cheese please" is not some Pythagorean theorem cats and kitties. 

Totally pass on this joint. its amateur night!

If you like bagels for lunch than this is the place. They don'y realize bagels are a breakfast food. Their hours say they are open at 7:00am I have been there a 1/2 dozen times and have never been able to get a bagel. Either they aren't open or the bagels won't be ready for an hour. The worst business people I've every run across.

The bagels are good when they are available.

Ummmm...NO. JUST NO. Tacky, tiny room. No fridge, microwave or coffee pot. Tv has about four channels on it and looks like its from 1995. Had to pay for wifi only to discover that my 4g phone wouldn't even pick up the signal. Four months pregnant and had to clean the pool myself before getting in, it was NASTY. Room is the size of most hotel bathrooms. Shower so tiny I couldn't even shave my legs. Never again!! Skip this over priced dump!

Service sucked.  That really set the tone for the visit. The restaurant was half empty on a Friday, and still it was slow service.  We had heard that the salmon salad was to die for, but the waitress warned us it would take 20 minutes, so she talked one of our party into ordering the Tuna salad.  Then 12 mintues later informed us that the tuna was gone.
