It was 'ok' but not worth raving about so the waitress said, "I don't have to make it with rum, I can use vodka!?" Mmm...ok!? Honestly, I think that was a mistake. It was fine but also meh. The salad I ordered had an entire avocado sliced over it and it wasn't quite ripe - a ripe avocado would have made the difference.  The dressing was fresh tasting but a teeny cup was all I was given. The fish tacos had store bought soft tacos which were lackluster and very wet cole claw under the fish which was tilapia. I ended up just eating the insides as the tacos themselves had no taste and fell apart from the liquid.  There was a teeny cup of guacomole which I could make so much better and there was so little salsa picante I couldn't really review it. The waitress (who also filled as the bartender) forgot our water, delivered hubby's tortilla soup (dull) a long while before my salad arrived, forgot my drink order (also for a long time) and when I asked for a second teeny cup of guacomole she said, "sure!" and never delivered it. She was friendly and apologetic for every wrong turn but with the cost of dining out (which we love to do), we won't be back.

This place has all the breakfast basics, so nothing really special. I ordered the 2 egg omelette with asparagus and prosciutto - the asparagus was tiny and did not contain any spears but the prosciutto was adequate. I do not eat bread so I asked for a substitute for my toast. The server refused any substitute saying that "it just comes out of the kitchen that way" - fine. There are maybe 11 tables in this very small space so she could have literally walked two paces to the cook and asked him for options - but I was told I could just not eat the toast. My husband had the seafood Benedict which was served on a biscuit instead of an English muffin so that was a unique offering. However, the hollandaise sauce was excessively citrusy. The food wasn't necessarily bad but overpriced for what it was and there are many better places in the French Quarter.

We sat for several minutes before we were noticed, the lack of attention persisted during our meal. The BBQ nachos were great but we didn't get plates . Our entrée orders were wrong when they arrived. We had to flag servers down for condiments and beverage refills while there were only two other parties in the restaurant. The hostess was welcoming and friendly but it seemed that the servers just wanted us to eat and get out.

I ordered the curry shrimp rice dish and I was not impressed. 
There were only 7 SMALL shrimp in it and the veggies were sparse. Not to mention the flavours didn't meld - it was definitely not a curry and more of a spicy ranch like concoction. The staff were arguing amongst themselves and very dismissive to the customers. 
I will not return

Horrible.  Everyone that was working looked confused, including cook/owner.  Small place with inexperienced help and cook/owner.  Waited for food a long time.  Waitress didn't know what sides came with breakfast (all she had to do was look at the board behind her).  Food was finally delivered one plate at a time ( the sides didn't even come out with eggs and vice versa)!  We had 4 in our party and I think about 6 different trips to our table finally completed our order.  Not sure how you ruin breakfast but nothing was cooked right.  One of the sides I ordered was scrapple...it came out extremely thin and so over cooked that it was harder than a rock and couldn't even be cut!   Seems like this was a dream of a person always wanting to open his own little place, but, has zero experience in the food and restaurant business.  Can't imagine any repeat customers.  Hope they obtain the needed knowledge and training asap or it may not last long.

So I went in for something pretty simple, and that was to get my eyebrows done. Something that would take 5 minutes tops. The salon was not closed and yet I was turned away. Great customer service......
Waiter was totally rude and a Di**. Huge beer selection but waiter was slow and as noted a D*ic*
Every single time (which is 2) ive eaten here the food is delish and then i have diarrhea for the rest of the evening......draw your own conclusions

3 stars for the standard seafood and buttered veggie fair. Less for the service and ambiance. The tables are rickety at best. My boyfriend and I had the worst pregnant server with an attitude. I feel bad because she was pregnant but maybe have a runner for her to help her get things requested like napkins and refills?! Anyway the crab legs are stand outs here. A lady approached a couple dining next to us and asked them to dance upstairs. We were not invited; was it our jeans? Certainly this establishment doesn't require a better dress code. Skip it and get takeout. So many places are doing the seafood veggie combos.

Rather than drive to Bala Cynwyd, my husband and I decided to order nova & bagels from Mrs. Marty's Deli as it's closer to our house.  The food wasn't bad, but for $10 per sandwich, we were expecting better.  All of the veg was cut entirely too thick. Next time, we'll just grab nova and make our own sandwiches.

Don't take your car to be serviced here. They try to take advantage of their customers.  My girlfriend took her car here 3 times in total.  The first time she asked for it to be serviced and clarified that she just wanted tires rotated and oil change.  Well when she picked up the car her bill was over 200 dollars.  Apparently they did an alignment and tire balance that she didn't ask for.  Well after fighting with them they took that off.  We figured well maye it was a honest mistake, or confusion. The second time she took it, everything went smooth 60 bucks (her car requires a more expensive oil), so we thought ehh the first time was a blip.  Well the third time she asks for the same thing as last time and they charge her 180.  Neadless to say we will no longer do business with Crest Honda.  They are theives.

One of the most terrible customer service that I have experienced for a long time. Don't go there for good customer service because you won't get it. 

What happened: I went to return a piece of furniture that I purchased from this store. Since it was over two weeks, the store didn't allow the return. I understand it is their return policy and I repesct that. However, I was really disappointed by how the male manager handled the situation. He was cold and unsympathetic. The way he talked to me made me feel like he was saying:" this is our policy. Deal with it!" When I was leaving the store with the furniture, there were two managers and one staff by the door, but no one offered to help me.

We did the tour today and were disappointed for the amount we paid.  We had a groupon and paid $79.  We made a total of 4 stops and tried jambalaya, gumbo, Muffiletta and a brisket po boy.  The samples are fairly small.  Our tour guide was Preston and he told the group multiple times he has been doing the job for three weeks, yet through other reviews it appears he has been doing tours for several months. His facts were not very accurate and he was a bit offensive to almost everyone in the group.  I would not recommend this tour. All of the foods we tried are easy to find and you will pay less to try them on your own. Groupon has other deals to get a walking tour and the information will be more accurate and entertaining.
Horrible cupcakes.   Very weird customer service. Don't waste your money.  I couldn't eat the cupcake I bought, very odd tasting and dry.

I don't recommend this place. their menu is no substitution, their prices are high, and their food is mediocre. besides, most of the people working there have an attitude. go to new station, it's right down the road.

We were there for a family dinner last Sunday and we had to be guided over to where the dinner was at. The place was huge! And parking, oh don't even get me started on that! The parking lot itself is a huge maze! The food was subpar, nothing like I would have expected from the Gaylord. The tropical plants were cool to look at, but it all felt very cheesy and overdone. The waiters were very nice. The only good thing about the dinner was the peach cobbler! I ate three little servings of that and I'm not a big fan of peach. I had to add some sugar to it but it was really good. That's the only reason why this place gets a 2 star rating instead of a 1 star rating.

I like Benihana. I think I wrote a review about the one in Jersey while I was at my last job. That being said... I am not a fan of this location.

First, when I walked in, there were two hostesses chatting intensely about some kind of personal business. Then one of them gave me a once-over -- I had walked over from work, so I was wearing my backpack, and it was raining, so I was wearing a hoodie -- and in her most Beverly Hills Boutique B*tches tone of disdain and condescent, informed me that I would not be seated until my date arrived.

At that point, I should have walked out and written a letter to the manager, but, again, it was a long walk, in the rain, and my date was on his way, so I decided to be patient. It was smooth sailing from then on, though.

Except that I asked for fried rice (was not offered it, had to ask) and got plain rice, then I heard the table next to us being offered fried rice, which totally hurt my feelings.

They made it better with that scoop of Haagen Dazs at the end, though.

Poor customer service and phone etiquette by some of the staff, Sig delays in responding to phone calls.  Woken up at 3AM by security loudly banging on the room door to ask if water was running in my room...No it's not, it's called "sound machine"!!! Waking me up for this??really??

The food is bland and not that great. Big portions don't make up for the lack in quality. Ice been twice and both times I've been sick after I ate. The price is good. The service was good. That's what the two stars are for. 

I've found better elsewhere.

This place could be really cool but It has been neglected and the food is kind of mediocre. Plus my waiter was terrible during a slow lunch it took forever to get anything. My coffee was cold and he brought me the wrong ramen. Instead of complaining I just paid and left.. what a way to spending a break from jury duty.

First visit-
My order: Poppy seed bagel with turkey, American cheese and mayo and a newspaper. 
I received: Toasted poppy with Swiss, tomato & mustard and a pre-read (you can always tell!) newspaper

Second (and final) visit-
My order (exact words): "Poppy seed bagel, untoasted, butter on the side"
I received: Poppy seed bagel, toasted, butter on the bagel

The wait was excruciating. Ten minutes for an incorrect order. I thought one of the other patrons was going to go Lord of the Flies while I was standing next to him

The service was salty. Is it too much to ask for eye contact and a greeting? At Delancey St Bagel, apparently it is. 

The prices are, as the kids would say, stoopid. Over two dollars for a bagel with butter? Say whaaaaat?

Unacceptable. I wanted to like this place, but I cant.

I stopped in for pickup at 6:30 on a Thursday night. I walked up the the take-out window already knowing what I wanted since I had looked online. I stood at the window for a couple minutes making noise, coughing, tapping on the metal counter, before I noticed a doorbell to get the attention of the three staff members standing there talking.

I ordered the Super Combo and headed home. When I got home I discovered that I didn't get any pork and that the beef brisket was pointlessly dry and cold. The upside was that the dry rub ribs were really good, but not enough to outweigh the rest of my experience. Maybe a different location may have better luck, but I don't see myself trying another location.

Nice atmosphere but um $15 for 6 ravioli- nothing else on the plate???? 
Im serious: NOTHING ELSE PROVIDED.

No salad no side

Then asked for bread and ACTUALLY GOT BURNED TOAST????

Are you kidding???

Nothing more to say.

I read 12 good reviews on here and had to try it. I was disappointed. The food was okay, the gumbo it not anything to write home about that is for sure. It is tomato based and had no roux, just like tomato soup with some seafood in it. I got fried grouper and scallops and my wife got fried shrimp. The fried scallops and shrimp tasted like the fish that had been fried in it before, but the grouper was good. The food was okay, but not great. We got gumbo, one drink and two dishes and it ended up at $38 without tip. That was too much for that quality food. I would probably not go again.

Was not impressed at all. I only gave it one star because the restaurant was actually clean. Employees were lazy and didn't really seem to care. All they talked about was when they were getting off work. The food came out cold and my burrito fell apart in my hands. I guacamole had a full avocado in it. It was expensive and not worth your time or money. Try Taco Bell!

Was there for lunch this past Sunday. Placed order and after waiting an hour four our food, decided we'd waited long enough and got up and left. Our order was anything but out of the ordinary....... a bowl of gumbo, a bowl of oatmeal with fresh fruit and a chicken salad sandwich. We weren't the only ones having a problem. There were two other diners that also were fed up with the wait and left after waiting a little longer than we had. If I could rate the restaurant ZERO stars, I would.

Sitting here with a macchiato and bagel - the drink is really good. I was looking forward to the fancy way they top/pour the espresso drinks but the guy did kind of a sloppy job, but still tastes nice.

In spite of the 2 stars, I will return (maybe). What's pissing me off is the service. The guy at the counter didn't say hi or even I'll be right with you. He was waiting on ONE person in front of me, I didn't get the impression he was overwhelmed. It's Halloween but I'm pretty sure it doesn't mean treat the customer like a ghost ...

Finally a second employee came out and took my order. It became apparent ReAnimator is one of THOSE places ... If you aren't a regular/local you can piss off. The unfriendly guy barista had a friend come in who was greeted before he even made it to the counter, conversation ensued and was even apologized to for the brief wait. 

Getting nice coffee is a treat and I rarely do it for money reasons, so I'm actually really disappointed in this experience.

Awwww dang. I so wanted to feel at home here since I live on Willow and can walk over in 2 minutes. I use about 1/10 of an ounce of non-dairy in my coffee. (Soy, coconut milk, whatever.) Buddy wants $1.00 for my splash. What about us vegans out here who like coffee. Get with the times and we will be back.

Does it smell bad? Yep. But most places with that much "fresh" fish aren't going to smell great. The produce was pretty good, lots of selections and 95% of it looked really fresh, the other 5% not so much. Lots of spices and different meats to choose from. The pork and beef... I have my suspicions on it. If I bought it I would only cook it well done. The fish... ah yes the fish. They have some live ones... and some dead ones floating in the live tank.. oops. Lots of selection on the "fresh" fish, mullet, sheepshead, croaker, blue fish, ribbon fish, salmon, catfish, tilapia etc. I say "fresh" because you can tell it's all frozen or previously frozen and some of it has that "I've been dead too long" look in their glassy frozen fish eyes. We elected to go with with the still alive catfish. Got 3 of them and the Hispanic gentlemen made a chopping motion that I assumed meant "chop their heads off?" I said yes and was rewarded with the catfish cut into oblivion, thrown in a bag and priced... but probably my fault. Anyway took em home, skinned em, and made nuggets. These aren't American catfish!!!! It's obvious both by the look, smell and the stain on the meat that these bad boys come from Asia. Not worth the price and the taste? Eh. Will I go back? Maybe.. but only if I'm driving by and need to blow a few minutes.

Pros: 
Family friendly, convenient location, great crowd, clean and no drunks in the restaurant during the day!

Cons:
Service is horrible, a little too expensive, waiters/waitresses pretend you're not there, the space is small and crammed, do not expect to get your food served right when it is ready, and if you ask something to the waiter/waitress they will get an attitude and get offended like you are inconveniencing them. (Don't get the french toast)

Conclusion: 
If you want to sit down and take a break from the walking and etc., this establishment should be not the last choice but one not your first.

Decor and ambiance did not match the name. This is a local bar trying to be different and failing badly.  American 70's hit music in a Cuban bistro? Really!? Table cloth had stains. No Cuban beers on menu. Only Cuban mixed drink is the mojito.

The menu was no different that what I would expect from Chipotle.  I ordered the burger.  Ordered my burger medium. Got a piece of charcoals.  I couldn't finish it.

My daughter ordered a "cuban sandwich". She left half on the plate.

So this is a mediocre sushi joint sitting on Washington Ave and I know that is a poor starter but let me support the statement. The only reason we decided to come to this joint was because of a few things. We had never been here before, the cigar bar we were hoping to hit up was right across the street, and we were trying to eat a lighter fare. We stroll into the restaurant and it is absolutely huge inside. A huge tv projection is up on the wall and this place has an extensive sushi bar and alcohol selection on the wall. We were easily able to sit down as the place was pretty empty and a server immediately came over. So this server was brand new and I could see she was very sweet and trying to keep up but she honestly did not know anything about the menu or what the restaurant offered. We felt bad because she looked very nervous and she repeated a few times that it was her first week working. Not a huge issue for us but maybe the restaurant should think about training their employees a bit more? Anyway we ordered a slew of rolls to try out various flavors. 

Honestly, all of the sushi was very bland and a very poor ratio of fish to rice with them overkilling on the rice. There was also a roll which was filled with imitation crab which was just absolutely terrible. The rolls here are also very small in comparison to the Wasabi or Sushi Ai. A lot of the sushi itself here tasted frozen and a lot of the rolls had a strong fishy taste to it showing that it is just not fresh. The issue on top is that this place charges the standard prices for rolls which means very expensive for what you are getting. Honestly I just suggest that you avoid this place all together and stick with the other sushi joints in the city.

Buyer beware!!  Ordered a bracelet and never received it. Salt Pines refuses to send another.  Very rude young man that answers phone.  No customer service. Too many nice businesses to work with to put up with this.

I've been a few times when they first opened. Food was good not great but good enough for me to return. The wait is ridiculous and that's why I stopped going for like a year.. Went today and ordered cheesesteak rolls and beef egg foo young. They changed the cheesesteak rolls. It was mushy inside and it was some type of pasty sauce and onions. Not a piece of steak at all. I cut open my 2nd roll because I wanted to examine it and again no steak. The it had a chicken bouillon flavor. So weird didn't want to finish it. The beef is the egg foo young was tough and flavorless.. The egg part was also mushy.  I normally eat at the Chinese store across from overbrook hs but they are a cash o my establishment. I didn't have any cash on me. Their food may not be halal but their food is 10x's better. Not going back to Chan halals again... waste of my time and money. Now I'm sitting here hungry.

We have a very long wait for food and we had just ordered wings and a salad.  When the wings finally arrived, I waived off the salad.  I let the waitress know politely that we had waited a while for the food.  She got defensive and blamed me for not telling her that I wanted the salad sooner.  I tried to clarify by saying we waited awhile for all the food, but she just walked off.  I got the boneless grilled wings with the Glory sauce.  The wings had a heavy smoke flavor and the sauce we almost inedible.  It tasted like vinegar with some honey.  I ate two pieces and left the rest.  The other party with me had the regular buffalo hot wings and they were pretty tasty.  When walking out the floor was wet and littered with food while the staff stood at the front door chatting.  Sadly we won't be going back.

Staff is more than unfriendly. My iced dirty soy chai was $7.50! I have no idea why I tipped them a dollar fifty on top of the outrageous price? Habit of kindness I suppose.  The espresso my business partner got was literally the worst burnt bitter espresso I've ever tasted. Do yourself a favor and walk a few blocks over to Steep and Grind on Frankford.

Went in last night at 7:50 pm - this location is listed as open until 11 pm.  1 staff is cleaning up the place, and the other is behind the counter working on some paperwork.  I walked up to the counter and stood there.  I continued to stand there, not three feet from the employee who is tending to her paperwork.  She noted my presence, but did not acknowledge me.  After another minute or so, I turned around and left.  This is ridiculous - I hope the franchise owner knows their employees are actively discouraging customers to minimize their own work.

My friend raved about the deep dish but we didn't feel like all the bread of a deep dish and got a hand tossed one instead. It was less than stellar..I honestly could've gotten a DiGorno's pizza and done better. We didn't initially ask for extra cheese so when our pie came out, we were underwhelmed. We asked if maybe we could add the extra cheese on the pie and pay for the extra topping but the lady told us that they don' t put pies back in the oven once they've come out. I've NEVER heard this before but not ones to start a whole back and forth, we paid for our pizza, took it home and put extra cheese on it and put it back in the oven. The worst part is, seeing that we were not satisfied, she NEVER ONCE asked us if there was something she could do to better our experience. I guess the customer is always right anyplace BUT here. WILL NEVER GO BACK TO THIS PLACE AGAIN IN LIFE AND PISSED THAT I WASTED THE CALORIES!!

I always have to be literal when I don't care for a place. Sadly, this hooters was a disappointment. They felt quite sterile and NOT a place to make your regular haunt. Beer is always a safe bet so points go there. Food tasted VERY pre-prepared and had chewy texture (wings). I used to go to the one off Tanque Verde in the 90's and it was great then.
If I were to offer advice to this franchise outlet it would be to go back to local sourcing. Other bars and restaurants are doing just that and they are AMAZING.

Great selection of beer. Great location. But........everything else kinda sucks. It seems dark,dank,smelly, and unkept. The men's bathroom is something out of a horror movie. It is the smallest, dirtiest bathroom i have ever seen. And that includes port-0-potties that I used in Jacksonville when the eagles were in the Super Bowl. I kid you not, the bathroom is around 2 foot by 2 foot. And NASTY. The food is not edible. It is all deep fried crap. If you don't want to eat, or go to the bathroom, then this place may be for you.

My wife and I got the feeling that they couldn't be bothered with us.  We are 40+ affluent creoles with executive jobs and know how to behave in a restaurant.  The Caucasians were treated exceptionally well while we were ignored and our order was mistreated.  My wife order a Bloody Mary that was never delivered but the white people that came in after us received a Bloody Mary.  This went on with several tables were there were Caucasian people who were treated very well while we were not. It's a shame.  I want every minority who visits the French Quarter to know that they are not welcome in the Cafe Fluer-de-lis!!!

Not sure what the fuss is all about...the food was below average, service was great, the ambiance is ok, but the overall impression is underwhelming. 

We ate here on a not busy Thursday evening. There were 4 other tables. Our server was polite and friendly. Free chips and salsa. The salsa was quite garlicky which seems tasty today but might be harsh tomorrow. Ordered the cheese dip which was very bland. My husband thought it resembled melted Kraft cheese singles. 

We ordered the Yolandes and a combination  dinner. The Yolandes were described as chicken enchiladas. They were more like chicken tonga stuffed in a corn tortilla with very little sauce. There was very little enchilada flavor. The sauce was more like the garlicky salsa they provided. 

The combination was 1 taco, 1 chalupa, and rice and beans. The taco had a seasoned ground beef moxture, lettuce, and cheese. I did not care for the seasoned ground beef. It tasted a little more like picadillo, had an odd texture, and almost tasted perfumey. The beans were just ok and not well seasoned. The rice was average. The chalupa came with beans, sour cream, lettuce, tomatoes,  and guacamole. The guacamole was more like a guacamole sauce and I did not like the taste of it at all. 

The decor was fun and upbeat. Music was peppy. 

The rest of the hotel is scary and depressing. The bathroom was in shambles but the front desk manager was too busy sitting on the sofa and watching TV. He was probably too busy to be bothered by those details. 

We both ordered sodas. Total with tax before tip was $25.07. Too many better Mexican Restaurants in our area to make a return visit here.

Let me start by saying I am a big supporter of the local and small business community. I will agree with Kevin F, I really wanted to give them a second chance. The last time I was in I was there on a  Thursday, so I decided I would try a different day. I stopped in on Monday, forgetting that it was a holiday. ( That should not matter.) I knew I was in trouble when I pulled up and saw both managers / supervisors sitting in the detail shop. ( I assume they were managers as they had red shirts.) There should be no surprise here, as that is where they were the last time I was here. I decided to stick it out and see what happened. I would have to say the job was worse than last time. There was no sense of urgency to get my vehicle done, and the wipe down was very sloppy and incomplete, both inside and out. I could not communicate with the employees working on my vehicle. They did not seem to care. They seemed more interested in goofing off. Maybe a supervisor that actually was working would make a difference. But I guess loafing is more of a priority. This is a very unprofessional outfit. Your money would be better spent elsewhere. I am done with them.

Gail Gloria

The drive thru order taker had an attitude the whole time. She was very snappy. She got our order wrong, our bag was missing 3 of our sandwiches, just to find out she rung us up for the wrong sandwiches, which she was very rude when we told to manager about our whole order being wrong. She blamed it on us. Never apologized. Do not recommend anybody going here. Will for sure never come back.
We stayed 3 weeks in the USA. We ate several times in Denny's restaurants. This one was realy awfull. I should have read commentaries on YELP before ordering...

OK - here I am sitting at the bar along with 3 other customers and, it's lunch time. Where is our bartenderess? Not servicing us to be sure..

She is instead immersed in non stop texting!! Non stop!!!

This may be a clue as to why there are only 4 of us at the bar and very few people at the tables!!

Service...service...service...

I didn't have a good experience with this place. First of all it took over a month for my furniture to be delivered. I entered the store in search of a bed, mattress set, and TV stand. I didn't really like anything they had in store but one of the salesman show me a catalog that they had. So I ordered everything out of the catalog that was on March 13, 2017. After several calls my furniture was finally delivered on April 17, 2017. That was over a month. My salesman kept telling me it was taking so long because they had to order the furniture from a warehouse. So when the furniture finally came I didn't realize it did not come already assembled. So  the delivery process that I thought  would only take about 15 minutes and it ended taking almost 2 hours because the delivery man had to assemble all of the furniture making me late for work. So after the furniture was finally assembled, The delivery guy told me that they could not take out the trash so there I was with two humongous boxes and all kinds of debris all around my apartment. At the end of the day I am pleased with the furniture I purchased, but the journey to it was long and annoying.

Worst. Hotel. Ever. Would be OK if I had paid $35 for this room but paid $180 for broken sink, broken springs in mattresses, mysteriously wet bed sheets, right next to freeway, nails coming through popcorn ceiling, grumpy front desk woman, towels the size of a welcome mat and just as soft. Don't ever stay here.

This was a horrible experience. Although the restaurant was clean and our takeout order was prepared quickly (the restaurant was empty) the food was awful. We ordered Vietnamese spring rolls, beef lo mein and catfish with eggplant. The rolls were supposed to contain shrimp and pork, but there was no shrimp in either, and only one dry, flavorless scrap of pork in each roll. Nothing else but lettuce and rice noodles. My husband couldn't eat the lo mein, and the catfish was so over-cooked, it was chewy. The eggplant was simply inedible. If their goal was to prevent us from returning, they achieved it!  We will never go back. Too bad -- we were looking for a nearby good restaurant, and we'll have to keep looking. Frankly, I'd give it no stars, but the site won't let me do that!

I am a little confused by this place. I had asked around for somewhere to get take out, and someone directed me here. They have what looks like semi-old pizza on the bar (I didn't try that). I ordered a pizza and it took what seemed like forever. The pizza itself was just meh, we ended up giving most of it away to people on the street.

Tried the 2 taco platter on an  chilly, windy day in the spring.  The food was cold and disappointing.  The concept and flavors should be there but it was hard to get past the cold food.  However; dId not care for the fiest corn salad.

I was very disappointed but I'd be willing to try them again.  Next time...the seemingly popular curried sweet potato taco.

Ruth's Chris prices for Red Lobster quality. Went with the boyfriend 2 yrs ago and it was phenomenal; however, when we went for Valentine's this year it was NOT good. They forced a set menu on the guests (along with automatic 18% gratuity for a 2 person table) and while I understand it was to make the kitchen's life easier... the food was lackluster and full of things that we did not like. We have had their actual menu and it is nice however we paid $250 for a boring low flavor appetizers, overly salted food, and meh desserts. We have gone to red ox had the same amount of food with great flavor and only paid $150 tip included. Also, the service was pitiful he paid us no attention since he was guaranteed a tip.
Conclusion: DO NOT GO HERE FOR VALENTINE'S.

Indianapolis regularly lets me down. I come here every year or so, and outside of a few notable exceptions, the food and drink regularly disappoint.  I usually chalk it up to the rush of the convention.  But this time, armed with Yelp, I thought I'd try out some top picks. 

The Libertine failed. 

On the food, I was willing to give it a pass.  I had a spicy roasted squab, so far done that it would be impossible to distinguish from chicken.  Disappointing.  There was a mushy pulled pork sandwich at my table, that I had no interest in trying having seen it.  The Bacon Sampler, pretty darn tasty. Overpriced, but novel and tasty. I was rather impressed by the fresh oysters. 

But the drinks, major fail.  The whole reason for coming was the promise of a good cocktail or three. I got one. A solidly made standard, a Negroni.  The trouble starts with the second drink to come to the table. A rose colored Martini, that was supposed to be Negroni #2. I tried to send it back, and the server leaves and returns, nope that one was made just like the first she says.  Well, I'm certainly not ordering another one of those, and I ask to have the barman make his "best" drink.

This is a cocktail bar; a "Bartender's Battle", cut glass barware, small batch bitters kind of cocktail bar.  And I have just given the barman carte blanch to create and serve anything he likes, I'll pay for it.  You know what I get?, a Dark & Stormy. Rum and soda, ginger flavored soda mind you, in a glass.  'Nuff said, Fail. Do not pass Go, Do not Collect $200.  The bill was more like $300, but you get the point.  

Thanks Indy.

My DISABLED friend and I were just asked to leave  with his service animal! They said no animals in the store unless she has papers... but she HAS papers and is a service animal AND WE PRESENTED THE PAPERWORK TO HER TOO!!!! The woman who kicked us out is named Jamie Rush (At least that's what she says) a RUDE blonde! We presented her with our paperwork and she became very very snotty, told us we couldn't have our sevice animal in the store without a card...we have signed from the doctor PAPERWORK which is legit and this is disrespectful! We asked for the managers number, however she refused to give it to us and instead gave us the store number!!
I sincerely hope a manager or better yet an owner gets ahold of me about this! Not only was it rude and disrespectful it is sooo illegal to even ASK us any questions other than, is it a SERVICE ANIMAL, and does it have shots..
She could have been nice to us without an attitude!!! As a matter of fact attitude while all we wanted to do was get a fricking COFFEE!? I will NEVER return here without this FIXED... and my poor DISABLED friend who was ostracized and singled out in front of a crowd had leave... I wouldn't be a bit surprised if he seeks civil retribution! This goes against the Americans With Disabilities Act!!!!!

Let me start by saying I appreciate the investment they made in this part of town. I really, really want this business to do well. We decided to try out The Depot on a Tuesday night. We did not feel welcome from the time we walked through the door. We had four adults and two children. The door staff said there was only two places in the restaurant that could seat six. Turns out our seating of six was two tables pushed together. There were other tables in the room that could have been pushed together as well. That made no sense. After waiting for one hour and fifteen minutes we were offered two shots of corn whiskey for the long wait. We were not there to drink hard liquor and we passed. I had pork belly tacos which were under cooked. A burger on our order came out rare instead of medium. All of the staff including our waiter knew we waited eighty five minutes to be seated but offered nothing but whiskey to make it right. I felt as though we were bothering them the whole time. Food was undercooked and overpriced but the service was the worst part. The place is beautiful in and out but I fear they won't make it if management doesn't figure it out soon.

OK Here we go, I had a friend in town and after a long day of sightseeing we ended up on Hillsborough ave heading to Cuban Breezes for a late dinner. After finally reaching the location m we noticed it was super mobbed and figured we would try something less crowded.  We stumbled upon Sonny's on account it was not busy ( that should've been a big clue in itself) Nevertheless we entered the rather drab and outdated restaurant, in hopes that the food would outweigh the depressing decor. 

I ordered the All you can eat dry rub ribs that came with 2 sides which I chose the mac n cheese and baked sweet potato. 
My friend ordered the sliced brisket however the server said they ran out of brisket, so instead she chose the pulled pork,
We received our food within 15 minutes and everything looked OK until our server came again and informed me that they ran out of dry rub ribs.. OK this is a Friday night around 8pm and they are out of brisket and dry rub ribs already????  
To add to the situation ,my friends pulled pork was not good it was dry, any sauce we applied on the pork was like it repelled it. 
On the slight plus side the few dry ribs I did get were decent as was the baked sweet potato,
unfortunately I can't say the same for the Mac and cheese which rivaled the Kraft variety.
The only shining light here was our server who was very friendly,attentive and apologetic to the situation. 
I would not go back here , there are much better BBQ options in the area that execute it much better and don't run out of key menu items on a Friday night.

just Meh... People go crazy over this place and I have to say the one's in the Phoenix area are better the food is like a mashup of American and Middle Eastern. IF you think The Olive Garden is Italian Food then you may be OK thinking this is Middle Eastern. Every thing just tastes OK, that is about it. I wouldn't rush to go there at all. Too many other good restaurants to choose from.

Great looking place with tons of potential, but that's where it stops. We have been there several times hoping it would change, no such luck. On our last visit, the order was long in coming. When we finally got our breakfast, the eggs where cold, the yolks were hard, and the order was completely wrong.  

The tables in the outside area are falling apart and the area had not been cleaned or cared for. To bad, with some effort and upkeep, it would be a real winner. Sadly, we won't be back

I had ants crawling in the bathroom and all over the mat. My girlfriend stepped out of the shower and screamed because they were crawling up her leg while she was drying off. I scooped up the mat and took it to the front desk. I requested that the manager call me. I waited 2 days, called, and requested the manager call. He never called. I called a 3rd time and left another message. Needless to say, they overcharge and don't give a sh*t.

Just an ok place, typical so-so food, nothing to write home about.

I ordered omlette Fleur De Lis. Other than a handful of tiny crawfish tails, it is not that different from a regular cheese omlette for any diner in NJ. Same could be said of potato, and coffee. Not really worth the price tag. 

Service is slow, the waitress was busy chatting with another customer that I had to remind her that my food was ready and getting cold at the counter.

We waited an hour and a half for our food while we saw at least 3 rotations of tables get their food before us. When confronting the manager about this, his excuse was they were "busy". Not too busy to serve 3 rotations of tables before us I guess. Will never go back and food wasn't worth the wait. The best part? We both ordered sandwiches that could've been made in less than 10 minutes.

They've changed their policy! They want twice as much now to have a little shampoo on your car seat even if everybody's just standing around. spoke to the owner danny is sticking to his guns he has lost a customer

I took my suit to Sandy's because it's a  little shop in my neighborhood. NEVER AGAIN! If you're a "tailor", how is it possible to completely botch the cuffs on a suit jacket? I know people say this all the time, but I really could've done a better job. They did a terrible job and the worst part about it was it was my wedding jacket, they had a month to do it and they were a day late! I took the suit back twice more to have them fix their mistakes but ended just paying for it because they never got fixed and I didn't want to deal with them anymore. They were plenty nice but took rush measurements and didn't really listen to me. I ended up taking it to Brentwood Tailor because I know they're fantasatic. Lesson learned.

Ordered the three meat dish which is very big.

They drown everything in sauce so if there was any smoke flavored meat I couldn't taste it.

Rubs were mush...way over cooked. Brisket was good once I scrapped the sauce off of it.

Loud with the band too.

I write this hesitantly, because I am a beer fan and I want this place to do well. We tried this place because its a cool new brewery, and why not? 

I was really excited to try their different beers, only to find that they were out of many of  them, which means they didn't plan well enough to keep themselves stocked. 

The food was mediocre, and that is based on trying the two sausage plate, pork belly tacos, and poutine. The poutine was great, in my opinion but the main dishes were lacklustre. I wished more of the beers were available, though I am a big fan of their saison, the rest of the beers offered were just okay. (American Pale Ale and American Sour Wheat). 

Also, the server was slow to respond, we had to keep reminding them of what we ordered, and it was just not a great experience. I've experienced better.

On the pro side, the place looked amazing, I loved the modern decor, they kept some of the original building features in tact (like a bank vault in the mens bathroom), and the lighting and ambiance was superb. 

I think this is a great place to come back and have a beer and a few appetizers, and not so great a place to come to eat and be waited on.

Food is okay, not great or amazing. The lo mein tasted a little plain to me, the pork fried rice is also a little plain (very cheap with the pork and the rice is yellow), and the egg roll was a little plain as well. I mean this place is good if you're in a rush to get Chinese food but remember it's not the greatest Chinese food you will have.

Went to order 5 tickets for an IRT production and was hit with a $20 online handling fee. Called the box office and was informed fees would be HIGHER if ordered by phone and the only way to avoid the fees was to purchase in person (which isn't realistic for most people). This is unreasonable. IRT should be ashamed to charge a 10%+ handling fee for purchasing tickets!

This place is much more diner than cafe, i.e., not the cleanest place I've ever brunched.  I ordered coffee and eggs benedict with hash browns.  The coffee was typical diner coffee, watery but okay; the hash browns were good but super salty (and this is coming from a girl that loves a well salted meal); and the eggs benedict caused me the most inner conflict..  The eggs were poached perfectly but a little on the wet side, the hollandaise sauce was done quite well but the biscuit in place of the english muffin throws off the texture, and to add insult to injury, the ham they used was the super thin sliced lunch meat style ham, which was disappointing.

Stopped by for happy hour, and we were looking for a wine bar. They offer A'Dello wines from Montgomery county,a local product. The wines are tapped straight from the barrel. I started with the Pinot Noir which in my opinion was barely drinkable. I sampled a hand crafted cocktail that was pretty good however. My second wine was a blueberry type, that wasn't overly sweet, that I did enjoy. We had the cheese platter which was plated very nicely and had a good assortment of cheeses. It took forever to be prepared and brought to our table outside. The service was extremely slow. When seated outside I don't enjoy my time when I have to go inside for additional cocktails. I'm paying $8 for a glass of wine, I expect table service. As I mentioned, for my taste, the Pinot Noir was barely drinkable. I did enjoy the not so sweet blueberry. This place has a lot of potential. It's a great space with a good concept. I expected more with wine being $8 a glass. This isn't Philly. When we arrived there was one person at the small bar, and service was still slow. I would also expect if you are the only wine bar in town and you are empty at 5:30 on a Friday, offer some happy hour specials. $6 a glass is not unreasonable for a happy hour special. I just don't see the quality of the wine being work almost $10. Three cocktails and a cheese plate, with tip was over $50. Probably won't return.

They have a good reputation and their bagels and cream cheese are decent.  I just think that they are a bit over-rated.  When I was there yesterday, they only had 3 types of bagels, the tables were dirty and there were no napkins.  Yes, this was a bit after lunch, but if you're a bagel place, come on!  I ordered a non-toasted bagel and cream cheese for there and got a dark toasted (extra toasted) bagel and cream cheese.  When I said they needed to make me a new one, two out of three people behind the counter argued over who was right abut the order.  I told them to argue later and just make the bagel.  I pretty much got it tossed at my head a minute later.  Oh well.

If you go there, please take notice of the menu board.  Half of it is marked out with white masking tape.  That's pretty cheesy (no pun intended).  They make a mint from the Jenkintown community, so I am sure they can afford a new sign.

Pro: Good Bagels and Cream Cheese
Con: Dirty Tables (had to clean on at least 3 visits), lack of bagels, charging 95%  full price for day old bread and pastry, parking lot, old detritus such as event signs and newspapers everywhere.  Bagel slicer in package from the 80s for more than it would cost me to get one from Bed, Bath and Beyond.

I grabbed a Groupon deal to bring my fiancee here. While there weren't any completely unforgivable things here, there wasn't anything positive to note either (aside from some admirable attempts at recovering the situation by the server).

We arrived and were seated quickly. We placed our orders, me for the corn risotto, her for linguini with clam sauce. She'll write about her meal in her review, but there was an incident involving miscommunication from the kitchen regarding her dish.

Plating at this place is bizarre, to put it kindly. Partially cooked and raw vegetables were stuck into the dishes in an artful(?) fashion, to the point that I thought her linguini with clam sauce was a pasta primavera at first. I was honestly confused by the plating.

My risotto was pointless. Overcooked rice mush plated with the whole pallet of the aforementioned cooked, partially cooked and raw veggies, some soggy mushrooms, and a dollop of goat's cheese in the middle, with corn mixed through. At the end of the meal, I realized that the corn was either canned or frozen. This should ultimately have been obvious from seeing it on the menu, since this isn't exactly sweetcorn season. Which is why you have, you know, season menus.

I know risotto is a time consuming dish, and is sensitive to overcooking, but many restaurants manage to parcook their rice and successfully finish it to the right level without overcooking or taking forever.

I think the most damning thing is that I didn't finish my meal. This is a very odd thing for me, as I was raised to clean my plate unless I'm full unto bursting, which I wasn't here. 

Other than our meal, my fiancee had two glasses of wine, supposed to be the same. Not sure how they screwed it up, but the wines were completely different. The server, though, was a sweetheart and offered an on the house dessert for our troubles with her meal, and comped the erroneous wine. We also sat and watched a salad languish in the window for a timed 15 minutes while waiting for our food.

We passed on the dessert, as it appeared to just be a Sysco food service selection.

I think there was a reason that we were the youngest patrons in the place by between 15 and 20 years.
Weird tasting food, and a waitress who acted like she really didn't want to be there. Sassafras was much better in my opinion

I spent 3 nights at this hotel attending business training meetings. I have never been more eager to check out of a hotel in my life. Had my balcony been a few levels higher, I might have leapt from it in despair.

If your idea of a great hotel is navigating through hordes of loud children and gawking old people moving in slow motion (and at 62 I can say that) then this is the place for you. The masses that descend on this temple of corporate excess that Gaylord built seem mysteriously satisfied to be trapped  there, enjoying mediocre entertainment and food.

The place offers little value for the money. My corporate rate was $250. Drinks were $14 at the lobby bar. Dinner will cost you $30-$40 each, assuming you are prepared to wait 60-90 minutes for a table. Parking and resort fees on top of all that are exorbidant. And what do you get for all this?  A 20 minute one-way walk from your room to the convention center during which time you will encounter thousands of people milling about. Yes, the atriums are pretty and redeeming but one feels constantly like this is all so fake and inauthentic.

Staff and service are polite enough but they are overwhelmed with so many people to serve they cannot  even fake personal attention, relying on such things as smiley face buttons and consider it done phone lines.

Why any corporation or trade association would subject its business trade to such a place, stuck miles from the dynamics of Nashville (which is a very decent city) is beyond me. They built a 6 lane highway for traffic to reach the hotel and all 6 lanes lead to mass gridlock. One night I spent 2 hours on Briley Parkway simply trying to return to the hotel. 

Enough said. I guess I am a snob. I just dont get this place, dont understand the appeal. Thousands and thousands of others seem to find something at this mecca and that is fine. But if I were ever offered a free weekend there, all expenses paid, I would pass.

I'm eating vegetarian for navratri, and I figure what's a good place for vegetarian.. Indian!   How can it go wrong.  I ordered vegetarian biryani-- it was not good.  Greasy and bland.  I also got paneer tikka pakodas-- tasted like paneer nuggets.  Also bland.   Eh.

Bartender was unprofessional, touching her hair and face as she poured drinks and touched glasses and straws.  She tried to pass a tap beer off as a higher priced bottle because she didn't want to (in her words) "to go up and down the F...ing stairs again"  Server was kind, but unfamiliar with the menu and had to call another server over to then put her arm across me and read off the menu the description of an item I asked about.  Food was then ok at best and we left feeling like we would not return.  OK for the just turned 21 crowd who really doesn't care about service of quality but not for a grown up who does not want to feel like an uninvited guest who is interrupting the tv watching.

My boyfriend and I went to Luna Bella on Friday night.  We were on our way  somewhere else, drove by and decided to give it a try.  The front desk was very friendly.  As the hostess led us to our table she encountered a walkway blocked by a server so, she threaded us in and out of a few tables to get us to ours.  In doing this we had to sidle by a table of diners and as I squeezed by, one of the gentlemen went to put down his menu and his elbow came in contact with my leg.  It was not a soft bump for either of us and he turned and gave me a very angry look as I apologized.  What's my point?  The tables are too close together in the main dining room!

We were seated in a back room that looks out onto the intersection of Fort Lowell and Swan - well it should look out - all of the plantation shutters were closed up tight - giving the effect of being in a banquet hall- While an intersection isn't the best view - it would have taken some of the emphasis off the inside  - which was very "golden girls" retirement community dining hallesque.  There was also a very strong smell of fish, cheese or something malodorous.  I have to say I am not a big fan of restaurants that use carpet in their dining rooms - hardwood or tile are much more appealing from an odor and hygiene perspective.  We also noted they are using scented candles on the tables - while I would prefer glade to old fish - it's still a no no.

The menu was not quite as varied as I would have liked but, we settled on the fried artichokes to share, a rib eye for him and pasta bolognese for me.  I had a nice glass of Guenoc Pinot Grigio - and we waited for the artichokes.  We were served a large portion - which is often not a good sign.  The breading was pretty heavy, they weren't warm and the accompanying sauce was a bit bland.  I actually enjoyed the undressed mixed veggies on the plate more than the artichokes - yellow peppers, some radishes.

I was really looking forward to my pasta -the menu had a paragraph about the chef, Steve Schultz,  stating that he had worked and trained in Europe and even more specifically, Switzerland.  Having travelled and lived in Europe, I thought this was a good sign that I would get a proper bolognese.  What I did get was an enormous bowl - huge -monstrous - of pasta with huge chunks of sausage in it.  Bolognese when done right has a very fine mix of veal, pork, beef, carrots - no sausage.   There are probably a hundred different variations of bolognese, but I have never had it with sausage.  The menu stated "chopped beef & veal" - no sausage.  Anyway, it wasn't horrible or inedible but it also wasn't pasta bolognese.  It had so much sausage and such big chunks it seemed like some of the pieces were still pink inside eww.  I almost forgot to mention, some of the pasta was cooked stuck together and hard - pasta faux pas - not al dente.

On to the rib eye - my boyfriend thought it was great - the gorgonzola gummy goo, i mean risotto, it was sitting on was not liked by either of us.  

Conclusion - would I go back?  Not for dinner.  They told us they have happy hour in the bar and  am a lot less picky when I am getting things at half off.

It was just ok. For the price I expected more but not a win or a total lost. The buffet could use a bit more. Idk maybe it was a off day for the cooks cause I hear a lot of good things about this place.

I was drawn here by the prospect of the sausage and gravy over biscuits, a favorite when I'm in Pa.  Nice neighborhood haunt, plenty of room and a counter for those dining alone & overflow.

The place reminds me of my grandmas kitchen nice ambiance.  The waiter, very nice, upbeat and attentive, now for the food.

The menu very eclectic lox, burritos, and scrapple, hitting all the bases.  The biscuits (2) were way too sweet for this dish, the sausage gravy had a generous portion of sausage but the gravy didn't cover both opened biscuits. The coffee was hot but not distinct, they constantly  mary all the pots so it defeats the purpose of brewing small batches.  I would come back and give the scrambled and lox a try.  I'm sure it's a cool place to sit on the sidewalk tables in the summertime and watch the world pass by.   2 eggs, biscuits, gravy, & coffee $16.50, I guess there's a premium for the ambiance....
The views are stunning.  The restaurants within it are mediocre at best.  The boat tour is a canned tape that loops and loops. Really?
I like the interior... but, the food is overpriced and the owner is very chatty.  Usually, I come in for lunch and need to eat fast and get back to work.

Yeah, like other reviewers, I'm confused... This place has vintage in the title. As the first word. The selection is much more present day, but still not really stuff I'd want to wear. Lots of used brand-name T-shirts. And discarded bridesmaid dresses, but like, not funky poofy ones. They do have some cute things, and some well curated displays of trinkets and knickknacks, but I was really disappointed in the clothing selection. I honestly feel like you have a better shot at finding something cool at Goodwill. 

On the plus side though, the prices were very reasonable. Formalwear for 20 to 30 bucks. Friendly staff. Not saying I'll never go back, but it just wasn't what I was looking for today.

Okay, I'm going to be good with this as much as I can. I was met with someone who had to of smoked two cartons of cigs prior. The smell killed me. He was very nice, but should perhaps cut back a bit. The costumes for me, were taken down, a total of five, thank you. I took them back, they were missing the strings, some sashes, and I couldn't put on one of their costumes without a deficit somewhere. Was upset, as I could not dare pay to rent w/o half the costume missing stuff. When I went back to say something, no one was there. At this time, I figured I could buy the same costume on Amazon much cheaper. Sorry, I hate not going local, but you dug your own grave.

Not impressed, went here for dinner based on the awesome reviews, wont be going back. Coconut shrimp was decent but a little too "fried". Cranberry salmon was kind of bland, crab cakes were burnt, appetizer took forever to come out and we were served the wrong dessert, although the dessert was the best part!
Fast delivery but my spring rolls were oily.... not so good. Not sure if I'll try again.

Before the movie begins, I wanted to warn not to get seat E8 in auditorium 5 -- it's currently broken and I've never been so uncomfortable (outside of being on an airline). Next up is two hours of torture.

Normally we enjoy this restaurant but not today. After waiting for nearly 30 minutes for our food, my pizza finally arrived...it wasn't burnt, it was incinerated... The waitress offered a new one, but after a 30 minute wait, I chose to not eat rather then wait again. After we got the bill, we waited for a bit and decided to just pay the bartender and go instead of sitting around.. First bad experience here, but I think I'm done with this place.

I have to second what other Yelpers are saying.  This is not at all a vintage shop.  It is literally a Goodwill with higher prices and less organization.  The staff was friendly, but that's about all I can say in this place's favor.  If you're looking for true vintage or unique clothing, this is probably not going to give you what you want.  

Their pricing seemed very inconsistent.  While I was there I looked at a blouse I liked marked for 16 and then an overcoat (nicer in my opinion) marked for 12.  I didn't understand the pricing at all.  I won't be back.

most of the food is good, but wheb it comes bad, you cannot stand.

I thought it was good until I got my entree, they put a used lemon in my lobster, I love seafood with lemon, but I couldn't squeeze a single drop from the lemon I had!!! the worst thing was the waitress and the store manager lied to me, they said it was a fresh cut, f... u, I don't suggest anybody go here.

So I went here with my boyfriend on a Friday night to look at lights. We basically parked and were inside for less than an hour and parking cost us $24+tax... would never go again for that price. Ridiculous.

Gave two stars because of the appearance of the hotel.. It is amazing. But that is where it stops. I judge a hotel on the helpfulness and the friendly and welcoming attitude of the staff and this staff has none of those qualities mentioned here.. 

Boarder line rude and nonchalant attitude with no desire to make you feel welcome.. All that I met so far is non caring  and wanting a big tip. Got no help in unloading my bags from the taxi, the gal who check us in had no clue what she was doing because when I asked her a question she said "whatever".. Great response.. 
I hope I didn't make a mistake and could have saved money, time and been welcomed somewhere else..
Average.  Food could be better, especially for the prices.  Nice place inside.  Thought  the service was above average (in that they made an effort-a rarety around here).

So disappointed! This used to be my go to place when ordering sushi but it seems I am going to have to find another place. #1 spicy mayo is now extra money now which is not a deal breaker,#2 found a long hair in my spicy salmon roll and had to throw the rest of my sushi out, #3 like in a previous review the dumpling sauce is filled not even a quarter of the way full, #4 No more sushi lunch specials that I could find on the menu. I really wish I could just go back in time and order from somewhere else. Guess I will not be back :(
Cute and romantic little Thai place. The food was tasty but both my boyfriend and I felt sick immediately after leaving the restaurant. There is definitely better Thai food in the area.

Bad Chinese. Fried rice was not good. Mushy and pasty. 
Kung pao was okay but sauce was runny and thin. Hot and sour soup lacked flavor. Don't bother.

I have eaten at Marathon (this location) more times than I care to admit, and I have no earthly idea why I kept coming back.  I used to live and work in the neighborhood, and it is convenient, but looking back, there are SO many other convenient places that (1) serve better food, (2) have better service, or (3) both.

Really, there are two Marathons here, and they each deserve a review.  There is the weekday lunch Marathon and the weekday evening/weekend Marathon.  It's almost as though these restaurants are the alter-ego of one another, and just happen to share space.  Very strange.

During the week and the lunch rush, Marathon is not a bad choice.  They are crowded and rushed, but the food gets out pretty fast (and generally correctly) and the tables turn quickly.  To accommodate the largest number of patrons, they do have tables packed so tightly together that you can literally hear entire conversations from nearby tables, but it can be interesting to occasionally eavesdrop and hear about someone else's dysfunctional life for a change!  Weekday lunch is also when the "A-team" of servers tend to work.  The service is pretty good, despite the apparent chaos throughout the restaurant (I can only imagine working the kitchen line during a weekday lunch shift), and the food is at its peak (which while generally a plus, isn't saying much here).  I quickly learned to stick with basic stuff like sandwiches and soups (the Matzo ball rocks!), because salads (yes, salads) and more complicated entrees get messed up way too frequently.

Then there is the evening shift.  This is where things really come unglued.  If the lunch shift servers are the "A-team," then this is the "D-team."  Most of the servers we have had in the evenings and on weekends seem like they wound up working at Marathon because pumping gas was too complicated.  In the 3 years I lived nearby and ate an occasional dinner at Marathon (apparently I am just lazy), I don't think we ever had a meal where everything was correct in our order and we enjoyed good service.  More often than not, the order was wrong in at least one way (seriously, how do you mess up a Caesar salad?), or the waiter disappeared for 20 minutes, or the temperature in the restaurant was hovering just above meat locker level.  Sandwiches with the wrong meat (yes, really), salads with the wrong dressing, and entirely wrong dishes were delivered with apathy and discourtesy on a routine basis.  On the rare occasion that I was either particularly motivated or so pissed off that I decided to speak with a manager, the line was always the same:  something about the server or kitchen staff being new/undertrained/high/mentally deficient.

And yet we returned.  Again and again.  I think the last straw was when they were changing menus a while ago and diners at our table got different menus and the server tried to explain that neither of the menus was correct but that he would tell us what the kitchen could make that night.  I love a good walking menu at a red gravy Italian joint, but at Marathon it was just too much.

The good:  Matzo ball soup (as good as any New York deli)
The bad: EVERYTHING ELSE
The ugly: see "bad, the"

Check in 1/5
Stayed here with family of 5 a week ago.  The check-in process might as well have a TSA sign above because the lines are so long.  While standing in line, I was able to download the Marriott app, sign in, check-in, tell it I was arriving in an hour (soonest option), wait, and then find out my room was available.   All of this before I'd even made it within the last long rope line to the check in desk (probably 30 minutes).  I suggest you download the app and do mobile check in.

Room 2/5 for price
They stuck my family in the middle of a hall when the entire rest of the hall was a high school choir group.  We were right in the middle of a high school locker bay essentially.  Kids running up the halls, doors all open and social hour at all times of the day.  Finally quieted down at about 10:30pm.  The room itself is nothing fancy and subpar for a Hampton Inn.  The bathroom area was VERY tight.  The tub had permanent stains.  The coffee maker is under the sink and the only place to plug it in is the sink GFI because the cord is so short.  We found out that each room is on it's own single circuit because trying to make coffee and use a hair dryer tripped the breaker for the entire room.  We had to have maintanence reset the breaker, wait for coffee to be finished and then proceed drying hair.

The hotel 4/5
They charge a "resort fee" which is basically just a way to justify charging more for the "extras" whether you use them or not.  Just be prepared to pay it regardless of usage.  We knew this going in, but some people don't pay attention are are shocked when their bill is much higher.  Parking is $26 self and about $34 valet.  The signage has you drive all the way up to the valet area, wait in a line of cars to have an attendant ask if you want valet or self.  If self, you then U turn and backtrack 1/4 mile to park your self.  Almost everyone ends up U turning.  Just poor traffic management.  I guess they hope that by the time you drive all the way up and wait in the line of cars, you then say screw it and turn to the valet area.  You can park close, but there isn't a huge price difference. 

There is lots to see and do within the "resort".  The food is resort prices and there aren't a lot of really good options.  Don't expect great meal experiences.

In general 2.5/5
The allure of the Opryland is the spectacle of the resort itself and the proximity to the GOO, which is about a ten minute walk.   I will probably stay somewhere else next time because I just can't justify spending 50% more than comparable room quality.

So disappointed!  We used to love this funky and fun late night spot- the drinks are still creative, the ambience is still homey and funky, but the food... oh what a downward turn.  It seems as if the kitchen had given up. The calamari was waaay overcooked. The steak dish was once a thick and velvety sauce of juniper and coffee, but this time it came out as a juniper and coffee dry rub with a bad flavor. And a side of plain grilled radicchio.  The fish dish had a decent flavor, but the side it came with was sautéed bok choy mixed with whole edamame, with hard shells that needed to be peeled.  Not a good combination!
We still like the waitress and bartender, the DJ is still great, but sadly we will no longer patronize Suis Generis. There are too many places to get great food.

I was a bit disappointed. Ordered the spicy beef soup and it wasn't hot or spicy enough. The soup had a flavor to it I didn't like. Their iced tea was great. Like that you can customize flavors. The place is very clean and the staff are friendly. I would go back just for the tea.

I really hate to give bad reviews. With that being said, how am I supposed to give a good review if I can't even get served here?! 

I have been here three times, and only ONE of those times I've been able to find a table. The other two times the two of us walk around and cannot find a table or a seat at the bar. 

That usually wouldn't be a problem if there was a hostess to help in situations like these to give us a wait time. I wouldn't even complain as much if there was seating in the door area to wait for a table, but notta. Both times we've had to walk out and no one has tried to help us. 

The ONE time I was able to get served food it was just meh. Go elsewhere, especially on the weekends.

Cute place with nice patio but that's about all that's good. I waited for my friend for about 10 minutes no one even came over to offer a drink or water for that matter. Way over priced for what you get.

The hot coffee was on the lukewarm.  I don't  think this was a fluke since all the selections were this way.  Ate a bun that had nuts and a hard Carmel on top.  Flavor was good but The Carmel was  cooked to the candy stage and was impossible to cut with a knife.   Ambiance is mellow and the dining area would be great to meet groups of friends.

The fun part of a manicure and pedicure is trying out different nail polish colors. Unfortunately, many of the nail polish bottles here looked half to almost empty. This was a bummer. I noticed my nail polish selection was based on the amount available in the bottles versus my initial color preferences. Next to the till, there were plenty of brand new nail polish bottles to buy. However, I don't usually buy nail polish bottles at the salon. 

The lady who did my pedicure was nice. The pedicure tools looked like they were sanitized and clean. The manicure and pedicure set up looked pretty average. However, my main concern and turnoff here was an obvious breach in cleanliness that I witnessed. 

The lady who did my pedicure put on latex gloves prior to handling my feet. While my toenails were drying she was helping another customer pay at the till. I was grossed out by the fact she was still using the gloves she wore helping me! She handled the debit/credit machine while wearing the gloves. I know those machines aren't clean as a baseline but still! I think some people feel a false sense of cleanliness when they wear gloves. What other breaches in sanitation are happening here?

You need to wash your hands with soap and water before and after using gloves! Hand hygiene is especially important in a place where you're handling a large number of hands and feet. People don't want to get infections! We need reassurance that we're in a clean environment. 

I won't be back here and I don't recommend it.
Overpriced and not worth the hype. The food is okay, but nothing I couldn't cook at home. I don't enjoy spending 15 dollars on a meal if I don't leave full and I'm not wowed by it.
Music was amazing, sound was outstanding. The food not so much. Go to listen to the music but find another place to eat. Drinks were decent but service was not so awesome.

There are plenty of veggies and fruits here but not the best quality.  We always walk in eager to get a bunch of stuff to hold us over until a trip to reading but the quality is just not there.  You can find grapefruit, tangerines, various types of apples, etc. bok choy, baby carrots, spring onions, regular onions and potatoes, tofu, and a small section of various asian ingredients.  They even have hummus and olives.  If I'm in an emergency, like I just ran out of shin ramen and i must have it to nurse a really bad hangover, I would stop by here.

You said it Yelp, "Meh. I've experienced better." The man and I went in for a light dinner/dessert/wine experience. The wine selection was good, extra points for having my  favorite Sauv Blanc. When we asked to see the menu the waitress brought over a chalkboard and sat it on the booth. Unconventional but could have passed as sort of kitchsy/cute had everything else gone well. We ordered the cheese plate and the crab cakes for dinner. The cheese plate was small, I mean really small, with uninspired accompaniments. Not worth the price at all. The crab cake was mushy and bland. There were only two options for dessert - a chocolate torte and an apple tart. The waitress (who was great, very attentive and friendly), explained to us that a few other guests had complained that the torte was too dry but the chef was trying to fix it. As a general rule, I always go with the chocolate dessert, so we went for it anyways. She was spot on, the cake was awkwardly dry even after it was drenched in chocolate syrup and microwaved. We apologized profusely for not heeding the warning, but told her we weren't going to finish the cake. She took it off the bill without incident, a nice touch considering it was our own fault. I've learned two things from this experience - 

1. Chocolate syrup can not fix everything, even in generous amounts 
2. Stick to Rumours East

- The Girlfriend

Video games were fine. Great pinball selection. Bar is too small, and too expensive for what they offer. 
It's Z Bar price, without the kickass bartenders.

G to the H to the ETTO.  What the hell?  I felt safer on secluded 7-Elevens in the abandoned streets of Bangkok or HCMC at three o'clock in the morning.  I felt like Reno 911 was coming by at any moment to take me away in a body bag.  Anyways, don't go here at night...feels freaky to me, and I like to venture out into danger!  I'm sure things are different in the daytime...

This place doesn't have much of a selection anyways.  The place isn't well lit at night.  The RedBox wouldn't take promo codes.  The food items looked iffy.  The employee dude was cool enough though.  Maybe I gotta come back in the daytime...maybe?

I kinda hate to give this place only two stars because the food was actually quite good and the space is actually quite lovely...but I can't say that our overall experience was more than 2 stars.  We arrived early (around 5:30) on a Saturday night and decided to have a drink at the bar before our dinner reservation. You'd think with an empty bar that the bartender would be happy to see customers. In fact, however, the bartender made us feel like we were an inconvenience. He was not at all friendly or welcoming and seemed annoyed about answering our questions about the cocktails. Not a good start. 
Our waitress was nice, but seemed totally clueless. I would expect a place this fancy to have a very professional/knowledgeable wait staff.  It seemed like this was her first wait job. There was another waitress there who seemed to know her stuff. 
Before dinner they brought us each a chocolate covered strawberry, which seemed very weird to me. I mean, know sometimes the chef will send out a little something or other (a little soup for example)...I forget what that's called. But a chocolate covered strawberry was just weird. I saved it and ate it after my dinner and it was delicious. 
The scallop appetizer was also delicious, but was not at all what the menu described. 
One of the reasons we went to this restaurant is because they feature grass-fed beef. Both beef specials were tomato based and quite similar sounding. My husband has an allergy to tomatoes, however, so we were a bit dismayed. Luckily, the chef was able to defrost a steak for him and it was quite good. I had a steak salad, which was also good. 
The flourless chocolate tort was also very good...but again, not as described on the menu. 
By the time we finished up, it was around 7:30...and the huge place was still basically empty...there were only 4 tables including ours...and they seated us all in the same room. I don't know if that was done purposefully to make it not feel so empty...but it was kind of weird because the room was fairly small...although the place is very big. 
In my reservation, I noted that this was our anniversary dinner. Any other time we've gone out for our anniversary, the restaurant has given us a little something...like a special dessert or glasses of champagne. I mean, I don't necessarily 'expect' it...I had only mentioned it in the reservation so we would get a nice romantic table. But truthfully, I was a little disappointed that they didn't make a little special effort...especially given how slow they were. 
Honestly, I don't see how they are staying afloat.  I'd like for them to do better because the food really is good...but a dining experience is more than just the food.

Cupcakes were okay. I got red velvet and a gluten free chocolate cupcake. The service however was poor. I walked in and the young girl who was working didn't acknowledge me. There was nobody in there so it was a little awkward. Finally I asked what type of chocolate ones they had. She said they were out. No other suggestions were made and the I pointed at the cupcakes that were sitting on the side of the wall and asked if they were chocolate flavored. And she said "yes they're gluten free." Uhhm okay... Then I mentioned that it was my birthday month and that I saw their website displayed a free cupcake during your birthday month. And she did confirm the special but added that she did not know how to process that. She didn't try get any help or even offer to have the owner call me. I ended up paying for both cupcakes. I'm not sure I'll be coming back here.

I was excited to find this place on my way out of town on a recent trip to New Orleans.  Has a great hippie vibe and was pretty packed. The menu looked great, it was crowded, it looked like exactly my kind of place. And it was. Kind of.

There were four of us dining. Three of us ordered the banana peanut butter pancake, which sounded delicious and while it was pretty good, it was also the size of a hubcap. A really, really large one. 

My problem with this is that they have to know that most people that order that can't possibly eat the whole thing. Which is fine, whatever. But to present yourself as a place that is kind of hippiesh and earth friendly, it doesn't settle well with me that it wasn't  mentioned when three of us ordered this that one would have been more than enough. It's like they know they're going to waste a bunch of food, and they're okay with that because hey, the more giant pancakes they sell, the more money they make. It just seems a bit,  I don't know, misleading.

All in all, the food was decent, but not outstanding like I had expected, and the pancake thing really turned me off.

My family and I stopped at Cold Spring Tavern on our way to vacation in Solvang. I imagined this was place was quite the Yelp discovery, but was left wanting more. 

The day we visited, it was pouring cats and dogs! However, I would say that it helped the rustic feel of the experience. The parking was simple - on the street wherever you can find room. Upon entering, my family and I were left sort of standing around waiting for someone to help us. The employees were going back and forth from the bar, but no one greeted us. Finally, we were sat in the front of the restaurant by the fireplace. 

Apparently, my family had just missed a huge rush of customers and they were understaffed. How do I know? The servers felt free to complain loudly about it every time they passed each other. Ultimately, the servers decided that enough was enough and decided to turn away customers earlier than their lunch's closing time of 3 PM. My family came in around 2:15 PM and they started turning customers away around 2:30 PM.  That was a bit unprofessional for my taste. I would have been very disappointed to make a special trip onto the mountain roads in the rain only to be turned away! Even the cook came out and questioned why he stopped receiving orders and laughed when they mentioned they were done for the day.
