The food we received was decent, but I have definitely had better. Perhaps the storm affected their heating because both my soup and the hot water we asked for were cold. Our server did not come around besides delivering the food, so we did not get a chance to ask for them to be heated. The BBQ sandwich I ordered did hit the right spot so I can't complain there.

PROS:
Ambiance/Uniqueness
Location

CONS:
Food Quality/Temperature
Staff Quality
Deviation from posted closing times

Overall, I think Cold Spring Tavern is worth visiting for the ambiance alone. The tavern will give you a nice break from reality, but the food may leave you wanting more. Hopefully, Cold Spring Tavern's staff can work on their demeanors to create a better experience for future customers.
very unusual for a chipotle - all but one server/ attendant was unmindful! They kept talking among themselves instead of serving the folks in line

This Resort Hotel was slightly disappointing. While beautiful, there were definitely some flaws. The Thanksgiving buffet at Ravello was priced extremely high and the food was only mediocre and actually a bit cold. One major disappointment was the fact that they advertised SmartTVs that had access to Netflix, and Netflix did not work! It kept stopping and reloading which made the viewing experience difficult to enjoy. It ruined a family tradition, and when we asked the front desk for help, all they did was unplug and replug the TV and it did not make a difference. My family and I spent 3 nights and 4 days at the resort and I would not do that again! Overall, my experience was not the best and I don't think I would recommend my friends and family to stay at this resort.

got food poisoning right after I left the restaurant  made a reservation a few hours before and they didnt have us down so we couldnt sit at a table for date night and instead sat at the community tables. Overall disappointing because it was over-priced for what you got :(

Just visited today and the I've never seen such sad animals kept in dog cages..the poor liger kept in a big dog pen and it's only shelter is a tin dog house wich had to be super hot in the summer..the sign says it's very sociable and loves water but is kept by himself and his pond is all dried up..

This is not a flea market, this is an upscale bazaar disgusing itself as a flea market. The price for many of the objects there are way over priced (who is going to pay 100 bucks for a flannel shirt from the 50s?!) and there were hardly any vendors worthwhile. They do have a decent food option, so that was really the only plus. I rather go to the Vintage/Antique Flea Market at Spring Garden because I find more interesting treasures than I would here. Sorry guys, but this "flea market" is not for me.

Sooo I wish I would have checked reviews before placing my order.. Everyone seems to love the bagels from what I've read I made the mistake of getting a egg and cheese croissant.. well croissant was perfectly delicious, but the sandwich yuck! They put slices of ice cold American cheese with no melting, did melt for me after I returned the sandwich and asked. Tiny place with minimal seating. Cashier was friendly and personable. 

I may give this place one more try and go with out of the "bagels." But for now, my review stands...
I'm am Puerto Rican and the food here is nothing like the food back home, you are selling "Latin" food with Soggy Tostones and Pasta Alfredo..? Not good not good

Not up to chipotle standards. As a whole, I love chipotle but this location is subpar. They are always out of something. Yesterday it was medium salsa, chips and hard tacos. Two weeks ago no brown rice, veggies or ice. The time before that no lemon or veggie. Before that, no steak or chicken. 

In the last 6 months I haven't been here when they haven't been out of at least two things. For such a limited menu, it is very impactful when they are out of items. And worst of all, there are no signs ahead of time so you wait through the line to the door to find out you can only get half of what you want. I've reached out to corporate as this is the only location I have these issues with, but nothing's changed. Go to the West Chester location or Taco Maya. Won't go back.

Breakfast was swing and a miss for me.  I want to like this place so much, but despite being a diner with a huge focus on breakfast, I think they shine better at dinner.  Service was ok, nothing special, and kind of slow.  The quality of the food was average to slightly below average.  My hubby's grits were downright nasty. They were watery sludge.  

It's so close, reasonably priced, and I loved dinner the one time I went to a different location.  I'll be back to try them for dinner.  Fingers crossed!

Someone told me this place had the best burgers in Philly. Apparently, Copabanana also shares this opinion about itself, based on its advertisements. Curious, I decided to give it a try, ordering a burger, Spanish fries, and a peach margarita. 

Like some other reviewers, I am also intrigued by the burgers. Although I ordered mine medium rare, my burger came out gray in the center instead of pink. They had literally cooked the **** out of it. No wonder it took 45 minutes for the food to arrive. It was like a piece of cement, sandwiched between two pieces of crumbly, stale buns. 

Onto the fries... This may be a matter of subjective taste, but I tend to prefer my fries crispy instead of soggy and oily. These so-called "Famous Spanish Fries" were basically a chewy mass of under-cooked soggy potatoes topped with melted cheese, peppers, and onion. Yummy. 

However... my peach margarita was awesome (hence, the two stars instead of zero, if that were possible). It was pricier than a margarita one would get at fancier restaurants, but if you live a block away and have a sudden/urgent margarita craving, this place will not disappoint. Just stay away from the food.

Friendly service but the food was very unsatisfying. The angel hair pasta arrived as a solid mass of pasta and marinara. My wife and I each took a couple of bites then pushed our plates away. I don't think we would eat here again.
We tried the burgers, curds, onion rings, fries an even the ice cream. All tasted average and fried items were greasy. Not worth the hype - disappointed.

I've had better. Of course when I went, I was like well, I definitely have to get the Prime Rib. Got the prime rib and it was just okay. I ordered it med rare, and it came out med rare but it just did not seem to have much flavor.  They loaded it with salt and pepper but thats not how I want me prime rib.

I think that for the price, it could be much better quality. Service was very good, but the tables are way too close adn I could hear eveything someone was saying right next to me. Not somewhere I would spend for a loved ones birthday again. We couldnt even enjoy eachother's company. Dissapointing!

I notice a lot of these begin with "I'm new to sushi".  Just to be clear, I am not new to sushi.  I've been eating sushi heavily for about 2 decades, on both coasts, throughout the Midwest (don't laugh, some of the Midwestern cities have large Asian populations and fantastic sushi) and in Japan.  I make my own sushi (usually chirashi, because, well, lazy, plus love that style of rice).  And I simply like to eat raw food, including steak, so I'm finnicky about freshness.

Mizu looked like it would be a nice spot to hit in St Louis; easy to find, and as a city-center restaurant, presumably pretty good to compete.  But it turned out to be pretty much all location.  I wouldn't go back, especially with so many other nearby options.

First, let's start with service.  Yes, they were absolutely understaffed.  Although the place was barely full (we were the only ones waiting) it still took a long time to get people their bills, get the table cleared, and get us seated.  Then, it took even longer for our waiter to get to us.  For some reason, he decided the girls at our table were in charge of ordering, and, after getting only part of their orders, scurried off....without even checking with the guys.  This sort of uncoordinated, fragmented, inefficient back-and-forth continued the whole time....in the end, we had orders we didn't make arriving at our table, orders we did place not showing, and absolutely no effort to keep us all eating at the same time.  

They skimp a bit on the food; standards like miso and salad weren't included, but hey, fine.  Tea was not replenished.  We ordered a few different appetizers and meals; because there was no tempura appetizer, we got that as a meal (appetizer quantity, plus rice).  Pretty much anything deep fried tasted fine.  Not mind-bending, but fine.  The pepper-based appetizer was lovely.

But you come to a sushi restaurant for some high quality fish and unique, tasty maki.  This place had neither.  The maki were bland, no unique flavoring even with unusual names and descriptions.  The sushi...well, I got my usually chirashi, and I wish I'd stuck to deep fried, because that might have covered the low quality of the fish.  Oh, one or two types were good...but the majority were mediocre.  Squashy and stale-tasting, with that crumbly texture that suggests freezing and freezer burn; the salmon tasted washed out, the tuna was definitely going off, and these are high-use sushi mainstays; of all the fish there, these should have been the types least likely to be sitting for the week it tasted like they'd been sitting.

How would it compare to my local sushi?  It had nothing on the Quad Cities area's two  top competitors, Shogun and Osaka, in terms of quality and freshness, despite beating them on price by about 25-30%.  Sad to say, it didn't even exceed my expectations for one of the QCA's older sushi restaurants, SuperTarget.  Yeah, that's right, SuperTarget outdoes this place on everything but style; they have an onsite chef who takes personal pride in his work and his fish, and I've almost never had less than fresh offerings there, although nothing so stellar as our actual Japanese restaurants.  The atmosphere leaves something to be desired, but it's a freakin' SuperTarget; the service is better.

In the end, the hostess came over to apologize, but it was waaaay too late.  And too little; I had a minor misorder issue at Taki in Des Moines, and despite it being a busy night, that restaurant's head chef took a personal interest, sent us over the best fish they had in that night, treated us to another appetizer and a round of drinks, came over to apologize personally, and did an impromptu round of sake bombs with us.  And we hadn't even been kept waiting long, had other food in the interim, and might never have realized our order hadn't come on time.  We essentially never stopped eating (especially once the free appetizers started showing up)...we just weren't eating in the order that chef felt best represented his restaurant, and he expected better from his staff.  He took so much pride in his work that, while busy, he noticed an issue and corrected it before it was an issue to us, and he did it in such a way as to make me supremely confident of good service and sushi at his restaurant.  (I will say that for take out, I favor Appare's chirashi.  It just...doesn't...end.  And has multiple types of preserves/ salads (octopus and seaweed).  And more sushi than even I know what to do with.)

Mizu is probably a fine place to go to be seen, hang out at the bar with a cocktail, and pretend you're into sushi, if that's still a reason to pay for a high-priced dinner and lousy service.  But if you actually like sushi, I wouldn't recommend it, and if you're just starting out on sushi, absolutely not!  you'll never eat it again.  Wait, more for me.  Yes, newcomers to sushi, you go there.

Food was B- at best. Not very busy and our waitress stood behind the bar chatting with coworkers. Refilled water once fails to turn our appetizer in until we reminded her and the she blamed kitchen staff. It was chips and salsa not hard. Very unimpressed and I had high hopes

These 2 stars are only in regards to the prices for less then impressive food. Yea, I saw the low rating but I was craving seafood and my homie's mom suggested it...so I took a leap of faith. The ambience was very date night-esque and the staff was oh-so friendly as well as eager to help. It's relatively easy to find and located under what I think used to be a train station...but if I'm ever back in St Louis, I won't bother going a 2nd time...nor will I suggest it to anyone.

I started with their Autumn Sangria because of it's inviting description...then I tasted it and my taste buds were invaded with cinnamon and apple. It would have been a little more pleasing to the palate if the flavors were more balanced. Imagine a great sparkling apple cider...and when they went to add a dash of cinnamon, the top came off...or they got distracted while shaving the cinnamon stick lol Instead of starting over, they added more apples. smh Autumn overload. 

Next, we ordered the seafood stuffed jalepenos which, like the sangria, sounded like a great idea. I imagined classic jalepeno poppers stuffed with shrimp, crabmeat and cream cheese goodness. I slowly tried one, you know, to try and appreciate the combination of flavors....it just didn't do it for me. The jalepenos weren't breaded enough and I think they overall forgot the seasoning. Jalepeno poppers of any kind are supposed to have a balance of a crispy outside with a warm and creamy center, while somehow holding together once you bite into it. The one I had...mehhh not so much.  This is where the great customer service came in because our waiter ( I wish I could remember his name) was very apologetic and gave me a nice list of options that I could trade the jalepenos for. On top of that, the manager came out and she was equally apologetic and provided us with bacon wrapped shrimp, free of charge for the inconvenience. I adore bacon and I adore shrimp...for some reason, Landry's attempt to put them together failed in a major way and no more then 2 pieces were eaten between my homie and I. But, because they were so nice about the whole thing, I didn't even bring my 2nd disappointment to their attention...I figured maybe the entree will compensate and didn't want my complaints to create a cause for "special sauce" in my food lol 

I ordered the shrimp 'n' grits with caesar salad as a side and he ordered the seafood jambalaya pasta. There was a slight issue with his order where they ran out of the pasta normally used in the dish; they gave him the option of rice pilaf or spaghetti pasta and he chose the spaghetti. Both came hot and in a timely manner...but just like the appetizers, it was swing and a miss. The salad was on point but that isn't saying much; it's hard to F up something as simple as a caesar. Now, don't get me wrong...the shrimp and grits were ok but with so many different components incorporated, I should be wow'd by bold flavors...both entrees were severely lacking. On top of that, the bill was like $60! When you're on a budget, paying $60 and still leaving hungry is some Grade A bull. Homie's mom absolutely appreciated the leftovers the next day for lunch, so money wasn't completed wasted...but still!

I really want to like this place, especially because of how accomodating the staff was...bless their hearts, they tried so hard lol Unfortunately, Landry's earned their low rating.

For a sushi place in Montecito, I was expecting MORE. All the employees were very nice and accommodating. I noticed they were quick to clean tables once customers left BUT they are definitely understaffed. My boyfriend and I came by during my lunch and it took 25 minutes (after he ordered) to get our first roll. The spicy tuna was also not spicy at all and it had a really weird consistency. It kind of made me sad considering I work 5 minutes away and when I need a "sushi pick-me-up" I was hoping this would be my "it" place. I'm super disappointed...

I love BBQ, especially being a born and raised Texan. Since moving here this summer, I drove past this place several times and have seen cars here and there. I have yet to find the best BBQ in the state of Florida. I decided to really try this place out. Walking in it felt comforting, reminded me of a country cafe which was cool.  One of the servers handed me a menu, didn`t really help me out just kind of starred at me weird. Another server asked me if I had questions about the menu, I told her I was new to the state and she gave me some really good recommendations ( I really liked her). The menu is tad confusing, but I ended up getting a lunch plate (I guess that`s what you call it), lol. I got sliced brisket (lunch portion), mac n cheese and a baked potato. Okay now here is where the stars started going downhill...... Brisket (yes I know being a southerner and from texas, brisket is the star). This brisket was very soft, very tender, very salty which I did not like, and very dry. I prefer my brisket very moist and with a good amount of seasoning. Mac n cheese was pretty good, the macaroni was kind of soggy but overall still pretty good. Baked potato was just your average joe, nothing special but very very small. I guess it`s true what they say in Texas, everything is bigger in texas. Be in reminder I don`t eat a lot but when it comes to BBQ, I expect a lot. Another reason why I gave it 3 stars was bc I didn`t like how the original togo person just threw my stuff into the bag. My sauce and everything was on the bottom and my boxed food was on top. I don`t expect a fancy bag or anything and i`m not that picky but come on, when you are handling items in that kind of industry your not only representing yourself but your also representing the company. Lastly I left a tip for the server that original took my order because she was so nice and such a great help, she wasn`t present when I signed my  slip so I told the original togo girl can you please make sure that this server gets this receipt, she kind of gave me an attitude and rolled her eyes. I felt like overall that was disrespectful. I probably won`t eat here again personally. But overall experience it was okay.

Being that you have to sell a kidney to see a movie at most places these days, there are many worse options in the city than the Bridge.  Some things that are good include: Location (by el & bus routes for those who heel toe express it like myself), concession selection (even though the prices are obscene, pretzel bites rule) Skip it and buy some goodies at WAWA before the show and add some danger to your life "sneaking" them in , atmosphere (have not had any movie narrater  experiences, something Philly seems to excel at), bathroom cleaniness.  Now the bad...  Staff , at the concessions, is as slow and uncaring as it gets.  I know you all probably make close to minium wage but have some pride in your job, be happy you have one.Cleanliness in the theater.  On more than one occasion did I step on something on the floor from the previous show.  Did I mention the prices?  Unless it is a movie you a dying to see, get Netflix and stay home.

Disgusting donuts. They all have the same horrible, artificial almondy flavor, with a rancid aftertaste. Weird, weird, taste, nothing like a donut. And WAY too oily. Tastes like low-quality artificially flavored sadness.

The chicken is pretty good though. I'd go back for the chicken and coffee.

AVOID THE DONUTS!

Never been to cafe but Carmel has a Huge amount of restaurants.  Decided to come in and try a biscuit with gravy, pancake, 2 eggs with coffee.   I could not believe the cost of my breakfast!  I've never paid $20 for breakfast and found myself disappointed in the food because the cost left a bad taste in my mouth! 
I recommend to give it a try, just stick to your menu selections so your not surprised by the added in cost.  The waitress and cook were very friendly.  However, I'll stick to a equally as good place elsewhere for half the price.

After hearing from neighbors that they really enjoyed their experience at revivals we decided to check it out for a mid week meal. My husband enjoyed his vegetable and chicken wrap. A variety of grilled vegetables with chicken, he said it tasted very good. I ordered something called a seafood chimi. It was gross. I was expecting seafood mixed with burrito like items, beans tomatoes etc. It was not. It was white fish and shrimp mixed together in some sort of sauce inside of a burrito fried. On the side was some pale colored iceberg lettuce, a handful of canned tomatoes and 2 tablespoons full of shredded cheese. The whole thing just tasted fried, and had no distinguishable flavor. There was a string quartet that was performing, which was very nice. We enjoyed listening to them as we ate. The servers were very attentive and nice.

I came in on Wednesday for the first time to try out pita jungle. I'd heard good things, so I was excited. The service wasn't that great from the beginning. The hostess was smiley and nice but after that it declined. Our waitress was cold and very unfriendly. At this point I was still hopeful to try the food. Unfortunately, I've been sick ever since and can't keep anything down. I ordered a falafel wrap and potatoes as a side. The food was just ok and def not worth being sick over. Will not return.

i wouldn't necessarily consider this a 3 star hotel but i only stayed for 1 night and didn't have a problem. 

the room was clean. decor is drab. the walls are thin- you can hear your neighbors talking or when they turn on the blowdryer, you can hear when cars come and go and your balcony door shares a common area where people can walk right up to your door. location wise, you do have to drive at least 2-3 miles out to get anywhere. 

if you're only visiting for a weekend, i would suggest splurging and getting a hotel located right on state st. in downtown, so that you can walk everywhere day/night.

I took a date to Maria's about a month ago for dinner (6/10/16).  I've eaten here before, about a year ago, and remembered it being good enough to deserve a return trip.  

Swing and a miss.

It was a Friday night, but pretty early, so the restaurant wasn't packed at all.  I was immediately struck by how the hostess was seemingly dressed for the gym, while everyone else was dressed like they worked at a restaurant, but that's not really what this review is about.

We ordered one of the appetizers on special--a stuffed mushroom...thing.  It was supposed to be portobello mushrooms stuffed wish cheese and spinach, with some balsamic glaze.  Instead, we received two stuffed mushrooms absolutely *swimming* in lukewarm balsamic sauce.  You couldn't taste anything except the sauce, which was a shame because I LOVE mushrooms.  

I also made a second mistake--ordering one of the special entrees.  This was shrimp and lobster in a spicy diavolo sauce over hand-rolled pasta.  The dish I received was ridiculously overcooked.  Both the shrimp and lobster were long-since rubberized, and I had to actually struggle to get the lobster out of the tail.  The fact it cost $32 just added insult to injury.  This wasn't a steak done medium instead of rare--this was two types of seafood cooked for so long that they no longer bore any resemblance to what they should be.

I will say that my date's dish (which was off the regular menu) was decent enough, though nothing spectacular.  Hence two stars.

I may give Maria's another shot at some point in the future, but one thing's for sure--I'm definitely NOT going to be trying anything from the specials menu.

The chef was angry and you could hear it,  the staff did their best to put smile on face so credit to them:)  the food was not properly cooked for example my wife ordered poached eggs 1 was perfect and the other was hard.   
I ordered scrambled eggs and they were runny:(  that is difficult to mess up.   

the hash browns were awesome
and the staff did their best 
but plenty of better options avail.

Went here on Sunday to attend an album release party so I was not dining in (and from the other reviews, I should count my blessings!) In the back of the restaurant through the doors is a really nice, intimate venue. It's ashame the bartenders suck... The bar was extremely crowded so I understood the long wait, however generally if there's and event , most places will staff more than one bartender. Its a novel idea, but it just might work! 

So after my friend and I got tired of waiting we went to the.main bar and the chick bartending had the nastiest attitude and horrible customer service. She proceeded to walk past us twice and serve other customers eventhough we told her we'd like to order, and the other patrons came up after us. So we went back to the slow bartender. And after he took 25 mins to get our order and spent another 20,mins reading the ingriedients on the menu to make our carribean punches we got our drinks in less than an hour. Based on that experience I would NEVER patronize the restaurant plus the parking situation is atrocious. Sux too cause the drink actually turned out good...

Super disappointing. After standing in the rain to get in, the food was not good. My husband ordered the 2 egg breakfast with bacon and hashbrowns. There was one slice of bacon and the hash browns were bland and watery. I got the shrimp and grits. The shrimp had a really good flavor but the grits were really bland. I have to believe that the kitchen was having an off day because of all the positive reviews. However, based on our experience we won't be back.

Food was good. Price are ok. Weird thing is that they have plenty of empty tables but the wait was 25 mins. We got seated and I asked for a different table, I was told that if I sit somewhere else it's a hour and had before I get served, Guy at the reception desk had no manners and rude. Borderline very aggressive. Food is good but I will never go back because of the weird arrangement and wait time.

I've eaten here a lot a because the kids like it.  I'm underwhelmed.  The food is good - we get the burgers, chicken tenders, and I get the Gardenburger.  The food quality is consistent, and everything gets to the table hot.  The service is almost always very slow.  Tonight the place was only half full and we waited about 45 minutes for the food to arrive.  The staff isn't great either.  They seem to disappear when you need them, or seem completely disinterested.  The hostess tonight took a while to acknowledge us even though we were standing right in front of her.  Sometimes it doesn't seem like the place is very clean either.  The tables  feel greasy and there is food on the floor. So...good food but 2 stars for the staff and cleanliness.

Fun place for bowling, and very well laid out. Very poorly managed though. What manager would ever schedule a new person (2nd day on the job), to work by herself on a Saturday afternoon. I didn't see any other servers in the entire area of 20+ lanes. Management should be ashamed. The girl did her best, but there needs to be more servers.

Once upon a time, Companion was my number one lunch spot in St. Louis. But since they closed their CWE location, I'm forced to drive to their Ladue location. And, let me tell you...it's just not the same! The menu has changed (for the worse IMO), the service is consistently poor, the prices are in no way fair. Also, on multiple visits I have spotted a mouse trap in the main dining room (empty, but still unappetizing)...The iced tea, cornbread and variety of soups are the only things that keep me coming back...well, and the memory of Companion's glory days in the CWE. So, Masochist's: if you're looking to choke down pretentious $13 salads and like eating at wobbly tables, Companion is your best bet!

Super fast but not that great. Oil used to fry tempura was old. Tuna not bright red, more dark indicating it is not that fresh. Gyoza was boiled and soggy.  House salad ginger dressing good. Would not return. Would rather seek out better options.

Unsure about the previous reviews but I will say the decor was great. I had high expectations from this but l was soon  disappointed unless you have had true authentic Caribbean food then you have nothing to go off I'll start with the beef patty which was very good and Flaky but the oxtails tasted more American then Caribbean they did not have the brown stew sauce then they were tough.   nice people nice decor but Americanized food

Had dinner with a private party of about 30. This was my first visit and the menu that was prepared for us and the room were both very nice. Somehow, though, the waiter was particularly attentive to some members of my party but definitely not to me. Of the four courses (wow, a lot of food) my dishes somehow always looked like a mess or simply lame.  It made me reluctant to eat.

I may give it another try sometime but there are so many places in Tucson that offer great service.  Personal touch always trumps food, in my opinion anyway.  For the first time ever I was tempted to leave no tip - lucky for him they included the gratuity.

Eehhhh. I wanted this to be better than it was. My niece was excited to visit an IHOP in the city. Food was poorly assembled and not as ordered. Service was mostly polite but kind of militant at times and neglectful at others.

About the food:
Hash browns were grossly packed with oil.I got their new specialty item of waffle croissant which was supposed to be crunchy light and fluffy. The reality was nowhere near that. It was flat, chewy, gummy, and some of the worst food I have ever given to me in my life. I politely said it wasn't crispy and asked if she would bring me two pancakes instead, so I could put the filling and stuff on those if desired. She insistently refused and then took the entire plate away from me and then brought me two pancakes with a bunch of the ingredients that were supposed to be on my croissant waffle thing, but there was way too much of some ingredients like caramel sauce and almost no banana so it was intensely over sweet. Plus, my pancake was really dark brown and awful. 

The bacon was requested extra crispy and came out mostly soft and floppy.

I would never come back. The next day we are at The Ruby Slipper and it was *amazing*

Been going here for years...will be rethinking that. My daughter went to pick up an order and was treated very rudely by one of the whiskey and cigarette "broads" that work there. A MAN came in behind my girl and they jumped to help him, gave him a free drink and just my daughter stew. There are just too many choices for them to behave badly to long time customers.
Keegans; here we come!

I live at Oak Lake in Indianapolis, IN. I have been living at this location for about four years now and I recently decided to renew my lease again and transfer to a bigger unit. In the process of me changing units there has been lack of professionalism, poor communication, as well as unorganized  staff. I have been inconvenienced so much over the past two weeks trying to get the staff at the office to properly prepare the unit I am moving in. I was being charged for their painters to come out and paint the unit a color I requested but the process the painters spilled blue paint all over the carpet which the office was unaware of. I notified Rhonda the property manager who promised to have the issue resolved by Monday December the 28 by 9 ambecause I had all my moving appointments set for 8 am. The carpet cleaner didn't arrive until 11am causing me to have to cancel the moving company that i hired to move my belongings to the unit. I also did the walk through of the apartment and it was a mess. It had not been cleaned, things were broken, and the carpet had lots of debris with huge blue paint spots in every room. I asked Kim ,the leasing agent at the apartments who has been helping my partner and I with the  transfer, if she saw the apartment because it was a mess she told me that it wasn't her job it was maintenance's job. The apartment looked like someone just moved out and not like it was ready for new movers to come make it their home. I am so unhappy with the way I was treated and the quality I received dealing with Oak Lake. 


Poor communication from office staff no maintenance staff very unorganized and  dealing with this property has been the worst experience Ive ever encountered

Had the hummus pita sandwich.  Nothing special- no seasoning or anything... Just store-bought hummus stuffed in a dry pita pocket with a couple pieces of shredded iceberg lettuce, red cabbage, and carrots.  Probably won't go back there...

One thing Starbucks does well is brand recognition. This was my first time to this location and it looks like all the others so I felt like I had been there a million times before. This can be seen as a positive and a negative - it is great because you are familiar with the layout and the menus etc but not so great because there is no individuality, uniqueness, or more personal feel to the place.

The drinks and treats are exactly what is expected from a Starbucks location (any Starbucks location). What separates the good from the bad is location and service. Unfortunately this particular Starbucks fails on both accounts.

I went at 4:30pm on a Saturday afternoon and the place was not busy however finding a parking spot and negotiating the parking lot was a feat all on its own. Unfortunately there is a T&T Supermarket directly across the parking lot and all of these customers were consuming the parking and walking haphazardly in the paved lot. This made it a unique challenge even to get in the door.

Once I had made it in I was able to walk right up to the counter and attempt to order. The baristas were all busy cleaning and so it took a while to get their attention but eventually I placed my order. The friend I was meeting had arrived before me so instead of waiting at the counter for my drink I sat with her and chatted for a while. Next thing I realize that ten minutes had gone by and my drink was still not prepared so back up to the counter I went. Again all of the baristas were cleaning machines and organizing items behind the counter - they all had forgotten about my drink. Luckily I was not in a rush because from the time I walked in until I received my beverage was over 15 minutes. 

There are two other Starbucks' at the intersection of 97th Street and 137th Ave and (without being to either) I would recommend trying both of them over this location.

Used to frequent this restaurant quite a bit. Haven't been there in quite a while. So I thought we would try it again. The food used to be really good but we were disappointed this time around. The service was good. But the pasta And the sauce just did not taste the same. Very tasteless food. We ordered a glass of wine which was very small but the price was high. Don't think that we will go back again.

Well it took a few trips in to finally get a table. Normally it gets so crowded I was left with terrible service standing in the front that twice after 15 min of waiting we left. So finally I got a table. Wa able to order a few drinks and an app. The drinks were just average. I was so ready for something stellar after reading the reviews but was left wanting to go elsewhere as you could taste the booze and the lack of special flare. I can think of two other places downtown I would go to before I come here again. As for the app we ordered, I got the pork belly tacos and they were tough to chew and way to garlicky or something. This is coming from someone who will eat whole roasted cloves at home too. Overall unless a friend wants to return, I will be taking my business elsewhere.

We're big wino's and try and check out wine bars wherever we are... one night we were downtown and decided to venture out and check out Rumors. It was a snowy, cold evening... and upon entering, it was very warm and cozy. The decor was minimal, but well thought-out and perfect for a wine bar!

The person behind the counter was too busy on their Mac to pay much attention to us... which made me a little disappointed and the person who I assume to be the owner was having a meeting at the bar about their taxes, which was a little distracting and could have been taken to any number of the empty tables (we were the only people in the entire place). 

When we did order... they were out of the wine that we wanted and instead of offering a suggested alternative, she went back to being on her computer. For a wine bar, we weren't impressed with their selection... which isn't really acceptable, for a WINE BAR. Probably needless to say, we didn't end up ordering anything other than 1 glass of totally unremarkable wine... despite WANTING to. Clearly the staff was too preoccupied to really care if we did or not and to me, service is an important part of my dining experience. 

I'd really love to try Rumors again... their patio area looked adorable (it was too cold the night we were there) and I'm guessing that this was just a "bad night" for us to go... but when you're in the service industry, bad nights can really make or break you. Considering our experience, I doubt we'll set out to try Rumors any time soon...

I found the place to be a bit noisy and the tables to close for my taste. As for the food ..I ordered the Monte Cristo. I guess it was just ok. The potatoes where just flat out dry. I literally just picked at it. The menu description of food and sleek website had me expecting something more exciting and intimate than an IHOP without booths.

Food is FINE. I had a big salad with chicken and BF had burger. I ordered a blueberry lemonade cocktail - but they were out of the blueberry vodka and they replaced it with something. Was pretty tasty. We were there around 10:30pm on a Friday and it was DEAFENINGLY loud. It ruined the night. Everyone around us in the front area of the place was laughing because we couldn't place orders - the waitress couldn't hear. My boyfriend asked the waitress if she'd be able to ask for it to be turned down a little and she said "we've already tried." Other diners were complaining too - you couldn't hear what the person sitting across from you was saying. What a stupid waste - to pay $5 cover and then not be able to hold a conversation. Will not return.

I've been here twice. First time was better than the second. The waitress was nice and attentive. The nigiri sushi I ordered had a few pieces that seemed slightly off. Also, I didn't really like their metal chopsticks. Not terrible but not great either.

I've never actually left a review on any place because it feels really mean but honestly I'm so poor and those $10 I spent were really a lot so I want to save everyone else from making the mistake I did. The name of the place is pho bistro, don't get the damn pad Thai. Literally do not do it. The name is PHO not Thai food, there unfortunately are very very limited options in the area so I was desperate but honestly just do not do it. I wanted authentic Thai not made by a lady named Sharon and...it was honestly the most disappointing and depressing pad Thai I've ever had. And I pretty much try to get pad Thai wherever I go. It was literally bright orange noodles with zero flavor and I feel so mean saying that because I work in food/service whatever (starbucks for over a year) and I get it, I know sometimes shit happens and the service was honestly great! Super nice, really friendly, food came out fast...it just sucked :( it was bright orange you guys what even is that, the chicken was...weird and scary, just had a bad taste, it didn't taste like pad Thai at all and I'm really bummed bc I can't even afford to be spending money like that and I should've just gone with their pho. I've had their pho before, that shit was hella dank I recommend that 100% the place is great their pad Thai just...I'm scarred tbh. Should've known better than to go for Thai at a place literally called pho bistro. Excuse me while I cry over the $10 down the drain (I couldn't finish it, honestly it just wasn't pad Thai the noodles literally looked like angel hair pasta I'm really upset and sad mostly sad because I feel like if I was a dish I would be that sad sorry excuse of pad Thai) anywho, their pho is great and they give you a good amount too. Don't get the pad Thai though. :-)

I live close to the Barrelhouse and like the Crooked Fence beers, so this place should be a natural for me.  However, their service is a real problem.  I play trivia here once a week, because the host is great, but I long ago gave up on ordering food.  They perpetually appear to be either understaffed, disorganized or both.  Drinks take 15-20 minutes and food takes well over a half-hour beyond that.  I have had some decent food here, and some that wasn't so great.  If I knew I could get it in a reasonable time frame I would find a few safe bets on the menu and go with them.  However, I have learned to always eat before I go here.  They make some great beers and host some other local taps as well.  A competent restaurant manager could make a go of this place, cutting their food offerings in half and teaching staff steps of service and consolidating tricks would be a good start.

Mediocre at best poor service they could not accommodate a friends Pepper allergy telling us that all of their sauces are premade in the morning and the chef that makes them is not there in the evening if a chef can't make  a fresh order of fettuccine Alfredo without Adding pepper one has to wonder what kind of a place this is My egg plant parm was  an unenthusiastic presentation of thick strips of baked unbreaded eggplant. In an overly salty sauce dessert in the form of tortoni and Taramisu although made in house were extremely disappointing ignore the wonderful smell of the restaurant food doesn't match it

Typical Indian buffet, but definitely on the high end of the scale. For a self-serve place the service is very good, they bring out naan, silverware, and drinks. The food is all fresh, warm and very good. The atmosphere is cozy, it's dimly lit with traditional Indian decorations and soft Indian music. 10/10

EDIT: I had tripped on my way to take a photo and was on the ground for a few seconds, as I got to the buffet I felt something crawling on my elbow, I slapped it and two bugs about stink bug size(but with the body structure of a ladybug) ran towards the the buffet and disappeared....literally seconds after I posted my original review. I was in shock for several minutes but took the photo of the buffet nonetheless... 3/10 because the food was nice and the decor was good but bugs on the floor...

This target used to be clean. The place is now a filthy mess. The food area with the pizza hut and starbucks was disgusting. Crumbs and trash all over the floor. The best part was when I went to throw something out at the trash can....I was attacked by a swarm of fruit flies hanging around the can. Management really needs to improve how this place is run....oh, and the caliber of the employees has really gone to crap.

I had my first Jamaican Jerk Hut experience while enjoying the PHS pop up garden on a Thursday around 6pm. After seeing it featured on Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives (odd that's what they picked to feature in Philly) we got excited to try it.

We ordered from the window connected to the garden.  Dining Partner ordered the jerk chicken wings, and I ordered the chicken leg platter with rice, plantains, and cabbage and a side of cornbread to share. It was expensive! $26 and we waited almost 25 minutes standing at the window for our food. There didn't seem to be a register in the back so every time someone gave the woman payment, she disappeared for a bit to bring back change. It seemed very inefficient. The chicken was very juicy and had quite a kick to it. Overall though, it was nothing special and not filling. Won't be back for more.

Have been a long time fan since I first came to Tucson, however their service has been really lacking recently.  I have learned to call ahead since during a busy day,  you can wait up to almost an hour for your food, which is ridiculous for a fast food Mexican place.  However, I have found that even when I call ahead and wait ten to twenty minutes past their quoted time, my order is still not ready.  Don't like that at all.  The thing that has bugged me the most is that when they go on vacation, they do not leave an answering machine message saying so.  Really guys, it's the 21st century!!  Get a freaking answering machine and say that you are not open for business.  I drove all the way over here and you're not open on a random day in the summer?  No thanks.  I'll take my business to Taco Urbano, a much closer, cleaner Mexican place for burritos.

I hate to be a Honey's party pooper but I have to agree with Robyn h. below about this spot being way overhyped. 

Umm, the fresh squeezed orange juice?? Tasted like nasty tang to both me and my dining companion. If that's fresh squeezed I'll take some Tropicana any day. Having been in NoLibs for a year I kept hearing about how amazing this place was. No one believed me that I hadn't yet been there. I chose a strategically favorable early weds afternoon. to make my debut at Honey's, as opposed to the weekend rush which I thought would make this a highly relaxed & enjoyable exp. We didn't have to wait for a table next to the open windows which was lovely, about as good as it gets in this place but it was still hella crowded and the waitress was indefferent/rushed. The place reminded me of my organic food coop in college, where everyone filed in canteen style and got served sometimes amazing sometimes yucky hippie food. This must've been one of the yucky days at Honey's Sit N Eat.

The latkes were wack. I was expecting jewish potato pancakes all golden crispy and instead i got some kinda heavy flat hashbrown type thing that had little flavor. The coffee wasn't that great either. The whole plate just fell short to me. everything tasted salty.  Maybe I take brunch way to seriously as a sacred institution but I'd like to repeat the sentiment that Sabrina's is way better, and way more worth the wait.

Sorry Nico's, but I have tried again and again to come to you for my mexican fast food cravings, but today was the last straw.

I ordered the super nachos at the drive-thu....the service was great, but the nachos not so much.  The beef is seriously overcooked and so salty I could only get a few bites down before calling it quits.

You guys used to be better than this.

I used to love this place but the last3 times I've gone its not been good. They messed up the order twice,and the waitress didn't even seem to care. Also one of my friends food was cold and we told the waitress and she wasn't very nice about having to send it back stating"it may be a while hope you're not hungry"and she didn't say it in a joking manner. I've worked in restaurants and what she should have done was got my friend an appetizer on the house or a drink or something. Then the bill was messed up one time,plus the food was not what it used to be. I hope theycan get back to what they had as i used to love coming here but after my last few experiences me nor my friends will be back.

The veg offerings are remarkably few... whatever options I've seen in other reviews are not offered anymore. After a long conversation I found something I could eat, and it was palatable, but it's not a happy place to go if you don't eat meat.

Typical sports bar. Bartender was nice enough to change the channel to the soccer game that we wanted to watch, but all the TVs are at weird angles and you can't really watch them without craning your neck. Pretty good funnel fries, even though they ran out of chocolate cake. Nice waitresses, even though after a while it was hard to flag one down for a cup of water. Bathrooms are disgusting....but you gotta make do, I guess.

I haven't reviewed a Place in a long time but I'm so upset by the way my service was I thought I should take the time to write it up. 

I used to LOVE this place great tri-tip, chili beans are ridiculous, onion rings are delicious. I was really looking forward to taking my man there. We drove three hours just to feast on the sandwiches.  

I was aware the kitchen closes at three but due to traffic we got there at 230, Later then i wanted but Just in time to order our food. The waitress unsure of her name but blonde girl, kinda thick with tattoos tells us kitchen closes in 30 mins. Ok still time. So we order our food and set up at table inside. It's obvious we r preparing to stay inside, we have a baby and all this baby stuff set up. 

There are people still inside and another couple comes in after us and sits down to order food as well. 

anyways food comes out fairly quickly. Waitress literally puts on table and walks away. my hamburger has fries and we had onion rings. So um excuse me can I get ketchup. I swear you would have thought I asked her to move a mountain. But she grabs it from another table next to us and gives it to me. 

Also asked for my hamburger and chili beans no cheese. She brings me chili beans with cheese and disappears. I wait at counter with my container of chili beans and tell her I ordered my chili beans with no cheese. She tells me "nobody told me" are u kidding me. Ur the waitress who else would I tell? 

When she reappears with new chili beans minus cheese I ask her if I can bother her for some eating utensils. She points to a container holding plastic silverware and says "yeah, it's over there, I thought you guys were eating outside but she goes to get us some plastic spoons and napkins Mind u the table next to us just got their food and is eating with real utensils. 

I really hope this girl was just having a bad day but it just totally ruined our experience. And it doesn't matter how good the food is nobody wants to be treated badly.

I had to wait for a preparer to finish chatting with his friends before he finally took my order.  Also all the seats were filthy.  The guy who does the cleaning needs to clean the seats, not just the tables.

As a Cuban-American, I was excited to hear that St. Louis once again had a place to go for Cuban food. I have had the food at Mi Caribe on three occasions (twice in the restaurant, and once as a to-go order)--overall, the quality is hit and miss. 
I understand that this is more of a Dominican restaurant instead of a Cuban or Puerto Rican restaurant, but there is some cross-over in cuisine with distinguishing features for each country. The mofongo is good here, and a sure thing. The Cuban sandwich is quite good and I have had it twice. The tostones and maduros are also great.
Ropa vieja--fair at best. 

We were asked by the manager how we liked our meal, my husband (who--like I--is one of those people who cannot stand cilantro--even the smallest sprig makes an entire dish taste like dish soap) expressed that he did not enjoy his rope vieja because he did not expect to find cilantro in it. The snarky reply from the manager was "we're Dominican" and he walked away. Fine...but don't call it rope vieja. Call it Dominican carne ripiada or Dominican Ropa Vieja, but don't tell a Cuban that it is OK to contaminate our national dish with the offensive herb. Rude.

Needless to say, I did not ask if they made Cuban coffee, and we asked for our check.

I guess Mi Caribe is not my cup of Caribe and we will probably not return.

Since they've switched Castle Coffee I no longer like their espresso drinks. Castle Coffee has a sour taste to it that I do not care for. The croissants are dense and heavy instead of flaky and light. They have had some unique pastries that are refreshing. If they had more vegetarian pastries that are savory instead of sweet I might come back.

My family and I have purchased 5 vehicles from Crest Honda in the past years, and we have always used their dealership to complete routine maintenance such as oil changes. There are "mile markers" when they will alert you that your car is due for a whatever- whatever and it'll cost hundreds of dollars. I always ask them to tell me exactly what is included in that check up, and then I take it to my local mechanic to do what needs to be done and charge me far less for the services. 

One of these maintenance mile markers included changing the serpentine belt, among other services. When I discussed this with my regular mechanic, he explained to me that for my year, make and model, I have a serpentine chain, which does not need to be replaced as the dealership told me it did. Basically,  the dealership MADE UP a service that I "needed" to have completed and were planning on charging me hundreds of dollars for it. 

I will continue to go for oil changes, as they have competetive prices on that front, the waiting room is comfortable, they are fast and always wash my car, but otherwise, I will take my other car business elsewhere.
Totally underwhelmed. A lackluster overpriced chain. Go somewhere else. Our server was nice and competent. Was my son's birthday and they couldn't be bothered to sing to him.
Awesome building and great cocktails... BUT the food was dreadful. It was way too expensive for the quality of food we got. I probably won't ever go back to eat.

Is this a bowling alley or a tacky nightclub?

Went here on a Saturday night for a friend's birthday. She wasn't able to reserve a lane in advance - first come first serve. Luckily she got there when a lane was available, but by the time I arrived 10 minutes later, there was a half hour wait for lanes. And there are a lot of lanes. 

Prices on the weekend are super high! $7.88 per game per person, plus $3.15 for shoe rental.

It was dark. At the end of the lanes there are huge screens, with some playing sports and some playing music videos. SUPER DISTRACTING. The music is incredibly loud. It was their version of glow bowling that night, with the pins lit up in different colours and the balls glowing neon, which looked pretty cool. 

The seating area was weird, with two lanes sharing a table along with the balls, and there weren't enough balls so there was a lot of awkwardly standing around waiting for the right size ball to come out and trying to grab it before the random stranger on the other lane.

There is typical bowling alley food - pizza, wings, nachos. I didn't have any but I glanced at the menu and it was not entirely reasonable. People all around me were eating all of the above mentioned items, and they looked pretty standard.

I noticed signs everywhere for good deals they have during the week - unlimited bowling for a certain price, toonie Tuesday, etc., but I don't think I will ever take advantage of them since I don't live anywhere near WEM.

Caruso's was recommended by a friend who lives in Tucson; we're from out of town and were in Tucson on business. We ate dinner at Caruso's last week (on a Wednesday). There's a lot to like about Caruso's but, all in all, we'll probably go somewhere else for an Italian food dinner next time we're in Tucson.

Likes:
 - Interesting, bustling historic neighborhood.
 - Beautiful historic building.
 - Nice, charming, old-style ambience.
 - Friendly, helpful servers and bussers.
 - Lots of menu choices.
 - Generous portion size.

Dislikes:
 - Food items look good but taste mostly just okay.
 - Prices are too high, especially given that the food is mostly just okay. Menu says minimum charge $8.00 per person, even at lunch as far as I can tell.
 - Parking can be a hassle. There is no on-site parking lot. There is plenty of street parking, if you can find a space that is. Be prepared to parallel park, and watch out for the commuter trains while you're trying to park. The street parking is metered, although weekdays after 5:00 p.m. it's free.

Wife had the seafood canelloni & cheese manicotti dinner ($15.75). She enjoyed it, but rated it as good, but not very good and certainly not excellent. Wife liked the dinner salad with homemade Italian dressing that came with the dinner. (She had a choice of dinner salad, soup or side of spaghetti with Caruso sauce. Caruso sauce is basically marinara sauce with meat.) Wife liked all the veggie goodies that were in the salad, and the dressing. However, she rated the salad as only good because it was made from what appeared to be iceberg lettuce; would have rated it very good if it had been made from a leafy lettuce like, say, romaine.

Dinners come with unlimited garlic toast. That's nice, but the garlic toast  wasn't anything special; I'd rate it as fair. Frankly, it was similar in it's bready texture and taste to a toasted hot dog or hamburger bun spread with butter and a little garlic powder. We ate the initial basket that was brought to our table while we waited for dinner to be served, but we didn't want any more.

I ordered the veal parmigiana dinner ($16.75 - the most expensive dinner on the menu), which came with my choice of dinner salad, soup or side of spaghetti with Caruso sauce. I chose the side of spaghetti with Caruso sauce. The spaghetti pasta was bland, although the Caruso sauce livened up the side dish.

The veal parmigiana looked great, served in a large skillet sort of dish and smothered with baked cheese and sauce. Key word: "looked" [great]. First, I tried to find the veal patty hiding under the cheese and sauce. I know the veal is supposed to be pounded a bit into a thin patty, but this veal was pounded into oblivion. I eventually located a paper-thin layer of meat. The meat, what little there was of it, had a dark brown color. The color struck me as unusual because the veal I've eaten elsewhere is normally a much lighter color. Then I tasted the meat - very strange flavor, and unpleasantly strange. Maybe the meat had somehow gone bad? I don't know and am not accusing, just trying to guess where the strange flavor and color came from.

I don't claim to be an expert on veal parmigiana but I have eaten it enough times over the years at enough different restaurants to know about what to expect. There are all those other veal parm dishes I've enjoyed at all those other restaurants, and then there is the veal parm at Caruso's. The latter stands out from the pack, but not in a good way. For $16.75 I expected much better; actually, for ANY price I expected much better.

Soon enough, the server came by and asked how our meals were. I told him about my entrée. To his credit, the server did not hesitate to offer a replacement entrée; no questioning, just apologies. It was the right way to react, and that is what he did.

I ordered the cheese manicotti dinner ($14.25), and it was brought out promptly. The cheese manicotti was better as a manicotti dinner than the veal parm was as a veal parm dinner. Even so, the manicotti was only good, but not very good and certainly not excellent.

So basically everything we were served was fair to good, except the veal parm which was downright bad. No food item was very good or better.

Oh yeah, one more thing: Caruso's has Alfredo sauce on the menu but not fettuccini noodles on which to serve it. What's up with that? They do offer spaghetti, rigatoni, linguine, and penne; but when it comes to Alfredo sauce, those pastas are not the same as fettuccini.

We're glad we tried out Caruso's. Not likely to go back, though, and not likely to recommend it to others except maybe with warnings.

We had a Groupon so decided to use it. Sat at the bar- no greeting from the bartender. Had to ASK the bartender every time we needed something- another drink, a menu, to order food, for the check, etc. Not once did they ask if we needed anything, how the food was, nothing! None of the staff smiled- very unfriendly and overall miserable. They spent more time checking their phones than working.
The food was very good but we won't be back as the bartenders were not even the least bit friendly. Left a 20% tip on the check(before discount) but didn't feel anyone there deserved it.
There are definitely better places in the area!!

I usually wouldn't write a review for a movie theater but after our gross experience last Friday I feel compelled to comment.

Saw a 9:30 movie which I know is one of the last ones of the night after a long day of crowds, but we still paid good money and expect something more acceptable.  First of all, the movie theater was majorly lacking in cleanliness and quality.  I understand that's its probably hard to clean all of the popcorn off the lobby floor in the middle of the evening, but our seats were awful.  Still trash in the cupholders and on the floor and giant holes in our seats.  And don't get me started on the bathrooms.  When I used the women's room after the movie it looked like a zombie apocalypse had went down.  Disgusting, toilet paper everywhere and whatever other messes everywhere, literally half of the sinks were out of order.

Besides cleanliness, customer service was also quite poor.  Order pretzel bites at the snack bar which should take one minute to prepare.  Waited at pickup for at least ten minutes.  Most annoyingly, we tried to get someone's attention in case our order was forgot or something but all staff were completely ignoring us.  Finally got someone's attention to get our slimy pretzels but were treated pretty rudely I felt.  Not to mention they forgot to turn off the lights during our movie until ten minutes in.  I didn't even think that was a manual process.  Sheesh, never going here again!

For an $11 burger/fries combo, I expect fireworks. Explosions going off, fog machines, F-18s overhead, maybe a dragon. After all, there are at least 4 fine contenders in town at that price point - locally raised beef, locally baked buns and all that. M.L. Rose fails to rise above the pack in terms of taste and quality, and the unholy racket in the dining room (trivia night or not) just isn't worth bothering with.

After all these years, we finally tried Pita Jungle tonight.  Upon walking in, I wondered if we had just walked into Hooters or the like due to the lack of attire worn by several servers. Stuff was poppin' out all over, if you know what I mean. Anyway, we ordered a couple margaritas which clearly tasted like they were made with soda pop. It had that carbonation thing going on plus it was just too sour. Asked our server (Megan) to please add some triple sec, which she did gladly.  Being my first time there, I was pesky and asked a lot of questions and the server was very patient with me :)   So...I ordered a small greek salad with a side of chicken. The salad was a good size for me, but since it was on a small plate, quite a bit of it kept falling onto the table as I tried cutting the oversized pieces. This is frustrating cuz whatever lands on the table, I won't eat. Call me a germaphobe. I think the 'cook' forgot to put some salad dressing on as the lettuce was bland, bland, bland.   The chicken was.....interesting.....was it supposed to be warm or cool? Some pieces were chilled (like out of the frig) and other pieces were warm (but  not hot).  I guess I watch too much Chef Ramsey, but dang it, I was expecting my chicken to be 'cooked to order'.  Definitely wasn't.  Plus, I'm just not sure how I'm supposed to be loving on cold and warm chicken at one time.   Hubby ordered the gyro, which he said was dry, but otherwise tasted ok.  We got the tiny happy hour side of tzatziki sauce and 3 hummus. The tzatziki was yummy, but c'mon! $1.50 for that tiny amount? Pappoule's doesn't charge a penny for extra tzatziki (plus the chicken is HOT too!)   The pita bread that came with it was cold and all dried out and you could tell just by looking at it.  Why would you put that on a plate for a paying customer?  We told the server and she replaced it quickly.  

Overall speaking, the service was fine, but the food wasn't anything to warrant another visit. If you are looking for real Greek food, this isn't it. The menu is all over the place, so it's kinda hard to determine what Pita Jungle is really all about.

This nail salon is very clean, and the workers seem to be wonderful. With that being said, I had the male nail technician, and he was absolutely terrible. He was working on my feet during a pedicure, and there was clearly something stuck in his teeth. He actually took his hand off of my foot, put his finger in his mouth to dig whatever it was out, and then went back to working on my pedicure. I was truly mortified because 1. It is not hygienic, and 2. His hand that was in his mouth was now working on my feet! This didn't happen once, but multiple times, even after I said I was uncomfortable with this. The lady who did my moms pedicure was wonderful, but he made my experience uncomfortable, and I truly do not think I will be going back.

I'm an airline pilot and ride bike share all over the country. I would say this one falls short for the following reasons:
Have to download an app and add money before you even get a bike.
Not real clear how the process of getting a bike works. I used the trial and error method.
Had to try 4 bikes before I could get one where both the keyboard and screen worked. You have to enter an account number and pin to unlock the bike.
Overpriced.  Most bike shares charge you a fee for the day and you get unlimited rides under a certain time limit. This program charges a flat $8 an hour (plus tax, I've never seen that!) which is the highest I've experienced in my dozens and dozens of bike share experiences.
Bikes are not in the best of shape and there is no variety. Personally I prefer straight handle bars but the bikes in this program only come with the bars that angle back.

Overall one of the poorest setups I've used.

We dined at Revivals on the weekend, party of eight people. We were seated quickly and the wait staff was very pleasant and attentive. The appetizer was good, but the entrees were bland and unimpressive. This would normally warrant 3/5 stars, but the plates for two of our guests had dirt/grime on them- not conducive to a good dining experience. This was our second mediocre experience in as many visits; we'll be going to the Perk or Hawthorne's instead in the future.

This is our third time at the restaurant and, unfortunately, it wasn't a charm. The beer is cold; guacamole and chips were good; the enchiladas were okay.  I expected more for the money spent.  It's probably best as a drinks and apps place for us.

Went there a few times for dinner. Our experience went from good to bad. We will not be going back.

Here's why:
The hot tea tasted stale and like stale coffee not green tea. must be using the same pot or some funny "green tea"
The sushi rolls were horrible--the avocado was brown and its rotten flavor overwhelmed the freshness of the raw fish. some of the fish on the rainbow roll was very fishy. the rice was overcooked. it was mushy and lacked any acidity or flavor.the salmon looked really pale and had no flavor. 
seems like the owners are trying to cut corners underestimating their clients' taste...

sadly the space was full

Nice ambience and owners but not a sushi place. a good daring spot if you're not hungry.

Got in there at 745. Wait for 15 minute to order (5 people ahead of me) and 823 the order is ready. Staff is loud to each other and yell back and forth for communication and I heard they call each other bixch. eyeroll to customer.  No table is clean. The star is for the food, better than the McDonald in kingshighway and Jefferson .

This use to be my favorite restaurant. The food was great. I have noticed it has hasn't been the same anymore. I went here for my birthday and had the worst experience. I think they had a new waitress and the restaurant was pretty empty so I still do not get why we had so many issues. First  they never brought us bread and we ordered 2 dinners. They brought our food out after 30 minutes and then brought the soup and salad out. When we said we didn't want it because we already had our food they could of cared less. We spoke with a young manager that I had never seen there before and all he offered was to pack the soup and salad so we can bring it home. No apologizes he was more interested in hanging out with staff. A real disappointment. We use to frequent CD 3 or more times a month when we had guest from out of town we always brought them here. Too bad it has changed so much.
The sweet potato hash and kombucha were good. But, I was disgusted when I used their restroom. It was dirty and there were flies all over.

Very cool at first glance.. huge and I mean HUGE layout. The different atriums are pretty sweet to walk through and check out. However, it's overwhelming. The simplest of tasks are instantly difficult to accomplish due to the fact that this place is massive and takes about 2-3 solid days of navigating to grasp. Need to head out to your vehicle because you forgot an item... that's a 20min ordeal. 

The rooms aren't anything special, I'm easy to please, but for the price of this place the rooms could use some TLC. 

Not a bad place to stay, just huge and overly complicated for me. I'm good at a holiday inn.....

Really good service. Very friendly clean environment. I ordered spring rolls and iced green tea. The spring rolls were good. I especially liked the mint in it. The iced green tea... That's a different story. HORRIBLE! This drink is beyond a reasonable doubt, spoiled. I can't get the taste out of my mouth. I didn't tell the owners anything because I hate making a scene and I always have the thought of "what if they spit in my new drink" thought. Pretty disappointing to say the least.

Gosh, I really wanted to love this place. I support privately owned coffee shops over the "Starbucks-Type" conglomerates any day. 

I was excited to try Buddy Brew, but we were disappointed in the product. However, the store's atmosphere is beautiful. 

We just came in for cold brew iced coffees. I had vanilla added to mine. They tasted very burnt and I couldn't finish mine. Also, I could not taste any vanilla. The options for milk were only skim or half & half and the sugar bar had raw sugar, splenda, etc. I like the raw sugar, but not the crystal form. Who wants crunchy sugar in their coffee? They should have the liquid form available and there should be more options for milk - almond, coconut, soy, etc. Maybe they have it if you ask, but I didn't see it on the menu. 

The shop is very clean and modern. It looks like a nice place to hang out, but they need to up their game for a specialized coffee shop. I'd like to see different brews of coffee offered, brewed flavors, more options for sweetener, etc.

The reason I am giving this place 2 stars is for service..We are local to the area and frequently go to this bagel store for the delicious bagels...but when will the staff get the orders right..seriously, the staff gets our order wrong like 80% of the time we are there and this is a common complaint of locals here. You MUST speak to the front counter help like they are in kindergarten because that's about where their brain skills are at. I just left and of course they messed up our order, This is how simple it was. One plain bagel with cream cheese and sliced lox, and One Lox sandwich on an everything bagel with only a little cream cheese. Both bagels came with a ton of cream cheese and only lox. Where is my lettuce, onion, cucumber, tomato, etc???? I was specific and said I wanted the whole Nova Lox sandwich as listed on the board. I am soooooooo tired of the KIDS getting it wrong so often

The staff was really unpleasant and my gel manicure didn't stay intact longer than 3 days. They usually last 3 weeks. Completely miserable about doing my daughters nails and toes that lasted less than a day.

I was not overly impressed.  The salad bar is ok but really highly priced at $8 as a side.  But hey they have real bacon bits??  The pizza was very low quality.  I actually think Chuck-E-Cheese pizza would have been better. The staff was very nice though.

I went to hughes to wash my car this weekend and was very disappointed.  It seems like every time I go the cycle gets shorter and shorter. The vacuum hardly sucks anything up, it merely gathered the small pebbles into a pile so I could remove them by hand. The vacuum also fell apart while I was using it, wasting what little of the cycle I has to try to put the attachment back on.  There was only 3 matt hangers as one was broken leaving me to wash my matt on the ground. The wash bay was dirty and littered with small papers. It was dry when I pulled in so they had been there a while.  I went to the 99st location and will not return. I am sure my bucket at home and hand held little vacuum can do the same job, without having to deal with the lazy and inattentive staff at the front of the store.

Way toOoo greasy: My "fish" sandwich was a breaded oil soppy mess...Did I just eat a sandwich... ? No fish?  It was if they cut a regular piece of fish in half and fried it into a bread crisp. I felt after eating that greasy mess,  my future would be on My 600 pound life.

Plenty of hidden charges. $18 required hotel fee that includes wifi? That should be included regardless. And you have to pay $28 to park your own car. Ridiculous. Made our stay expensive and a pain in the rear. 

Fine rooms and property. Just bad business model. We won't return.

I'm a loyal customer at another branch and since I was in the area, I decided to just go to this mucho burrito for lunch. Food was okay, but the employees looked mad and bored and felt like they were all rushing me even though no one was behind me.

We visited this place once a few weeks ago. It's a definitely a different setup inside. They have a small narrow bar area and a large room with a stage for a band. There is another room upstairs with a few pool tables. Then there is the outside that has seating and a live band. The band was good. We played pool upstairs for a bit. It was quiet upstairs so we opened the window so we could hear the live music from outside. We went downstairs to see if there was a menu to order food from and the bartender told our friend the only thing they had to eat was frozen pizza, hot pockets and chips and salsa. The weird thing is that we've seen other reviews that show that people have actually ordered menu items. It was a Saturday night and there were hardly any people there. It's a good place to go if you just want to have a beer and listen to the live band outside or play a couple of rounds of pool. This is more of a place for regulars Who want to sit at the bar and chat and not a happening place if you are looking for excitement.

Been here a couple of times.. Today I have to say it was by far the worst service and Mimosa I ever had..
Waitress had  the worst attitude and personality..
Waited for 30 minutes to be seated.  Once seated waited 45 minutes for food...(bagel sandwich, muffin and cinnamon roll).  While seated we could see the frustration of other tables as their orders were coming out wrong . This place might be good during the week but weekend rush comes the left hand doesn't know what the right one is doing.. Kitchen is definitely understaffed and place is poorly managed...

I didn't like that the burgers are not really built to order (nor does the menu allow that option). You have to order burgers that have titles and predetermined toppings.  Mostly all burgers have shredded lettuce salad (shredded lettuce caked in mayo i.e. Big Mac style) and tomato. I liked that you can choose your bread cheese and sides. 

I am a firm believer in allowing the customer to choose what they want on their burger as it is the American way. Service was great.

Lunch = 10 wings, side salad, iced tea..$22.00 before tip, $26 with tip.  Food took 35 minutes to get to table and the place was empty.  Food was good, especially the smoked wings, but the prices will drive them out of town in all likelihood.

The atmosphere is great and the service is good. However, in my opinion the food is grossly over rated and the prices are quite high for what you get. Order a soda and it comes in a can with no refills. Pretty cheesy. They offer happy hour specials that appeal to kids like chicken sliders. We were told our son couldn't have them because he wasn't 21. Instead we were charged $5 more for a kids plate. What a joke.

Went to the restaurant with no expectations.   The restaurant is simple with little to no ambiance. The hot buffet items were luke warm at best with the exception of the roasted potatoes.  The rotisserie items brought out were fresh, hot and very tasty (bacon wrapped chicken, sirloin & sausage).  The family and wait staff were very friendly.  The biggest disappointment came when we checked out.   Before we went, I checked the website menu for prices.  The weekday price was $14.99 per person with rotisserie items (and still does at the time of this posting).   We were charged a whopping 50% more at $21.99/person.   We felt taken advantage of by that price difference and will not return.

The prior reviews are true; services here is just not that good at all. The staff were high schoolers who appeared to not be receiving too much supervision. I was there for 10 minutes and never saw a supervisor. I was there to get a dilly bar and a baby blizzards; again 10 minutes. The staff had remembered to get me a dilly bar, but was too busy talking, laughing all the while taking the next person's order that they forgot about the blizzard. The quality of the DQ was good, but I will not to to this location ever again. Its not worth it.

My company booked me here for training this week, and while the rooms were ok for a 3-Star I found that the hotel a bit under-maintained.  For example, I don't think they did a really good job cleaning the bathroom as I found "errant" hairs around the bathtub and floor.  If you are here for a personal visit, it is probably ok.  But I had to knock it down to two stars because if you are here for business it is completely inadequate, because the "High Speed" Internet is slower than dial up.  Not to mention  they are doing maintenance work on the elevators and I have to factor a 10 minute wait for an elevator into my morning commute (I am seriously not joking).

I will say there are a few positives, they have an Elephant and Castle Pub on site with a good selection of beers.  It is centrally located to a lot of things, including the very cool Comcast Center and City Hall.  There is a rooftop pool which looked nice (I didn't get to use it because we were busy with work).  And, the sheets are clean (which to be honest is one of the most important things to me).  But overall, I can't really give it in good faith more than 2 stars.

I read another reviewer's post about the sleep package, but they didn't offer it to me, so I am not sure if they still do that anymore.  Would be neat if they did.
Greasy, slow, and the menu doesn't vary much. Disappointing because the owner is real cool. Sandwiches would benefit from being cut in half. Crappy and expensive. It's just too bad.

Mediocre sushi. Appetizers are a joke - overpriced and portion sizes are ridiculously small (forget about sharing). Sunomono appetizer was barely a couple of spoonfuls. It's trendy allright - this place is for you if you just want to be seen and don't care about getting quality food for your money.

Though I admittedly haven't eaten here in quite a while I've heard that little has changed. Their food is was unimpressive and service takes forever.

The pluses are: the prices are very reasonable and they do have a great patio.

I came here for the "half-off burger" Wednesday deal! I got there pretty early, but by 12:30, the place got super packed pretty fast.
