My sister and I ordered the Beef Sirloin burger with the Monte Carlo topping (mushroom and cheddar) and another with the Yucatan topping (fried onion, jalapeño, and jack cheese). Personally, I was a little disappointed by their burgers. Maybe I came with too high of expectations...but I didn't enjoy the burgers as I had hoped. I felt as if they skimped out on the toppings. The burger was not very flavorful and it was kind of hard. Sadly, even the fries were a disappointment. 

However, I think the  1/2 half off deal is pretty nice! The fact that it runs from 11:30am-5pm gives a lot of time for people to make it! I've also heard a lot of good things about their $5 margaritas for Mexican Mondays, so I'm definitely giving this place another try for their solid deals!

I should first say that I did end up purchasing a used Honda Certified car from Crest Honda, and that's due to two things: 1, their competitive price, and 2, their floor manager Larry. He was personable and offered a solution when leather seats -- a must-have for me and my dog -- didn't come with the car.

But I'll back up and start with my first visit, this summer. I had a Honda Accord I was ready to sell, and I wanted a newer Accord but hadn't driven any. I took an Accord out for a test drive with my husband and a salesperson. (The salesperson asked where we both worked, and he said he'd been to where my husband worked and wasn't impressed. Okie-doke. You want our business, right?)

Since this was our first car-buying venture in the game, we really just wanted to test drive. But we went through the credit check to see what our options were and also had our current Accord appraised. It came back at about $800. When I said we'd keep that in mind and would return, the salesperson offered $1,000. I said, "Thank you, but we just aren't ready to buy today." He responded, "What can we do to make this happen?" I said, "It isn't going to happen today, but thank you."

Walked out to the car, backed up the car, and Glen, the salesperson's manager, comes jogging out and knocks on the window. We stop, roll the window down, and Glen says, "What if we can offer you $2,000?"

I said I wouldn't go back to Crest Honda. After great experiences at CarMax in Cool Springs and Rivergate, and after trolling Trickett Honda and Redell Honda's websites, we spotted an Accord at Crest Honda we were pretty sure we wanted.

We returned and were connected with the same salesperson again, such is the luck. Once we decided on the car and negotiated our current car's trade-in value, it took probably three hours; at least two hours of which, the salesperson was missing. He kept leaving to print off paper, and he'd be gone for 25 minutes; we weren't still in the deciding or negotiating phase, so he didn't need any tactics.

Tonight, Crest Honda called me, and here's the transcription:

Her: "Hi, this is [NAME] with Crest Honda, and I have a few questions about your recent experience with us. This call will be recorded."

Me: "Sure, OK."

Her: "I see here that you did not purchase with us, is that right?'

Me: "No, I did."

Her: "OK, and that purchase was a 2011 Honda Accord, is that right?"

Me: "No, it was a 2012."

Her: "Oh, OK, congratulations. And I have a few questions to go through here. For your email address, we have your email on file for, um, for, for product, services announcements, and it's [reads email address, which is one letter off]."

Me: No, it's [gives correct email address."

Her: "OK, well, congratulations on that, and I hope you have a good night."

What? I have no idea what the purpose of that call was. I have an appointment with them later this month to install leather seats, but they'd have no notation of that if they don't know I just paid them thousands of dollars for a newer car. They mostly don't have their act together, some of them feel sleazy and inexperienced, and their prices and Larry's demeanor are all that kept us there. I would not recommend them if customer service and decency matter to you.

This used to be one of my fave places to visit. Now the customer service is horrible. You walk in on a light day (2 customers) and they act as if they don't see you. They don't seat you. They don't give you a menu. You just stand there for 20 minutes looking stupid until you get fed up and walk out. Never again will they be graced with my presence.

veggie burger was house made (kudos!), yet fries soggy. overpriced. cute location during the fair weather days when you can sit outside. nothing was spectacular, everything was expensive. brunch is inordinately packed and impractical for sub par omelettes, do not recommend for this occasion. if you're gluten free, this spot has very limited and tasteless options. nope.

Not terrible, but so ridiculously expensive. Unless you stuck to appetizers and soup, your meal was going well north of 30 dollars. Ceasar salad was ok, and the onion roll they serve for their bread basket was yummy, but not worth it. At all.

Pros: Free parking, good location, felt safe, reasonably comfortable bed.

Cons:  No tub.  Long hair on towel, missing wash cloth

This is not somewhere I would stay for more than a quick late night check in/early am check out.

NOISY!!!  Since it's next to the freeway, you can hear every car going up and down the freeway all day and night.  Other sounds that kept me up all night included: dogs barking across the street, an old truck with a modified muffler in the parking lot, the toilet that kept refilling itself, the sound of people slamming their doors, the sound of loud voices next door.  These are sound that are common to hotels but not as loud as it is here.  So if you are deaf... this is the place to go!  It's also a little musty.

Used to love this place, but it's gone downhill quickly in the last six months. Fewer staff, and a reduction in cleaning equipment in the wash. You're car now leaves wet with spots. Even the interior clean has been scaled back. New management?

We've been regulars of The Natural for years, maybe more like decades. The food is very consistent but has remained unchanged for years. Specials are usually good.

But their new location has really changed our Natural experience. From the garden feel with nice, warm wood tables, chairs, floor and service area, lots of plants and earthy decor, we now get cheapo, fast food tables, intrusive lighting, and a completely uninviting ambience.  We no longer linger and chat over a meal but take it to go. Our kids are big Natural fans from birth and used to request it regularly.  After the first couple of visits, they haven't suggested it and when it was brought up, said they'd rather not.

We're very sad that Natural Cafe didn't stick to its roots with this new location. Now, it just screams, "Goletans don't deserve what Santa Barbarans get downtown and at La Cumbre." Thanks for making one of our favorites just another unpleasant Goleta dining experience.

I had terrible customer service when ordering delivery through doordash. I payed for a vermicelli bowl with egg rolls but only received egg rolls. When I called the staff, they said it wasn't their problem it was doordash's.

Despite all the upgrades this AMC theatre is sorely lacking in adequate staffing.  We always go to the early matinee shows and more often then not we have to suffer just one  cashier at the ticket booth and empty ticket registers that could be open to accommodate the growing lines that occur when there are customers asking questions or have a problem choosing their seats.
The updated seating is a pain because the touch features at times get stuck or do not work at all which means a broken seat that you selected. The seating at the Regal around the corner is much better with one touch controls that don't stress you out. This theater needs to improve its staffing and seating as well as maintain a cleaner environment in which to enjoy a movie

Beer......my favorite food. I should love WOB, but I don't.  A good selection of brews on tap and many more in cans.  Local beers are under represented though.  My big beef with this place is the high prices and short pours, but I guess this is the trend of beer bars.  Call me a fossil, but I can't shell out $7 or $8 on a snifter or tulip glass of a craft beer.  

Ordered a pint of some IPA and it had about 1" of head, I think I'm gonna stick to bottles from now on.  Also ordered the nachos with green chili chicken.  I never got the chili, but instead was served a small scoop of grey, diced chicken.  Also, the nachos were served with that gloppy cheez wiz, I guess that is the "Brown Ale Beer Cheese" but they left off "sauce" in the description.  Nachos are such a cheap and easy dish to prepare and a delight when made right, but this was a total miss and a rip off at $11.50. Use shredded cheese, two layers, and an oven.  But it's easier to take a shortcut.

Usually when I want Jamaican food I drive to Philly and I will continue to do so! Ordered from here online than get a phone call they don't have oxtails!! What Jamaican restaurant runs out of oxtails? So I said ok I don't want a replacement. We will just order the one platter curry chicken, please put gravy on the rice. I called 2x to make sure I wasn't being charged for two platters! The chicken was ok but minimal for the price and of course no gravy on the rice!! Food was ok but I can get way better from most places in Philly for less money! More food and they never run out of oxtails !!! Don't waste your time! And of course I was charged for food I didn't receive so now I have to deal with that!!!!!

I'd have to agree with Eva H. The long waits, inconsistency of refill when packed inside-just for coffee & water -ridiculous!! The couple times went there, waited like 50-1 hour & 1/2. Not like the food was worth the wait, like Sabrina's. Rustic inside, the servers cant handle the crowds, once food out, you may never see them again-refill??! What refill. I didn't like it,if your a savory person, egg rancheros,etc..maybe but even Cafe Lift, quicker then that, I  dont need to wait over 1 hour, Hell Could have went to NY by then. Not the best, not the worst, but not going back.

Do not believe the hype.  This place is only given the credit it is given because it is in Indianapolis, and has no real competition.  By the standards of any halfway decent city, it would be average at best for what it is.  

Case in point...last time I was here with a group, there were no seats (everyone has to be seated), but we were told we can have a table at the front.  This would have been fine, except that it is literally just a table...no chairs, no stools, and nowhere to hang our coats.  And we had to wait to get a waitress, and then even longer to get drinks, as service leaves something to be desired.  Seriously?  If you're going to seat people, then SEAT them.  Otherwise, drop the pretension, and let people sit or stand where they want, and let them get their own drinks from the bar.  Or, better yet, go to Ball and Biscuit, where you can get the same drinks with less attitude.  

Oh, and they do last call at 12:45.  So good luck, and consider yourself warned.

I ordered the turkey burger- honestly it was pretty amazing, smoked Gouda, bacon, and came out piping hot.  

My sister ordered the eggs Benedict salmon meal and it was fairly disappointing- watery hollondaise sauce and wilted spinach.  She was so upset about the way it looked that she was holding back the tears, I felt really bad.  I offered her my meal, but she was too proud to take it.  It was pretty emotional. 

I always really liked it here- but now I must choose my fate- whether I should return for the delicious burger, or follow in my sisters footsteps and explore other brunch options.

Nice looking place and good service yet, coming from P.R., we didn't get what we ordered / were expecting, i.e. ordered a skirt steak ("churrasco") and received cube meat ("bistec"). The same happened with a "Mofongo" that we ordered that was actually a "Mangu" presented as a "Mofongo". Aside from the misinterpretation of these two typical Caribbean entrees, the rice with chicken ("arroz con pollo") and "sancocho" were very nice. Also, a bit pricey for the quality of food we received.

I had pretty low expectations coming here for dinner and drinks. Sadly they were met for the most part. The general decor is pretty dingy. Tables that have seen better days, sticky floors, and bathrooms that could be cleaned more than one a day. Our server was friendly, but our food took FOREVER to arrive - most of us had sandwiches and burgers! My margarita was okay. I wish they had actual margarita classes and not martinis, but I appreciate that they gave me the shaker. I rarely complain about something being too strong, but my drink was literally just tequila. I asked for a shaker with just mix to even it out a bit. I had the Charleston burger. The toppings were tasting, the bun was soft, and the medium rare patty was....raw. I didn't want to wait for another one, so I ate around the middle. The bonus was the extremely stomachache I suffered for several hours afterward.

I'm consistently disappointed by the brewpub based on the things I hear & read. I adore them for their wide variety of vegetarian (and vegan) options, but they always seem to fall flat. 

I will keep going when friends ask, but I'm waiting for the inspiring meal everyone promises me.

Sorry, The Prime Rib restaurant. I've had better. Perhaps, I've been very spoiled by House of Prime Rib in San Francisco. 

My friend had a craving for some Prime Rib. How could we pass up $35 Prix Fixe menu? I guess I could have hind-sight. For appetizer, I had Littleneck clams and Caesar salad. The clams were big and ok. Caesar dressing was terrible. It was tangy and there was a lot of acidity. It didn't taste like Caesar dressing. For entree, I ordered medium rare Prime Rib. I was thinking of having crab dish but went against it since we were at a restaurant that serves Prime Rib. Wow! My medium rare was PINK!?!?!? Really? Ok, I'll eat it. The meat was way chewy for a medium rare. The taste of the meat was OK. I think it was somewhat bland. There was some taste of meat but I wasn't enjoying it. Shaved horseradish didn't help with the taste either.  Thank goodness I wasn't paying full price for the meal. It wasn't worth it. I was expecting something better since I was in The Prime Rib restaurant.

I wanted to love this place, I truly did.  Anytime a new, upscale lounge comes to town I immediately want to embrace it and become a regular, but Sip makes it hard to do so.

It's different than most upscale night clubs in St. Pete as it's more geared towards the "bottles" crowd.  It seems that the old Vintage crowd has just moved down a block since it's closed and now frequent Sip.  It's hard to get a drink at the bar and one time I was there it was extremely hot and smoky (not sure if smoking is allowed inside but my eyes were watering).  The music is decent though and the dance floor area is large, so if dancing is your main priority - there won't be any problems with accomplishing it at Sip.

I really don't understand  the good reviews. It's possible  they're written by people who only go to fast food restaurants?  I don't know. This is my second time eating here and both were bad. The only thing I like about it  is I only have to venture downstairs to eat. The chicken wings were bad. This time around I got fried green tomatoes  and brisket sliders. I was starving when I ordered and I found both to be almost inedible.  Tomatoes,  flavorless and soggy. Brisket mushy. Cole slaw ,store bought.  Definitely  do not make a special trip to eat here.

First, let say I love my 2017 RAV 4 Hybrid. It has literally everything I wanted. The gentleman (Inri) who showed my wife and I the car, the two worked with us on financing, (Frank and another whose name escapes me) were quite professional and helpful (especially Inri).  In fact, the only part of the deal that was negative and almost led to my wife and I going somewhere else, was dealing with the guy who is probably going to get the commission for selling it to us -- Mr. Wes Hoffner(so?).  He started out okay. I called told someone -- maybe him-- what I was looking for. He called left a message he could get the car. My wife and I checked Yelp and the reviews were not stellar. He called any got me and o told him his reviews sucked and we were going to go somewhere else. He said okay. He sent an email thanking me for my honesty but claimed Yelp would not print good reviews so readily if the agency didn't pay Yelp. He asked me to Google them. I did and the reviews were okay. We decided(foolishly) to take a chance. We called him back and said it was a go. He said fine, he'd send us a color wheel to choose our color. We said we didn't care about color as long Aditya wasn't red or white. We knew what we wanted on the car and asked if we could talk about equipment l. He said for us to choose a color first. We assured him we didn't care about color other than red or white. We were interested in which options we could get and wanted to talk about that. He refused and insisted we choose a color first. We should've stopped but we didn't. We tried one more time but then fussed that maybe Toyota or the state of California or his boss required him to have customers choose color first. My wife was angry that he kept demanding we choose a color over our statements that color didn't matter. She wanted to go somewhere else, I talked her into staying. We acquiesced and had him send color choices. The colors came and we decided on a color. We sent the color back to him along with all the optional stuff we wanted. We talked after a couple of days and he said he couldn't find the color we chose and asked if we would settle on another color.  This got both my wife and me. We had said we didn't care, he'd insisted we choose, we did, he said we couldn't have the color we chose.  My wife was very angry, because she thought he had bullied us and that we had let him. Believe it or not, I talked her into staying with deal because I really liked the car. We told him we take Dark Gray be cause we figured he could get that. He had claimed the color we initially had chosen couldn't be had in California. Anyway, we show up to pick up our car and this Hofner is nowhere to be found. We were going to tell him that choosing colors was a nuisance. We thought he'd be by to thank us for buying from them and him. We were at dealership for almost four hours and, though, we had asked to see him, he never swung by. We saw him shoot the breeze with some other salesmen, go on and off the phone, kill time on the floor, but not get to us. We mentioned time Inri that we found it insulting that now he'd made the sale, we were history. Later, after the close to four hours, I drove the car off the lot to home, while my wife drove our Prius home in front. I became aware that I had driven off in a car that had been started for me but that the keys were back at the dealership. I drove back, got the keys to drive away, when Hofner came out to see me. He had been alerted that my wife and I were unhappy we him. This guy, who is the head sales honcho I gather, said hello and then commented how great a color gray was and how it was his wife's favorite. That!s when I told him how unhappy we were that we had been forced to choose a color we then were denied and that he hadn't come to see us to apologize for the inconvenience. His response was to defend the color of our car. I told that wasn't the issue. The issue was forcing us to choose a color and then deny our choice and not say one word of "I'm sorry." He persisted in defending the color. I got angry at his persistence to defend the color even after I explained again that color wasn't the issue.  He walked away.  The only car we buy are Toyotas. My son down south has a Prius. My son up north has two Toyotas. All I ever owned since the 70's has been Toyota. My driveway has the 2017 RAV, a 1998 RAV, and a 2010 Prius.  I had a Camry I finally sold. It had 398,000 mile on the original engine. I do a lot of driving and I count on Toyotas. I tell you this because I am going to sell the Prius and donate the old RAV and then buy one more Toyota to compliment my RAV 2017 but I will not buy from Toyota of Santa Barbara. Up date #2 (#1 update below) They sold me a Lo-Jack for $600 but failed to tell me that Lo-Jack doesn't cover me where I live).

Didn't like anything!

I got 4 different pastries from there incl carrot cake and a chocolate cake.  Horrible!  I won't be back.  Maybe I should have got cookies.  I gave two stars b/c the service was awesome.  All the food went in the trash.

Not sure who is giving this place 5 stars, but it certainly doesn't deserve it.

While I will give them credit for quantity (almost too much), it doesn't make up for the lack of quality.

I ordered the "Scooter" which was eggs, choice of meat, pancakes, home fries and toast.  That and a cup of coffee was only 10.87, which was a good deal.

I specifically ordered my bacon extra crispy.  It wasn't even close to crispy and I even left some behind (which I never do!).

The over easy eggs were very well done - certainly not what you expect from a restaurant specializing in breakfast.

I will give them credit for the fluffy, huge pancakes. These were probably some of the best pancakes I've ever eaten.

The unfortunate part about the pancakes is that the cook just pours the batter on the grill with whatever scraps are left on the grill.  My pancakes had lots of extra bits of other food in them.  Someone needs to teach the chef that customers can get very sick from cross contaminating food.

I went to this place because of the reviews, but I was very disappointed.

I stopped in hopping to have a good cuban sandwich. But was unable to enjoy the sandwich and beer due to the fruit flys, they were everywhere.  When I asked the bartender about it he just said " ya we have that problem we did get rid of them a while ago but they are back now". I am not sure that is how it should be addressed. I wont ever go back at this point.

Service was slow, and without the busboy, would have been non-existent. Imho, a pizza place needs to have a few great beers on tap, which it didn't.  (Bud light doesn't count!) Pizza was burnt on the crust, a little too sweet on the sauce, and very macabre decor inside. Overall, I would skip this place- only thing was prices were decent, but not worth the wait.

I was in town visiting family, who had just moved into the area, during the holiday season, and I saw on the website that new guests could visit the gym for a free three day trial. "We are happy to let you try out the facility, and we offer promotions that are designed to assist in whatever category you have the most interest. Please complete the form below to receive a FREE 3-day pass and special offers sent directly to your inbox." I was pretty excited to try the gym out and thought that if the gym was awesome, I would refer my brother and his family. I showed up at the gym at 6:30am, it was not busy at all, and was told I would not be able to utilize the trial as I myself am not a local resident with a local ID. This was NOT stated on the website, but, it was stated in small print at the bottom of the pass sent to my inbox. As I had already read the info. on website, I didn't think to double check the fine print on the pass sent to my inbox. In my opinion, good customer service is transparent. Also, I would have thought the front desk person would have been a bit more accommodating as I was already there at 6:30am and, again, the facility was not busy. Needless to say, I will not recommend this gym to my family new to the area.

Visited this place while we are staying at a friend's house near Philly. Wanted to give 1 star but giving 2 stars because sushi was decent. Tempura udon was just.... bland. I have had udon at many places but this was the worst ever. It didn't taste like anything! The tempura that came on the side was bland too. No flavor at all, and it seemed undercooked/under-fried. So disappointing. Price wasn't that great either, :(

What a disappointment.  The burger was well prepared and staff was friendly,  but the  burger was small, the size of a Wendy's dollar meme item.  The onion rings were frozen chopped pieces of onion with lots of batter coating.  I'm sorry to say this was my first and last visit to Zac's.

Paddywagon is fine but it really doesn't hold a candle to some of the similar bars in the area. The outside seating is nice and covered. They have a good selection of beer and really good drink specials. My greatest complaint is about the lack of staff. We decided to pop in for a beer after the Gasparilla Art Festival, and even though there were only a handful of people there it took about half an hour for the three of us to get our drinks. Another annoying element is that even though we were seated at a table we were not served and had to go inside to the bar the place the order.

First time to visit Kelp today. Started off on wrong foot,  was told no military discount during initial greeting. I never seek out places that do give discounts. So Kind of made me feel I was there in search of a hand out,  which I was not. Menu choice a bit lack luster. Lots of cooked items. So if that's not your speed. This place is a skip. Nice enough wait staff,  don't think I would return.

The food is amazing; lots of healthy choices! I typically work late on Wednesdays and go to Panera in the morning to grab food for the day. I love Panera and my Wednesday routine, however it may have to change based on my recent experiences with customer service. Each and every time I visit this location, JOHN, who prepares the food orders is extremely rude. On my last couple of visits my order was prepared incorrectly and/or special requests were not accommodated. When asked for anything, JOHN consistently makes rude remarks and his overall demeanor is generally unpleasant. I will not be visiting this location as often for that reason.

I'll admit we didn't have a pizza.  It was Saturday brunch so I tried the lemon ricotta pancakes and they were just heavy and very sweet.  Not to my liking.  My wife's artichoke salad was OK but not worth the price.  My kid had French toast which was just useless.  The bread was 4 inches think and once you cut it, it mashed down to a dry yuck.  There was no egg applied to the bread that I could sense.  Just a sugary coating cooked onto it.  

The worst part is the noise.  It's just plain loud.  Concrete floors, brick walls, windows, and a plaster ceiling all reflect sound and you can't hear people sitting next to you at normal voice.  Don't bother going there unless you want to yell a conversation or try to go when no one else is likely to be there.  

Since I didn't try the pizza, I might give them another chance on a nice day when I want to sit outside.  That's about it though.  Too loud, expensive, and they mess up the basics.

Past two times here have been mediocre at best. Service is consistently bland & slow. The food quality does not match the price tag. I ordered a pizza that was nearly 20 dollars. The pizza I get at Fired Pie for $8 was better than this one. They got rid of the specialty cocktail menu which was a neat thing they had going on for a while and a plus of going here. Overall, entrees are not impressive, service is poor, and there are just so many better places to go eat dinner in Tucson. This one doesn't stand out at all.

We live practically around the corner from Pita Jungle, but have never really considered going because my husband and myself aren't really hummus/black bean/falaffal eating people.  But it's a new year, and we're trying to open ourselves up to new experiences, so this is where we found ourselves on Friday afternoon.  

The interior is nicely done, very open. The bar (where we ususally hang out) was huge and had some nice flatscreens, one tuned to Sportscenter (what we usually watch), so we were happy so far.  It looked like we got there just as the shifts were changing, because 3 or 4 bartenders were switching out money at the till, and it took a while to get anyone's attention for a drink order.  But we eventually got ourselves a beer and some menus.  My husband had a pasta dish with grilled chicken, and I ordered a hot pita with chicken, veggies and garlic sauce.  At the last minute I requested some tatziki sauce on the side, and boy am I glad I did, because the garlic sauce (paste) was pretty disgusting.  The rest of my pita wrap was pretty good though.  My husband liked the pasta as well, but at $10.99, it was a bit expensive.  

So, we really liked the atmosphere, kinda liked the food, but what we didn't like was the service.  While our bartender was very nice, getting her to refill our drinks was a real struggle.  And we weren't the only ones.  The people sitting next to us had to yell and wave their hands to get their final bill.  

Improve the service and I could see ourselves back for some drinks, but overall, I still don't think we're the hummus/black bean/falaffal eating type.

I find the place strange big rustic mirror on one wall giant medal flowers on another wall. WHY are you trying to sell the art!! This is something highway diners do!!! The food was OK it didn't stand out or make me want to come back. The details seemed to be missed (pits in the olives) simple things that make a difference I asked the server "what beers they had on tap?" she gave me that look and turned my menu over and pointed at the drink section. 

If your place is called "PITA JUNGLE" and you serve Pitas from a box you have to have the amazing service and make sure all the little details are taken care of

Their food here is really good. Boyfriend took me out on a sunday night and the place was not packed but yet me and my boyfriend still had to wait for 10 minutes to be seated? We saw lots of booths and seating area that were empty. Anyways the service could have been better because boyfriend and I saw our waiter standing there talking to her coworkers while we waited about another 15 minutes just to get our check and a box to go.

This was a big disappointment. My husband and I love Thai food, but the food here was average at best. The curry dishes were small and unfulfilling. Also, we were a group of 8 that had to wait for over an hour for our order. When we asked the waiter about it he was indifferent and didn't try to apologise or compensate us.

Came for Restaurant week menu.  No room in the dining room so we ate at the bar.  The 2 times we tried to come both had no reservations available.  

The toasted ravioli is from Lombardos, which is our fav in town.  Unfortunately, they do not compare.  The filling was a hard lump and fell out after the first bite.  Was also overcooked and the sauce they poured over them made it mushy very quickly.

Bread service is nothing special.

The strip steak was overcooked and underseasoned.  Tasted more like a sirloin.

Broccoli was disgusting.  Swimming in a pool of separated cheese/butter and raw.

Risotto was ok.  Could have been a bit more creamy.

Sicilian chicken was ok.  Spicy red sauce was good but chicken was a bit overcooked too.

The cheesecake was surprisingly really good.  Tasted like Hanks.  Whipped cream was nice too.

Our server was really nice and they kept my water filled often.

If you're looking for the latest The Colonel has to offer, you won't find it here. I pop in here for lunch a couple of times a year to see if they've got any of the new things I've seen advertised, but the menu has remained totally unchanged for at least three years. 

Don't be fooled by the big posters outside, either. Though they may advertise something new and different (currently "$5 fill ups" meals), such things do not appear on the menu inside and mentioning them will make the staff look at you like you've grown another head. The most recent addition is the Dip 'Ems combo, a meal intended go showcase KFC's new sauces. This restaurant doesn't actually have any of those though, just the same three mediocre sauces they've always had.

Service is slow except during the lunch rush. Expect to stand around for several minutes before someone gets  around to taking your order.

There's not much else to say. Parking is free and plentiful. The place is reasonably clean. The bathroom is located outside the restaurant and presumably the staff must be asked for the key. I've never used it because I never wanted to trouble the staff. 

The food is as good as any other KFC I've ever eaten at. This one just seems to have stagnated circa 2010. It's not terrible, but this particular franchise is doing nothing to save a dying brand.

We had eggplant parmigiana and lasagna al forno. Eggplant was cooked perfectly but the sauce was heavy and boring and the lasagne was just plain boring. I am alway surpirsed by the ratings that Caruso's gets for food and service. This place is always busy and actually I found the service to be very good. Without a reservation at 6:00 PM, they said about 30 minutes and it was about 45 minutes (our bad - have a reservation). We went next door is the Surly Wench Pub since there was no seating in Caruso's large waiting area for us that night.

Local beer was awesome! The food and complimentary fitters were solid. 

Service was HORRIBLE. This isn't meant to be a rant, but I guess we caught the staff on a bad day. Front of house was rude and our waitress didn't make things any better. I unfortunately won't be coming back here.

We ate at this place on 21st of Sept.  Waitress was fun and was on top of things but that is as far as it goes, we went one time and would not return:  the brisket plate meat had a funny taste, they so smothered it in bbq sauce, but the taste of old meat was unmistakable.  Meatloaf and entertainment were average.  Please don't waste your time

Stopped in here today and ordered off their 3-5 menu. Best part of the whole meal were the Philly Cheesesteak Won-Tons. Fried to a crispy perfection and served with a wiz cheese sauce that had a drop of Sriracha in it. I ordered the Salmon burger which was terrible. Overly fishy tasting and had a slimy film over it and was topped with wads of some sort of gooey green. Texturally it just didn't work. My husband had the burger. It was really dense and mostly dry. Service wasn't anything special. I walked in and had to prompt the man at the podium to speak to me. Our waitress was nice but was rushing away from us before we even finished giving her our orders. She always didn't check back in with us after brining food over. I would not dine here again for the price there are a lot of other nicer places to go out to.

The historical setting is cute and worth a visit. The service was efficient. However, the food, which is ultimately the reason why we came here was mediocre. The sauces from the salad dressing to the sauces in the main dishes tasted packaged and not fresh. We will not be returning or recommending this place.
Went here for a meetup. Eh, not really that impressed with the place. Beer selection was OK, but not great. Even when it wasn't crowded, it took a long time to get a beer. Not really impressed.

Not happy with this place. Came on a Sunday afternoon with my wife and another couple. Server came to take our order and acted annoyed that I didn't realize my burger came with a choice of various side items. It was my first time there, I'm sorry to annoy you with my business! Anyway, drinks came out and we waited, and waited, and waited for our appetizer and lunch entrees. After 35-40 minutes, server comes to us and apologizes for the wait, saying the kitchen is backed up. Ok, no problem. Thank goodness for good company with our friends. Finally, after 45 minutes, we get our appetizer-a deep fried mixture of jalapeños and okra. It was seriously good and came with an incredible ranch/sour cream dip. Definitely the best part of the trip. 

Fast forward to over an hour after placing the order and we finally get our meals. I had the Nash Vegas, a burger said to have pimento cheese amongst other things. That's what sold me, but what I got was a burger with four tiny little dollops of pimento cheese, no bigger than a dime. Ugh, not what I expected. Once we got the check, I was seriously annoyed that the server had charged us 25 cents for a small serving of the ranch/sour cream dip my wife had asked for for her fries. I know 25 cents is trivial, but when we waited all that time for lunch.....I felt the least they could do is NOT charge for something we didn't even know there was a charge for. Hell, I would've even comped the drinks or something just to smooth things over, but alas, I'm but a mere customer.

Could've been great, but the annoyance, the wait, the underachieving burger, and the stupid 25 cent charge....it's unlikely that we'll be back.

I just tried The Cha Cha truck,and I must say I was'nt impressed at all. I mean,I know its street food but I think the food I recieved for lunch today should be the late-night stuff. It was way to small for $3.00 a taco. The tortilla was'nt even warmed up,I guess it was  but not long enough. It started to crumble on my first bite.Which is a sure sign of not being warmed up enough. I got there at 1:00pm and they were already out of hamburgers.Half of downtown does'nt even get out for lunch untill 1:00. I think they could do alot better.I ordered 2 tacos and I'm still hungry. Now if I was stumbeling out of some bar or concert and I was drunk and hungry maybe these tacos would've done the trick,only because I would'nt realize I was getting ripped-off untill the next day! C'mon step up your game. Its going to have to be a whole lot better than today for you guys to make it. I do love the idea though!

Long wait for a table, which we didn't mind at all since we waited at the bar.  Bar service was great, very friendly bartenders with a huge selection of beers.  Finally got seated, the table was dirty and the server took a long time to come over and even take our order.  Never brought us silverware or cleaned the table.  Understand it's a busy night but these are basic tasks you expect from any restaurant.  The food runner had to get us napkins and silverware AFTER our meal came out (got through the appetizer without ever having them).  Server never even came back to get us more drinks, we had to go to the bar to order them because she took so long.  Ordered and finished those drinks before she even looked our way again!  Once our food was done it was at least 30 minutes before she stopped by again and we asked for the check.  Now I get why there was a long wait- they definitely don't value speed of service or turning tables quickly here!  Wouldn't be a bad thing if the servers kept the drinks flowing, but they didn't!  Definitely had potential to be a great spot but unfortunately will be hindered by the poor service.  May go to the bar there again, probably will never eat there again.

Yeah, this hut has great outdoor space. Probably it's only saving grace. I make better jerk chicken and peas and rice. And I'm BAHAMIAN. (if I said "white" it probably would be more emphatic. but, still. just sayin')
Whole foods has better cornbread. And they're WHOLE FOODS. 

The only reason why this place doesn't get one star is because I enjoyed watching my mom dunk her shrimp in her water glass because it was too spicy. And the waitress didn't even flinch. Love it!

I was told that this place was better than my favorite Mexican spot in StL (El Burro Loco - central west end). 

I was not impressed by this place in the slightest. The food is mediocre and lacks depth of flavor. I knew that I was not going to be impressed when I had the salsa that was average at best. 

I ordered the tacos Al pastor and was left underwhelmed. 

The service was fine and prices were fair.

Food took 35 mins and place was dead. Then from the time the first plate out of 8 came to the table to the time the last one came it was about 20 mins. Unfortunately everywhere else had a long wait and we were in a rush... Lesson learned.

Sorry but the place has changed for the worse.  I have been fortunate enough to stay at all the Gaylord's, Orlando, Washington, Dallas, and here in Nashville  more than a few times over the years for business. This location is far from what it used to be. First the resort add-on fee is grossly inflated and self parking prices should come with a full detail for the rate.  
Valet prices is equivalent to a nice dinner, lol. 

Food is subpar and expensive is not a fair word, let's go with ripoff.  Mediocre food at insane prices.  Skeleton staff.  Most of the nicer restaurants are not open every day. Room service add 20% PLUS a delivery fee. Sad because this was once a great place. Staff attitude is also very dry.  Seems they just want the day to end so they can go home.  Much better options for what they charge.
Second and third time ordering were not as good! Tonight was actually terrible!!! There was no flavor to any of it! We will not be ordering from this location anymore!

As a resort this place briefs well and looks really good on the surface, but don't believe the hype.  I've given it a shot 3 times now (hoping I stayed on bad weekends) and all three were disappointments.  Minus the Old Hickory Steakhouse, the food is horrible...carnival quality with white tablecloth prices.  Speaking of prices, you are hit with daily resort fees and outrageous parking add ons.  The housekeeping services are quality, but they do not separate the extended stays from the checkouts.  Bottom line, expect to be woken up early by a motovated housekeeping staff member.  Resort services are lacking (all though you pay for them), simple things like towels available at pools, spa appointments (Crazy Expensive BTW), and checkout options.  As a Marriott regular, I will not stay at this property again.
It was very cute and great tasting but I felt so cluttered. It's very small but you could make it feel so much more spacious if you removed some of the toys and stuff.

The banh mi at Pho Bistro was huge and stuffed with grilled meat of your choice, cucumber, jalapeno, carrot, mayo, onion, tomato, and cilantro.  At under $4 each,  it makes a great meal for hungry students.  The grilled pork and beef had a sweet robust marinade and were more flavorful than the other Vietnamese places in town.  The downside was that the grilled meat had too much char.  A little char and smoke is great for flavor, but in this instance, there were several pieces of meat that I had to pick out because I didn't want to put that much polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons in my body.

The cha gio was generously sized, but the filling had an unappetizing sour note.  I don't know if they purposely seasoned it that way or was it because the filling wasn't fresh.  The nouc mam was nicely concocted, not too watery, with a good balance of salty and sweet.

Mediocrity is what defines this restaurant. It's not downright horrible but nothing makes you stop and say that this restaurant is all that memorable. 

Visually, the sushi looks appealing but, oh, if only the taste matched. I believe the fish is not all that fresh and I've had to, along with my partner, make some trips to the bathroom afterwards.

I didn't care for the meal we had on a Sunday afternoon. My kids and I were in the Plymouth Meeting Mall and decided to try Benihana for Lunch. We enjoy the Hibachi chain of restaurants so we thought this would be as good. It turns out we should have stuck with Hibachi. 

The restaurant wasn't too busy at 2pm and the 3 of us were seated at a table for 8 by ourselves. The waiter came and took our drink orders right away and brought them quickly. He then took the food orders. 

Our chef arrived shortly after wards, but we were disappointed that that Carlos was not the traditional Asian chef. He didn't give us the typical jokes about the foods he was cooking. No flaming grill or onion volcano. No egg role jokes. He managed to drop the spatula a few times during his prep but thankfully it landed on the grill. 

I didn't care for the salad. The dressing seemed a bit bitter with almost no ginger flavor. 

Since it was lunch, the portions were a bit smaller. The kids and I each ordered a chicken dish and the serving appeared to be about the size of half a breast. 

The chef over peppered the veggies but did make a 'beating heart' our of the rice which my daughter found a bit creepy. 

The Teryaki Chicken was more of a lemon chicken but was cooked very well. My spicy chicken had a decent flavor. 

The rice with dinner was very good. 

The 3 of us, with tip was about $45 so the price wasn't bad, but I ended up being disappointed with the meal. I probably won't go back.

My first visit was a week ago, on a Sunday. This was a trip to satisfy my curiosity about this chain. I think the hostess needs to be more attentive. She looked completely zoned out into lala-land, oblivious of hungry and waiting customers. She somehow had lost the waiting list. It took me locating the manager and saying, "Look, she's told us to come back in 40 minutes. We went shopping and came back. I just want to know where we are on the waiting list." (It was frustrating)

Cheap and big portions. If you're both a food lover and an economics geek, the utility curve goes down at the end of your meal as your food gets cold.

Coffee tasted like decaf or watered down coffee. (Oh, I get it! IHOP's got limited coffee beans!) It's funny because it was served in the decaf bottomless pot; the server said, "That's regular coffee."

I had the spinach and chicken crepe, served with hollandaise sauce. The hollandaise sauce was borderline passable, but only with the help of a bottle of ketchup. The first crepe was not bad. My fiance had french toasts. You can't screw french toasts up. It's a safe choice.

We probably won't be rushing back. There are better places to have affordable breakfasts in the city on a Sunday: for example, Koffee Cafe (in Millwoods), Duggan's Boundary Irish Pub. I mean, just search for "breakfast" on Yelp. :)

So the food was average, certainly under cooked. The food however isn't what I had a problem with, it was the service. Walked in and the staff was sitting around not paying any attention. The hostess  couldn't keep her eyes open at all, bumping in to table and even asked a costumer for a chair so she can sit down and pick her nose. While sitting down she fell asleep. Managed to get a picture to prove it. I think you guys need to drug test who you hire.

Went here because it was the only sushi place while we were walking around the mall. I was not impressed by the sushi. It seemed subpar. The teriyaki chicken seemed bland and dry. It could have used a little more teriyaki sauce. The salad that came with the bento box was delicious though. That was the best part of the meal. Overall the experience was 'Meh.' They did offer outdoor seating which is awesome.

For a place called mojito they should probably learn how to make one.  It was terrible. We got the paella which was a bowl of rice with about a dozen mussels on top and some dry chicken. It was not good. The fried avocado appetizer was very good. I wouldn't go back on our next visit. Boy that drink was bad. The person next to us complained about the drink they had and it was not a mojito.

This Gaylord hotel is supposably the biggest nationwide hotel without casino.  I came here for the corporate event and I am not impressed at all.  Sure, this place is massive.  It is equipped with 2000+ guest rooms, shops, restaurants along with several sizable ballrooms and 152 meeting rooms.  

However, almost everything was disappointing.   In my room, it should have been cleaned before I got there.  I found some bugs on my bed.  YUCK!  Also, when I was working late(probably 11PM), somebody knocked a door.  As I wasn't expecting anyone, I just assumed it was next door.  Then, I heard some guy's voice calling some girl's name(I cannot remember the name... could have been Britney, I don't know..) as he unlocked my door.  Then, I screamed.  He apologized and ran away.  Umm.. EXCUSE ME??!!  What was that?  He definitely was one of staff since he seemed to have a master key.  Was he trying to meet other staff at guest's room?  Is that mean some staff are are using guest rooms even before a guest gets there???  This just wasn't acceptable.  This is just horrifying experience.  Also, an iron that I had in my room was totally unusable as it stunk when I turned it on.  Their hair dryer looked like a 80's hair dryer.  I was shocked that it was actually working.  

I used their airport shuttle as they offered the corporate rate for the airport shuttle.(40 bucks for a round trip)  First of all, it seemed to be a rip-off since airport shuttle usually is offered complementary at most hotels.  The biggest problem was that it never runs on time.  It was supposed to run every 30 minutes but I waited for a bus for about 40 minutes.  Also, my colleague waited for 45 minutes.  

Also, restaurants inside the hotel were mostly tasteless and overpriced for what you get.  Sure, I do understand they need to make up for high operating cost.  Then, they should at least offer better service.  Service that I got from restaurants and bars were just substandard. 

As for their meeting rooms, I didn't have any complaints at all.  It was big enough to hold almost 2000 of us and catering staff(not sure they worked for a hotel) seemed fairly professional.  

I wish that my experience was better than what I had.  I probably will not come back here unless I am required to be here.
Tried this place out to test it against the chains. Was disappointed. Bagels were hard, restaurant is dirty, and coffee was average. Sorry the little guys just don't match up.

I went to their Grand Opening today because I misread and was thinking that the 1st 200 member customers would receive $5 off their minimum purchase of $5.  However, the special was to receive an email of $5 (like a coupon) toward a FUTURE purchase at that store only (although the cashier told me that I could use it at any Goodwill).

On the positive, I bought this floral-pictured napkins placement holder of an English collection for $7.

This store, as by its name, has antique-like items of adult clothes and shoes, frames, games, purses, jewelry, etc.  Simply put, the store is unique.  It is located in the same atmosphere as other Broad Ripple stores.

Great that there were 50% off racks.

However, I don't know if I would return there.  I enjoy the typical Goodwill stores much better.

I don't understand how this place is rated as 1 $ in price. Dinner here will cost a single person about $20. I went alone last time and that's what my bill (including tip, probably) came out to. To get my fix of Viet food in IV, this is the place to go. But I've definitely experienced better and for cheaper.

Not impressed. 2 out of 3 of us had a bad dinner. Salads were good, great homemade Minestrone soup. Their veal parm looked like thin slices of spam hiding under the sauce. The other meal was a combo with chicken and something else. Chicken tastes like it had been reheated. The eggplant parm was good. I would go elsewhere next time

I am not a fan.  
 
This hotel was large and confusing (the provided map was worthless, as it didn't show the different levels, or that all the levels don't connect, which makes getting around this huge biosphere-like habitrail ridiculous).  The restaurants were average with short menus and high prices (not many healthy options available). Some of the staff were helpful (when you could find them), and the rooms were clean, albeit small. It's located quite a distance from downtown, so be prepared for a shuttle or taxi ride to get anywhere off hotel property (think Las Vegas scale hotel minus the casino).
 
If we would've been greeted and helped by a bellman, things may have started off on a better foot; however, there were none to be found at 6:00 pm on a Saturday.  I travel frequently for work (as for this trip), and typically go with the flow; however there were too many things that were difficult in this particular location and on this trip to not submit a review.  The front desk agent was professional, but not friendly (at all).  After my 45 minute wait in a Disney-like winding line and an additional 35 minutes of searching for my reservation in a system that had been changed ("Mam, we do NOT have letters in our confirmation codes", despite the email I had received from the hotel earlier that week listing my confirmation code, which clearly contained 3 letters), she finally handed me my key with a map, and looked at the next person in line.  It was basically, "here's your key, here's your map, good luck.  Next."  Still no bellman to help us with our 5 OVERSIZE bags (we're exhibiting at this conference...) or show us to our room. 
 
The hotel is so large, that there should be a brief orientation to tell guests that there's only ONE restaurant (you know, the kind where you sit down and they bring you a menu...) that serves breakfast for all 3000 rooms, or that if you want something other than sushi at the "tapas" bar, you have to be there between specific hours.  Forget a "quick" bite.  The hotel was so full between 2 large national conferences, that the wait in any of their restaurants was no less than 45 minutes (which doesn't help a whole lot if you only have an hour or less in which to eat).  While I can appreciate the beautiful water features and foliage at this hotel, being in a terrarium for 5 days wasn't a replacement (for me) for fresh air.  When we were asked to change rooms on the last night of our stay because it had been pre-booked (and published in the next conference's phone book), it pretty much capped off our already disappointing trip there.  I will advise the conference I attended to steer clear of Opryland in the future.  Hope I never have to go back....

Ouch!  Generally when you spend over $200 for a room they do not then charge you a $15 resort fee which gets you two bottles of water and a dial up speed internet connection. A 8 oz bottle of orange juice is $3.21 in the gift shop!  I can buy a gallon for that price.  This is a nice hotel but they gouge you on the prices. Bring a lot of money if you are going to stay here. Also the room is small and I have a view of the parking lot! I guess someone has to get the third rate rooms.

The food here is decent, but not great...the service was horrible! Sat at the sushi bar and asked the lady about looking at the happy hour menu and she said no and walked away...I then spoke to the sushi chef who just handed me a regular menu. I asked him a second time about the happy hour menu they have advertised on huge signs on their window and he said happy hour isn't from 5-7 anymore, and only until 6 pm. So i ordered a few rolls on the regular menu and a soup and salad...about ten minutes later, two people were seated next to me and had asked for happy hour menus and were able to order at happy hour prices with it being 6:20 pm. At this point during my meal, my check was already brought to the table for some odd reason although I wasn't done ordering or eating and wasn't asked anything at all and I  did inform her I would be getting a few other pieces of nigiri. When I asked why the people next to me were able to order happy hour items and I was not, the lady just sighed and said ok what you want... well, besides my water that you never brought me (although I'm almost done with my meal), I did want a few pieces of the $1 nigiri sushi on special. It's not like I was ordering 20 pieces of Salmon as the people next to me did. I ordered 4 pieces and still got attitude from the lady who still did not want to change the receipt to reflect any happy hour prices...then she just never came back after she charged my card and dropped it back off to me...I'd rather go elsewhere next time regardless of the convenience of this location to my work...

So it's a Tuesday when we decide to order to-go food from here. We would have never had to go here, but our favorite spot, King Wok, down the road, is closed on Tuesdays. 

My boyfriend placed the order to go on the phone and told me they were rude and they said the food would be 20 minutes because they were busy. I didn't see it as a big deal because even though 20 minutes is a long time in Asian take-out land, it's nothing in any other take-out land. 

I walk into the restaurant and the guy on the phone wasn't lying, it was busy. But when we order to-go food from King Wok, it is always busy and they always have our food ready for us at the door. There was a young kid checking me out at the front and he honestly had no idea what he was doing. He had to ask each customer that was paying their bill what hey had ordered instead of having some type of ticket system with the other waiter. It was a disaster. I added on two drinks to my order. My iced coffee came out immediately, then I waited about 10-15 minutes more for the food, granted it took us 15-20 minutes after my boyfriend ordered to even get to the restaurant. When the food came out, they had forgotten to make my frozen drink, so then I had to wait another 5 minutes to get that. 

When we got home, I asked my boyfriend if their vermicelli dish and spring rolls were even comparable to King Wok's. He replied that it wasn't. All would have been forgiven if the food was better, but it wasn't. Needless to say, we will be resisting our Vietnamese urges on Tuesdays and won't be returning.

I chose this place to have a birthday dinner for a friend. The food was great. But I wasnt impressive with the service at all. We had a party of 13, only 2 was running late, they did not allow us to be seated. This is not the place you want to have dinner at for a big party. They seated us in two seperate table. But we was told that we would be seated at a rectangle table that would fit all of us. Now had I known that, I wouldnt even think twice to make a reservation here. The whole entire night, it felt so awkward. It felt like we were seperate groups. The birthday girl had to go back and forth between two table. When the cake came out, the other table felt left out because they werent notified. We spent a pretty decent amount of money here but only to be treated awkwardly? We could have been satistfied somewhere else. I truly did made a bad choice.

I'm absolutely amazed that this place had a line out the door! Felt like I needed to write a review to keep you from wasting your hard earned money. Prices are outrageous as the previously reviews have mentioned - $6.25 for an omelette. Want toast? $1.50. Want fried potatoes with that? $1.75. The food is not even good so I just don't get the line out the door. Maybe this is the only decent breakfast spot on Carmel? I'd recommend putting the extra $ toward gas and drive south for a breakfast in Indianapolis. I ordered the breakfast sandwich. Menu said the eggs were fried over well. Told the waitress I'd like them fried hard which I thought was the same thing anyways. She repeated the fried hard part back and then brough out over easy eggs. Urgh. Extra star is for its location on the Monon. So at least you can walk off the grease.

Was really looking forward to coming here and finally had a chance during downtown dining week for lunch. Made a reservation at 11 so we could beat the lunch rush. Got a potato leek soup, and had the risotto with braised short rib and my friend got the bison meat loaf. Soup was ok (lukewarm), meats were really tender, risotto was average. The meat loaf tasted like it had char siu sauce in it, interesting! After we finished our entrees the waiter asked if we would like to see the dessert menu and we said yes. He returns 2 minutes later and says "Actually, since you guys didn't have a reservation we will need to have this table back for 12... which is in 5 minutes". I told him we did indeed have a reservation, but he said well either way we need this table because it gets really busy here at noon. Um... what?! Did we, two polite, paying customers just get kicked out?!? With a 5 minute warning?!!? We were getting along with our waiter quite well.. It's one thing if we didn't have a reservation or if they told us they had a reservation at noon when we sat down...  I don't hold it against the waiter and he was obviously told my his boss that this needed to happen but I've never had an experience like this in my life and the food was not good enough for us to be returning here.

This isnt a bar that i have ever heard of people going to because there was a desire to go there, rather more people go as a result of circumstance. 

Literally the only bar that you can see from 30th, close to center city and in university city. As a result its usually packed for happy hour but dead every other time of been there.

In short its overpriced. One dollar off happy hour beers... making a happy hour beer $5-6... what is happy about that. Landmark down the street has $3 beers.

lunch will cost you $15+.  Not really sure what the justification is as there are dozens of food trucks and places with lunch specials in the area.

The service is slow, the bartenders dont seem terribly interested in you, and they do things at thier own pace. 

Juke box is way too loud. When someone picks a song that is all you will hear, somce its an odd mox of patrons get ready for country, screamer music, dirty rap... alternating, at a very high volume.

My brother and I have been dining here since they opened. Generally the food has been great. Only significant concern? Cooks get flustered with orders and have great difficulty in getting all plates ready and out at the same time.  We usually go every weekend for breakfast between 7:30am and 9:30am. Not a huge crowd but there were always the same faithful faces we see each weekend.
   Well, now we will never go again. On mother's day morning I go about 9:30am for breakfast and get stopped at the door by a young female who says breakfast is not until 10am. I asked since when. She said for two weeks now. I hate it when some clueless waitress gives a canned incorrect answer as if it was my first time there. Told her not true, I was here last weekend.. I asked why the change. She replied that most people are in church Sunday morning.
    Not let me get this straight, On mother's Day, you decide to turn away your breakfast crowd (two carloads of women were leaving when they found out no breakfast), the one day you can get scores of new customers. You stereotype that black folk are in church Sunday mornings, (no ideal where they figure folks are at Saturday morning), even though Kizzin Cuzzins, Bob Evans and Cracker Barrel are slammed on the weekends for breakfast starting at 7AM. No one at Sylvia's thought to have the waitresses tell there weekend breakfast regulars in advance that their hours were changing?  That's a slap in the face to loyalty. I will not be returning. Really regretful, but that level of disregard for loyal customers is unacceptable.

Food is great as original Katie's in Rock Hill but it is disturbingly loud in there - terrible acoustics - we will not return until management corrects this huge issue. It is also unhealthy for their workers who should be equipped with noise cancelling headphones to avoid hearing loss. Wow! The loudest dining experience I have ever had the displeasure of enduring.

Average food! There is some sort of animosity from Lao owners toward Thai people? After learning we are Thai the service went through the floor. Waitress took our order then didn't see her again until time to leave! Tiger tear was below average. Nothing special here!

I walked in and was so glad they could take me right away since I was leaving for vacation the next day and needed a bikini wax.  The woman who waxed me was super nice but didn't seem like she really knew what she was doing.  It was painful and waxes never bothered me before. She also had to do areas multiple times which I found odd.  When she was done, I checked the area to find obvious hairs still there.  She said she missed them and was sorry.  I wasn't impressed at all.  The hair grew back very quickly like stubble as if I had shaved.  Other waxes lasted me weeks but this grew in within a week and I ended up having to shave anyway on my trip.  Would not be going back.  The people at the desk were trying to sell me packages before I even walked in...didn't like the vibe there.  Sorry-will not be going back!  Prefer the old school waxes!

Visited Chesheim after three visits and I unfortunately have to say it was horrible. To start, my bf asked to taste two beers on tap. The waiter showed up with full size beers that we decided to drink anyway because he clearly didn't listen to the request. We visited with my daughters and asked for a kids brunch menu. He let us know he could make any brunch menu item in a kids portion. He showed up with regular portions when the food came out. We asked for the check and realized we were charged adult prices for their meals. I asked him about it and he shrugged and said, "yeah".... After waiting 15 minutes he finally came back and ran our card for the bill. Service is very important to me and I have to say this visit is enough for me not to return. The restaurant was not full at all during peak brunch hours. There is a reason for that. I won't be surprised if they are not in business for another six months.

I have had both dinner and breakfast here.  First, the service wasn't bad.  Beyond that, however, there is not much to recommend here.  The seafood pasta dish was loaded with lobster and scallops, but the sauce was thin and lacked any flavor at all.  The clam chowder was ok, but noting special.  The Key Lime pie was unremarkable.  As for breakfast, the hot cakes were very good, but the seafood omelet was a real disappointment.  There was shrimp, but there was also plenty of fake crab (the menu listed it as "crab") and it was covered with a slice of melted american cheese.  If you operate a restaurant on the Gulf coast, why are you using crab sticks instead of the real deal?  It also came with a biscuit that was anything but southern.  

There are plenty of dining options in the Largo/Clearwater area that are far better.

My sons friend recommended this place. We were excited to eat some good Italian food but it was really disappointing. One plate came out with one out of two manicotti half cooked and another plate came out barely warm. The lasagna was good and the eggplant was really tasty. We love desserts but didn't finish either one. The staff was really nice, but don't think we would go back.

I wanted to love it but I don't. It was a good experience since it was built 1865. It's adorable and unique. The food was not impressive. We had the Tri-Tip sandwich, onion rings and chili fries. The tri-tip was too salty. Service was fast but we did go at 11:30 am on a Thursday because it does get very crowded maybe it's the ambiance because I don't think it's the food.

Usually I really enjoy coming here but today was below par with their normal service.  Firstly, the waitress spilled ice water twice on my cousin.  We were pretty upset about that but kept our cool.  Instead of bringing the manager right away, the waitress kept standing there.  When the manager came he said the waitress would be right over to assist us but she didn't come for about 10 minutes.  Then a second manager came and gave us a iron brewery shirt.  Why do we need the shirt? The waitress didn't even bring our replacement water until I asked for it and they charged us instead of taking off the bill what my cousin are because their waitress spilled water twice on her.  My experience here was very bad and not sure what changed because i usually got good service here.

My two stars are for the service -- very nice and accommodating. However the bathroom and area just before the bathroom (which looks like a play area for the workers' children) are really too gross. My husband almost couldn't eat imagining what the kitchen may look like. Both my husband's Pho and mine (different types) were surprisingly lacking in flavor. I had to add outrageous amounts of soy sauce and chili sauce to eat mine, and I'm a Pho lover. I can't bear to go back.

I went to Makiman tonight.  Service sucked, sushi was, at best, mediocre.  I don't know what possessed me to get "Mama's roll", but it was inedible.  I did manage to eat the rest of the other rolls.  The sushi was just kinda sloppy and definitely didn't taste fresh.  Best part of the dish was the pork dumpling soup as an appetizer.  I have high standards because Umai Umai is my favorite place on earth...this wasn't in the same league.  Will not go back.

I've stayed at a few ES and I wouldn't put this one on my favorite list. The best thing is the overall staff. Most were really friendly and helpful. The guy who took care of housekeeping in the bar and common areas was really great and helpful. The evening manager was really good and made you feel like he cared about any issues you had. Now...as for the room. A contest for housekeepers to finish first. Gee, what could go wrong there? That was the biggest downside. It's a VERY busy hotel and it seems to me they are greatly understaffed. Housekeeping became a constant battle of half cleaning, not changing the sheets when asked, forgotten trash and just plain rush jobs. While it wasn't filthy, you could tell the room just needed a really good cleaning. Doesn't look like the bathroom floor in our room had ever been washed, and the shower and sink had what looked like mold in areas. You don't expect that at an ES. We had an issue with something in the room that could have caused injury and the manager sent someone right away, who just looked at it and said there wasn't anything he could do. We didn't feel like repacking, changing and cleaning another room so I had to summon some brain power and come up with a simple solution. If we had really needed to use that piece of furniture it would have been an issue. That I did not like at all. I don't want to have to use my brain on vacation! The clerk didn't explain the Hilton Honors benefits when we checked in so we missed out on some stuff until the trip was almost over. Make sure you ask for the benefits for Honors members. In comparison to other properties, the Manager's Reception could definitely use some imagination and an upgrade on the food. I know they want you to use the restaurant but fried pickles ain't cuttin' it. **Note to management: Some people have parental brain freeze. Closing the reception area on Sundays somehow allows parents to think (I use that word liberally) it's okay to bring their kids to the bar. Having a 7 yr old crawl up onto the stool beside me made me and the other grown ups "uncomfortable" to say the least. You might want to consider opening the reception or suggesting to mom and dad the bar is not the best place for kids to play tag. Lost a lot of bar money as we adults looked elsewhere to watch football. I didn't eat at the restaurant but others who did really liked the food, except for the one night when it was pretty busy and my friend's burger was dust bowl dry. For $12 you don't want to eat saw dust. The vent in our room had an odor like chlorine. By our last day it was pretty unbearable. I certainly wasn't going to call maintenance and have to think again. At that point they would have needed to offer me the job, if I was going to have to keep fixing stuff. The business center was good and well stocked. They even have a small conference room that I'm not sure if you have to pay to use. I can't tell you how many business trips that would have been great to have for quick meetings. With few exceptions, the staff is their strong point.  I say fix the housekeeping issue and I'd raise the rating much higher, even with fried pickles.

I love the service of this place, they were very friendly. However, their food was kinda bland. It wasn't spicy and made me feel full but unfulfilled with what I ate. The place made me feel empty, although, the servers were kind I wasn't a huge fan of their food. I recommend other places for Vietnamese cuisine.

A group of us visited Bacchanal during a recent trip to New Orleans. After visiting several other Yelp-endorsed restaurants, I went with very high hopes. 
  This is a "food-court" style restaurant (you go to several locations throughout the building to select and order your food), but the restaurant is set outdoors ("backyard-style") instead of inside a shopping mall. 
When you arrive, you select your wine and appetizers (great cheeses, etc) in the front room. Then you find a picnic table (in the "back yard" of the house).  You place your order at a "to-go" style window at the back of the restaurant. (Tip: Be sure to give the cashier at the window a sufficiently large tip, lest you be pelted with an overly sarcastic "Thank You SOOOO very much!!!" -- which happened to the gentleman in front of me.  This surprised me a bit, as I was not originally planning to leave a huge tip for the window cashier, either. )    
   The food is delivered to your table in "drips-and-drabs" so if you want to eat with the rest of your group, expect to wait. (Fortunately, the live music is really pretty good!)  The food, once it arrived, was not too bad! (Much tastier than your typical food-court fair.)

Tip:  As in other food courts, solicitors and vendors tend to be everywhere.  Bring extra cash for them. (Because there is no cover charge, the bands also typically "pass the hat" -- be sure to get the attention of the "hat-passer" though. On our visit, he actually walked passed four patrons trying to contribute to his cause!)

Tip: Bring bug spray. You are dining outdoors, and the Mississippi River is across the street. Be prepared for visitors!

Tip: Most importantly, do not park in the dirt strip across the street (south) from the restaurant -- this is an unmarked "No parking" zone, and you will get ticketed.  After leaving the restaurant, we saw a line of about 20 cars (including ours) with $40 citations. Apparently, this is a very common place to park -- but the restaurant mentioned nothing about this when we arrived. (In similar situations, I've known restaurants to go to great lengths to keep their customers happy and eager to return.) A simple sign or warning inside the restaurant, such as "Don't Park Across The Street" would have been extremely welcome. 

Overall, I'm not a huge fan of the Food-Court dining experience. But the food was really pretty good --  my favorites were the Abita Root Beer, which is hard to get at home; and the Point Reyes Blue Cheese!

Spoiler:  We didn't like it, didn't each much of it, and probably won't be back.

We may have ordered the wrong item, but I was looking forward to a good breakfast burrito and ordered a chorizo burrito with beans, potatoes and egg. What I got was a tortilla that held 90% chorizo that wasn't well spiced, no beans, and a skimpy amount of potatoes (frozen) and little egg. I did opt for the red chili sauce to smother it and it also quite bland.

My querida ordered chilaquiles and received a container of greasy chips slathered with the too mild chili sauce, shredded iceberg, tiny bit of cheese, and a huge dollop of sour cream. Inedible. 

We took home a dozen tortillas and they are good, but nothing extraordinary in this tortilla rich town.

Best thing about this was the waitress that had been working there 3 days.  Lady at cash register was gruff, cut me off walking at least three times from the moment we walked in the door.  Food was meh at best, grossly overpriced.  $15 Burger overcooked.  Crab cake was tasty but fell out of the gross, floppy bun constantly.  Finally had to shed the bun and eat with fork.  Tasted no Old Bay seasoning on the fries whatsoever.  VERY loud and too tight of quarters inside.  They should have half the amount of tables in there that they do but were adding more as we sat there.  I would never go back to this place again.

I hate to say this, but I think prep and pastry is highly overrated. I have been there several times now, always giving it another chance since everyone seems to like it so much. The last and final attempt was at the fried chicken and waffles - the chicken was crunchy but raw inside. Raw, not cooked rare. The manager informed us that that is how the chicken is supposed to look because this chicken doesn't have hormones... ? I've never seen chicken served raw and I know it's certainly not healthy, but I think the manager's attitude about it was what bothered me the most. 

Every other meal I have had here was just ok, nothing special.

I absolutely hate leaving bad reviews, but I also hate spending $20 on a breakfast I couldn't eat. There was so much butter (Whirl I assume for those in the biz), it was disgusting. Actually made me gag. In all fairness I didn't order off the menu. I was trying to eat healthy. Perhaps they don't drench all their omelettes in grease. The menu did look delicious.

The fruit was good. Friendly server. But she probably should have asked how my food was. I may be back to try something else.

Drove 30 minutes here for the seats to be honest. There was no line on a Tuesday night you could get tickets from a machine or from guest services. Concession was empty as well coke freestyle machines are a nice touch but burnt popcorn not so much. The theaters are nice however the staff is not helpful. 3D glasses are in a bin that we didn't grab or notice until we asked. Seats and bathrooms are gross especially on a Tuesday night. Overall I probably will be back because the boyfriend is easy to please. If you like a clean theater I'd skip. There is a bar didn't try it will update if I do.

This was our second visit. The waitress took our order wrong and brought us Shahi paneer instead of Kadahi paneer, which in most places is a dry dish. The manager insisted that theirs was creamy. (their baingan bharta is also creamy which is uncommon). It turned out not so bad after all. The channa masala, however, was like a BP oil spill. The Kashmiri pulao was also pretty mediocre and oily. The naan and rotis were good. It was overall a bit disappointing, given our first experience was quite good.

I have never been in such a disgusting theater in my life. I understand that it's late on a Saturday night, but when we left, every single garbage bin we passed was overflowing with trash. Garbage was strewn on the  ground. My husband and I went into a movie just after 10 and the trash was overflowing in both bathrooms even then. The bathroom was so gross when we left that I chose to wait the 20 minutes after a lot of soda and over two and a half hours of movie. Maybe it's fine other times of the day, but yuck.

The seats, picture, and sound quality are fine. They need more and/or better cleaning staff.

*sigh* I have been to Caruso's several times and it has gotten to the point that I do not initiate the visits. I cannot understand why this place is so hugely popular. It is a convenient location and has a lovely back patio, but the food is not all that delicious. It's good, but not delicious. I've had good service -- though the wait is horrendous at times -- but I just don't get the fan craze over it.

Our service was great. The atmosphere (though early) was paled by the party on the deck..and it was poppin'! However, the crab cakes weren't very good.
