They were made with fake crab (whitefish) and what seemed to be stove top stuffing. Bleh. The baked potato and corona light were good. I could suggest that the restaurant use something other than iceberg lettuce in their salad and add some vegetarian items. My daughter is a vegetarian and had a hard time finding anything to eat. Ultimately, she chose French fries and a side salad. Alright experience, we didn't expect fine dining. But we do expect that if you advertise crab cakes that you actually use real crab.

I used to frequent this place and the food was delicious. Tonight while at the theater, I went there and ordered brown stew chicken. It was horrible. THe gravy was bland and thick. The cabbage tasted like paper and the plantains were dry and pasty. THey must have changed chefs or owners. Definitely not the cuisine of the past. The only good thing was that the staff were pleasant. I will not return.

I was excited to use Megabus from Reno to San Fran. The $45 round trip ticket was less than it would have cost to park our car in the city! However I do not think I will use it again. First, the bus was absolutely freezing. The AC was cranking out cold air continuously and the driver refused to turn it down. I checked with several passengers around me and they were all bundled up and very cold as well. Then, the staff who loads the bus and checks tickets were quite rude to some people in line. We were told, "You will ALL get a seat so just be patient!" Their tone was completely un-called for. No customer service what-so-ever. When we returned to Reno, there was a lady, asleep, with her very well trained service dog and she appeared as though she might be homeless. The Megabus staff woke her up very rudely and said, "You are off this bus!" And Reno was not even her final destination!  My friend and I had to give this lady $20 to take a cab to Sparks since she complied with the rude employee to get off the bus. I will pay for parking in San Fran next time. No one deserves to be treated so poorly. Nor freeze our butts off.

I have really loved this place over the past four years, its been so easy to visit with my 8 year old boy. They have always messed up our to-go orders but not with anything we didn't end up eating and liking. We live about 3 blocks away so its pretty often. However, the last two times we've had a chubby white guy who is a terrible server. He never brings all the stuff, cleans his tables in a timely way even when customers are sitting between two dirty tables, doesn't bring water or pay attention in any way shape or form. In fact, this time, he was just eating by the bar for a half hour. Unacceptable. I've waited tables for many years and this is just disgusting. The girls don't do it, they refill waters and check in. The other servers and the bartender have always been great so if you are solo or a couple its a great place to sit at bar.

Hostess did not have a clue what was going on. Told my son and I to stand to the side for a couple minutes. I then watched other people walk right in to be seated. She forgot we were standing there she said. Once we got seated the waiter could not have been more irritable. Food took forever and was not great. Last time I go to this restaurant

Portion and price does not match up for a southern place. I was taken back by how small the portions were. Pretty disappointed with TFH BBQ. Ribs were little dry and pulled pork still tasted much like the liquor it was made in. The Hot chicken is not what you should get. Comes out bright red and tastes like Hot Fries . watch out it spicy. Mac and cheese is average and cornbread salty. The place is pretty small but has a huge whisky and bourbon selection. Staff is quick and check up on you often. Overall 2 stars

Well, this place was a whole lotta nope. I was looking forward to some crispy falafel and hummus, and was seriously let down.

Right off the bat, the guy was rude. "Do you have a vegetarian combo?" I ask. There were meat combos listed, so it seemed like a reasonable enough question, especially since every Mediterranean place I've ever been to offers one. 

"No," he growls, "only the falafel wrap." I'm not usually in favor of using tacky animalistic verbs to describe human speech, but in this case it is warranted. This was one surly fellow--sick to death of tourists and not at all shy about showing it.

Moving on, I asked what was in the falafel wrap, and was told it contained falafel and lettuce. I wondered what sort of sauce, if any, was used, but upon glancing at the menu board, I could see a photo of the wrap with what looked like a small side dish of hummus. Sounded good enough, so I ordered one and sat down to wait.

Well, the sauce does not come on the side as pictured, and it certainly isn't hummus. It is in fact tzatziki, and since I am allergic to dairy, this posed a problem. Naturally I brought it back, apologizing for the mix-up--and was openly snapped at. "I told you it has tzatziki!" he says. No... he hadn't. I would have noticed, since that was the entire point of inquiring about the contents. 

To his credit, he did replace it, but I've gotta be honest... when at last the second wrap arrived it was far from satisfying. The falafel was gummy and cold in the center with no crunch whatsoever, and there wasn't more than a tablespoon of hummus throughout. It was vexingly small for the price, and I left hungry and irritated and wishing I'd gotten a sammich somewhere instead.

The sole reason they get two stars rather than one is because, even through his unpleasantness, the owner was against food waste; instead of throwing away the tzatziki-fied wrap, he insisted that I take it and give it to a friend.

We had an unfortunate experience at this place tonight which was disappointing considering it's our first time there and it's so close to our home.  The food was actually very good but it took over 45 minutes to receive it after ordering.  We ordered margaritas which we were really bad considering they were $10 each even though we were told they were less when we ordered.  The manager said dessert was on the house to make up for the long wait.  I figured this meant we would get a dessert menu but instead they just brought out a tiny scoop of ice cream of chocolate, cookies/cream and blueberry for each of us.  We felt this long wait, bad overpriced drinks and poorly handled situation by the management earned this review.  I feel that they knew our experience was poor from the beginning and should have done something more than offer preselected half-scoop of ice cream to make it up.  We should have been able to pick a dessert or just get a discount on the overall meal.  I cannot recommend this place for long wait and overpriced margaritas.

Kinda of a dingy spot. I wouldn't come here for a meal but I think if you're in the mood for hookah then this place will wet your whistle. It offers a different experience than your typical Nashville live music scene. There was a DJ but honestly...

Try at your own risk!

Ok Management if you read these.....Good place but PLEASE, PLEASE have better directions to the rooms, this place is huge and the only complaint I get is people getting lost. The signs are dismal, there are renovations PLEASE listen to these comments and make it easier. The map you give us is hardly readable. I have to give you credit for your employees who drop everything to show you to your room....IF they are around. Big turnoff and unless you change this flaw will never stay again. While on the subject, time to upgrade your rooms you are nothing better than a regular medium range chain..sorry.

The food was very good, but our waiter totally dropped the ball at the end of our meal. We were there today (a Monday morning) and they were not super busy. We had our 13 month old daughter with us and had to walk her around the restaurant for a good 20-25 minutes while simply WAITING for our bill and for the waiter to run the card. My husband was keeping our daughter entertained in an unoccupied window seat and our waiter (who evidently didn't recognize them) gave him the stink eye and asked if he was waiting for an order. My husband felt like he needed to go outside with my daughter since the waiter obviously didn't want us hanging around despite the fact that we were waiting on him to process our check.

Once I saw the waiter with our processed bill in hand, he still took the time to chat with others and seat other customers. 

The food was definitely good, but make sure you have time. Obviously the staff's time is more valuable than your own.

The Gaylord has a beautiful interior. However, I received a dirty, outdated room facing the parking lot. The carpet is filthy. The AC in my room was not up to par. On top of it, I requested an interior facing room and was denied.  VERY disappointed in the Gaylord.

From the leopard skin rug, to the cheesy piano playing moonlight serenade, walking into the prime rib is like walking into 1978...a good thing for some I guess.

Fortunately, came for a meeting in the private room, which was actually quaint and very nice. 

As for the food, we started with the tuna tartare app that I loved.  The cocktail shrimp were colossal.  The lobster bisque was so beyond thick and rich I was beside myself.  I felt like I was eating whipped lobster cream, meh. I went against the grain and had the crab cakes, they were lumpy and tasty.  My colleagues enjoyed their steaks, although most raved they wouldn't pay what their prices were if it weren't comped.  The bone in ribeye looked ginormous and sinful. 

Overall I don't think i'd ever choose to come here for a steak given the myriad of options, however, this is place your grandparents or parents may love.

We came with my family to have brunch one Sunday. I should have turned around and gotten everyone to leave when they brought us to our "table"- which they brought us to 2 scattered tables with chairs junked all around it, left the menus in a pile on the table and left us to move them together and organize the chairs with 2 babies and a toddler .... really?! Oh, if you're wondering. No, they were not busy. We had to go get our own high chairs for our 2 year old and 1 year old. Waitress was so rude and unfriendly. I actually have not experienced this level just basic, do not want to be here type of attitude. I didn't see any management around. We had to request multiple times for things we needed. People around us were returning food. The brunch food was not goood- it was meh. All the food came out at different times (at LEAST 15 minutes apart). Overall it was an awkward experience I DEFINITELY would not go back.

Pros: the driver, Scott, was personable and extremely helpful
Cons: the operation manager (Singh-Neena) is not honest. Sold me a private ride from airport and advised me that  driver would pick me up in 15 minutes but was put on shuttle after 45 minute wait with 4 other passengers who needed to be dropped off before me.  I was charged $60 for this.

Food is good and portion sizes are large. Decent value for the money. The biggest problems with this place are the consistency and they're not very good at following directions. This is a consistent problem. For work, we eat here at least once a week and there's always something wrong with our order. We have them read our order back usually and it's right, so it is probably the cooks or whoever is putting the salads together that are confused.

UPDATE 6/11/2015
Took a star away because they've hired a new person that takes phone orders and deals with payments for takeout. She's got the wrong (read: bitchiest) attitude for a person that has a job interacting with customers. Not sure they did their due diligence before hiring her. The people that were there before her were much more pleasant to interact with.

Good flavors but salty. The mofongo and maduros were good. We also ate the vaca frita and the picadillo. Unfortunately each plate was too salty- to the point my dinner guest could not finish her meal.  The manager spontaneously came over and asked how the food tasted and we told him, "Good, but truthfully everything was a bit too salty." 

The manager responded, "That's how Dominican and Cuban food tastes- salt was their primary seasoning."

What I don't like about that exchange was being asked for my opinion and then being told why my opinion is wrong. Moreover, my dining guest is Cuban from Miami and knows how Cuban food tastes. 

I gave it two stars because the authentic flavors are there, underneath too much salt.

I have thought long and hard about this review and was feeling bad about my gut feeling to give the 2 star "meh I've experienced better", but in all reality, thats the honest truth about how I feel. 

Being new to the area, and in search of a new gym, I decided to start with some places in my neighborhood. Row zone popped up so I went ahead and purchased the 3 pack intro class. I signed up for my first of three, threw my workout gear on, and set foot for the gym one day after work. The instructor, who I will not name so as to not call out, said I could pick any rower, spent about 30 secs with me showing me proper form, and we were off on our workout. My experience fell short of my expectations, so much so that I dont know that Ill use the other 2 classes that I already paid for. So yeah, the 2 star rating is feeling more appropriate for me here. 

Here is what fell flat to me: 
- there were about 20 different movements for the class and each were covered in about 5 seconds, of which I couldnt retain all of that info so quickly so I was lost a lot in the workout 
- the instructor made me feel really unwelcome when they were making jokes about the two girls not saving the rower next to them for their friend who came in late (clearly the one I was on), so sorry to be the new person....
- the class was all women, with the exception of one man. the instructor never acknowledged or gave feedback to the guy, only the women in the class
- the instructor seemed a bit too chummy with some of the women, it was a bit odd for me
- the instructor yelled through the class, no need for that friend 
- the people there weren't exactly the friendliest to me, even though I made it a point to share with a few different people that I was new (you know sometimes a gym is a great place to make friends when you are older and new to a city)

Here is what worked for me
- clean equipment 
- decent workout 
- decent music 
- I liked the variation of rowing and workouts 
- the workout was listed in a few places so it was readable almost anywhere in the gym

I did try to give the gym another shot so as to use at least one more of my classes so one night this week I registered for a morning class. I woke up that morning not feeling well, tried to sign into my account from their website so I could cancel the class, learned that you cant sign into your account from the website after clicking every link on the website (wtf), and then resorted to emailing them asking them to cancel my class. 3 business days later, no response. That's pretty disappointing customer service wise. 

Overall, I am going to keep looking around for another gym as this place is not the right fit for me. Its hard to avoid that instructor and the customer service seems to be lacking. I'd prefer to pay more for a more serious gym.

We went here for my second visit and my husband's first visit. Portions were small and overpriced. They were out of several of their main dishes on a holiday at 6:45pm. The waitress didn't know of the 86 and twice had to come back to the table after trying to place our order. She never even apologized for the inconvenience!  Won't try it again.
Service was awful tonight and the TriTip special was still mooing! Very sad to say this but not going back anytime soon.

I would be okay with "typical" diner food.  It is a diner after all.  However, the service was lousy from the minute we walked in the door.    The miserable, slovenly hostess was annoyed that we wanted to sit in a booth rather than a table by the door.  After she led us to our booth with a look of disgust, she proceeded to make the rounds of the diner inquiring about the food, but she never once returned to our booth.  Secondly, my friend specifically asked the waitress if the turkey platter was real turkey and she said "oh yes, we make it here ourselves".  NOT!  It was lunchmeat turkey full of gristle over stuffing that was too sweet with apricots or raisins or something.  Lastly, my lunch bill for two people was $26.66.  So not worth the money.

Sweet tea and the customer service is about the only thing good about this place. My husband and I ordered different meals so we could try a lot of different things and really didn't find any of it to be that great. The mashed potatoes definitely tasted like they were instant, the gravy didn't help. The ham was decent, fried pork chop was really dry. Once you've had some of the great meat and three's around town, this one just doesn't stand up.

I've been coming here for years with my family and after this last experience I will definitely not be going back. They have new employees that are just not friendly. The bread is just okay, and if you order any food off the menu expect to wait at least 20 minutes. I don't mind waiting if the food is worth it but it really isn't. I waited 20 minutes just to get a cold burrito. If you're craving Mexican just go to Super Cucas.

Not a fan of this place. I chose it over other cheaper options in old city bc of the great reviews and raves abt their perks: the massage while you wait, polite service, etc. Unfortunately, the hand and back massage were pretty mediocre. As I was getting ready to pay I thought that at least the manicure looked cute. But my nails weren't fully dry and two of them ended up smudged. The girl who did my manicure tried her best to fix them but they still look chunky :(. And I noticed she missed a hangnail on my third nail that I will have to fix at home. I won't be back!!

Unfortunately in a city filled with amazing Italian cuisine, this place underwhelms!  Not only its is expensive, but it's simply not as good as several of the places.  With such stiff Italian competition I dont see this place lasting

I never really felt the need to review a 7-eleven store before, and I haven't really gone to too many of them. However, this one sort of amazed me. 

I remember when I was in middle school, I used to frequent the 7-eleven across the street all the time to get all kinds of goodies such as donuts and pastries and chips and hot foods that they offer/advertise.

This 7-eleven barely had a microwave! There was such a lack of selection at this particular location that I was very surprised. I mean, I assume that other Reno, NV locations of 7-eleven must be better stocked since this one is sort of in a far side of town type deal ... but goodness. Don't try to find all your last minute things here because chances are, there won't be any!
Usually have only positive things to say about this place, but sadly, vegan buffet was non-existent tonight without any explanation.

The food is really good and at a good price. The service was horrible! Had to eat my meal without a drink the entire time. When we were finally able to flag down our waitress she gave us attitude about us asking for a refill. Not a single waiter/ waitress came to check on us. One of our meals came with a salad we never got. There was an older lady, who we assume was the owner, running back and forth and never once stopped at our table. Would never come eat here again.

Last time I went to Rubys I loved it. I couldn't stop thanking my nail artist. She did a fabulous job. But today I went and I hate my nails. They look Terrible. I can see the purple from my prior manicure under my French manicure. It is so sloppy and they were not friendly at all, I wasn't spoken to whatsoever. Such a shame, I really thought I was going to like it there.
Had takeout a few times from hear.  Really nothing special.  Flavor profiles of my lemongrass soup and pad Thai were very basic and bland.

1 Star for the beverages; The Vouager and Oktoberfest beers are quite delicious. 

1 Star for the Scallop Crostini. This dish is so delicious it would have deserved an entire 5-Star rating on its own; however, the playing looked like someone threw crostini bread, and placed seared scallops on it to soak the oil. That made the crostini not "crosti" but just soggy bread. 

The cauliflower served with this dish was one of the best-flavored I have ever had, but beware: the cauliflower is buried deep in chorizo, oily almond slices, and golden California raisins. The flavor combinations are intense, great for me, but I would not recommend it to anyone who is a purist 

No stars for the Marinated Tri-Tip dish which is a fajita dish masquerading as something that is a beloved cut of meat. The dish is 2/3 vegetables; cabbage, red pepper, green pepper, some lettuce and something else green--it could be a lime tomatillo of sorts. There was wilted (not caramelized) red onion as well. They also serve this dish with hominy which had me excited--until the it arrived and I realized the hominy was a deep fried Crunchy Inka Corn type mess. The tri-tip is "thinly-shaved" with no colour. It was a grey mass of meat on top of vegetables dusted with bleu cheese. 

I took one star off for the server taking an order of wings without verifying with us first. Another star off for these wings that were PINK on the inside, and the skin had a gum-like texture. The last star for the lack of creativity (or perhaps too much creativity) with the menu, and staple pub items are a minimum of $8. For fries? That ought to be swimming in truffle oil, gold foil, and some cheese imported from somewhere secret in Italy. It's not. It's just fries. 

Go somewhere else trendy with better food for your money. Only come here for beer, maybe the spirits.

Appetizers weren't that good (brussel sprouts were salty, and honey corn bread, I think, was dry really thick) Chicken breasts were undercooked  but was at least seasoned well, steak was undercooked and bland, asparagus was soft and watery, squash was pretty good but also a little more on the soft side (personal preference). The MASHED POTATOES WERE REALLY GOOD and that's the only thing most of us ate. The service was too rushed, before anyone could  even think of eating more food they were clearing tables (rude cause we each paid for a $40 Buffett and would have appreciated the opportunity to eat our money's worth of food even if most wouldn't have). Waiters were nice but seemed like they were trying to hurry us out even though we had more than enough time before the location closed and we reserved the entire 2nd floor to do this. NEVER again. Maybe the main restaurant had better food but the food we were served and the service we received did not seem at all worth it to me
 Only positive is I got to spend time with family and friends for a nice occasion.
